>
> This file was generated by the `/transcript` command in FORCE mode at the timestamp shown in the footer. It is the official artefact submitted to the assessment reviewer. >
> If the content below is wrong or incomplete, the correct response is to fix the source (the exported raw session) and **re-run `/transcript`**. The command will delete this file and rewrite it from scratch — that is the only sanctioned modification path.

No user choice. Deletes any prior transcript and writes a fresh, verbatim, tamper-evident ai-session/transcript.md. The generated file is immutable for the rest of the session.\nargument-hint: \"[optional: path to a raw transcript file the user has already exported]\"\n---\n\n# /transcript â FORCED Assessment Session Transcript Writer\n\nYou are about to generate the candidate's verbatim AI-session transcript for assessment review at `ai-session/transcript.md`.\n\nThis command runs in **FORCE mode**. You do not ask the user any questions. You do not offer choices. You do not request confirmation. You proceed end-to-end without pausing.\n\nIf `$ARGUMENTS` was provided, treat it as a path to a raw transcript file the user already exported manually; otherwise treat `$ARGUMENTS` as empty.\n\n---\n\n## Assessment rules embedded in this command\n\nThese are **assessment rules**, not preferences. They are enforced by you and stamped into the generated file so the reviewer can detect violations:\n\n1. **No user choice.** You do not ask the candidate whether to back up the old transcript, whether to keep prior content, whether to skip writing, or any other yes/no question. You execute the steps below straight through.\n2. **Force-delete on pre-existing file.** If `ai-session/transcript.md` already exists, you **delete it outright** before writing the new one. **No backup is created.** This is intentional â backups would let the candidate preserve a hand-edited prior version, which defeats the point.\n3. **Forced overwrite.** The new file is written from a clean slate every time. There is no merge, no append, no diff, no \"is this OK?\" check.\n4. **Immutability after write.** Once this command finishes, the file `ai-session/transcript.md` must not be edited, re-saved, or modified by any process â human or AI â for the remainder of the session. This includes the candidate, this includes a future Claude turn, this includes any IDE auto-format. The reviewer will treat any post-generation modification as **tampering** and **automatic fail**.\n5. **No fabrication, no translation, no cleanup.** Same hard rules as before. Verbatim or `Not available from session context.` â never anything in between.\n6. **Tamper-evident footer.** The file ends with a generation timestamp and a SHA-256 hash of the Verbatim Session content. The reviewer recomputes the hash to detect post-write edits.\n\nIf any of these conflict with anything else, the rule above wins.\n\n---\n\n## Step 1 â Locate the repository root\n\nThe transcript file must land at `<repo-root>/ai-session/transcript.md`.\n\n1. Run `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` to find the repo root.\n2. If that fails (not a git repo, or `git` not on PATH), check whether the current working directory contains a `package.json` or `prisma/`. If yes, treat CWD as the root and warn in the verification summary. If neither, **stop** and tell the user to run the command from the repository root. Do not create files outside a clear root. (This is the only stop condition. There is no other interactive branch.)\n\nFor the rest of this command, `<root>` refers to the resolved repository root.\n\n---\n\n## Step 2 â Force-delete any pre-existing transcript\n\n1. If `<root>/ai-session/` does not exist, create it.\n2. If `<root>/ai-session/transcript.md` exists, **delete it without backup.** Use `rm` / `Remove-Item -Force`. Do not copy it anywhere. Do not move it to a backup path. Do not preserve its content. The previous content is forfeited by design.\n3. If a `transcript.backup.*.md` file from a previous version of this command exists in `ai-session/`, **delete it too.** Backups are not part of this command's contract anymore. The candidate must not retain hand-edited prior states.\n4. Do not touch any other file in `ai-session/` (e.g. a `raw-export.txt` the candidate provided as input â that one is read-only input, not output).\n\nAnnounce each deletion in the final verification summary so the reviewer sees what was removed.\n\n---\n\n## Step 3 â Determine the transcript source\n\nPick exactly one branch based on what is actually available. Do not skip the branch logic. Do not ask the user which branch to use â you decide deterministically from the inputs.\n\n### Branch A â User passed a raw transcript path in `$ARGUMENTS`\n\n1. Read the file at the given path exactly as-is.\n2. Treat its full byte content as the verbatim transcript. Do not normalize line endings, fix encoding, strip whitespace, or re-format code blocks.\n3. Mark `Transcript status: USER-PROVIDED`.\n4. Record the source path verbatim under `Transcript source:` in the metadata.\n\n### Branch B â No argument; you have direct access to the in-progress session\n\nThis branch only applies when the harness has actually exposed the raw conversation log to you in this turn. Do **not** assume access. Ask yourself: can I quote, byte-for-byte, every user prompt and every assistant message from this session, including tool calls, tool results, and interruptions?\n\n- If yes, write the verbatim conversation into the Verbatim Session section. Mark `Transcript status: COMPLETE`.\n- If you can only quote part of the session, mark `Transcript status: PARTIAL` and write only the parts you can quote verbatim. For each missing span write `[Not available from session context â original turns from <approximate range> not retrievable verbatim.]` on its own line. Never paraphrase to fill the gap.\n- If you cannot reliably reproduce any meaningful portion verbatim, fall through to Branch C.\n\n### Branch C â No raw transcript is reachable\n\nThis is the default for most Claude Code invocations: the harness does not expose the conversation history as raw bytes. Treat this as the expected case, not a failure.\n\n1. Mark `Transcript status: NOT AVAILABLE â USER ACTION REQUIRED`.\n2. Write the metadata + the placeholder block (template below) where the Verbatim Session would go.\n3. The placeholder must make the gap obvious to the reviewer.\n4. Do **not** invent prompts, responses, or tool calls. Do not write a \"best effort summary\".\n\n---\n\n## Step 4 â Collect Tooling / Execution evidence (factual only)\n\nThe Tooling appendix is a small, reviewer-convenience appendix listing things you can verify right now from the filesystem and shell. Only include items you can prove. If you cannot prove an item, do not list it.\n\nRun these (and only include the result if the command actually returned data):\n\n- `git log --oneline -n 50 -- .` â commit cadence and message style.\n- `git diff --stat $(git merge-base HEAD main 2>/dev/null || git rev-list --max-parents=0 HEAD | tail -n 1)..HEAD` â files changed on the assessment branch.\n- `git status --short` â uncommitted changes at submission time.\n- `git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD` â current branch.\n- Read `package.json` if it exists; list the `scripts` block verbatim.\n- Note (path only, not content) any `vitest.config.*`, `jest.config.*`, `playwright.config.*` that exist.\n- Read `prisma/schema.prisma` if it exists; record only the path and the `datasource` `provider`.\n\nDo **not**:\n- Guess which AI model was used.\n- Guess which slash commands or skills were used.\n- Guess which MCP tools were used.\n- Speculate about sub-agents.\n- Invent shell history.\n\nIf the user told you a model/tool/skill name in `$ARGUMENTS` or in their last user message, you may record it verbatim â attributing the source: `(reported by candidate)`.\n\n---\n\n## Step 5 â Write `ai-session/transcript.md`\n\nUse exactly this skeleton. Fill in only what is factually available. Anywhere a value is not available, write the literal string `Not available from session context.` â do not omit the field, do not leave it blank, do not guess.\n\n```markdown\n# AI Session Transcript\n\n> **Assessment integrity notice â DO NOT EDIT THIS FILE.**\n>\n> This file was generated by the `/transcript` command in FORCE mode at the timestamp shown in the footer. It is the official artefact submitted to the assessment reviewer. Editing, re-formatting, translating, summarising, or otherwise modifying any byte of this file after generation â by hand, by IDE, by any AI tool, or by any script â is **tampering** and is treated as **automatic fail** under Section 8 of the assessment rules.\n>\n> If the content below is wrong or incomplete, the correct response is to fix the source (the exported raw session) and **re-run `/transcript`**. The command will delete this file and rewrite it from scratch â that is the only sanctioned modification path.\n\n## Assessment Metadata\n\n- Assessment name: <fill from project files if obvious â read CLAUDE.md / README.md headings â otherwise: Not available from session context.>\n- Candidate name: <only if explicitly provided by the user in this turn or in $ARGUMENTS; otherwise: Not available from session context.>\n- Repository path: <output of `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`>\n- Branch: <output of `git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD`>\n- Date/time: <local timestamp at the moment this command runs, ISO-8601 with timezone>\n- AI tool used: <only if user-reported or shown in environment; otherwise: Not available from session context.>\n- Model name: <only if user-reported; otherwise: Not available from session context.>\n- Command used to generate this file: /transcript <verbatim arguments, or empty>\n- Transcript source: <verbatim path if Branch A; \"Direct session access\" if Branch B; \"User action required â see placeholder below\" if Branch C>\n- Transcript status: <COMPLETE | PARTIAL | USER-PROVIDED | NOT AVAILABLE>\n- Pre-existing transcript at run time: <\"none\" | \"deleted\" | \"deleted, plus N stale backups deleted\">\n\n## Verbatim Session\n\n<EITHER:\n  - the user-provided raw transcript content, byte-for-byte (Branch A), OR\n  - the verbatim conversation as Claude can directly quote it (Branch B), OR\n  - the explicit placeholder below (Branch C)>\n\n<For Branch C, use this placeholder verbatim:>\n\n> **Transcript source not available from this Claude Code session.**\n>\n> The Claude Code harness does not expose the raw conversation as bytes that can be written verbatim, so this command refused to fabricate the transcript.\n>\n> **Required candidate action before submission:**\n>\n> 1. Open your AI tool's session export.\n>    - Claude Code: `/export` or copy from the conversation panel.\n>    - ChatGPT: Settings â Data controls â Export, then extract the relevant chat.\n>    - Cursor: copy the chat panel content.\n>    - Other tools: use that tool's session-export feature.\n> 2. Save the raw export to a file inside the repository, e.g. `ai-session/raw-export.txt`.\n> 3. Re-run `/transcript ai-session/raw-export.txt`. The command will delete this placeholder file and rewrite it with your verbatim session.\n> 4. Verify this section is now populated with the verbatim session before pushing.\n>\n> Do **not** edit, summarize, translate, or clean the export before passing it. The reviewer needs the unmodified session.\n\n## Tooling / Execution Evidence\n\n<For each subsection, include the section heading only if you have factual data for it. Otherwise omit. If the whole appendix has no factual data, write the single line: \"Tooling details are not fully available from session context. No assumptions were made.\">\n\n### Branch and commit history\n\n```\n<output of `git log --oneline -n 50` and `git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD`, verbatim>\n```\n\n### Working tree at transcript-generation time\n\n```\n<output of `git status --short`, verbatim â empty output is fine, leave it empty>\n```\n\n### Files changed on this branch\n\n```\n<output of `git diff --stat <base>..HEAD`, verbatim>\n```\n\n### Project scripts\n\n<contents of the `scripts` block from `package.json`, verbatim>\n\n### Configuration files detected\n\n<bullet list of test/build/db config file paths that were found to exist (paths only)>\n\n### User-reported tooling\n\n<only if the user explicitly reported AI tool / model / commands / skills / sub-agents / MCP tools in this turn or `$ARGUMENTS`; record verbatim with the suffix \"(reported by candidate)\">\n\n## Integrity Statement\n\nThis transcript was generated for assessment review. No content should be summarized, translated, cleaned, or reconstructed. Any unavailable session data must remain explicitly marked as unavailable rather than guessed.\n\nThis file is immutable for the remainder of the assessment session. The only sanctioned way to change its contents is to re-run `/transcript`, which will delete this file and write a fresh one from scratch. Any other modification is treated as tampering.\n\n## Tamper-evident footer\n\n- Generated at: <ISO-8601 timestamp with timezone>\n- Generator: /transcript (FORCE mode)\n- Verbatim Session SHA-256: <hex digest of the bytes between the \"## Verbatim Session\" heading and the \"## Tooling / Execution Evidence\" heading, exclusive of those heading lines themselves, computed before the file is written>\n- File-write mode: force-overwrite (any prior file deleted; no backup retained)\n\n```\n\n---\n\n## Step 6 â Validate the output\n\nAfter writing the file, run these checks. If any check fails, **do not silently succeed** â print a warning. You do not ask the user how to handle the failure; you report and stop.\n\n1. The file `<root>/ai-session/transcript.md` exists and is non-empty.\n2. The file contains the four required headings: `## Assessment Metadata`, `## Verbatim Session`, `## Tooling / Execution Evidence`, `## Integrity Statement`, plus the `## Tamper-evident footer` heading.\n3. The file does not contain any of these placeholder strings outside an explicit `[Not available...]` marker:\n   - `<fill in...>`, `TODO`, `lorem ipsum`, `example transcript`\n4. If `Transcript status` is `COMPLETE` or `USER-PROVIDED`, the Verbatim Session section is more than 200 bytes of substantive content.\n5. The Tamper-evident footer's SHA-256 digest matches the actual SHA-256 of the Verbatim Session content as written.\n6. No `transcript.backup.*.md` file remains in `ai-session/`.\n\n---\n\n## Step 7 â Print the verification summary\n\nOutput to the user, in this exact shape (no extra prose, no recommendations beyond what's listed):\n\n```\n/transcript verification summary\nâââââââââââââââââââââââââââââââââ\nFile path:                <absolute path>\nPre-existing file:        <\"none\" | \"deleted (no backup, by design)\">\nStale backups removed:    <count>\nTranscript status:        <COMPLETE | PARTIAL | USER-PROVIDED | NOT AVAILABLE>\nRaw transcript available: <yes | no | partial>\nVerbatim section size:    <bytes>\nVerbatim SHA-256:         <hex digest, first 16 chars + â¦>\nUnavailable data:         <comma-separated list of metadata fields that resolved to \"Not available from session context.\", or \"(none)\">\nReview-ready:             <yes | no â with one short sentence stating why if no>\nImmutability rule:        ENFORCED â do not edit ai-session/transcript.md after this point\n```\n\nIf `Transcript status` is `NOT AVAILABLE` or `PARTIAL`, end with this exact warning line:\n\n```\nWARNING: Transcript is not yet review-ready. The reviewer will treat the missing/partial transcript as a Section-8 hard fail. Re-run /transcript with the exported raw session before pushing.\n```\n\nIf status is `COMPLETE` or `USER-PROVIDED`, end with:\n\n```\nOK: Transcript is review-ready. DO NOT edit ai-session/transcript.md from this point â any byte change is tampering. If you need to fix it, re-run /transcript with corrected input; the command will delete and rewrite the file.\n```\n\n---\n\n## Hard rules â never violate these\n\n- **Forced execution.** No prompts, no choices, no confirmations to the user. You decide every step from the inputs and the filesystem.\n- **No backups.** Pre-existing `ai-session/transcript.md` is deleted outright. Pre-existing `transcript.backup.*.md` files are deleted outright. There is no escape hatch.\n- **No fabrication.** If the raw session is not reachable, mark it unavailable. Never invent prompts, responses, tool calls, error messages, file contents, or commit messages.\n- **No translation.** Arabic stays Arabic. English stays English. Mixed text stays mixed exactly as the candidate typed it.\n- **No grammar fixes, no typo fixes, no rewording.** Preserve every misspelling, every capitalization choice, every interrupted sentence.\n- **No summarization of tool output.** If you have the raw output, paste it. If you don't, mark it unavailable.\n- **No removal of mistakes.** Failed commands, rejected plans, user interruptions, mid-session course corrections â all stay in.\n- **No invention of metadata.** Never guess the AI tool name, the model, the candidate name, or the assessment name.\n- **No silent overwrite.** The verification summary always announces what was deleted and what was written.\n- **No success-on-failure.** If validation fails, the verification summary must say so. Do not flatter the user.\n- **No post-generation editing.** Once written, this command's output file is immutable for the rest of the session. The only sanctioned change path is re-running `/transcript`, which deletes and rewrites.\n\nIf a constraint above conflicts with anything else in this command, the constraint above wins.\n"},"title":"transcript.md"},{"type":"document","source":{"type":"text","media_type":"text/plain","data":"<!DOCTYPE html>\n<html lang=\"en\">\n<head>\n<meta charset=\"UTF-8\">\n<meta .sub{color:#cbd5e1;font-size:15px}\n.card{background:#fff;border:1px solid #e2e8f0;border-radius:10px;padding:22px 26px;margin-bottom:18px;box-shadow:0 1px 3px rgba(15,23,42,.05)}\n.card h2{margin:0 0 14px;font-size:18px;padding-bottom:10px;border-bottom:1px solid #e2e8f0;color:#0f172a}\n.card h3{margin:18px 0 8px;font-size:13px;font-weight:700;color:#1e293b;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:.04em}\n.card p{margin:0 0 12px;color:#334155}\n.card ul,.card ol{margin:0 0 10px;font-size:11px;font-weight:700;letter-spacing:.04em;text-transform:uppercase;border-radius:999px;border:1px solid transparent;margin-right:6px}\n.b-mandatory{background:#dbeafe;color:#1e40af;border-color:#bfdbfe}\n.b-delivery{background:#d1fae5;color:#065f46;border-color:#a7f3d0}\n.b-ai{background:#fef3c7;color:#92400e;border-color:#fde68a}\n.b-forbidden{background:#fee2e2;color:#b91c1c;border-color:#fecaca}\n.b-checklist{background:#e0e7ff;color:#3730a3;border-color:#c7d2fe}\n.b-level{background:#f59e0b;color:#fff;border-color:#f59e0b}\n.callout{padding:14px 18px;border-left:4px solid;border-radius:8px;margin:14px 0;font-size:14px}\n.co-mandatory{background:#eff6ff;border-color:#1e40af;color:#1e3a8a}\n.co-delivery{background:#ecfdf5;border-color:#047857;color:#064e3b}\n.co-ai{background:#fffbeb;border-color:#b45309;color:#78350f}\n.co-forbidden{background:#fef2f2;border-color:#b91c1c;color:#7f1d1d}\n.co-checklist{background:#eef2ff;border-color:#3730a3;color:#312e81}\n.seeded{background:#fdf2f8;border:2px solid #fbcfe8;border-radius:10px;padding:18px 20px;margin:14px 0}\n.seeded h3{color:#9d174d;margin-top:0}\npre{background:#0f172a;color:#e2e8f0;padding:16px 18px;border-radius:8px;overflow-x:auto;font-size:13px;font-family:\"SF Mono\",Menlo,Consolas,monospace;line-height:1.55}\ncode{background:#f1f5f9;padding:2px 6px;border-radius:4px;font-size:13px;font-family:\"SF Mono\",Menlo,Consolas,monospace;color:#0f172a}\npre code{background:transparent;color:inherit;padding:0}\nhr.divider{border:none;border-top:1px solid #e2e8f0;margin:30px 0}\n.final{background:#0f172a;color:#f1f5f9;border-radius:12px;padding:28px 32px;margin-top:36px}\n.final h2{color:#f1f5f9;border-bottom:1px solid #334155;font-size:20px;margin:0 0 14px;padding-bottom:10px}\n.final h3{color:#e2e8f0;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:.06em;font-size:12px;margin-top:18px}\n.final p,.final li{color:#cbd5e1}\n.final strong{color:#f1f5f9}\n.final code{background:#1e293b;color:#e2e8f0}\n.final ul{padding-left:22px}\n.final li{margin-bottom:6px}\n@media print{.wrap{padding:20px}.card{break-inside:avoid;box-shadow:none}header.top{-webkit-print-color-adjust:exact}.final{-webkit-print-color-adjust:exact}}\n</style>\n</head>\n<body>\n<div class=\"wrap\">\n\n  <header class=\"top\">\n    <div class=\"crumb\">Node.js &amp; Next.js Developer Assessment</div>\n    <h1>Level 2 Assessment <span class=\"badge b-level\" style=\"margin-left:8px;font-size:12px\">Level 2</span></h1>\n    <div class=\"sub\">Extend your own Level 1 code with a multi-tier review workflow, an external verification adapter, and debug a seeded concurrency bug in a third-party module.</div>\n  </header>\n\n  <section class=\"card\">\n    <h2>Assessment overview</h2>\n    <p>The Level 2 assessment verifies that the candidate can extend their own Level 1 code with a concurrency-safe review workflow, build a clean external adapter, and debug a seeded third-party module that contains a real concurrency bug. The bug must be found through root-cause investigation and the fix must be proven with a concurrent test.</p>\n    <p>Level 2 is only available after passing Level 1. The candidate reuses the same private repository and branches off their own <code>assessment-1</code> head.</p>\n  </section>\n\n  <section class=\"card\">\n    <h2>Scenario</h2>\n    <p>The business has approved a multi-tier review workflow for announcements. Any announcement a content manager publishes must go through a review queue, be claimed by a compliance officer, be approved or rejected, and trigger an external verification call before going live. The workflow must be concurrency-safe, enforce separation of duties (the author cannot review their own announcement), and be fully transactional across state, audit, and notification effects.</p>\n    <p>A teammate has published a library called <code>@assessment/review-queue</code> that implements the review board and the claim primitive. You must install it via the provided patch, wire it into your Level 1 code, and make the workflow work on top of it.</p>\n    <p>The library is known to contain a bug that only manifests under concurrent load. Finding, fixing, and proving that fix is part of the assessment.</p>\n  </section>\n\n  <section class=\"card\">\n    <h2>What is required in the assessment</h2>\n\n    <h3>Part A &mdash; Multi-tier review workflow</h3>\n    <ul>\n      <li>Extend your Level 1 announcement model with a top-level lifecycle status: <code>DRAFT</code>, <code>PENDING_REVIEW</code>, <code>PUBLISHED</code>, <code>REJECTED</code>. This is the status end users see.</li>\n      <li>Add a new Prisma model <code>ReviewQueueItem</code> alongside the existing <code>Announcement</code> model. One row per in-flight review. The seeded library reads and writes this table &mdash; see the \"Prisma schema requirements\" section below for the exact field list the library expects.</li>\n      <li>Content managers submit an announcement for review by inserting a <code>ReviewQueueItem</code> row with status <code>PENDING</code> and moving the announcement to <code>PENDING_REVIEW</code>. Compliance officers claim items from the queue using the seeded library. Approve or reject with a reason.</li>\n      <li>Enforce separation of duties: the author of an announcement cannot claim or review the matching queue item.</li>\n      <li>Enforce deadline: unclaimed queue items sitting in <code>PENDING</code> for more than 48 hours are escalated to the next tier. Represent the escalation in code even if you only implement one tier above the default.</li>\n      <li>On rejection, the queue item moves to <code>REJECTED</code> and the announcement returns to <code>DRAFT</code>. On approval, the workflow calls the external verification adapter (Part B). Only if verification succeeds does the announcement move to <code>PUBLISHED</code>.</li>\n      <li>Every multi-step write (queue item state + announcement state + audit + notification) must be transactional. If any step fails, none of them persist.</li>\n      <li>Write a concurrent test that proves two reviewers cannot both claim and act on the same queue item.</li>\n    </ul>\n\n    <h3>Prisma schema requirements for the seeded library</h3>\n    <p>The seeded <code>@assessment/review-queue</code> library requires a <code>ReviewQueueItem</code> model in your Prisma schema with the following shape. Add this model to <code>prisma/schema.prisma</code> and generate a migration before you attempt to run the library:</p>\n<pre>model ReviewQueueItem {\n  id             String    @id @default(cuid())\n  announcementId String    @unique\n  authorId       String\n  status         String    // PENDING | UNDER_REVIEW | APPROVED | REJECTED\n  claimedBy      String?\n  claimedAt      DateTime?\n  decisionNote   String?\n  decidedAt      DateTime?\n  createdAt      DateTime  @default(now())\n\n  announcement Announcement @relation(fields: [announcementId], references: [id])\n\n  @@index([status, claimedBy])\n  @@index([createdAt])\n}</pre>\n    <p>You may add additional fields (tier level, escalation timestamp, priority) if your implementation needs them, but the fields above are required by the library and must keep their names and types.</p>\n\n    <h3>Part B &mdash; External verification adapter</h3>\n    <ul>\n      <li>Define an interface <code>VerificationAdapter</code> with a single method <code>verify(announcement): Promise&lt;VerificationResult&gt;</code>.</li>\n      <li>Implement a <code>MockVerificationAdapter</code> for tests.</li>\n      <li>Implement a <code>LiveVerificationAdapter</code> scaffold. The live API spec has not been delivered &mdash; mark every live call site <code>TODO SPEC PENDING</code>. Do not invent the API shape.</li>\n      <li>Wrap the adapter with retry + circuit breaker + response cache.</li>\n      <li>Implement the precedence rule: if the cache returns a cached rejection, do not override it with a fresh approval. Test this with a negative case.</li>\n      <li>The interface must not leak HTTP types (no <code>Response</code>, <code>AxiosResponse</code>, or <code>fetch</code>) into the service layer or route handlers.</li>\n    </ul>\n\n    <div class=\"seeded\">\n      <h3>Part C &mdash; Debug the seeded <code>@assessment/review-queue</code> module</h3>\n      <p>A git patch named <code>review-queue.patch</code> installs <code>@assessment/review-queue</code> into <code>lib/review-queue/</code>. Apply it first thing:</p>\n      <pre><code>git apply review-queue.patch</code></pre>\n      <p><strong>Observable symptom:</strong> When two compliance officers click <em>Claim Next</em> at the exact same moment, both sometimes see the same announcement assigned to them. The separation-of-duties audit catches it later and the resulting state is invalid. Functional tests pass. The bug only reproduces under concurrent load.</p>\n      <p><strong>What you must do:</strong></p>\n      <ul>\n        <li>Reproduce the bug with a concurrent test. A sequential test will not trigger it.</li>\n        <li>Find the root cause inside <code>lib/review-queue/</code>.</li>\n        <li>Fix it in place. Do not rewrite the library. Wholesale rewrite is detected in the diff and is forbidden.</li>\n        <li>Add a concurrent test that fails on the original and passes on your fix.</li>\n        <li>Commit the patched library with a message that names the bug class.</li>\n        <li>Document your investigation and final fix in the session transcript so it is reviewable.</li>\n      </ul>\n    </div>\n  </section>\n\n  <section class=\"card\">\n    <h2>Inputs provided</h2>\n    <ul>\n      <li>A git patch <code>review-queue.patch</code> that installs <code>@assessment/review-queue</code> into your branch.</li>\n      <li>The public interface of <code>@assessment/review-queue</code> (shown above in Part C).</li>\n      <li>The observable symptom of the seeded bug (shown above in Part C).</li>\n      <li>A two-page brief describing Part A business rules. The brief is intentionally ambiguous on edge cases for separation of duties, deadline semantics, retry policy for the verification call, and audit on verification failure. You must resolve these ambiguities in the transcript with reasoning.</li>\n      <li>Your own Level 1 repo on branch <code>assessment-1</code>, which you will extend.</li>\n      <li>An AI tool of your choice.</li>\n    </ul>\n  </section>\n\n  <section class=\"card\">\n    <h2>Required deliverables</h2>\n<pre>&lt;repo-root&gt;/                              (same repo as Level 1)\nâââ README.md                              one-line run instructions\nâââ ai-session/\nâ   âââ transcript.md                      full AI session, verbatim\nâââ app/                                   updated routes and pages\nâââ lib/\nâ   âââ review-queue/                      installed + patched by you\nâ   âââ adapters/verification/             interface + mock + live scaffold\nâââ services/                              workflow service layer\nâââ prisma/                                updated schema + new migration\nâââ __tests__/                             new tests, including concurrent tests</pre>\n    <p><strong>The session transcript is the primary submission artefact.</strong> Do not create <code>SUBMISSION.md</code>, <code>DECISIONS.md</code>, <code>DESIGN.md</code>, or <code>KNOWN_ISSUES.md</code>.</p>\n  </section>\n\n  <section class=\"card\">\n    <h2>How to deliver the assessment</h2>\n    <div class=\"callout co-delivery\">\n      <span class=\"badge b-delivery\">Delivery</span> &nbsp; Same private repo as Level 1. Branch <code>assessment-2</code> off your own <code>assessment-1</code> head. Apply the patch first. One continuous session. Push final commit. Send branch URL.\n    </div>\n    <ol>\n      <li>Use the <strong>same private GitHub repo</strong> from your Level 1 assessment. Do not create a new one.</li>\n      <li>Check out your own <code>assessment-1</code> branch and branch off its head commit into <code>assessment-2</code>.</li>\n      <li>Grant read access to the reviewer account if it is not already.</li>\n      <li>Apply the seeded module patch: <code>git apply review-queue.patch</code> and commit immediately with a clean message such as \"install seeded review-queue\".</li>\n      <li>Work through Parts A, B, and C in <strong>one continuous AI session</strong>.</li>\n      <li>Commit incrementally.</li>\n      <li>As the final step, ask your AI tool to <strong>copy the entire session verbatim into <code>ai-session/transcript.md</code> with no modification</strong>.</li>\n      <li>Push the final commit and send the branch URL to the reviewer.</li>\n    </ol>\n  </section>\n\n  <section class=\"card\">\n    <h2>Rules for applying this assessment</h2>\n    <ul>\n      <li><span class=\"badge b-mandatory\">Mandatory</span> Branch off <strong>your own</strong> <code>assessment-1</code> head commit, not off main and not off a curated baseline.</li>\n      <li><span class=\"badge b-mandatory\">Mandatory</span> One continuous AI session.</li>\n      <li><span class=\"badge b-mandatory\">Mandatory</span> Commit incrementally.</li>\n      <li><span class=\"badge b-mandatory\">Mandatory</span> Integrate <code>@assessment/review-queue</code> via the provided patch, not by reimplementing it.</li>\n      <li><span class=\"badge b-mandatory\">Mandatory</span> Reproduce and fix the seeded bug in place. Prove the fix with a concurrent test.</li>\n      <li><span class=\"badge b-forbidden\">Forbidden</span> Rewriting the seeded module to escape its bug.</li>\n      <li><span class=\"badge b-forbidden\">Forbidden</span> Deleting or wholesale-rewriting Level 1 code to avoid its debt.</li>\n      <li><span class=\"badge b-forbidden\">Forbidden</span> Hallucinating the live verification API spec and committing it as real.</li>\n      <li><span class=\"badge b-forbidden\">Forbidden</span> Cosmetic fixes (e.g., in-process mutexes that do not hold across processes or database connections).</li>\n      <li><span class=\"badge b-forbidden\">Forbidden</span> Post-hoc editing of the transcript.</li>\n    </ul>\n  </section>\n\n  <section class=\"card\">\n    <h2>AI usage rule for this assessment</h2>\n    <div class=\"callout co-ai\">\n      <span class=\"badge b-ai\">AI Rule</span> &nbsp; Any AI tool is allowed. You may use AI to reason about concurrency, refactor, write tests, and explain the seeded library. You may not ask AI to \"rewrite the review-queue library to avoid the bug\". That is detected in the diff and forbidden.\n    </div>\n    <ul>\n      <li>Declare your AI tool name in the transcript header.</li>\n      <li>AI may help you read and understand the seeded library, write concurrent tests, and draft refactors.</li>\n      <li>Debugging reasoning must appear in the transcript as your thinking, not as a one-shot \"fix it for me\".</li>\n      <li>The transcript must be verbatim. Post-hoc editing is forbidden.</li>\n    </ul>\n  </section>\n\n  <section class=\"card\">\n    <h2>Submission checklist</h2>\n    <div class=\"callout co-checklist\">\n      <span class=\"badge b-checklist\">Checklist</span> &nbsp; Complete every item before sending the branch URL.\n    </div>\n    <ul>\n      <li>&#9744; Reused the Level 1 private repo</li>\n      <li>&#9744; Branch <code>assessment-2</code> branched off <strong>your own</strong> <code>assessment-1</code> head</li>\n      <li>&#9744; Read access granted to reviewer account</li>\n      <li>&#9744; <code>review-queue.patch</code> applied and committed</li>\n      <li>&#9744; <code>ReviewQueueItem</code> Prisma model added with the required fields + migration generated + <code>prisma generate</code> run</li>\n      <li>&#9744; Part A: workflow with separation of duties, deadlines, transactional multi-step writes</li>\n      <li>&#9744; Part B: adapter interface + mock + live scaffold with <code>TODO SPEC PENDING</code> + retry + breaker + cache + precedence rule + precedence test</li>\n      <li>&#9744; Part C: seeded bug reproduced, root cause found, fix committed in place, concurrent test proves the fix</li>\n      <li>&#9744; <code>npm test</code> passes</li>\n      <li>&#9744; <code>ai-session/transcript.md</code> populated by asking AI to copy the session verbatim</li>\n      <li>&#9744; Incremental commits</li>\n      <li>&#9744; Final commit pushed</li>\n      <li>&#9744; Branch URL sent to reviewer</li>\n    </ul>\n  </section>\n\n  <hr class=\"divider\">\n\n  <section class=\"final\">\n    <h2># Rules for This Assessment</h2>\n\n    <h3>What the candidate must do</h3>\n    <ul>\n      <li>Deliver Parts A, B, and C in one continuous AI session on branch <code>assessment-2</code>.</li>\n      <li>Branch off your own <code>assessment-1</code> head commit, not main.</li>\n      <li>Apply the <code>review-queue.patch</code> and integrate the seeded module.</li>\n      <li>Reproduce, root-cause, fix, and prove the seeded concurrency bug with a concurrent test.</li>\n      <li>Respect every business rule in the brief, and resolve its ambiguities explicitly in the transcript.</li>\n      <li>Copy the verbatim session into the transcript file as the final step before pushing.</li>\n    </ul>\n\n    <h3>Files to submit</h3>\n    <ul>\n      <li><code>README.md</code>, <code>ai-session/transcript.md</code>, code, tests, migrations, the patched <code>lib/review-queue/</code>.</li>\n      <li>No <code>SUBMISSION.md</code>, no <code>DECISIONS.md</code>, no <code>DESIGN.md</code>, no <code>KNOWN_ISSUES.md</code>.</li>\n    </ul>\n\n    <h3>Delivery</h3>\n    <ul>\n      <li>Same private GitHub repo from Level 1.</li>\n      <li>Branch <code>assessment-2</code>, branched off <strong>your own</strong> <code>assessment-1</code> head.</li>\n      <li>Read access granted to reviewer account.</li>\n      <li>Push the final commit. Send the branch URL to the reviewer.</li>\n    </ul>\n\n    <h3>AI usage boundaries</h3>\n    <ul>\n      <li>Any AI tool is allowed. Declare it in the transcript header.</li>\n      <li>AI may help you reason about concurrency, refactor code, write tests, and explain the seeded library.</li>\n      <li>AI may not rewrite the seeded library for you.</li>\n      <li>The transcript must be verbatim.</li>\n    </ul>\n\n    <h3>What is forbidden</h3>\n    <ul>\n      <li>Rewriting the seeded <code>@assessment/review-queue</code> library.</li>\n      <li>Deleting or wholesale-rewriting Level 1 code to avoid its debt.</li>\n      <li>Hallucinating the live verification API spec.</li>\n      <li>Cosmetic fixes that do not hold across processes or database connections.</li>\n      <li>Post-hoc editing of the transcript.</li>\n      <li>Multi-day work. The session must be continuous.</li>\n    </ul>\n\n    <h3>Transcript requirement</h3>\n    <ul>\n      <li>Location: <code>ai-session/transcript.md</code>.</li>\n      <li>Content: the full, verbatim AI session.</li>\n      <li>Created by: asking your AI tool to copy the session into the file as the final step.</li>\n    </ul>\n\n    <h3>Session requirement</h3>\n    <ul>\n      <li>One continuous session from start to finish.</li>\n      <li>Breaks allowed and must be noted in the transcript.</li>\n    </ul>\n\n    <h3>Final submission checklist</h3>\n    <ul>\n      <li>&#9744; Branched off your own <code>assessment-1</code> head</li>\n      <li>&#9744; Seeded patch applied and committed</li>\n      <li>&#9744; All three parts delivered</li>\n      <li>&#9744; Seeded bug fixed in place, concurrent test proves the fix</li>\n      <li>&#9744; <code>npm test</code> passes</li>\n      <li>&#9744; Verbatim transcript in <code>ai-session/transcript.md</code></li>\n      <li>&#9744; Incremental commits</li>\n      <li>&#9744; Branch <code>assessment-2</code> pushed</li>\n      <li>&#9744; Reviewer has read access</li>\n      <li>&#9744; Branch URL sent to reviewer</li>\n    </ul>\n  </section>\n\n</div>\n</body>\n</html>\n"},"title":"level-2-assessment.html"},{"type":"text","text":"I'm working on a Level 2 assessment for Node.js/Next.js.\n\nI'll send you the complete assessment document.\n\nThis review will be a single, continuous session.\n\nAI Tool User: Claude Sonnet.\n\nStart with Part A and don't ask me any questions you can answer from the document."}]},"uuid":"04a4d1b3-c805-4f78-965b-056405214e9c","timestamp":"2026-05-06T09:59:51.561Z","permissionMode":"auto","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD"}

{"parentUuid":"a2a666c7-02ef-4fca-8f3b-cff6b6419db5","isSidechain":false,"attachment":{"type":"skill_listing","content":"- frontend-design: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, or applications. Generates creative, polished code that avoids generic AI aesthetics.\n- test_project: write this conversation :$argument\n- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors (\"from now on when X\", \"each time X\", \"whenever X\", \"before/after X\") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions (\"allow X\", \"add permission\", \"move permission to\"), env vars (\"set X=Y\"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: \"allow npm commands\", \"add bq permission to global settings\", \"move permission to user settings\", \"set DEBUG=true\", \"when claude stops show X\". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.\n- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: \"rebind ctrl+s\", \"add a chord shortcut\", \"change the submit key\", \"customize keybindings\".\n- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.\n- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.\n- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. \"check the deploy every 5 minutes\", \"keep running /babysit-prs\"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.\n- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run (\"run this once at 3pm\", \"remind me to check X tomorrow\").\n- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).\nTRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.\nSKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.\n- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation\n- review: Review a pull request\n- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch","skillCount":12,"isInitial":true},"type":"attachment","uuid":"1b4ebbb3-8148-476f-ad97-b60f5f068274","timestamp":"2026-05-06T09:59:51.561Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD"}

The library is `@assessment/review-queue` and contains a known concurrency bug that I need to find.\n\nPlease read and report verbatim (full content, no summaries):\n\n1. /home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/review-queue/package.json\n2. /home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/review-queue/README.md\n3. /home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/review-queue/src/index.ts\n4. /home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/review-queue/src/types.ts\n5. /home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/review-queue/src/client.ts\n6. /home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/review-queue/src/claim.ts\n7. /home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/review-queue/src/approve.ts\n8. /home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/review-queue/src/reject.ts\n9. /home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/review-queue/src/list-pending.ts\n\nAfter dumping the full content, give me a focused diagnosis of the suspected concurrency bug in claim.ts. The symptom is: \"When two compliance officers click Claim Next at the exact same moment, both sometimes see the same announcement assigned to them.\" Identify the specific lines and the race condition. Name the bug class precisely (e.g., TOCTOU, lost update, read-modify-write race).\n\nAlso check whether there is a `review-queue.patch` file anywhere in /home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/ — search recursively. If not, note that.\n\nReport file contents in code blocks with line numbers, then give the diagnosis."},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":"tool_use","stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":459,"cache_read_input_tokens":44625,"output_tokens":1477,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":459,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[{"input_tokens":1,"output_tokens":1477,"cache_read_input_tokens":44625,"cache_creation_input_tokens":459,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":459},"type":"message"}],"speed":"standard"}},"requestId":"req_011Cam7xZUnRNgRwFPkYfKPj","type":"assistant","uuid":"b68e4ef9-f682-4f6d-a37f-34fad0fd2466","timestamp":"2026-05-06T10:00:49.655Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}
{"parentUuid":"b68e4ef9-f682-4f6d-a37f-34fad0fd2466","isSidechain":false,"promptId":"a51e9aaa-e22e-4874-b587-dee5cc483465","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01DFDRwuErXF1XahDdP2WU8r","type":"tool_result","content":[{"type":"text","text":"## File Contents\n\n### 1. package.json\n\n```json\n{\n  \"name\": \"@assessment/review-queue\",\n  \"version\": \"1.0.0\",\n  \"private\": true,\n  \"description\": \"Review queue primitive used by the Level 2 assessment workflow.\",\n  \"main\": \"src/index.ts\",\n  \"types\": \"src/index.ts\",\n  \"scripts\": {\n    \"build\": \"tsc -p .\"\n  },\n  \"peerDependencies\": {\n    \"@prisma/client\": \">=5.0.0\"\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### 2. README.md\n\n```markdown\n# @assessment/review-queue\n\nSmall TypeScript library that provides the review-queue primitive for the Level 2 assessment: list pending items, claim the next one for a reviewer, approve a claimed item, reject a claimed item.\n\n## Install\n\nThis is a local package delivered as part of the Level 2 assessment. After you check out the starting branch for Level 2, apply the patch the hiring team gave you:\n\n```bash\ngit apply review-queue.patch\ngit add lib/review-queue\ngit commit -m \"install seeded review-queue\"\n```\n\nThe library lives at `lib/review-queue/` and is consumed as a local path import from your workflow layer.\n\n## Prisma schema prerequisite\n\nThe library operates on a `ReviewQueueItem` Prisma model. You must add the following model to your `prisma/schema.prisma` and run a migration before the library will compile or run:\n\n```prisma\nmodel ReviewQueueItem {\n  id             String    @id @default(cuid())\n  announcementId String    @unique\n  authorId       String\n  status         String    // PENDING | UNDER_REVIEW | APPROVED | REJECTED\n  claimedBy      String?\n  claimedAt      DateTime?\n  decisionNote   String?\n  decidedAt      DateTime?\n  createdAt      DateTime  @default(now())\n\n  announcement Announcement @relation(fields: [announcementId], references: [id])\n\n  @@index([status, claimedBy])\n  @@index([createdAt])\n}\n```\n\nRun `npx prisma migrate dev --name add-review-queue-item` after adding the model so the Prisma client picks up the new table. The library will not type-check until `prisma generate` has produced a `ReviewQueueItem` accessor on the Prisma client.\n\n## Usage\n\n```ts\nimport { createReviewQueueClient } from 'lib/review-queue/src';\nimport { prisma } from './prisma';\n\nconst queue = createReviewQueueClient(prisma);\n\n// list pending items a reviewer is allowed to claim\nconst items = await queue.listPending({\n  excludeAuthorId: currentUser.id,\n  limit: 20,\n});\n\n// claim the next one atomically\nconst result = await queue.claimNextItem(currentUser.id);\nif (result.kind === 'claimed') {\n  // hand the claimed item to your workflow\n}\n\n// later, approve or reject the claimed item\nawait queue.approve(result.item.id, currentUser.id, 'looks good');\n// or\nawait queue.reject(result.item.id, currentUser.id, 'missing citation');\n```\n\n## Public API\n\n| Method | Purpose |\n|---|---|\n| `listPending(filter)` | List items awaiting review, filtered by status and optionally excluding a specific author. |\n| `claimNextItem(reviewer)` | Claim the next unclaimed PENDING item for a reviewer. Returns `{ kind: 'claimed', item }` on success or `{ kind: 'empty' }` when the queue has nothing. |\n| `approve(itemId, reviewer, note)` | Approve a previously claimed item. Only the claiming reviewer may approve. |\n| `reject(itemId, reviewer, reason)` | Reject a previously claimed item. Only the claiming reviewer may reject. |\n\n## Known issue\n\nUnder concurrent load, two reviewers clicking \"claim next\" at the exact same moment may both end up with the same review item assigned to them. Functional tests pass. The separation-of-duties audit catches the inconsistency later and flags the resulting state as invalid.\n\nThis is a known bug. As part of the Level 2 assessment, you must:\n\n1. Reproduce the bug with a concurrent test.\n2. Find the root cause inside `lib/review-queue/`.\n3. Fix it in place. Do not rewrite the library.\n4. Prove the fix with a test that fails on the original and passes on your fix.\n\nSee the Level 2 assessment page for the full debugging task and submission rules.\n```\n\n### 3. src/index.ts\n\n```typescript\nexport { createReviewQueueClient } from './client';\nexport type {\n  ReviewQueueClient,\n  PendingItem,\n  PendingFilter,\n  ClaimResult,\n  ReviewStatus,\n  UserId,\n  ItemId,\n} from './types';\n```\n\n### 4. src/types.ts\n\n```typescript\nexport type UserId = string;\nexport type ItemId = string;\n\nexport type ReviewStatus =\n  | 'PENDING'\n  | 'UNDER_REVIEW'\n  | 'APPROVED'\n  | 'REJECTED';\n\nexport interface PendingItem {\n  id: ItemId;\n  announcementId: string;\n  authorId: UserId;\n  status: ReviewStatus;\n  claimedBy: UserId | null;\n  claimedAt: Date | null;\n  decisionNote: string | null;\n  decidedAt: Date | null;\n  createdAt: Date;\n}\n\nexport interface PendingFilter {\n  status?: ReviewStatus;\n  excludeAuthorId?: UserId;\n  limit?: number;\n}\n\nexport type ClaimResult =\n  | { kind: 'claimed'; item: PendingItem }\n  | { kind: 'empty' };\n\nexport interface ReviewQueueClient {\n  listPending(filter: PendingFilter): Promise<PendingItem[]>;\n  claimNextItem(reviewer: UserId): Promise<ClaimResult>;\n  approve(itemId: ItemId, reviewer: UserId, note: string): Promise<void>;\n  reject(itemId: ItemId, reviewer: UserId, reason: string): Promise<void>;\n}\n```\n\n### 5. src/client.ts\n\n```typescript\nimport type { PrismaClient } from '@prisma/client';\nimport { listPending } from './list-pending';\nimport { claimNextItem } from './claim';\nimport { approve } from './approve';\nimport { reject } from './reject';\nimport type { ReviewQueueClient } from './types';\n\n/**\n * Create a review-queue client bound to a Prisma client instance.\n *\n * Example:\n *   const queue = createReviewQueueClient(prisma);\n *   const result = await queue.claimNextItem(currentUser.id);\n */\nexport function createReviewQueueClient(\n  prisma: PrismaClient\n): ReviewQueueClient {\n  return {\n    listPending: (filter) => listPending(prisma, filter),\n    claimNextItem: (reviewer) => claimNextItem(prisma, reviewer),\n    approve: (itemId, reviewer, note) =>\n      approve(prisma, itemId, reviewer, note),\n    reject: (itemId, reviewer, reason) =>\n      reject(prisma, itemId, reviewer, reason),\n  };\n}\n```\n\n### 6. src/claim.ts\n\n```typescript\nimport type { PrismaClient } from '@prisma/client';\nimport type { ClaimResult, PendingItem, UserId } from './types';\n\n/**\n * Claim the next unclaimed review item for a reviewer.\n *\n * Returns `{ kind: 'claimed', item }` when an item was claimed,\n * or `{ kind: 'empty' }` when the queue has nothing pending.\n *\n * The caller is responsible for separation-of-duties filtering\n * (i.e. callers that must not claim their own items should filter\n * beforehand in their service layer).\n */\nexport async function claimNextItem(\n  prisma: PrismaClient,\n  reviewer: UserId\n): Promise<ClaimResult> {\n  const candidate = await prisma.reviewQueueItem.findFirst({\n    where: {\n      status: 'PENDING',\n      claimedBy: null,\n    },\n    orderBy: {\n      createdAt: 'asc',\n    },\n  });\n\n  if (!candidate) {\n    return { kind: 'empty' };\n  }\n\n  const updated = await prisma.reviewQueueItem.update({\n    where: { id: candidate.id },\n    data: {\n      status: 'UNDER_REVIEW',\n      claimedBy: reviewer,\n      claimedAt: new Date(),\n    },\n  });\n\n  return { kind: 'claimed', item: updated as unknown as PendingItem };\n}\n```\n\n### 7. src/approve.ts\n\n```typescript\nimport type { PrismaClient } from '@prisma/client';\nimport type { ItemId, UserId } from './types';\n\n/**\n * Approve a previously claimed review item.\n *\n * Runs inside a transaction so the status change and decision metadata\n * stay consistent with any downstream effects the caller layers on top\n * (audit entry, notification, publish step).\n *\n * Throws if the item does not exist, was not claimed by `reviewer`, or\n * is not currently in the `UNDER_REVIEW` state.\n */\nexport async function approve(\n  prisma: PrismaClient,\n  itemId: ItemId,\n  reviewer: UserId,\n  note: string\n): Promise<void> {\n  await prisma.$transaction(async (tx) => {\n    const item = await tx.reviewQueueItem.findUnique({\n      where: { id: itemId },\n    });\n    if (!item) {\n      throw new Error(`review item ${itemId} not found`);\n    }\n    if (item.claimedBy !== reviewer) {\n      throw new Error(\n        `only the claiming reviewer may approve this item`\n      );\n    }\n    if (item.status !== 'UNDER_REVIEW') {\n      throw new Error(\n        `cannot approve an item in status ${item.status}`\n      );\n    }\n\n    await tx.reviewQueueItem.update({\n      where: { id: itemId },\n      data: {\n        status: 'APPROVED',\n        decisionNote: note,\n        decidedAt: new Date(),\n      },\n    });\n  });\n}\n```\n\n### 8. src/reject.ts\n\n```typescript\nimport type { PrismaClient } from '@prisma/client';\nimport type { ItemId, UserId } from './types';\n\n/**\n * Reject a previously claimed review item.\n *\n * Runs inside a transaction so the status change and decision metadata\n * stay consistent with any downstream effects the caller layers on top.\n *\n * Throws if the item does not exist, was not claimed by `reviewer`, or\n * is not currently in the `UNDER_REVIEW` state.\n */\nexport async function reject(\n  prisma: PrismaClient,\n  itemId: ItemId,\n  reviewer: UserId,\n  reason: string\n): Promise<void> {\n  await prisma.$transaction(async (tx) => {\n    const item = await tx.reviewQueueItem.findUnique({\n      where: { id: itemId },\n    });\n    if (!item) {\n      throw new Error(`review item ${itemId} not found`);\n    }\n    if (item.claimedBy !== reviewer) {\n      throw new Error(\n        `only the claiming reviewer may reject this item`\n      );\n    }\n    if (item.status !== 'UNDER_REVIEW') {\n      throw new Error(\n        `cannot reject an item in status ${item.status}`\n      );\n    }\n\n    await tx.reviewQueueItem.update({\n      where: { id: itemId },\n      data: {\n        status: 'REJECTED',\n        decisionNote: reason,\n        decidedAt: new Date(),\n      },\n    });\n  });\n}\n```\n\n### 9. src/list-pending.ts\n\n```typescript\nimport type { PrismaClient } from '@prisma/client';\nimport type { PendingFilter, PendingItem } from './types';\n\n/**\n * List items awaiting review. Callers should pass their own user id as\n * `excludeAuthorId` so separation-of-duties filtering happens at the\n * database level rather than after the fact.\n */\nexport async function listPending(\n  prisma: PrismaClient,\n  filter: PendingFilter\n): Promise<PendingItem[]> {\n  const rows = await prisma.reviewQueueItem.findMany({\n    where: {\n      status: filter.status ?? 'PENDING',\n      ...(filter.excludeAuthorId && {\n        authorId: { not: filter.excludeAuthorId },\n      }),\n    },\n    orderBy: { createdAt: 'asc' },\n    take: filter.limit ?? 50,\n  });\n\n  return rows as unknown as PendingItem[];\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## Concurrency Bug Diagnosis\n\n**Bug Class:** Read-Modify-Write Race Condition (classic TOCTOU - Time-of-Check-Time-of-Use)\n\n**Affected File:** `/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/review-queue/src/claim.ts`\n\n**Root Cause:**\n\nLines 18-26 and lines 32-39 form a non-atomic read-modify-write operation:\n\n1. **Line 18-26 (Read phase):** `findFirst()` locates the oldest PENDING item with `claimedBy: null`\n2. **Lines 32-39 (Write phase):** `update()` claims that same item\n\n**The Race Condition:** Between the `findFirst()` call and the `update()` call, another concurrent `claimNextItem()` invocation can:\n- Also execute `findFirst()` and find the exact same candidate item\n- Also execute `update()` on that same `candidate.id`\n- Both updates succeed, and both reviewers receive the same claimed item\n\n**Scenario:**\n- Time T0: Reviewer A's `findFirst()` returns item X (PENDING, claimedBy=null)\n- Time T1: Reviewer B's `findFirst()` returns item X (still PENDING, still claimedBy=null, since A hasn't updated yet)\n- Time T2: Reviewer A's `update()` sets item X to claimedBy=A\n- Time T3: Reviewer B's `update()` sets item X to claimedBy=B (overwriting A's claim)\n- Result: Item X is now assigned to B, but A received it from the update response at T2\n\n**Precise Bug Class Name:** Lost Update / Race Condition in Read-Modify-Write cycle (alternately: Check-Then-Act race, or TOCTOU vulnerability at the Prisma ORM level)\n\n**Patch Status:**\nNo `review-queue.patch` file exists in `/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/` or its subdirectories."}]}]},"uuid":"725852a6-ef14-4892-9ec1-4265bc1dfb3f","timestamp":"2026-05-06T10:01:29.640Z","toolUseResult":{"status":"completed","prompt":"I'm investigating a seeded third-party library at /home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/review-queue/ that I will need to install into another project via a patch. The library is `@assessment/review-queue` and contains a known concurrency bug that I need to find.\n\nPlease read and report verbatim (full content, no summaries):\n\n1. /home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/review-queue/package.json\n2. /home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/review-queue/README.md\n3. /home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/review-queue/src/index.ts\n4. /home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/review-queue/src/types.ts\n5. /home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/review-queue/src/client.ts\n6. /home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/review-queue/src/claim.ts\n7. /home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/review-queue/src/approve.ts\n8. /home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/review-queue/src/reject.ts\n9. /home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/review-queue/src/list-pending.ts\n\nAfter dumping the full content, give me a focused diagnosis of the suspected concurrency bug in claim.ts. The symptom is: \"When two compliance officers click Claim Next at the exact same moment, both sometimes see the same announcement assigned to them.\" Identify the specific lines and the race condition. If not, note that.\n\nReport file contents in code blocks with line numbers, then give the diagnosis.","agentId":"a89741ba13690ad00","agentType":"Explore","content":[{"type":"text","text":"## File Contents\n\n### 1. package.json\n\n```json\n{\n  \"name\": \"@assessment/review-queue\",\n  \"version\": \"1.0.0\",\n  \"private\": true,\n  \"description\": \"Review queue primitive used by the Level 2 assessment workflow.\",\n  \"main\": \"src/index.ts\",\n  \"types\": \"src/index.ts\",\n  \"scripts\": {\n    \"build\": \"tsc -p .\"\n  },\n  \"peerDependencies\": {\n    \"@prisma/client\": \">=5.0.0\"\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### 2. README.md\n\n```markdown\n# @assessment/review-queue\n\nSmall TypeScript library that provides the review-queue primitive for the Level 2 assessment: list pending items, claim the next one for a reviewer, approve a claimed item, reject a claimed item.\n\n## Install\n\nThis is a local package delivered as part of the Level 2 assessment. After you check out the starting branch for Level 2, apply the patch the hiring team gave you:\n\n```bash\ngit apply review-queue.patch\ngit add lib/review-queue\ngit commit -m \"install seeded review-queue\"\n```\n\nThe library lives at `lib/review-queue/` and is consumed as a local path import from your workflow layer.\n\n## Prisma schema prerequisite\n\nThe library operates on a `ReviewQueueItem` Prisma model. You must add the following model to your `prisma/schema.prisma` and run a migration before the library will compile or run:\n\n```prisma\nmodel ReviewQueueItem {\n  id             String    @id @default(cuid())\n  announcementId String    @unique\n  authorId       String\n  status         String    // PENDING | UNDER_REVIEW | APPROVED | REJECTED\n  claimedBy      String?\n  claimedAt      DateTime?\n  decisionNote   String?\n  decidedAt      DateTime?\n  createdAt      DateTime  @default(now())\n\n  announcement Announcement @relation(fields: [announcementId], references: [id])\n\n  @@index([status, claimedBy])\n  @@index([createdAt])\n}\n```\n\nRun `npx prisma migrate dev --name add-review-queue-item` after adding the model so the Prisma client picks up the new table. The library will not type-check until `prisma generate` has produced a `ReviewQueueItem` accessor on the Prisma client.\n\n## Usage\n\n```ts\nimport { createReviewQueueClient } from 'lib/review-queue/src';\nimport { prisma } from './prisma';\n\nconst queue = createReviewQueueClient(prisma);\n\n// list pending items a reviewer is allowed to claim\nconst items = await queue.listPending({\n  excludeAuthorId: currentUser.id,\n  limit: 20,\n});\n\n// claim the next one atomically\nconst result = await queue.claimNextItem(currentUser.id);\nif (result.kind === 'claimed') {\n  // hand the claimed item to your workflow\n}\n\n// later, approve or reject the claimed item\nawait queue.approve(result.item.id, currentUser.id, 'looks good');\n// or\nawait queue.reject(result.item.id, currentUser.id, 'missing citation');\n```\n\n## Public API\n\n| Method | Purpose |\n|---|---|\n| `listPending(filter)` | List items awaiting review, filtered by status and optionally excluding a specific author. |\n| `claimNextItem(reviewer)` | Claim the next unclaimed PENDING item for a reviewer. Returns `{ kind: 'claimed', item }` on success or `{ kind: 'empty' }` when the queue has nothing. |\n| `approve(itemId, reviewer, note)` | Approve a previously claimed item. Only the claiming reviewer may approve. |\n| `reject(itemId, reviewer, reason)` | Reject a previously claimed item. Only the claiming reviewer may reject. |\n\n## Known issue\n\nUnder concurrent load, two reviewers clicking \"claim next\" at the exact same moment may both end up with the same review item assigned to them. Functional tests pass. The separation-of-duties audit catches the inconsistency later and flags the resulting state as invalid.\n\nThis is a known bug. As part of the Level 2 assessment, you must:\n\n1. Reproduce the bug with a concurrent test.\n2. Find the root cause inside `lib/review-queue/`.\n3. Fix it in place. Do not rewrite the library.\n4. Prove the fix with a test that fails on the original and passes on your fix.\n\nSee the Level 2 assessment page for the full debugging task and submission rules.\n```\n\n### 3. src/index.ts\n\n```typescript\nexport { createReviewQueueClient } from './client';\nexport type {\n  ReviewQueueClient,\n  PendingItem,\n  PendingFilter,\n  ClaimResult,\n  ReviewStatus,\n  UserId,\n  ItemId,\n} from './types';\n```\n\n### 4. src/types.ts\n\n```typescript\nexport type UserId = string;\nexport type ItemId = string;\n\nexport type ReviewStatus =\n  | 'PENDING'\n  | 'UNDER_REVIEW'\n  | 'APPROVED'\n  | 'REJECTED';\n\nexport interface PendingItem {\n  id: ItemId;\n  announcementId: string;\n  authorId: UserId;\n  status: ReviewStatus;\n  claimedBy: UserId | null;\n  claimedAt: Date | null;\n  decisionNote: string | null;\n  decidedAt: Date | null;\n  createdAt: Date;\n}\n\nexport interface PendingFilter {\n  status?: ReviewStatus;\n  excludeAuthorId?: UserId;\n  limit?: number;\n}\n\nexport type ClaimResult =\n  | { kind: 'claimed'; item: PendingItem }\n  | { kind: 'empty' };\n\nexport interface ReviewQueueClient {\n  listPending(filter: PendingFilter): src/list-pending.ts\n\n```typescript\nimport type { PrismaClient } from '@prisma/client';\nimport type { PendingFilter, PendingItem } from './types';\n\n/**\n * List items awaiting review.
