You can now start coding. Start with updating your todo list if applicable\n\nYour plan has been saved to: /home/sameh/.claude/plans/delightful-sleeping-crane.md\nYou can refer back to it if needed during implementation.\n\n## Approved Plan (edited by user):\n# Level 2 Assessment — implementation plan\n\n## Context\n\nThe candidate must extend their Level 1 admin-panel (Next.js 16 / Prisma 7 / PostgreSQL / Vitest) with a multi-tier review workflow, an external verification adapter, and the in-place fix of a seeded concurrency bug in `@assessment/review-queue`. All three parts must land in one continuous AI session on a new `assessment-2` branch, with a verbatim transcript and the seeded module integrated via the supplied patch step. The brief is intentionally ambiguous on edge cases — those resolutions go in the transcript.\n\nThis plan is a roadmap for the implementation phase. Each section names the files to touch, the contract to satisfy, and the verification step. Where the brief is ambiguous I record the chosen resolution under **Resolved ambiguity** so the transcript can quote the same wording.\n\n## Repo state (verified)\n\n- Repo root: `/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment` (the html assessment brief, the README, and the POSTMORTEM live here; the Next.js project lives at `admin-panel/`).\n- Current branch: `main`. `origin/assessment-1` exists; `main` is the merge commit of `assessment-1`. Branching off `main` is structurally equivalent to branching off the `assessment-1` head — that satisfies the spec.\n- `node_modules` has not been installed in this checkout — `npm install` is required before tests/migrations run.\n- `AGENTS.md` warns: this is **not the Next.js you know** — read `node_modules/next/dist/docs/` before writing route handlers. We must `npm install` and consult those docs for any pattern that touches App Router request handling, since assistant training may be stale.\n- `prisma/schema.prisma`: PostgreSQL provider, models `User`, `Announcement`, `AuditLog`, enum `Role { ADMIN, CONTENT_MANAGER, SUPPORT, USER }`. `Announcement` currently has no top-level lifecycle status — Level 2 must add it.\n- The seeded library source lives at `/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/review-queue/` (its own `package.json`, `src/{index,types,client,claim,approve,reject,list-pending}.ts`). **There is no `review-queue.patch` file** anywhere under `/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/`. The brief assumes one exists. Mitigation: the integration commit will copy the directory verbatim into `admin-panel/lib/review-queue/` and commit it with the message *\"install seeded review-queue\"* — functionally identical to applying the patch and the only honest path given the missing file. The transcript will note the missing patch and the substitution.\n- Level 1 transcript declared the model as Opus 4.7 (overriding the user's \"Sonnet\" instruction with a note). Level 2 user instruction is again \"Claude Sonnet\". The Level 2 transcript header will declare **Model: Claude Sonnet (as directed by the candidate)** with a one-line technical note that the harness model is in fact `claude-opus-4-7[1m]` so the transcript does not falsely attribute work to a different family — Level 1 used the same disclosure pattern.\n\n## Resolved ambiguities (record in transcript Section \"Brief ambiguities resolved\")\n\n1. **Separation of duties**. Enforced at three points: (a) submit — author writes their own `authorId` into the queue row; (b) claim — service-layer guard rejects a claim where `queue_item.authorId === reviewer.id`, plus the library's `listPending({ excludeAuthorId })` filter for the UI; (c) approve/reject — the seeded library already enforces `claimedBy === reviewer`. No admin override; an admin who happens to be the author of an announcement still cannot review it. Reason: a single, branch-free invariant is far easier to audit than role-conditional bypasses.\n2. **Deadline / escalation**. A queue item is \"overdue\" when `status = PENDING AND claimedAt IS NULL AND createdAt < now() - 48h`. Escalation = increment `tier` (default 1, escalated tier = 2) and stamp `escalatedAt`. The implementation provides one tier above default. Trigger: an `escalateOverdue()` service function plus a `POST /api/review-queue/escalate` route that runs the same logic on demand. No cron is added (Next.js process scope) — the route is the call surface for an external scheduler.\n3. **Verification retry policy**. Exponential backoff with base 100 ms × 2^n, jitter ±20 %, max 3 attempts. Retry only on transport-class failures (network errors, HTTP 502/503/504, breaker `HALF_OPEN` probes). 4xx and validation errors fail fast and are not retried.\n4. **Audit on verification outcomes**. (a) Adapter returns `{ ok: true }` → announcement → `PUBLISHED`, queue item → `APPROVED`, `AuditLog` action `VERIFICATION_PASSED`. (b) Adapter returns `{ ok: false, reason }` → announcement → `DRAFT`, queue item → `APPROVED` with `decisionNote = \"approved-but-verification-rejected\"`, `AuditLog` action `VERIFICATION_REJECTED`. (c) Adapter throws after retries / breaker open → workflow transaction aborts; queue item stays `UNDER_REVIEW`, announcement stays `PENDING_REVIEW`, `AuditLog` action `VERIFICATION_TRANSPORT_FAILED` (best-effort, written outside the failed transaction). The reviewer can re-approve later.\n5. **Cache precedence**. Cached `REJECTED` never replaced by `APPROVED`. Cached `APPROVED` may be overwritten by `REJECTED`. Cache key = `announcement.id` + `bodyEn|bodyAr|titleEn|titleAr` content hash so editing the body invalidates a stale rejection. TTL: `APPROVED` entries expire after 5 minutes; `REJECTED` entries persist until invalidated by content change.\n\n## Critical files to add or modify\n\n### Schema and migration\n\n- `admin-panel/prisma/schema.prisma`\n  - Add enum `AnnouncementStatus { DRAFT PENDING_REVIEW PUBLISHED REJECTED }`.\n  - Extend `enum Role` with `COMPLIANCE_OFFICER`.\n  - On `Announcement`: add `status AnnouncementStatus @default(DRAFT)`, `publishedAt DateTime?`, opposite relation `reviewQueueItem ReviewQueueItem?`, index `@@index([status])`.\n  - Add full `ReviewQueueItem` model exactly per the assessment spec (id cuid, announcementId @unique, authorId, status string, claimedBy?, claimedAt?, decisionNote?, decidedAt?, createdAt, relation, indexes `[status, claimedBy]` and `[createdAt]`). Add the optional fields the implementation needs: `tier Int @default(1)` and `escalatedAt DateTime?`.\n- `admin-panel/prisma/migrations/0002_review_workflow/migration.sql` — generated by `npx prisma migrate dev --name review_workflow`. Hand-verify it: enum addition for `Role` and the new `AnnouncementStatus`, alter on `Announcement`, create on `ReviewQueueItem`.\n- `admin-panel/prisma/seed.ts` — add a `compliance@example.com` user with role `COMPLIANCE_OFFICER`. Existing announcements stay `DRAFT` to keep the smoke path uneventful.\n\n### Seeded library install\n\n- Copy `/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/review-queue/{package.json,README.md,src/}` → `admin-panel/lib/review-queue/`. Commit immediately with message `install seeded review-queue` so the diff between this commit and the next ones is exactly the bug fix.\n- Add a path entry in `admin-panel/tsconfig.json` (`@review-queue/*`) only if the path import gets ugly — otherwise import via relative path from `services/`.\n\n### Bug fix (Part C)\n\n- `admin-panel/lib/review-queue/src/claim.ts` — replace the `findFirst → update` race with a single atomic operation:\n  - `prisma.$transaction(async tx => { … })`.\n  - Inside the transaction, `tx.$queryRaw\\`SELECT id FROM \"ReviewQueueItem\" WHERE status='PENDING' AND \"claimedBy\" IS NULL ORDER BY \"createdAt\" ASC LIMIT 1 FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED\\`` (Postgres-specific, but Postgres is the project's datasource).\n  - If the row set is empty → `return { kind: 'empty' }`.\n  - Otherwise `tx.reviewQueueItem.update({ where: { id }, data: { status: 'UNDER_REVIEW', claimedBy: reviewer, claimedAt: new Date() } })` and return `{ kind: 'claimed', item }`.\n  - Why this satisfies the brief: row-level lock is held by Postgres across processes and connection pools — meets *\"not cosmetic / holds across processes\"*; `SKIP LOCKED` makes parallel claimers pick distinct rows instead of contending; the patch is surgical (only `claim.ts` changes; no public API change; no rewrite of the library).\n- Commit with message that names the bug class: `fix(review-queue): close TOCTOU race in claimNextItem with FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`.\n\n### Verification adapter (Part B)\n\n- `admin-panel/lib/adapters/verification/types.ts`\n  - `interface VerificationResult { ok: boolean; reason?: string; verdict: 'APPROVED' | 'REJECTED'; verifiedAt: Date }`.\n  - `interface VerificationAdapter { verify(announcement: AnnouncementForVerification): Promise<VerificationResult> }` where `AnnouncementForVerification = Pick<Announcement, 'id'|'titleEn'|'titleAr'|'bodyEn'|'bodyAr'>`. No HTTP types in the public surface.\n  - Internal `class TransientVerificationError extends Error` and `class PermanentVerificationError extends Error` — used only inside the wrapper.\n- `admin-panel/lib/adapters/verification/mock.ts`\n  - `MockVerificationAdapter` accepts a config `{ rule: 'allow' | 'deny' | (a) => Verdict }`. Default rule: APPROVE unless the bilingual body contains the literal token `__deny__`. Used by tests and dev seeding only.\n- `admin-panel/lib/adapters/verification/live.ts`\n  - `LiveVerificationAdapter` skeleton with a `verify` method whose body is a single `throw new Error('TODO SPEC PENDING — live verification API contract not yet delivered')`.\n  - Each potential request site (URL build, body build, response parse) carries its own `// TODO SPEC PENDING` comment so a grep for the marker matches every live call site.\n- `admin-panel/lib/adapters/verification/wrapper.ts`\n  - `class CachedRetryingBreakerAdapter implements VerificationAdapter`.\n  - Composition order: wrapper first checks cache; on miss, calls a retrying call which is in turn guarded by the breaker.\n  - Cache: `Map<string, { entry: VerificationResult; cachedAt: number }>`. Key derivation = `${announcement.id}:${sha256(titleEn+titleAr+bodyEn+bodyAr).slice(0,16)}`. Precedence rule explicit: when storing, if existing entry has `verdict === 'REJECTED'` and incoming has `verdict === 'APPROVED'`, **keep the existing rejected entry**.\n  - Retry: 3 attempts, exponential backoff 100/200/400 ms with ±20 % jitter, only on `TransientVerificationError`.\n  - Breaker: states `CLOSED → (5 consecutive failures) → OPEN → (30 s) → HALF_OPEN → (1 success) → CLOSED`. While `OPEN`, all calls reject with `TransientVerificationError('breaker open')`.\n- `admin-panel/lib/adapters/verification/index.ts` — barrel.\n\n### Workflow service layer (Part A)\n\n- `admin-panel/services/review-workflow.ts`\n  - `submitForReview(announcementId, user)` — must be CONTENT_MANAGER or ADMIN, must be `Announcement.status === 'DRAFT'`. Inside `prisma.$transaction`: update announcement to `PENDING_REVIEW`; create `ReviewQueueItem { status: 'PENDING', authorId: user.id, announcementId, tier: 1 }`; write audit `SUBMIT_FOR_REVIEW`; enqueue notification stub (an `AuditLog` with action `NOTIFICATION_QUEUED` and metadata describing recipient = compliance officers — actual delivery is out of scope for the assessment).\n  - `claimNext(reviewer)` — must be COMPLIANCE_OFFICER or ADMIN; service queries `listPending({ excludeAuthorId: reviewer.id })` → if any, calls `queue.claimNextItem(reviewer.id)` and re-checks `result.item.authorId !== reviewer.id` (defence in depth against the library claiming a self-authored row), throwing `SelfReviewError` if violated.\n  - `approveItem(itemId, reviewer, note, deps)` — `deps.verification: VerificationAdapter` injected. Inside `prisma.$transaction`: read item, run `queue.approve(itemId, reviewer, note)`, then call `deps.verification.verify(announcement)`:\n    - `verdict === 'APPROVED'` → `Announcement.status = PUBLISHED`, `publishedAt = now()`, audit `VERIFICATION_PASSED`, notification stub.\n    - `verdict === 'REJECTED'` → `Announcement.status = DRAFT`, audit `VERIFICATION_REJECTED` with `reason`, notification stub.\n    - thrown error → transaction rolls back; outside the transaction we write best-effort `VERIFICATION_TRANSPORT_FAILED` audit and rethrow.\n  - `rejectItem(itemId, reviewer, reason)` — inside `$transaction`: `queue.reject` + `Announcement.status = DRAFT` + audit `REJECTED` + notification stub.\n  - `escalateOverdue(now = new Date())` — within `$transaction`: `prisma.reviewQueueItem.updateMany({ where: { status: 'PENDING', claimedBy: null, tier: 1, createdAt: { lt: now - 48h } }, data: { tier: 2, escalatedAt: now } })`; for each row updated, write `ESCALATED` audit with metadata. Returns `{ escalated: count }`.\n\n### RBAC update\n\n- `admin-panel/lib/rbac.ts` — extend the `Action` union with `'review' | 'submitForReview' | 'escalate'` and the `can()` switch:\n  - `review` → ADMIN, COMPLIANCE_OFFICER.\n  - `submitForReview` → ADMIN, CONTENT_MANAGER.\n  - `escalate` → ADMIN.\n\n### Routes (App Router)\n\nEach handler follows the existing pattern in [admin-panel/app/api/announcements/route.ts](admin-panel/app/api/announcements/route.ts) — auth → RBAC → JSON parse → Zod → service call → JSON response. Status codes: 200/201 success, 401 unauth, 403 forbidden, 404 not found, 409 state conflict, 422 validation, 500 fallback.\n\n- `admin-panel/app/api/announcements/[id]/submit-for-review/route.ts` (POST)\n- `admin-panel/app/api/review-queue/route.ts` (GET — list pending excluding self-authored)\n- `admin-panel/app/api/review-queue/claim/route.ts` (POST — claim next)\n- `admin-panel/app/api/review-queue/[id]/approve/route.ts` (POST — `{ note }`)\n- `admin-panel/app/api/review-queue/[id]/reject/route.ts` (POST — `{ reason }`)\n- `admin-panel/app/api/review-queue/escalate/route.ts` (POST — admin only)\n\nFor each route: read `node_modules/next/dist/docs/` first (see AGENTS.md) and confirm the App Router signature for `params` (Level 1 already uses `params: Promise<{ id: string }>` — same pattern).\n\n### UI\n\n- `admin-panel/app/admin/review-queue/page.tsx` — server component, lists pending items the current reviewer is allowed to claim; only renders when `can(user, 'review')`.\n- `admin-panel/app/admin/review-queue/ReviewActions.tsx` — client component with **Claim Next**, **Approve**, **Reject** buttons; posts to the routes above.\n- Update `admin-panel/app/admin/announcements/[id]/edit/EditForm.tsx` to expose **Submit for review** when `status === 'DRAFT'` and `can(user, 'submitForReview')`. The status badge replaces the implicit \"live\" assumption.\n- Update `admin-panel/app/admin/announcements/page.tsx` list to show `status`.\n- Update `admin-panel/app/admin/layout.tsx` to add a \"Review queue\" sidebar link visible only when `can(user, 'review')`.\n\n### Tests (Vitest)\n\nReuse the existing pattern in `__tests__/announcements.test.ts` (mock-based unit tests). Add the following:\n\n- `__tests__/review-queue-claim-race.test.ts` — DB-backed integration test (Postgres). Seeds 1 PENDING row; spawns N concurrent `claimNextItem` calls via `Promise.all`. Asserts exactly 1 returns `kind:'claimed'` and the rest return either `kind:'empty'` or fail to claim. **The assessment requires this test fail on the original library and pass on the fix.** Skipped by default unless `DATABASE_URL` is set, since pure-mock cannot exercise real lock semantics. The test file declares the skip condition explicitly and the transcript records both runs (pre-fix red, post-fix green).\n- `__tests__/verification-precedence.test.ts` — unit; constructs the wrapper with a stub adapter; first call returns REJECTED, second call returns APPROVED with the same content key; asserts the wrapper still returns REJECTED (cached precedence). Negative case: when the body is edited (cache key changes), the new APPROVED is honoured.\n- `__tests__/verification-breaker.test.ts` — unit; transient errors trip the breaker after threshold; calls during OPEN reject without invoking the adapter; after the half-open delay one call probes through.\n- `__tests__/workflow-separation-of-duties.test.ts` — unit on the service; author cannot claim or approve their own item — expects `SelfReviewError`.\n- `__tests__/workflow-transactional.test.ts` — unit on the service; force the audit write to throw and assert the announcement status did not change. Uses `prisma.$transaction` with a mocked client that simulates rollback.\n\n`npm test` must pass all suites; the race test runs only with `DATABASE_URL` set, which the README documents.\n\n### Documentation and submission artefacts\n\n- `admin-panel/README.md` (root README at repo level — Level 1 uses the repo-root README, no `admin-panel/README.md`) — add Level 2 sections: new run flow, new routes, env vars, mock adapter usage, race-test caveat.\n- `ai-session/transcript.md` — verbatim Level 2 session, generated by `/transcript` as the final step. Header declares model \"Claude Sonnet\" per candidate instruction with a one-line technical disclosure that the harness model is `claude-opus-4-7[1m]` (matches Level 1 pattern). Includes investigation log for Part C: hypothesis, repro (a concurrent `Promise.all` over `findFirst+update`), discovery of the read-modify-write window, fix design (FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED inside a tx), test results before and after.\n- No `SUBMISSION.md`, `DECISIONS.md`, `DESIGN.md`, or `KNOWN_ISSUES.md` — the brief forbids them.\n\n## Implementation order (commits, smallest first)\n\nEach commit is tight and reversible. Commit messages follow Level 1's terse style.\n\n1. `chore: branch from assessment-1; install seeded review-queue` — copy seeded files in.\n2. `feat(schema): add AnnouncementStatus, ReviewQueueItem, COMPLIANCE_OFFICER` — schema + migration + seed update + `npx prisma generate`.\n3. `feat(rbac): add review/submitForReview/escalate actions` — `lib/rbac.ts` + tests.\n4. `feat(verification): adapter interface, mock, live scaffold` — `lib/adapters/verification/{types,mock,live,index}.ts`.\n5. `feat(verification): retry+breaker+cache wrapper with precedence` — `wrapper.ts` + `verification-precedence.test.ts` + `verification-breaker.test.ts`.\n6. `feat(workflow): service layer for submit/claim/approve/reject/escalate` — `services/review-workflow.ts` + unit tests for separation-of-duties and transactional rollback.\n7. `feat(api): review-queue routes and submit-for-review` — six route handlers.\n8. `feat(ui): review queue page and submit-for-review button` — pages + client components + sidebar link.\n9. `test(review-queue): concurrent claim test reproduces the race (red)` — write the failing race test against the un-patched library; commit it red so the bisect shows the moment of fix.\n10. `fix(review-queue): close TOCTOU race in claimNextItem with FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED` — surgical patch to `lib/review-queue/src/claim.ts`; the race test now passes.\n11. `docs(readme): document level 2 routes, env, race-test prerequisite`.\n12. `chore: write verbatim ai session transcript` — generated by `/transcript`.\n\nThe patch-then-fix split (steps 9 and 10) gives the reviewer a concrete diff that proves the test fails before the fix, mirroring the Level 1 buggy-then-fixed health route.\n\n## Verification (end-to-end)\n\nAfter the final commit, run from `admin-panel/`:\n\n1. `npm install` (first time only).\n2. `npx prisma migrate deploy` against a clean Postgres.\n3. `npm run db:seed`.\n4. `npm test` — all suites green when `DATABASE_URL` is set; suites except the race test green even without DB.\n5. `npm run dev` and walk:\n   - Login as `cm@example.com`, create a draft announcement, submit for review.\n   - Login as `compliance@example.com`, claim next, approve.\n   - Inspect the announcement: status `PUBLISHED`, audit chain present.\n   - Repeat with a body containing `__deny__` to exercise mock rejection.\n   - Try to claim an item authored by the same compliance user (after seeding such a row): expect 403 `SELF_REVIEW`.\n6. Manually re-run `npm test` against the un-patched `claim.ts` (revert the `claim.ts` change locally) to confirm the race test fails — record the output in the transcript.\n7. Run `/transcript` once at the very end to write `ai-session/transcript.md` from the live session. Do not edit it after.\n\n## Risks\n\n- **Missing `review-queue.patch`** — addressed by copying the seeded directory verbatim and disclosing the substitution. If the reviewer expects a literal `git apply`, the audit trail (single commit named `install seeded review-queue` and matching tree contents) is still defensible.\n- **Postgres-only `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`** — the schema declares Postgres, so this is safe. If the project ever switches DB engines, the fix would need a portable equivalent (advisory lock or compare-and-swap via `updateMany`).\n- **Race test depends on real Postgres** — without a running DB the test is skipped. README documents this. CI would need a service container; outside scope.\n- **Next.js 16 unfamiliarity** — `AGENTS.md` warns the assistant against guessing. Before each route handler is written, read the relevant doc under `node_modules/next/dist/docs/`. Ditto for any change to `prisma.config.ts` patterns.\n- **Transcript verbatim requirement** — `/transcript` must be the last action, not edited afterwards. No generated docs that the brief forbids.\n","tool_use_id":"toolu_01Gkq3neDFWud52wsBHAyHDp"}]},"uuid":"f795a9d6-cf59-4843-bef0-469e6c1a3d09","timestamp":"2026-05-06T10:07:50.431Z","toolUseResult":{"plan":"# Level 2 Assessment — implementation plan\n\n## Context\n\nThe candidate must extend their Level 1 admin-panel (Next.js 16 / Prisma 7 / PostgreSQL / Vitest) with a multi-tier review workflow, an external verification adapter, and the in-place fix of a seeded concurrency bug in `@assessment/review-queue`. All three parts must land in one continuous AI session on a new `assessment-2` branch, with a verbatim transcript and the seeded module integrated via the supplied patch step. The brief is intentionally ambiguous on edge cases — those resolutions go in the transcript.\n\nThis plan is a roadmap for the implementation phase. Each section names the files to touch, the contract to satisfy, and the verification step. Where the brief is ambiguous I record the chosen resolution under **Resolved ambiguity** so the transcript can quote the same wording.\n\n## Repo state (verified)\n\n- Repo root: `/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment` (the html assessment brief, the README, and the POSTMORTEM live here; the Next.js project lives at `admin-panel/`).\n- Current branch: `main`. `origin/assessment-1` exists; `main` is the merge commit of `assessment-1`. Branching off `main` is structurally equivalent to branching off the `assessment-1` head — that satisfies the spec.\n- `node_modules` has not been installed in this checkout — `npm install` is required before tests/migrations run.\n- `AGENTS.md` warns: this is **not the Next.js you know** — read `node_modules/next/dist/docs/` before writing route handlers. We must `npm install` and consult those docs for any pattern that touches App Router request handling, since assistant training may be stale.\n- `prisma/schema.prisma`: PostgreSQL provider, models `User`, `Announcement`, `AuditLog`, enum `Role { ADMIN, CONTENT_MANAGER, SUPPORT, USER }`. `Announcement` currently has no top-level lifecycle status — Level 2 must add it.\n- The seeded library source lives at `/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/review-queue/` (its own `package.json`, `src/{index,types,client,claim,approve,reject,list-pending}.ts`). **There is no `review-queue.patch` file** anywhere under `/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/`. The brief assumes one exists. Mitigation: the integration commit will copy the directory verbatim into `admin-panel/lib/review-queue/` and commit it with the message *\"install seeded review-queue\"* — functionally identical to applying the patch and the only honest path given the missing file. The transcript will note the missing patch and the substitution.\n- Level 1 transcript declared the model as Opus 4.7 (overriding the user's \"Sonnet\" instruction with a note). Level 2 user instruction is again \"Claude Sonnet\". The Level 2 transcript header will declare **Model: Claude Sonnet (as directed by the candidate)** with a one-line technical note that the harness model is in fact `claude-opus-4-7[1m]` so the transcript does not falsely attribute work to a different family — Level 1 used the same disclosure pattern.\n\n## Resolved ambiguities (record in transcript Section \"Brief ambiguities resolved\")\n\n1. **Separation of duties**. Enforced at three points: (a) submit — author writes their own `authorId` into the queue row; (b) claim — service-layer guard rejects a claim where `queue_item.authorId === reviewer.id`, plus the library's `listPending({ excludeAuthorId })` filter for the UI; (c) approve/reject — the seeded library already enforces `claimedBy === reviewer`. No admin override; an admin who happens to be the author of an announcement still cannot review it. Reason: a single, branch-free invariant is far easier to audit than role-conditional bypasses.\n2. **Deadline / escalation**. A queue item is \"overdue\" when `status = PENDING AND claimedAt IS NULL AND createdAt < now() - 48h`. Escalation = increment `tier` (default 1, escalated tier = 2) and stamp `escalatedAt`. The implementation provides one tier above default. Trigger: an `escalateOverdue()` service function plus a `POST /api/review-queue/escalate` route that runs the same logic on demand. No cron is added (Next.js process scope) — the route is the call surface for an external scheduler.\n3. **Verification retry policy**. Exponential backoff with base 100 ms × 2^n, jitter ±20 %, max 3 attempts. Retry only on transport-class failures (network errors, HTTP 502/503/504, breaker `HALF_OPEN` probes). 4xx and validation errors fail fast and are not retried.\n4. **Audit on verification outcomes**. (a) Adapter returns `{ ok: true }` → announcement → `PUBLISHED`, queue item → `APPROVED`, `AuditLog` action `VERIFICATION_PASSED`. (b) Adapter returns `{ ok: false, reason }` → announcement → `DRAFT`, queue item → `APPROVED` with `decisionNote = \"approved-but-verification-rejected\"`, `AuditLog` action `VERIFICATION_REJECTED`. (c) Adapter throws after retries / breaker open → workflow transaction aborts; queue item stays `UNDER_REVIEW`, announcement stays `PENDING_REVIEW`, `AuditLog` action `VERIFICATION_TRANSPORT_FAILED` (best-effort, written outside the failed transaction). The reviewer can re-approve later.\n5. **Cache precedence**. Cached `REJECTED` never replaced by `APPROVED`. Cached `APPROVED` may be overwritten by `REJECTED`. Cache key = `announcement.id` + `bodyEn|bodyAr|titleEn|titleAr` content hash so editing the body invalidates a stale rejection. TTL: `APPROVED` entries expire after 5 minutes; `REJECTED` entries persist until invalidated by content change.\n\n## Critical files to add or modify\n\n### Schema and migration\n\n- `admin-panel/prisma/schema.prisma`\n  - Add enum `AnnouncementStatus { DRAFT PENDING_REVIEW PUBLISHED REJECTED }`.\n  - Extend `enum Role` with `COMPLIANCE_OFFICER`.\n  - On `Announcement`: add `status AnnouncementStatus @default(DRAFT)`, `publishedAt DateTime?`, opposite relation `reviewQueueItem ReviewQueueItem?`, index `@@index([status])`.\n  - Add full `ReviewQueueItem` model exactly per the assessment spec (id cuid, announcementId @unique, authorId, status string, claimedBy?, claimedAt?, decisionNote?, decidedAt?, createdAt, relation, indexes `[status, claimedBy]` and `[createdAt]`). Add the optional fields the implementation needs: `tier Int @default(1)` and `escalatedAt DateTime?`.\n- `admin-panel/prisma/migrations/0002_review_workflow/migration.sql` — generated by `npx prisma migrate dev --name review_workflow`. Hand-verify it: enum addition for `Role` and the new `AnnouncementStatus`, alter on `Announcement`, create on `ReviewQueueItem`.\n- `admin-panel/prisma/seed.ts` — add a `compliance@example.com` user with role `COMPLIANCE_OFFICER`. Existing announcements stay `DRAFT` to keep the smoke path uneventful.\n\n### Seeded library install\n\n- Copy `/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/review-queue/{package.json,README.md,src/}` → `admin-panel/lib/review-queue/`. Commit immediately with message `install seeded review-queue` so the diff between this commit and the next ones is exactly the bug fix.\n- Add a path entry in `admin-panel/tsconfig.json` (`@review-queue/*`) only if the path import gets ugly — otherwise import via relative path from `services/`.\n\n### Bug fix (Part C)\n\n- `admin-panel/lib/review-queue/src/claim.ts` — replace the `findFirst → update` race with a single atomic operation:\n  - `prisma.$transaction(async tx => { … })`.\n  - Inside the transaction, `tx.$queryRaw\\`SELECT id FROM \"ReviewQueueItem\" WHERE status='PENDING' AND \"claimedBy\" IS NULL ORDER BY \"createdAt\" ASC LIMIT 1 FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED\\`` (Postgres-specific, but Postgres is the project's datasource).\n  - If the row set is empty → `return { kind: 'empty' }`.\n  - Otherwise `tx.reviewQueueItem.update({ where: { id }, data: { status: 'UNDER_REVIEW', claimedBy: reviewer, claimedAt: new Date() } })` and return `{ kind: 'claimed', item }`.\n  - Why this satisfies the brief: row-level lock is held by Postgres across processes and connection pools — meets *\"not cosmetic / holds across processes\"*; `SKIP LOCKED` makes parallel claimers pick distinct rows instead of contending; the patch is surgical (only `claim.ts` changes; no public API change; no rewrite of the library).\n- Commit with message that names the bug class: `fix(review-queue): close TOCTOU race in claimNextItem with FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`.\n\n### Verification adapter (Part B)\n\n- `admin-panel/lib/adapters/verification/types.ts`\n  - `interface VerificationResult { ok: boolean; reason?: string; verdict: 'APPROVED' | 'REJECTED'; verifiedAt: Date }`.\n  - `interface VerificationAdapter { verify(announcement: AnnouncementForVerification): Promise<VerificationResult> }` where `AnnouncementForVerification = Pick<Announcement, 'id'|'titleEn'|'titleAr'|'bodyEn'|'bodyAr'>`. No HTTP types in the public surface.\n  - Internal `class TransientVerificationError extends Error` and `class PermanentVerificationError extends Error` — used only inside the wrapper.\n- `admin-panel/lib/adapters/verification/mock.ts`\n  - `MockVerificationAdapter` accepts a config `{ rule: 'allow' | 'deny' | (a) => Verdict }`. Default rule: APPROVE unless the bilingual body contains the literal token `__deny__`. Used by tests and dev seeding only.\n- `admin-panel/lib/adapters/verification/live.ts`\n  - `LiveVerificationAdapter` skeleton with a `verify` method whose body is a single `throw new Error('TODO SPEC PENDING — live verification API contract not yet delivered')`.\n  - Each potential request site (URL build, body build, response parse) carries its own `// TODO SPEC PENDING` comment so a grep for the marker matches every live call site.\n- `admin-panel/lib/adapters/verification/wrapper.ts`\n  - `class CachedRetryingBreakerAdapter implements VerificationAdapter`.\n  - Composition order: wrapper first checks cache; on miss, calls a retrying call which is in turn guarded by the breaker.\n  - Cache: `Map<string, { entry: VerificationResult; cachedAt: number }>`. Key derivation = `${announcement.id}:${sha256(titleEn+titleAr+bodyEn+bodyAr).slice(0,16)}`. Precedence rule explicit: when storing, if existing entry has `verdict === 'REJECTED'` and incoming has `verdict === 'APPROVED'`, **keep the existing rejected entry**.\n  - Retry: 3 attempts, exponential backoff 100/200/400 ms with ±20 % jitter, only on `TransientVerificationError`.\n  - Breaker: states `CLOSED → (5 consecutive failures) → OPEN → (30 s) → HALF_OPEN → (1 success) → CLOSED`. While `OPEN`, all calls reject with `TransientVerificationError('breaker open')`.\n- `admin-panel/lib/adapters/verification/index.ts` — barrel.\n\n### Workflow service layer (Part A)\n\n- `admin-panel/services/review-workflow.ts`\n  - `submitForReview(announcementId, user)` — must be CONTENT_MANAGER or ADMIN, must be `Announcement.status === 'DRAFT'`. Inside `prisma.$transaction`: update announcement to `PENDING_REVIEW`; create `ReviewQueueItem { status: 'PENDING', authorId: user.id, announcementId, tier: 1 }`; write audit `SUBMIT_FOR_REVIEW`; enqueue notification stub (an `AuditLog` with action `NOTIFICATION_QUEUED` and metadata describing recipient = compliance officers — actual delivery is out of scope for the assessment).\n  - `claimNext(reviewer)` — must be COMPLIANCE_OFFICER or ADMIN; service queries `listPending({ excludeAuthorId: reviewer.id })` → if any, calls `queue.claimNextItem(reviewer.id)` and re-checks `result.item.authorId !== reviewer.id` (defence in depth against the library claiming a self-authored row), throwing `SelfReviewError` if violated.\n  - `approveItem(itemId, reviewer, note, deps)` — `deps.verification: VerificationAdapter` injected. Inside `prisma.$transaction`: read item, run `queue.approve(itemId, reviewer, note)`, then call `deps.verification.verify(announcement)`:\n    - `verdict === 'APPROVED'` → `Announcement.status = PUBLISHED`, `publishedAt = now()`, audit `VERIFICATION_PASSED`, notification stub.\n    - `verdict === 'REJECTED'` → `Announcement.status = DRAFT`, audit `VERIFICATION_REJECTED` with `reason`, notification stub.\n    - thrown error → transaction rolls back; outside the transaction we write best-effort `VERIFICATION_TRANSPORT_FAILED` audit and rethrow.\n  - `rejectItem(itemId, reviewer, reason)` — inside `$transaction`: `queue.reject` + `Announcement.status = DRAFT` + audit `REJECTED` + notification stub.\n  - `escalateOverdue(now = new Date())` — within `$transaction`: `prisma.reviewQueueItem.updateMany({ where: { status: 'PENDING', claimedBy: null, tier: 1, createdAt: { lt: now - 48h } }, data: { tier: 2, escalatedAt: now } })`; for each row updated, write `ESCALATED` audit with metadata. Returns `{ escalated: count }`.\n\n### RBAC update\n\n- `admin-panel/lib/rbac.ts` — extend the `Action` union with `'review' | 'submitForReview' | 'escalate'` and the `can()` switch:\n  - `review` → ADMIN, COMPLIANCE_OFFICER.\n  - `submitForReview` → ADMIN, CONTENT_MANAGER.\n  - `escalate` → ADMIN.\n\n### Routes (App Router)\n\nEach handler follows the existing pattern in [admin-panel/app/api/announcements/route.ts](admin-panel/app/api/announcements/route.ts) — auth → RBAC → JSON parse → Zod → service call → JSON response. Status codes: 200/201 success, 401 unauth, 403 forbidden, 404 not found, 409 state conflict, 422 validation, 500 fallback.\n\n- `admin-panel/app/api/announcements/[id]/submit-for-review/route.ts` (POST)\n- `admin-panel/app/api/review-queue/route.ts` (GET — list pending excluding self-authored)\n- `admin-panel/app/api/review-queue/claim/route.ts` (POST — claim next)\n- `admin-panel/app/api/review-queue/[id]/approve/route.ts` (POST — `{ note }`)\n- `admin-panel/app/api/review-queue/[id]/reject/route.ts` (POST — `{ reason }`)\n- `admin-panel/app/api/review-queue/escalate/route.ts` (POST — admin only)\n\nFor each route: read `node_modules/next/dist/docs/` first (see AGENTS.md) and confirm the App Router signature for `params` (Level 1 already uses `params: Promise<{ id: string }>` — same pattern).\n\n### UI\n\n- `admin-panel/app/admin/review-queue/page.tsx` — server component, lists pending items the current reviewer is allowed to claim; only renders when `can(user, 'review')`.\n- `admin-panel/app/admin/review-queue/ReviewActions.tsx` — client component with **Claim Next**, **Approve**, **Reject** buttons; posts to the routes above.\n- Update `admin-panel/app/admin/announcements/[id]/edit/EditForm.tsx` to expose **Submit for review** when `status === 'DRAFT'` and `can(user, 'submitForReview')`. The status badge replaces the implicit \"live\" assumption.\n- Update `admin-panel/app/admin/announcements/page.tsx` list to show `status`.\n- Update `admin-panel/app/admin/layout.tsx` to add a \"Review queue\" sidebar link visible only when `can(user, 'review')`.\n\n### Tests (Vitest)\n\nReuse the existing pattern in `__tests__/announcements.test.ts` (mock-based unit tests). Add the following:\n\n- `__tests__/review-queue-claim-race.test.ts` — DB-backed integration test (Postgres). Seeds 1 PENDING row; spawns N concurrent `claimNextItem` calls via `Promise.all`. Header declares model \"Claude Sonnet\" per candidate instruction with a one-line technical disclosure that the harness model is `claude-opus-4-7[1m]` (matches Level 1 pattern). Includes investigation log for Part C: hypothesis, repro (a concurrent `Promise.all` over `findFirst+update`), discovery of the read-modify-write window, fix design (FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED inside a tx), test results before and after.\n- No `SUBMISSION.md`, `DECISIONS.md`, `DESIGN.md`, or `KNOWN_ISSUES.md` — the brief forbids them.\n\n## Implementation order (commits, smallest first)\n\nEach commit is tight and reversible. Commit messages follow Level 1's terse style.\n\n1. `chore: branch from assessment-1; install seeded review-queue` — copy seeded files in.\n2. `feat(schema): add AnnouncementStatus, ReviewQueueItem, COMPLIANCE_OFFICER` — schema + migration + seed update + `npx prisma generate`.\n3. `feat(rbac): add review/submitForReview/escalate actions` — `lib/rbac.ts` + tests.\n4. `feat(verification): adapter interface, mock, live scaffold` — `lib/adapters/verification/{types,mock,live,index}.ts`.\n5. `feat(verification): retry+breaker+cache wrapper with precedence` — `wrapper.ts` + `verification-precedence.test.ts` + `verification-breaker.test.ts`.\n6. `feat(workflow): service layer for submit/claim/approve/reject/escalate` — `services/review-workflow.ts` + unit tests for separation-of-duties and transactional rollback.\n7. `feat(api): review-queue routes and submit-for-review` — six route handlers.\n8. `feat(ui): review queue page and submit-for-review button` — pages + client components + sidebar link.\n9. `test(review-queue): concurrent claim test reproduces the race (red)` — write the failing race test against the un-patched library; commit it red so the bisect shows the moment of fix.\n10. `fix(review-queue): close TOCTOU race in claimNextItem with FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED` — surgical patch to `lib/review-queue/src/claim.ts`; the race test now passes.\n11. `docs(readme): document level 2 routes, env, race-test prerequisite`.\n12. `chore: write verbatim ai session transcript` — generated by `/transcript`.\n\nThe patch-then-fix split (steps 9 and 10) gives the reviewer a concrete diff that proves the test fails before the fix, mirroring the Level 1 buggy-then-fixed health route.\n\n## Verification (end-to-end)\n\nAfter the final commit, run from `admin-panel/`:\n\n1. `npm install` (first time only).\n2. `npx prisma migrate deploy` against a clean Postgres.\n3. `npm run db:seed`.\n4. `npm test` — all suites green when `DATABASE_URL` is set; suites except the race test green even without DB.\n5. `npm run dev` and walk:\n   - Login as `cm@example.com`, create a draft announcement, submit for review.\n   - Login as `compliance@example.com`, claim next, approve.\n   - Inspect the announcement: status `PUBLISHED`, audit chain present.\n   - Repeat with a body containing `__deny__` to exercise mock rejection.\n   - Try to claim an item authored by the same compliance user (after seeding such a row): expect 403 `SELF_REVIEW`.\n6. Manually re-run `npm test` against the un-patched `claim.ts` (revert the `claim.ts` change locally) to confirm the race test fails — record the output in the transcript.\n7. Run `/transcript` once at the very end to write `ai-session/transcript.md` from the live session. Do not edit it after.\n\n## Risks\n\n- **Missing `review-queue.patch`** — addressed by copying the seeded directory verbatim and disclosing the substitution. If the reviewer expects a literal `git apply`, the audit trail (single commit named `install seeded review-queue` and matching tree contents) is still defensible.\n- **Postgres-only `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`** — the schema declares Postgres, so this is safe. If the project ever switches DB engines, the fix would need a portable equivalent (advisory lock or compare-and-swap via `updateMany`).\n- **Race test depends on real Postgres** — without a running DB the test is skipped. README documents this. CI would need a service container; outside scope.\n- **Next.js 16 unfamiliarity** — `AGENTS.md` warns the assistant against guessing. Before each route handler is written, read the relevant doc under `node_modules/next/dist/docs/`. Ditto for any change to `prisma.config.ts` patterns.\n- **Transcript verbatim requirement** — `/transcript` must be the last action, not edited afterwards. No generated docs that the brief forbids.\n","isAgent":false,"filePath":"/home/sameh/.claude/plans/delightful-sleeping-crane.md","planWasEdited":true},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"5432da5b-1923-478d-98f1-ddf9c093a13d","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}

{"parentUuid":"613a4600-33c2-4fd2-8cd3-f694203659e3","isSidechain":false,"message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-7","id":"msg_01DKqd5jvVUqtGxa1Ms8X1C4","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Two important findings before I run anything:\n\n1. **The DB has prior Level 2 work in it.** `admin_panel_dev` already contains a `ReviewQueueItem` table and an `Announcement.status` column from a migration `20260505105104_add_review_queue_item` applied 2026-05-05 — but that migration is **not in our git tree** (our branch only has `0001_init`). Someone started Level 2 before and abandoned it, leaving the DB in a partial state.\n2. **No `.env` file exists** — only the runtime, gitignored one (now absent). Level 1's README assumed `.env.example` existed, but it isn't checked in.\n\nBefore I touch anything destructive (e.g. `prisma migrate reset` to clear the orphan migration) I want to confirm the path with you. Three options:\n\n| Option | What it does | Risk |\n|---|---|---|\n| **A. Use a fresh DB** (`admin_panel_l2`) | Create a new database for Level 2 work, leave `admin_panel_dev` alone | Zero risk to existing data; cleanest |\n| **B. Reset `admin_panel_dev`** | `prisma migrate reset --force` — drops all data, re-applies migrations from git, re-seeds | Destroys any seeded users/announcements you had |\n| **C. Reuse the DB as-is** | Point `.env` at it; introspect to make our git schema match what's already there | Avoids migration; but our `prisma/migrations/` tree won't match what's deployed, which violates the \"schema + migration\" deliverable |\n\nLet me also report npm install status:"}],"stop_reason":"tool_use","stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1946,"cache_read_input_tokens":103458,"output_tokens":2179,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":1946,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[{"input_tokens":1,"output_tokens":2179,"cache_read_input_tokens":103458,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1946,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":1946},"type":"message"}],"speed":"standard"}},"requestId":"req_011Cam9Y3pPxo723dbhuXAz3","type":"assistant","uuid":"69e75d94-0ece-42bb-aecb-bbd46cf446fa","timestamp":"2026-05-06T10:21:44.133Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}

{"parentUuid":"bd17386f-965a-4476-ac96-d35a9d9e1252","isSidechain":false,"message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-7","id":"msg_01DcYPWaN6Qw81JGRhhK6nob","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The dev server is running at `http://localhost:3000`. Here are all the routes available right now (Level 1 baseline — Level 2 routes will be added in later steps):\n\n### Pages (browser)\n\n- [http://localhost:3000/](http://localhost:3000/) — landing page\n- [http://localhost:3000/admin](http://localhost:3000/admin) — redirects to `/admin/announcements`\n- [http://localhost:3000/admin/announcements](http://localhost:3000/admin/announcements) — list (paginated, non-deleted)\n- [http://localhost:3000/admin/announcements/new](http://localhost:3000/admin/announcements/new) — bilingual create form\n- `http://localhost:3000/admin/announcements/<id>/edit` — edit + soft-delete (replace `<id>` with a real id)\n- [http://localhost:3000/admin/users](http://localhost:3000/admin/users) — user lookup (ADMIN sees 7 cols, SUPPORT 4)\n\nReal seed ids you can edit right now:\n- [http://localhost:3000/admin/announcements/cmotwu4h70003tgekkrbxv9nq/edit](http://localhost:3000/admin/announcements/cmotwu4h70003tgekkrbxv9nq/edit) — *Scheduled maintenance tonight*\n- [http://localhost:3000/admin/announcements/cmotwu4h70004tgekijnfjz6j/edit](http://localhost:3000/admin/announcements/cmotwu4h70004tgekijnfjz6j/edit) — *New leave policy is now in effect*\n\n### API (curl / fetch)\n\n- `GET  http://localhost:3000/api/health` → `{ \"status\": \"ok\" }`\n- `GET  http://localhost:3000/api/health?include=latest` → latest announcement or `latest: null`\n- `POST http://localhost:3000/api/announcements` — create (needs ADMIN/CONTENT_MANAGER session)\n- `PATCH http://localhost:3000/api/announcements/<id>` — edit\n- `DELETE http://localhost:3000/api/announcements/<id>` — soft-delete\n- `GET  http://localhost:3000/api/users?q=&page=1&pageSize=10` — search\n\n### Switching the mock session\nYour current session is **ADMIN** (`admin@example.com`). To test as another role, edit [admin-panel/.env](admin-panel/.env) and change the `MOCK_USER_*` block, then the dev server will pick it up on the next request:\n\n| Role | id (from seed) | email |\n|---|---|---|\n| ADMIN | `cmotwu4ga0000tgek0amk2can` | admin@example.com |\n| CONTENT_MANAGER | `cmotwu4gj0001tgekxilwh55v` | cm@example.com |\n| SUPPORT | `cmotwu4gq0002tgekn1oz358r` | support@example.com |\n\nIf by \"sicle\" you meant something else (a specific route name), let me know the word you intended and I'll point you straight at it."}],"stop_reason":"end_turn","stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":6,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1328,"cache_read_input_tokens":120051,"output_tokens":1152,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":1328,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[{"input_tokens":6,"output_tokens":1152,"cache_read_input_tokens":120051,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1328,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":1328},"type":"message"}],"speed":"standard"}},"requestId":"req_011CamA2ttSMKD6GMDksXUQY","type":"assistant","uuid":"91229add-a0d5-455c-966d-bb0e39bac21b","timestamp":"2026-05-06T10:27:59.548Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}

Callers should pass their own user id as\n * `excludeAuthorId` so separation-of-duties filtering happens at the\n * database level rather than after the fact.\n */\nexport async function listPending(\n  prisma: PrismaClient,\n  filter: PendingFilter\n): Promise<PendingItem[]> {\n  const rows = await prisma.reviewQueueItem.findMany({\n    where: {\n      status: filter.status ?? type { PrismaClient } from '@prisma/client';\n2\timport type { ClaimResult, PendingItem, UserId } from './types';\n3\t\n4\t/**\n5\t * Claim the next unclaimed review item for a reviewer.\n6\t *\n7\t * Returns `{ kind: 'claimed', item }` when an item was claimed,\n8\t * or `{ kind: 'empty' }` when the queue has nothing pending.\n9\t *\n10\t * The caller is responsible for separation-of-duties filtering\n11\t * (i.e. callers that must not claim their own items should filter\n12\t * beforehand in their service layer).\n13\t */\n14\texport async function claimNextItem(\n15\t  prisma: PrismaClient,\n16\t  reviewer: UserId\n17\t): Promise<ClaimResult> {\n18\t  const candidate = await prisma.reviewQueueItem.findFirst({\n19\t    where: {\n20\t      status: 'PENDING',\n21\t      claimedBy: null,\n22\t    },\n23\t    orderBy: {\n24\t      createdAt: 'asc',\n25\t    },\n26\t  });\n27\t\n28\t  if (!candidate) {\n29\t    return { kind: 'empty' };\n30\t  }\n31\t\n32\t  const updated = await prisma.reviewQueueItem.update({\n33\t    where: { id: candidate.id },\n34\t    data: {\n35\t      status: 'UNDER_REVIEW',\n36\t      claimedBy: reviewer,\n37\t      claimedAt: new Date(),\n38\t    },\n39\t  });\n40\t\n41\t  return { kind: 'claimed', item: updated as unknown as PendingItem };\n42\t}\n43\t"}]},"uuid":"4e1d1d58-f08c-466d-8e10-35e3ae970684","timestamp":"2026-05-06T10:34:03.196Z","toolUseResult":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment/admin-panel/lib/review-queue/src/claim.ts","content":"import type { PrismaClient } from '@prisma/client';\nimport type { ClaimResult, PendingItem, UserId } from './types';\n\n/**\n * Claim the next unclaimed review item for a reviewer.\n *\n * Returns `{ kind: 'claimed', item }` when an item was claimed,\n * or `{ kind: 'empty' }` when the queue has nothing pending.\n *\n * The caller is responsible for separation-of-duties filtering\n * (i.e. callers that must not claim their own items should filter\n * beforehand in their service layer).\n */\nexport async function claimNextItem(\n  prisma: PrismaClient,\n  reviewer: UserId\n): Promise<ClaimResult> {\n  const candidate = await prisma.reviewQueueItem.findFirst({\n    where: {\n      status: 'PENDING',\n      claimedBy: null,\n    },\n    orderBy: {\n      createdAt: 'asc',\n    },\n  });\n\n  if (!candidate) {\n    return { kind: 'empty' };\n  }\n\n  const updated = await prisma.reviewQueueItem.update({\n    where: { id: candidate.id },\n    data: {\n      status: 'UNDER_REVIEW',\n      claimedBy: reviewer,\n      claimedAt: new Date(),\n    },\n  });\n\n  return { kind: 'claimed', item: updated as unknown as PendingItem };\n}\n","numLines":43,"startLine":1,"totalLines":43}},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"8cf488bb-ba8b-4993-a3ba-1cf9040170c6","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}

{"parentUuid":"548a7bd4-0391-48dd-a122-8799ce67de9f","isSidechain":false,"promptId":"55b9d351-6daa-432d-883f-dab10839507d","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01BE3h2EiRZE6K7jLpfbxQho","type":"tool_result","content":"1\timport type { PrismaClient } from '@prisma/client';\n2\timport type { ItemId, UserId } from './types';\n3\t\n4\t/**\n5\t * Approve a previously claimed review item.\n6\t *\n7\t * Runs inside a transaction so the status change and decision metadata\n8\t * stay consistent with any downstream effects the caller layers on top\n9\t * (audit entry, notification, publish step).\n10\t *\n11\t * Throws if the item does not exist, was not claimed by `reviewer`, or\n12\t * is not currently in the `UNDER_REVIEW` state.\n13\t */\n14\texport async function approve(\n15\t  prisma: PrismaClient,\n16\t  itemId: ItemId,\n17\t  reviewer: UserId,\n18\t  note: string\n19\t): Promise<void> {\n20\t  await prisma.$transaction(async (tx) => {\n21\t    const item = await tx.reviewQueueItem.findUnique({\n22\t      where: { id: itemId },\n23\t    });\n24\t    if (!item) {\n25\t      throw new Error(`review item ${itemId} not found`);\n26\t    }\n27\t    if (item.claimedBy !== reviewer) {\n28\t      throw new Error(\n29\t        `only the claiming reviewer may approve this item`\n30\t      );\n31\t    }\n32\t    if (item.status !== 'UNDER_REVIEW') {\n33\t      throw new Error(\n34\t        `cannot approve an item in status ${item.status}`\n35\t      );\n36\t    }\n37\t\n38\t    await tx.reviewQueueItem.update({\n39\t      where: { id: itemId },\n40\t      data: {\n41\t        status: 'APPROVED',\n42\t        decisionNote: note,\n43\t        decidedAt: new Date(),\n44\t      },\n45\t    });\n46\t  });\n47\t}\n48\t"}]},"uuid":"211ecfcc-2af8-49cf-8fbd-e05f32a67ab2","timestamp":"2026-05-06T10:34:09.116Z","toolUseResult":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment/admin-panel/lib/review-queue/src/approve.ts","content":"import type { PrismaClient } from '@prisma/client';\nimport type { ItemId, UserId } from './types';\n\n/**\n * Approve a previously claimed review item.\n *\n * Runs inside a transaction so the status change and decision metadata\n * stay consistent with any downstream effects the caller layers on top\n * (audit entry, notification, publish step).\n *\n * Throws if the item does not exist, was not claimed by `reviewer`, or\n * is not currently in the `UNDER_REVIEW` state.\n */\nexport async function approve(\n  prisma: PrismaClient,\n  itemId: ItemId,\n  reviewer: UserId,\n  note: string\n): Promise<void> {\n  await prisma.$transaction(async (tx) => {\n    const item = await tx.reviewQueueItem.findUnique({\n      where: { id: itemId },\n    });\n    if (!item) {\n      throw new Error(`review item ${itemId} not found`);\n    }\n    if (item.claimedBy !== reviewer) {\n      throw new Error(\n        `only the claiming reviewer may approve this item`\n      );\n    }\n    if (item.status !== 'UNDER_REVIEW') {\n      throw new Error(\n        `cannot approve an item in status ${item.status}`\n      );\n    }\n\n    await tx.reviewQueueItem.update({\n      where: { id: itemId },\n      data: {\n        status: 'APPROVED',\n        decisionNote: note,\n        decidedAt: new Date(),\n      },\n    });\n  });\n}\n","numLines":48,"startLine":1,"totalLines":48}},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"548a7bd4-0391-48dd-a122-8799ce67de9f","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}

type { PrismaClient } from '@prisma/client';\n2\timport type { ItemId, UserId } from './types';\n3\t\n4\t/**\n5\t * Reject a previously claimed review item.\n6\t *\n7\t * Runs inside a transaction so the status change and decision metadata\n8\t * stay consistent with any downstream effects the caller layers on top.\n9\t *\n10\t * Throws if the item does not exist, was not claimed by `reviewer`, or\n11\t * is not currently in the `UNDER_REVIEW` state.\n12\t */\n13\texport async function reject(\n14\t  prisma: PrismaClient,\n15\t  itemId: ItemId,\n16\t  reviewer: UserId,\n17\t  reason: string\n18\t): Promise<void> {\n19\t  await prisma.$transaction(async (tx) => {\n20\t    const item = await tx.reviewQueueItem.findUnique({\n21\t      where: { id: itemId },\n22\t    });\n23\t    if (!item) {\n24\t      throw new Error(`review item ${itemId} not found`);\n25\t    }\n26\t    if (item.claimedBy !== reviewer) {\n27\t      throw new Error(\n28\t        `only the claiming reviewer may reject this item`\n29\t      );\n30\t    }\n31\t    if (item.status !== 'UNDER_REVIEW') {\n32\t      throw new Error(\n33\t        `cannot reject an item in status ${item.status}`\n34\t      );\n35\t    }\n36\t\n37\t    await tx.reviewQueueItem.update({\n38\t      where: { id: itemId },\n39\t      data: {\n40\t        status: 'REJECTED',\n41\t        decisionNote: reason,\n42\t        decidedAt: type { PrismaClient } from '@prisma/client';\nimport type { ItemId, UserId } from './types';\n\n/**\n * Reject a previously claimed review item.\n *\n * Runs inside a transaction so the status change and decision metadata\n * stay consistent with any downstream effects the caller layers on top.\n *\n * Throws if the item does not exist, was not claimed by `reviewer`, or\n * is not currently in the `UNDER_REVIEW` state.\n */\nexport async function reject(\n  prisma: PrismaClient,\n  itemId: ItemId,\n  reviewer: UserId,\n  reason: string\n): Promise<void> {\n  await prisma.$transaction(async (tx) => {\n    const item = await tx.reviewQueueItem.findUnique({\n      where: { id: itemId },\n    });\n    if (!item) {\n      throw new Error(`review item ${itemId} not found`);\n    }\n    if (item.claimedBy !== reviewer) {\n      throw new Error(\n        `only the claiming reviewer may reject this item`\n      );\n    }\n    if (item.status !== 'UNDER_REVIEW') {\n      throw new Error(\n        `cannot reject an item in status ${item.status}`\n      );\n    }\n\n    await tx.reviewQueueItem.update({\n      where: { id: itemId },\n      data: {\n        status: 'REJECTED',\n        decisionNote: reason,\n        decidedAt: new Date(),\n      },\n    });\n  });\n}\n","numLines":47,"startLine":1,"totalLines":47}},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"c4ac9582-a89e-452c-bb3d-3ec3a4955858","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}

[src/types.ts](admin-panel/lib/review-queue/src/types.ts) | Type definitions | `UserId`, `ItemId`, `ReviewStatus`, `PendingItem`, `PendingFilter`, `ClaimResult`, `ReviewQueueClient` |\n| [src/client.ts](admin-panel/lib/review-queue/src/client.ts) | Factory binding all four ops to a single Prisma client | `createReviewQueueClient(prisma): ReviewQueueClient` |\n| [src/list-pending.ts](admin-panel/lib/review-queue/src/list-pending.ts) | Read pending items, optionally excluding self-authored | `listPending(prisma, filter): Promise<PendingItem[]>` |\n| [src/claim.ts](admin-panel/lib/review-queue/src/claim.ts) | **Claim** the next unclaimed PENDING item | `claimNextItem(prisma, reviewer): Promise<ClaimResult>` |\n| [src/approve.ts](admin-panel/lib/review-queue/src/approve.ts) | Approve a previously claimed item (tx-wrapped) | `approve(prisma, itemId, reviewer, note): Promise<void>` |\n| [src/reject.ts](admin-panel/lib/review-queue/src/reject.ts) | Reject a previously claimed item (tx-wrapped) | `reject(prisma, itemId, reviewer, reason): Promise<void>` |\n\n### All exported function signatures\n\n```ts\n// from src/client.ts (the factory)\nexport function createReviewQueueClient(prisma: PrismaClient): ReviewQueueClient;\n\n// from src/types.ts (the public contract on the returned client)\nexport interface ReviewQueueClient {\n  listPending(filter: PendingFilter): Promise<PendingItem[]>;\n  claimNextItem(reviewer: UserId): Promise<ClaimResult>;\n  approve(itemId: ItemId, reviewer: UserId, note: string): Promise<void>;\n  reject(itemId: ItemId, reviewer: UserId, reason: string): Promise<void>;\n}\n\n// underlying free functions (the factory just curries `prisma` into them)\nexport function listPending(prisma: PrismaClient, filter: PendingFilter): Promise<PendingItem[]>;\nexport function claimNextItem(prisma: PrismaClient, reviewer: UserId): Promise<ClaimResult>;\nexport function approve(prisma: PrismaClient, itemId: ItemId, reviewer: UserId, note: string): Promise<void>;\nexport function reject(prisma: PrismaClient, itemId: ItemId, reviewer: UserId, reason: string): Promise<void>;\n\n// supporting types\nexport type UserId = string;\nexport type ItemId = string;\nexport type ReviewStatus = 'PENDING' | 'UNDER_REVIEW' | 'APPROVED' | 'REJECTED';\nexport interface PendingItem {\n  id: ItemId;\n  announcementId: string;\n  authorId: UserId;\n  status: ReviewStatus;\n  claimedBy: UserId | null;\n  claimedAt: Date | null;\n  decisionNote: string | null;\n  decidedAt: Date | null;\n  createdAt: Date;\n}\nexport interface PendingFilter {\n  status?: ReviewStatus;\n  excludeAuthorId?: UserId;\n  limit?: number;\n}\nexport type ClaimResult =\n  | { kind: 'claimed'; item: PendingItem }\n  | { kind: 'empty' };\n```\n\n### The \"claim\" operation\n\nThe claim file is **[src/claim.ts](admin-panel/lib/review-queue/src/claim.ts)** — exporting `claimNextItem(prisma, reviewer)`. The factory in `client.ts` line 20 binds it as `claimNextItem`, which is the public method on `ReviewQueueClient`.\n\n### Exact code of the claim function\n\n```ts\n// admin-panel/lib/review-queue/src/claim.ts\nimport type { PrismaClient } from '@prisma/client';\nimport type { ClaimResult, PendingItem, UserId } from './types';\n\n/**\n * Claim the next unclaimed review item for a reviewer.\n *\n * Returns `{ kind: 'claimed', item }` when an item was claimed,\n * or `{ kind: 'empty' }` when the queue has nothing pending.\n *\n * The caller is responsible for separation-of-duties filtering\n * (i.e. callers that must not claim their own items should filter\n * beforehand in their service layer).\n */\nexport async function claimNextItem(\n  prisma: PrismaClient,\n  reviewer: UserId\n): Promise<ClaimResult> {\n  const candidate = await prisma.reviewQueueItem.findFirst({\n    where: {\n      status: 'PENDING',\n      claimedBy: null,\n    },\n    orderBy: {\n      createdAt: 'asc',\n    },\n  });\n\n  if (!candidate) {\n    return { kind: 'empty' };\n  }\n\n  const updated = await prisma.reviewQueueItem.update({\n    where: { id: candidate.id },\n    data: {\n      status: 'UNDER_REVIEW',\n      claimedBy: reviewer,\n      claimedAt: new Date(),\n    },\n  });\n\n  return { kind: 'claimed', item: updated as unknown as PendingItem };\n}\n```\n\n### Anatomy of the suspected race (just flagging it for later — not fixing yet)\n\nTwo unrelated database round-trips on the same row:\n\n1. Lines 18–26 — `findFirst` reads the oldest `PENDING / claimedBy=null` row and returns it (no lock, no condition).\n2. Lines 32–39 — `update` writes `status='UNDER_REVIEW'`, `claimedBy=reviewer`, `claimedAt=now()` keyed on `candidate.id` (no `where` predicate beyond id, so it updates whatever the row is *now*, not whatever it was at find time).\n\nBetween (1) and (2) any number of other callers can also `findFirst` and pick the same id. Each then `update`s by id and gets a happy `{ kind: 'claimed', item }` back. The DB ends up with whichever update committed last as `claimedBy`, but every concurrent caller already returned a \"claimed\" result to its UI.\n\nBug class: **read-modify-write race / TOCTOU on the queue row** — non-atomic check-then-act with no row lock and no compare-and-swap predicate.\n\nStopping here as instructed. Awaiting confirmation before Step 4."}],"stop_reason":"end_turn","stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":714,"cache_read_input_tokens":128558,"output_tokens":2397,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":714,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[{"input_tokens":1,"output_tokens":2397,"cache_read_input_tokens":128558,"cache_creation_input_tokens":714,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":714},"type":"message"}],"speed":"standard"}},"requestId":"req_011CamAY43etVqGXJH6PLWfK","type":"assistant","uuid":"9ceab031-5cb4-4eb8-9190-dede9183f78d","timestamp":"2026-05-06T10:34:46.207Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}

Now Step 4 — write the race test."}],"stop_reason":"tool_use","stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1058,"cache_read_input_tokens":144929,"output_tokens":1307,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":1058,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[{"input_tokens":1,"output_tokens":1307,"cache_read_input_tokens":144929,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1058,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":1058},"type":"message"}],"speed":"standard"}},"requestId":"req_011CamAxGxD7urH8wgavQSCV","type":"assistant","uuid":"46c9c046-cce5-4115-b941-11b77b1db49f","timestamp":"2026-05-06T10:39:49.681Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}
{"parentUuid":"46c9c046-cce5-4115-b941-11b77b1db49f","isSidechain":false,"message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-7","id":"msg_01XYwhqwY33mmQ1MHtcPWpBt","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01RGbTs1v4kSL7Qdd3wakciZ","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment/admin-panel/__tests__/review-queue-claim-race.test.ts","content":"import \"dotenv/config\";\nimport { describe, it, expect, beforeAll, afterAll } from \"vitest\";\nimport { PrismaClient } from \"@prisma/client\";\nimport { PrismaPg } from \"@prisma/adapter-pg\";\n\nimport { claimNextItem } from \"@/lib/review-queue/src/claim\";\n\n// This is a real-Postgres integration test. It exercises the claim primitive\n// with five concurrent callers on five separate connections and asserts the\n// queue invariant: \"exactly one reviewer wins each pending item.\" Without a\n// database the suite is skipped — pure mocks cannot reproduce row-level\n// locking semantics.\nconst HAS_DB = !!process.env.DATABASE_URL;\nconst dbUrl = process.env.DATABASE_URL ?? \"\";\n\nfunction newClient() {\n  return new PrismaClient({\n    adapter: new PrismaPg({ connectionString: dbUrl }),\n  });\n}\n\ndescribe.skipIf(!HAS_DB)(\"claimNextItem (Postgres concurrency)\", () => {\n  const setup = HAS_DB ? newClient() : (null as unknown as PrismaClient);\n\n  let userId: string;\n  let announcementId: string;\n  let itemId: string;\n\n  beforeAll(async () => {\n    const user = await setup.user.create({\n      data: {\n        email: `race-author-${Date.now()}-${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 8)}@test.local`,\n        name: \"Race Test Author\",\n      },\n    });\n    userId = user.id;\n\n    const announcement = await setup.announcement.create({\n      data: {\n        titleEn: \"Race test announcement\",\n        titleAr: \"إعلان اختبار التزامن\",\n        bodyEn: \"race body en\",\n        bodyAr: \"محتوى اختبار\",\n        authorId: userId,\n      },\n    });\n    announcementId = announcement.id;\n\n    const item = await setup.reviewQueueItem.create({\n      data: {\n        announcementId,\n        authorId: userId,\n        status: \"PENDING\",\n      },\n    });\n    itemId = item.id;\n  });\n\n  afterAll(async () => {\n    if (!HAS_DB) return;\n    await setup.reviewQueueItem.deleteMany({ where: { id: itemId } });\n    await setup.announcement.deleteMany({ where: { id: announcementId } });\n    await setup.user.deleteMany({ where: { id: userId } });\n    await setup.$disconnect();\n  });\n\n  it(\"exactly one of five concurrent claims succeeds; four return empty\", async () => {\n    const reviewers = [\"reviewer-A\", \"reviewer-B\", \"reviewer-C\", \"reviewer-D\", \"reviewer-E\"];\n    // Each caller gets its own PrismaClient so they never share a connection\n    // — guarantees real overlap at the database layer, not pool serialisation.\n    const clients = reviewers.map(() => newClient());\n\n    try {\n      const results = await Promise.all(\n        clients.map((c, i) => claimNextItem(c, reviewers[i]))\n      );\n\n      const claimed = results.filter((r) => r.kind === \"claimed\");\n      const empty = results.filter((r) => r.kind === \"empty\");\n\n      expect(claimed.length).toBe(1);\n      expect(empty.length).toBe(4);\n    } finally {\n      await Promise.all(clients.map((c) => c.$disconnect()));\n    }\n  });\n});\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":"tool_use","stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1058,"cache_read_input_tokens":144929,"output_tokens":1307,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":1058,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[{"input_tokens":1,"output_tokens":1307,"cache_read_input_tokens":144929,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1058,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":1058},"type":"message"}],"speed":"standard"}},"requestId":"req_011CamAxGxD7urH8wgavQSCV","type":"assistant","uuid":"97679c85-d0f2-4f01-8c72-aea8d48c904e","timestamp":"2026-05-06T10:40:02.200Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}

It exercises the claim primitive\n// with five concurrent callers on five separate connections and asserts the\n// queue invariant: \"exactly one reviewer wins each pending item.\" Without a\n// database the suite is skipped — pure mocks cannot reproduce row-level\n// locking semantics.\nconst HAS_DB = !!process.env.DATABASE_URL;\nconst dbUrl = process.env.DATABASE_URL ?? \"\";\n\nfunction newClient() {\n  return new PrismaClient({\n    adapter: new PrismaPg({ connectionString: dbUrl }),\n  });\n}\n\ndescribe.skipIf(!HAS_DB)(\"claimNextItem (Postgres concurrency)\", () => {\n  const setup = HAS_DB ? newClient() : (null as unknown as PrismaClient);\n\n  let userId: string;\n  let announcementId: string;\n  let itemId: string;\n\n  beforeAll(async () => {\n    const user = await setup.user.create({\n      data: {\n        email: `race-author-${Date.now()}-${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 8)}@test.local`,\n        name: \"Race Test Author\",\n      },\n    });\n    userId = user.id;\n\n    const announcement = await setup.announcement.create({\n      data: {\n        titleEn: \"Race test announcement\",\n        titleAr: \"إعلان اختبار التزامن\",\n        bodyEn: \"race body en\",\n        bodyAr: \"محتوى اختبار\",\n        authorId: userId,\n      },\n    });\n    announcementId = announcement.id;\n\n    const item = await setup.reviewQueueItem.create({\n      data: {\n        announcementId,\n        authorId: userId,\n        status: \"PENDING\",\n      },\n    });\n    itemId = item.id;\n  });\n\n  afterAll(async () => {\n    if (!HAS_DB) return;\n    await setup.reviewQueueItem.deleteMany({ where: { id: itemId } });\n    await setup.announcement.deleteMany({ where: { id: announcementId } });\n    await setup.user.deleteMany({ where: { id: userId } });\n    await setup.$disconnect();\n  });\n\n  it(\"exactly one of five concurrent claims succeeds; four return empty\", async () => {\n    const reviewers = [\"reviewer-A\", \"reviewer-B\", \"reviewer-C\", \"reviewer-D\", \"reviewer-E\"];\n    // Each caller gets its own PrismaClient so they never share a connection\n    // — guarantees real overlap at the database layer, not pool serialisation.\n    const clients = reviewers.map(() => newClient());\n\n    try {\n      const results = await Promise.all(\n        clients.map((c, i) => claimNextItem(c, reviewers[i]))\n      );\n\n      const claimed = results.filter((r) => r.kind === \"claimed\");\n      const empty = results.filter((r) => r.kind === \"empty\");\n\n      expect(claimed.length).toBe(1);\n      expect(empty.length).toBe(4);\n    } finally {\n      await Promise.all(clients.map((c) => c.$disconnect()));\n    }\n  });\n});\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null,"userModified":false},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"97679c85-d0f2-4f01-8c72-aea8d48c904e","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}

The other 22 Level 1 tests still pass.\n\nNow Step 5 — surgical fix to `claim.ts` only."}],"stop_reason":"tool_use","stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":806,"cache_read_input_tokens":147385,"output_tokens":2209,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":806,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[{"input_tokens":1,"output_tokens":2209,"cache_read_input_tokens":147385,"cache_creation_input_tokens":806,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":806},"type":"message"}],"speed":"standard"}},"requestId":"req_011CamAzrxf94CYWY4N2asRQ","type":"assistant","uuid":"07f46d6e-ab40-40cd-a65d-9ce4de4b7f4d","timestamp":"2026-05-06T10:40:40.881Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment/admin-panel","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}
{"parentUuid":"07f46d6e-ab40-40cd-a65d-9ce4de4b7f4d","isSidechain":false,"message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-7","id":"msg_01L3FumKwhnLU6ukbhQpmmJ2","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01XQyX7py6gdUu36HrRF3zJs","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment/admin-panel/lib/review-queue/src/claim.ts","content":"import type { PrismaClient } from '@prisma/client';\nimport type { ClaimResult, PendingItem, UserId } from './types';\n\n/**\n * Claim the next unclaimed review item for a reviewer.\n *\n * Returns `{ kind: 'claimed', item }` when an item was claimed,\n * or `{ kind: 'empty' }` when the queue has nothing pending.\n *\n * The caller is responsible for separation-of-duties filtering\n * (i.e. callers that must not claim their own items should filter\n * beforehand in their service layer).\n */\nexport async function claimNextItem(\n  prisma: PrismaClient,\n  reviewer: UserId\n): Promise<ClaimResult> {\n  // Single transaction so the row-level lock taken by SELECT ... FOR UPDATE\n  // SKIP LOCKED is held until the matching UPDATE commits. SKIP LOCKED makes\n  // concurrent claimers pick distinct rows instead of contending on the same\n  // one, closing the read-modify-write race the original two-step had.\n  return prisma.$transaction(async (tx): Promise<ClaimResult> => {\n    const candidates = await tx.$queryRaw<Array<{ id: string }>>`\n      SELECT \"id\" FROM \"ReviewQueueItem\"\n      WHERE \"status\" = 'PENDING' AND \"claimedBy\" IS NULL\n      ORDER BY \"createdAt\" ASC\n      LIMIT 1\n      FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED\n    `;\n\n    if (candidates.length === 0) {\n      return { kind: 'empty' };\n    }\n\n    const updated = await tx.reviewQueueItem.update({\n      where: { id: candidates[0].id },\n      data: {\n        status: 'UNDER_REVIEW',\n        claimedBy: reviewer,\n        claimedAt: new Date(),\n      },\n    });\n\n    return { kind: 'claimed', item: updated as unknown as PendingItem };\n  });\n}\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":"tool_use","stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":806,"cache_read_input_tokens":147385,"output_tokens":2209,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":806,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[{"input_tokens":1,"output_tokens":2209,"cache_read_input_tokens":147385,"cache_creation_input_tokens":806,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":806},"type":"message"}],"speed":"standard"}},"requestId":"req_011CamAzrxf94CYWY4N2asRQ","type":"assistant","uuid":"8429ea24-3913-44d5-8743-d2b0972812b7","timestamp":"2026-05-06T10:40:48.320Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment/admin-panel","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}

item: updated as unknown as PendingItem };","+    return { kind: 'claimed', item: updated as unknown as PendingItem };","+  });"," }"]}],"originalFile":"import type { PrismaClient } from '@prisma/client';\nimport type { ClaimResult, PendingItem, UserId } from './types';\n\n/**\n * Claim the next unclaimed review item for a reviewer.\n *\n * Returns `{ kind: 'claimed', item }` when an item was claimed,\n * or `{ kind: 'empty' }` when the queue has nothing pending.\n *\n * The caller is responsible for separation-of-duties filtering\n * (i.e. callers that must not claim their own items should filter\n * beforehand in their service layer).\n */\nexport async function claimNextItem(\n  prisma: PrismaClient,\n  reviewer: UserId\n): Promise<ClaimResult> {\n  const candidate = await prisma.reviewQueueItem.findFirst({\n    where: {\n      status: 'PENDING',\n      claimedBy: null,\n    },\n    orderBy: {\n      createdAt: 'asc',\n    },\n  });\n\n  if (!candidate) {\n    return { kind: 'empty' };\n  }\n\n  const updated = await prisma.reviewQueueItem.update({\n    where: { id: candidate.id },\n    data: {\n      status: 'UNDER_REVIEW',\n      claimedBy: reviewer,\n      claimedAt: new Date(),\n    },\n  });\n\n  return { kind: 'claimed', item: updated as unknown as PendingItem };\n}\n","userModified":false},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"8429ea24-3913-44d5-8743-d2b0972812b7","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment/admin-panel","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}
