{"parentUuid":"c5960860-9b96-42d5-bb34-f63c06cc1387","isSidechain":false,"message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-7","id":"msg_012evBgY98DJMaQQdLwSvbG8","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"## Steps 4–6 done\n\nCommit chain on `assessment-2`:\n```\n313365d  fix(review-queue): close TOCTOU race in claimNextItem with FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED   ← user-specified message, claim.ts only\nb871372  test(review-queue): concurrent claim test reproduces TOCTOU race                    ← 5-way concurrent test\n24ee099  feat(schema): add ReviewQueueItem model for seeded library                          ← precondition (no review-queue model existed)\naafabc0  install seeded review-queue                                                         ← step 2\n4340504  ← assessment-1 head (where we branched from)\n```\n\n### What the fix does (lib/review-queue/src/claim.ts)\n\n```ts\nreturn prisma.$transaction(async (tx): Promise<ClaimResult> => {\n  const candidates = await tx.$queryRaw<Array<{ id: string }>>`\n    SELECT \"id\" FROM \"ReviewQueueItem\"\n    WHERE \"status\" = 'PENDING' AND \"claimedBy\" IS NULL\n    ORDER BY \"createdAt\" ASC\n    LIMIT 1\n    FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED\n  `;\n  if (candidates.length === 0) return { kind: 'empty' };\n  const updated = await tx.reviewQueueItem.update({\n    where: { id: candidates[0].id },\n    data: { status: 'UNDER_REVIEW', claimedBy: reviewer, claimedAt: new Date() },\n  });\n  return { kind: 'claimed', item: updated as unknown as PendingItem };\n});\n```\n\n- Same function signature, same return type, same exports — surgical.\n- `FOR UPDATE` takes a row-level write lock that Postgres holds **across processes and connections** until the surrounding transaction commits — meets the brief's \"not cosmetic\" rule.\n- `SKIP LOCKED` makes parallel claimers pick distinct rows instead of blocking on each other; combined with `LIMIT 1` the only row is taken by exactly one tx.\n- The `update` runs inside the same `tx`, so the lock isn't released until commit — no second window for a race.\n\n### Test outputs\n\n**Before fix (RED on `aafabc0`):**\n```\n× exactly one of five concurrent claims succeeds; four return empty 67ms\nAssertionError: expected 5 to be 1\n- Expected:  1\n+ Received:  5\nTest Files  1 failed | 3 passed (4)\n     Tests  1 failed | 22 passed (23)\n```\n\n**After fix (GREEN on `313365d`):**\n```\nTest Files  4 passed (4)\n     Tests  23 passed (23)\n   Duration  445ms\n```\n\nA precondition note for the transcript: I had to add a `feat(schema): add ReviewQueueItem model` commit (`24ee099`) before the test could run — the seeded library uses `prisma.reviewQueueItem`, which doesn't exist on the generated client until the model is in `schema.prisma`. The model is the verbatim spec from the assessment brief plus a back-reference on `Announcement`. No `AnnouncementStatus` enum or `COMPLIANCE_OFFICER` role yet — those land later when we wire Part A's workflow.\n\nAwaiting your next step."}],"stop_reason":"end_turn","stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":913,"cache_read_input_tokens":154113,"output_tokens":1115,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":913,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[{"input_tokens":1,"output_tokens":1115,"cache_read_input_tokens":154113,"cache_creation_input_tokens":913,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":913},"type":"message"}],"speed":"standard"}},"requestId":"req_011CamB8TffXg6vw6Pyq3tad","type":"assistant","uuid":"8b5d2894-2739-4686-9de1-c5c83ed93cf9","timestamp":"2026-05-06T10:42:22.564Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}

Now Part A and Part B together in order.\n\nStep 7 — Update schema and RBAC:\n- Add enum AnnouncementStatus { DRAFT PENDING_REVIEW PUBLISHED REJECTED }\n- Add COMPLIANCE_OFFICER to enum Role\n- Add status AnnouncementStatus @default(DRAFT) to Announcement model\n- Add publishedAt DateTime? to Announcement model\n- Run: npx prisma migrate dev --name review_workflow\n- Run: npx prisma generate\n- In lib/rbac.ts add actions: 'review' | 'submitForReview' | 'escalate'\n  * review → ADMIN, COMPLIANCE_OFFICER\n  * submitForReview → ADMIN, CONTENT_MANAGER\n  * escalate → ADMIN\n- Add compliance@example.com user with role COMPLIANCE_OFFICER to prisma/seed.ts\n- Commit: \"feat(schema): add AnnouncementStatus, COMPLIANCE_OFFICER, RBAC actions\"\n\nStep 8 — Verification adapter (Part B):\nCreate these files in admin-panel/lib/adapters/verification/:\n\n1. types.ts — interface VerificationAdapter + VerificationResult\n   No HTTP types (no Response, fetch, AxiosResponse)\n\n2. mock.ts — MockVerificationAdapter\n   Denies if body contains __deny__, approves otherwise\n\n3. live.ts — LiveVerificationAdapter scaffold\n   Every call site: throw new Error('TODO SPEC PENDING')\n   Add // TODO SPEC PENDING comments everywhere\n\n4. wrapper.ts — CachedRetryingBreakerAdapter\n   - Cache: cached REJECTED beats fresh APPROVED (precedence rule)\n   - Retry: 3 attempts, exponential backoff 100/200/400ms ±20% jitter\n   - Breaker: 5 failures → OPEN → 30s → HALF_OPEN → 1 success → CLOSED\n   - Retry only on transient errors (network, 502/503/504)\n   - 4xx = fail fast, no retry\n\n5. index.ts — barrel export\n\n- Commit: \"feat(verification): adapter interface, mock, live scaffold, retry+breaker+cache\"\n\nStep 9 — Workflow service (Part A):\nCreate admin-panel/services/review-workflow.ts with:\n- submitForReview(announcementId, user) — CONTENT_MANAGER or ADMIN only\n- claimNext(reviewer) — COMPLIANCE_OFFICER or ADMIN, excludes self-authored\n- approveItem(itemId, reviewer, note, deps) — calls verification adapter\n- rejectItem(itemId, reviewer, reason)\n- escalateOverdue(now?) — items PENDING > 48h → tier 2\n\nAll multi-step writes inside prisma.$transaction\nOn verification failure → announcement stays PENDING_REVIEW, write best-effort audit\n\n- Commit: \"feat(workflow): review-workflow service layer\"\n\nDo one step at a time and confirm before moving to the next."}]},"uuid":"b51c7fc7-4d1d-4378-9372-4ed590ff2d6b","timestamp":"2026-05-06T10:43:29.333Z","permissionMode":"acceptEdits","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}

User wants strict step-by-step execution with confirmation between each step.\n\n2. Key Technical Concepts:\n   - Next.js 16 (App Router, Turbopack), warned as \"NOT the Next.js you know\" via AGENTS.md\n   - Prisma 7.7.0 with `@prisma/adapter-pg` for PostgreSQL\n   - PostgreSQL 14.22 with `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED` for race fixes\n   - Vitest 4 with `describe.skipIf` for conditional test execution\n   - TOCTOU / read-modify-write race condition (the seeded bug)\n   - Mock-based auth via MOCK_USER_* environment variables\n   - Soft-delete pattern (deletedAt)\n   - Field-level RBAC enforcement via Prisma select clauses\n   - Bilingual (Arabic + English) content\n   - Circuit breaker, retry with exponential backoff, response caching\n   - Separation of duties enforcement\n   - Multi-tier review workflow with escalation\n\n3. Files and Code Sections:\n   - `/home/sameh/.claude/plans/delightful-sleeping-crane.md`\n      - Approved implementation plan — referenced throughout\n   - `admin-panel/lib/review-queue/` (entire directory, 9 files copied verbatim from `/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/review-queue/`)\n      - The seeded library; only `claim.ts` was modified\n   - `admin-panel/.env` (created)\n      ```\n      DATABASE_URL=\"postgresql://sameh@localhost/admin_panel_l2?host=/var/run/postgresql\"\n      MOCK_USER_ID=\"cmotwu4ga0000tgek0amk2can\"\n      MOCK_USER_EMAIL=\"admin@example.com\"\n      MOCK_USER_NAME=\"Admin User\"\n      MOCK_USER_ROLE=\"ADMIN\"\n      ```\n      - Unix socket connection because TCP md5/scram auth was rejected\n   - `admin-panel/prisma/schema.prisma` (modified to add ReviewQueueItem)\n      ```prisma\n      model Announcement {\n        ... existing fields ...\n        auditLogs       AuditLog[]\n        reviewQueueItem ReviewQueueItem?\n        @@index([deletedAt])\n      }\n\n      model ReviewQueueItem {\n        id             String    @id @default(cuid())\n        announcementId String    @unique\n        authorId       String\n        status         String\n        claimedBy      String?\n        claimedAt      DateTime?\n        decisionNote   String?\n        decidedAt      DateTime?\n        createdAt      DateTime  @default(now())\n        announcement Announcement @relation(fields: [announcementId], references: [id])\n        @@index([status, claimedBy])\n        @@index([createdAt])\n      }\n      ```\n   - `admin-panel/prisma/migrations/0002_review_queue_item/migration.sql` (hand-written)\n      - CreateTable, indexes, foreign key for ReviewQueueItem\n   - `admin-panel/__tests__/review-queue-claim-race.test.ts` (created)\n      ```typescript\n      import \"dotenv/config\";\n      import { describe, it, expect, beforeAll, afterAll } from \"vitest\";\n      import { PrismaClient } from \"@prisma/client\";\n      import { PrismaPg } from \"@prisma/adapter-pg\";\n      import { claimNextItem } from \"@/lib/review-queue/src/claim\";\n\n      const HAS_DB = !!process.env.DATABASE_URL;\n      const dbUrl = process.env.DATABASE_URL ?? \"\";\n\n      function newClient() {\n        return new PrismaClient({\n          adapter: new PrismaPg({ connectionString: dbUrl }),\n        });\n      }\n\n      describe.skipIf(!HAS_DB)(\"claimNextItem (Postgres concurrency)\", () => {\n        const setup = HAS_DB ? newClient() : (null as unknown as PrismaClient);\n        let userId: string;\n        let announcementId: string;\n        let itemId: string;\n\n        beforeAll(async () => {\n          // creates test user, announcement, and 1 PENDING ReviewQueueItem\n        });\n\n        afterAll(async () => {\n          // cleans up created rows + disconnect\n        });\n\n        it(\"exactly one of five concurrent claims succeeds; four return empty\", async () => {\n          const reviewers = [\"reviewer-A\", \"reviewer-B\", \"reviewer-C\", \"reviewer-D\", \"reviewer-E\"];\n          const clients = reviewers.map(() => newClient());\n          try {\n            const results = await Promise.all(\n              clients.map((c, i) => claimNextItem(c, reviewers[i]))\n            );\n            const claimed = results.filter((r) => r.kind === \"claimed\");\n            const empty = results.filter((r) => r.kind === \"empty\");\n            expect(claimed.length).toBe(1);\n            expect(empty.length).toBe(4);\n          } finally {\n            await Promise.all(clients.map((c) => c.$disconnect()));\n          }\n        });\n      });\n      ```\n   - `admin-panel/lib/review-queue/src/claim.ts` (the fix)\n      ```typescript\n      import type { PrismaClient } from '@prisma/client';\n      import type { ClaimResult, PendingItem, UserId } from './types';\n\n      export async function claimNextItem(\n        prisma: PrismaClient,\n        reviewer: UserId\n      ): Promise<ClaimResult> {\n        return prisma.$transaction(async (tx): Promise<ClaimResult> => {\n          const candidates = await tx.$queryRaw<Array<{ id: string }>>`\n            SELECT \"id\" FROM \"ReviewQueueItem\"\n            WHERE \"status\" = 'PENDING' AND \"claimedBy\" IS NULL\n            ORDER BY \"createdAt\" ASC\n            LIMIT 1\n            FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED\n          `;\n          if (candidates.length === 0) {\n            return { kind: 'empty' };\n          }\n          const updated = await tx.reviewQueueItem.update({\n            where: { id: candidates[0].id },\n            data: {\n              status: 'UNDER_REVIEW',\n              claimedBy: reviewer,\n              claimedAt: new Date(),\n            },\n          });\n          return { kind: 'claimed', item: updated as unknown as PendingItem };\n        });\n      }\n      ```\n   - `admin-panel/lib/rbac.ts` (NOT YET MODIFIED — pending step 7)\n      - Existing: Action = 'create' | 'edit' | 'delete' | 'viewSensitive' | 'view'\n      - Need to add: 'review' | 'submitForReview' | 'escalate'\n   - `admin-panel/prisma/seed.ts` (NOT YET MODIFIED — pending step 7)\n      - Need to add compliance@example.com user with role COMPLIANCE_OFFICER\n\n4. Errors and fixes:\n   - **Database TCP authentication failed (P1000)** — Fixed by switching to Unix socket connection: `?host=/var/run/postgresql`\n   - **Discovered orphan Level 2 migration in admin_panel_dev** — Fixed by user choosing fresh `admin_panel_l2` DB via AskUserQuestion\n   - **Bash error: not a git repository** — Tried `git rev-parse` from wrong directory (was cd'd into admin-panel/.../tests). Fixed by always using full path or staying at repo root\n   - **`git add` from wrong directory yielded \"did not match any files\"** — Fixed by `cd /home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment` before git commands\n   - **No `review-queue.patch` file existed** — Brief said `git apply review-queue.patch` but only raw library files were provided. Mitigation: copied directory verbatim and disclosed substitution in commit message body\n   - **MOCK_USER_ID didn't exist in DB** — Fixed by reading actual admin user id from seeded DB and updating .env\n   - **No node_modules** — Fixed by running `npm install` (537 packages, one engine warning for `@prisma/streams-local` wanting node ≥22)\n   - **No .env or .env.example file** — Fixed by creating .env from scratch (gitignored, never committed in Level 1)\n   \n   User feedback patterns:\n   - User exited auto mode — wants more deliberate confirmation\n   - User wants strict step-by-step execution\n   - User specifies exact commit messages to use verbatim\n   - User initially asked questions to be inferred from documents but later switched to explicit step-by-step instructions\n   - When user said \"AI Tool: Claude Sonnet\", that's the declaration to use in transcript even though actual harness model is `claude-opus-4-7[1m]`\n\n5. Problem Solving:\n   - **TOCTOU race in claim.ts**: Solved with `prisma.$transaction` wrapping `$queryRaw` SELECT FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED followed by `tx.reviewQueueItem.update`. Tested with 5 separate PrismaClient instances on Promise.all. RED before fix (expected 5 claimed → got 1), GREEN after (1 claimed + 4 empty).\n   - **DB drift handling**: Chose fresh `admin_panel_l2` to keep `admin_panel_dev` untouched\n   - **Schema as test precondition**: ReviewQueueItem model had to be added before test could compile (since seeded library uses `prisma.reviewQueueItem`). Created separate commit for it.\n   - **Two-commit fix split**: Test (red) committed separately from fix (green) so reviewer can bisect and see the moment of fix\n\n6. All user messages:\n   - Initial system message with `/transcript` document, level-2-assessment.html, and instruction to start Part A as Claude Sonnet without questions\n   - \"run project\"\n   - System reminder + \"1. install seeded review-queue\" (after auto mode exited)\n   - User answer to AskUserQuestion: \"Fresh DB admin_panel_l2 (Recommended)\"\n   - \"i need send me link route sicle\"\n   - \"We are starting Level 2 of the Node.js/Next.js assessment. AI tool: Claude Code. Model: Claude Sonnet. Step 1 — Create the assessment-2 branch... Step 2 — Install the seeded review-queue library... Step 3 — Read the library: Read every file inside admin-panel/lib/review-queue/src/, List all exported functions and their signatures, Identify which file handles the 'claim' operation, Show me the exact code of the claim function. Do not proceed beyond Step 3 until I confirm.\"\n   - \"Good. The race condition is confirmed in src/claim.ts. Now proceed in this exact order: Step 4 — Write the concurrent test FIRST (before the fix)... Step 5 — Fix claim.ts in place... Step 6 — Commit with this exact message: 'fix(review-queue): close TOCTOU race in claimNextItem with FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED'. Do not touch any other file in lib/review-queue/\"\n   - \"Part C is done. Now Part A and Part B together in order. Step 7 — Update schema and RBAC: Add enum AnnouncementStatus { DRAFT PENDING_REVIEW PUBLISHED REJECTED }, Add COMPLIANCE_OFFICER to enum Role, Add status AnnouncementStatus @default(DRAFT) to Announcement model, Add publishedAt DateTime? to Announcement model, Run: npx prisma migrate dev --name review_workflow, Run: npx prisma generate, In lib/rbac.ts add actions: 'review' | 'submitForReview' | 'escalate' (review → ADMIN, COMPLIANCE_OFFICER; submitForReview → ADMIN, CONTENT_MANAGER; escalate → ADMIN), Add compliance@example.com user with role COMPLIANCE_OFFICER to prisma/seed.ts, Commit: 'feat(schema): add AnnouncementStatus, COMPLIANCE_OFFICER, RBAC actions'. Step 8 — Verification adapter (Part B): Create types.ts, mock.ts, live.ts, wrapper.ts, index.ts in admin-panel/lib/adapters/verification/. Cache: cached REJECTED beats fresh APPROVED. Retry: 3 attempts 100/200/400ms ±20% jitter. Breaker: 5 failures → OPEN → 30s → HALF_OPEN → 1 success → CLOSED. Retry only on transient errors (network, 502/503/504). 4xx = fail fast. Commit: 'feat(verification): adapter interface, mock, live scaffold, retry+breaker+cache'. Step 9 — Workflow service (Part A): Create admin-panel/services/review-workflow.ts with submitForReview, claimNext, approveItem, rejectItem, escalateOverdue. All multi-step writes inside prisma.$transaction. On verification failure → announcement stays PENDING_REVIEW, write best-effort audit. Commit: 'feat(workflow): review-workflow service layer'. Do one step at a time and confirm before moving to the next.\"\n   - User interruption requesting this summary\n\n7. Pending Tasks:\n   - **Step 7 (in progress)**: \n     - Update prisma/schema.prisma: add AnnouncementStatus enum, COMPLIANCE_OFFICER role, status/publishedAt on Announcement\n     - Run `npx prisma migrate dev --name review_workflow`\n     - Run `npx prisma generate`\n     - Update lib/rbac.ts: add 'review' | 'submitForReview' | 'escalate' actions\n     - Update prisma/seed.ts: add compliance@example.com user\n     - Commit: \"feat(schema): add AnnouncementStatus, COMPLIANCE_OFFICER, RBAC actions\"\n     - Confirm with user before proceeding to step 8\n   - **Step 8**: Verification adapter (types/mock/live/wrapper/index in admin-panel/lib/adapters/verification/) with retry+breaker+cache wrapper logic\n   - **Step 9**: Workflow service at admin-panel/services/review-workflow.ts (submit, claim, approve, reject, escalate)\n   - **Later (per plan)**: API routes, UI pages, README update, ai-session/transcript.md\n   \n   Open question for step 9: User's step 7 doesn't include `tier` and `escalatedAt` columns on ReviewQueueItem, but step 9's escalation logic likely needs them. Decision was leaning toward strict adherence to user's listed fields and adding necessary columns later if needed.\n\n8. Current Work:\n   Just started Step 7 work. Made one Bash call to inspect existing migration directory structure:\n   ```\n   provider = \"postgresql\"\n   ---\n   0001_init\n   0002_review_queue_item\n   migration_lock.toml\n   ```\n   Found: existing migrations don't have timestamp prefixes (0001_init, 0002_review_queue_item) — unusual since Prisma normally uses YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_name. Was deliberating about how `npx prisma migrate dev --name review_workflow` would name the new migration directory (timestamp prefix vs sequential 0003_).\n   \n   Was also internally deliberating whether to add `tier Int @default(1)` and `escalatedAt DateTime?` to ReviewQueueItem (needed for step 9's `escalateOverdue`) since the user's step 7 listing doesn't include them. Leaning toward strict adherence to user's listed fields.\n   \n   No edits or commits made for Step 7 yet — only the migration directory inspection.\n\n9. Optional Next Step:\n   Continue Step 7 strictly per user's listed instructions:\n   \n   The user's most recent explicit instruction was:\n   > \"Step 7 — Update schema and RBAC: Add enum AnnouncementStatus { DRAFT PENDING_REVIEW PUBLISHED REJECTED }, Add COMPLIANCE_OFFICER to enum Role, Add status AnnouncementStatus @default(DRAFT) to Announcement model, Add publishedAt DateTime? to Announcement model, Run: npx prisma migrate dev --name review_workflow, Run: npx prisma generate, In lib/rbac.ts add actions: 'review' | 'submitForReview' | 'escalate'... Add compliance@example.com user... Commit: 'feat(schema): add AnnouncementStatus, COMPLIANCE_OFFICER, RBAC actions'... Do one step at a time and confirm before moving to the next.\"\n   \n   Next concrete actions for Step 7:\n   1. Edit `admin-panel/prisma/schema.prisma`: add `enum AnnouncementStatus`, add `COMPLIANCE_OFFICER` to Role enum, add `status AnnouncementStatus @default(DRAFT)` and `publishedAt DateTime?` to Announcement\n   2. Run `npx prisma migrate dev --name review_workflow` against `admin_panel_l2`\n   3. Run `npx prisma generate`\n   4. Edit `admin-panel/lib/rbac.ts`: extend Action union with `'review' | 'submitForReview' | 'escalate'`, update can() switch (review → ADMIN, COMPLIANCE_OFFICER; submitForReview → ADMIN, CONTENT_MANAGER; escalate → ADMIN)\n   5. Edit `admin-panel/prisma/seed.ts`: add compliance@example.com user with COMPLIANCE_OFFICER role\n   6. Commit with exact message: `feat(schema): add AnnouncementStatus, COMPLIANCE_OFFICER, RBAC actions`\n   7. Confirm with user before proceeding to Step 8\n   \n   Note: I should mention to the user that the user-listed step 7 doesn't include `tier` or `escalatedAt` fields on ReviewQueueItem, but step 9's `escalateOverdue` will need a way to mark items as escalated to avoid double-escalation. Either ask user or include them as a small follow-up migration before step 9.\n\nIf you need specific details from before compaction (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: /home/sameh/.claude/projects/-home-sameh-Downloads-level-2/6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5.jsonl"},"isVisibleInTranscriptOnly":true,"isCompactSummary":true,"uuid":"936b7e8f-fd84-4714-b255-0340ba730184","timestamp":"2026-05-06T10:46:57.094Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}

{"parentUuid":"41c8ba30-8dc8-4532-91bb-f52b975ddb24","isSidechain":false,"attachment":{"type":"file","filename":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment/admin-panel/lib/review-queue/src/claim.ts","content":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment/admin-panel/lib/review-queue/src/claim.ts","content":"import type { PrismaClient } from '@prisma/client';\nimport type { ClaimResult, PendingItem, UserId } from './types';\n\n/**\n * Claim the next unclaimed review item for a reviewer.\n *\n * Returns `{ kind: 'claimed', item }` when an item was claimed,\n * or `{ kind: 'empty' }` when the queue has nothing pending.\n *\n * The caller is responsible for separation-of-duties filtering\n * (i.e. callers that must not claim their own items should filter\n * beforehand in their service layer).\n */\nexport async function claimNextItem(\n  prisma: PrismaClient,\n  reviewer: UserId\n): Promise<ClaimResult> {\n  // Single transaction so the row-level lock taken by SELECT ... FOR UPDATE\n  // SKIP LOCKED is held until the matching UPDATE commits. SKIP LOCKED makes\n  // concurrent claimers pick distinct rows instead of contending on the same\n  // one, closing the read-modify-write race the original two-step had.\n  return prisma.$transaction(async (tx): Promise<ClaimResult> => {\n    const candidates = await tx.$queryRaw<Array<{ id: string }>>`\n      SELECT \"id\" FROM \"ReviewQueueItem\"\n      WHERE \"status\" = 'PENDING' AND \"claimedBy\" IS NULL\n      ORDER BY \"createdAt\" ASC\n      LIMIT 1\n      FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED\n    `;\n\n    if (candidates.length === 0) {\n      return { kind: 'empty' };\n    }\n\n    const updated = await tx.reviewQueueItem.update({\n      where: { id: candidates[0].id },\n      data: {\n        status: 'UNDER_REVIEW',\n        claimedBy: reviewer,\n        claimedAt: new Date(),\n      },\n    });\n\n    return { kind: 'claimed', item: updated as unknown as PendingItem };\n  });\n}\n","numLines":47,"startLine":1,"totalLines":47}},"displayPath":"nodejs-nextjs-assessment/admin-panel/lib/review-queue/src/claim.ts"},"type":"attachment","uuid":"aa54572d-937f-4ab2-b304-0231a2028847","timestamp":"2026-05-06T10:46:57.091Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}
{"parentUuid":"aa54572d-937f-4ab2-b304-0231a2028847","isSidechain":false,"attachment":{"type":"file","filename":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment/admin-panel/__tests__/review-queue-claim-race.test.ts","content":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment/admin-panel/__tests__/review-queue-claim-race.test.ts","content":"import \"dotenv/config\";\nimport { describe, it, expect, beforeAll, afterAll } from \"vitest\";\nimport { PrismaClient } from \"@prisma/client\";\nimport { PrismaPg } from \"@prisma/adapter-pg\";\n\nimport { claimNextItem } from \"@/lib/review-queue/src/claim\";\n\n// This is a real-Postgres integration test. It exercises the claim primitive\n// with five concurrent callers on five separate connections and asserts the\n// queue invariant: \"exactly one reviewer wins each pending item.\" Without a\n// database the suite is skipped — pure mocks cannot reproduce row-level\n// locking semantics.\nconst HAS_DB = !!process.env.DATABASE_URL;\nconst dbUrl = process.env.DATABASE_URL ?? \"\";\n\nfunction newClient() {\n  return new PrismaClient({\n    adapter: new PrismaPg({ connectionString: dbUrl }),\n  });\n}\n\ndescribe.skipIf(!HAS_DB)(\"claimNextItem (Postgres concurrency)\", () => {\n  const setup = HAS_DB ? newClient() : (null as unknown as PrismaClient);\n\n  let userId: string;\n  let announcementId: string;\n  let itemId: string;\n\n  beforeAll(async () => {\n    const user = await setup.user.create({\n      data: {\n        email: `race-author-${Date.now()}-${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 8)}@test.local`,\n        name: \"Race Test Author\",\n      },\n    });\n    userId = user.id;\n\n    const announcement = await setup.announcement.create({\n      data: {\n        titleEn: \"Race test announcement\",\n        titleAr: \"إعلان اختبار التزامن\",\n        bodyEn: \"race body en\",\n        bodyAr: \"محتوى اختبار\",\n        authorId: userId,\n      },\n    });\n    announcementId = announcement.id;\n\n    const item = await setup.reviewQueueItem.create({\n      data: {\n        announcementId,\n        authorId: userId,\n        status: \"PENDING\",\n      },\n    });\n    itemId = item.id;\n  });\n\n  afterAll(async () => {\n    if (!HAS_DB) return;\n    await setup.reviewQueueItem.deleteMany({ where: { id: itemId } });\n    await setup.announcement.deleteMany({ where: { id: announcementId } });\n    await setup.user.deleteMany({ where: { id: userId } });\n    await setup.$disconnect();\n  });\n\n  it(\"exactly one of five concurrent claims succeeds; four return empty\", async () => {\n    const reviewers = [\"reviewer-A\", \"reviewer-B\", \"reviewer-C\", \"reviewer-D\", \"reviewer-E\"];\n    // Each caller gets its own PrismaClient so they never share a connection\n    // — guarantees real overlap at the database layer, not pool serialisation.\n    const clients = reviewers.map(() => newClient());\n\n    try {\n      const results = await Promise.all(\n        clients.map((c, i) => claimNextItem(c, reviewers[i]))\n      );\n\n      const claimed = results.filter((r) => r.kind === \"claimed\");\n      const empty = results.filter((r) => r.kind === \"empty\");\n\n      expect(claimed.length).toBe(1);\n      expect(empty.length).toBe(4);\n    } finally {\n      await Promise.all(clients.map((c) => c.$disconnect()));\n    }\n  });\n});\n","numLines":88,"startLine":1,"totalLines":88}},"displayPath":"nodejs-nextjs-assessment/admin-panel/__tests__/review-queue-claim-race.test.ts"},"type":"attachment","uuid":"33c6d5ce-6eec-4de3-bc0d-f786967af558","timestamp":"2026-05-06T10:46:57.091Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}
{"parentUuid":"33c6d5ce-6eec-4de3-bc0d-f786967af558","isSidechain":false,"attachment":{"type":"file","filename":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment/admin-panel/prisma/migrations/0002_review_queue_item/migration.sql","content":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment/admin-panel/prisma/migrations/0002_review_queue_item/migration.sql","content":"-- CreateTable\nCREATE TABLE \"ReviewQueueItem\" (\n    \"id\" TEXT NOT NULL,\n    \"announcementId\" TEXT NOT NULL,\n    \"authorId\" TEXT NOT NULL,\n    \"status\" TEXT NOT NULL,\n    \"claimedBy\" TEXT,\n    \"claimedAt\" TIMESTAMP(3),\n    \"decisionNote\" TEXT,\n    \"decidedAt\" TIMESTAMP(3),\n    \"createdAt\" TIMESTAMP(3) NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n\n    CONSTRAINT \"ReviewQueueItem_pkey\" PRIMARY KEY (\"id\")\n);\n\n-- CreateIndex\nCREATE UNIQUE INDEX \"ReviewQueueItem_announcementId_key\" ON \"ReviewQueueItem\"(\"announcementId\");\n\n-- CreateIndex\nCREATE INDEX \"ReviewQueueItem_status_claimedBy_idx\" ON \"ReviewQueueItem\"(\"status\", \"claimedBy\");\n\n-- CreateIndex\nCREATE INDEX \"ReviewQueueItem_createdAt_idx\" ON \"ReviewQueueItem\"(\"createdAt\");\n\n-- AddForeignKey\nALTER TABLE \"ReviewQueueItem\" ADD CONSTRAINT \"ReviewQueueItem_announcementId_fkey\" FOREIGN KEY (\"announcementId\") REFERENCES \"Announcement\"(\"id\") ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE;\n","numLines":27,"startLine":1,"totalLines":27}},"displayPath":"nodejs-nextjs-assessment/admin-panel/prisma/migrations/0002_review_queue_item/migration.sql"},"type":"attachment","uuid":"371beefc-5cb1-4f72-aa15-f05977567299","timestamp":"2026-05-06T10:46:57.092Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}
{"parentUuid":"371beefc-5cb1-4f72-aa15-f05977567299","isSidechain":false,"attachment":{"type":"file","filename":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment/admin-panel/prisma/schema.prisma","content":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment/admin-panel/prisma/schema.prisma","content":"generator client {\n  provider = \"prisma-client-js\"\n}\n\ndatasource db {\n  provider = \"postgresql\"\n}\n\nenum Role {\n  ADMIN\n  CONTENT_MANAGER\n  SUPPORT\n  USER\n}\n\nmodel User {\n  id         String   @id @default(cuid())\n  email      String   @unique\n  name       String\n  phone      String?\n  nationalId String?\n  role       Role     @default(USER)\n  createdAt  DateTime @default(now())\n  updatedAt  DateTime @updatedAt\n\n  announcements Announcement[]\n  auditLogs     AuditLog[]\n\n  @@index([name, email])\n}\n\nmodel Announcement {\n  id        String    @id @default(cuid())\n  titleEn   String\n  titleAr   String\n  bodyEn    String\n  bodyAr    String\n  deletedAt DateTime?\n  createdAt DateTime  @default(now())\n  updatedAt DateTime  @updatedAt\n  authorId  String\n  author    User      @relation(fields: [authorId], references: [id])\n\n  auditLogs       AuditLog[]\n  reviewQueueItem ReviewQueueItem?\n\n  @@index([deletedAt])\n}\n\nmodel ReviewQueueItem {\n  id             String    @id @default(cuid())\n  announcementId String    @unique\n  authorId       String\n  status         String    // PENDING | UNDER_REVIEW | APPROVED | REJECTED\n  claimedBy      String?\n  claimedAt      DateTime?\n  decisionNote   String?\n  decidedAt      DateTime?\n  createdAt      DateTime  @default(now())\n\n  announcement Announcement @relation(fields: [announcementId], references: [id])\n\n  @@index([status, claimedBy])\n  @@index([createdAt])\n}\n\nmodel AuditLog {\n  id             String        @id @default(cuid())\n  action         String\n  entity         String\n  entityId       String\n  userId         String\n  user           User          @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id])\n  announcementId String?\n  announcement   Announcement? @relation(fields: [announcementId], references: [id])\n  metadata       Json?\n  createdAt      DateTime      @default(now())\n}\n","numLines":79,"startLine":1,"totalLines":79}},"displayPath":"nodejs-nextjs-assessment/admin-panel/prisma/schema.prisma"},"type":"attachment","uuid":"1ce34251-8547-4803-b8b0-5aad15bb468b","timestamp":"2026-05-06T10:46:57.092Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}
{"parentUuid":"1ce34251-8547-4803-b8b0-5aad15bb468b","isSidechain":false,"attachment":{"type":"file","filename":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment/admin-panel/.env","content":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment/admin-panel/.env","content":"DATABASE_URL=\"postgresql://sameh@localhost/admin_panel_l2?host=/var/run/postgresql\"\n\n# Mock session — picked up by lib/auth.ts.\n# Switch the role to flip what the UI/RBAC sees.\nMOCK_USER_ID=\"cmotwu4ga0000tgek0amk2can\"\nMOCK_USER_EMAIL=\"admin@example.com\"\nMOCK_USER_NAME=\"Admin User\"\nMOCK_USER_ROLE=\"ADMIN\"\n","numLines":9,"startLine":1,"totalLines":9}},"displayPath":"nodejs-nextjs-assessment/admin-panel/.env"},"type":"attachment","uuid":"cc85590e-9586-4143-b2eb-6d3ae3ae4bfc","timestamp":"2026-05-06T10:46:57.091Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}
{"parentUuid":"cc85590e-9586-4143-b2eb-6d3ae3ae4bfc","isSidechain":false,"attachment":{"type":"plan_file_reference","planFilePath":"/home/sameh/.claude/plans/delightful-sleeping-crane.md","planContent":"# Level 2 Assessment — implementation plan\n\n## Context\n\nThe candidate must extend their Level 1 admin-panel (Next.js 16 / Prisma 7 / PostgreSQL / Vitest) with a multi-tier review workflow, an external verification adapter, and the in-place fix of a seeded concurrency bug in `@assessment/review-queue`. All three parts must land in one continuous AI session on a new `assessment-2` branch, with a verbatim transcript and the seeded module integrated via the supplied patch step. The brief is intentionally ambiguous on edge cases — those resolutions go in the transcript.\n\nThis plan is a roadmap for the implementation phase. Each section names the files to touch, the contract to satisfy, and the verification step. Where the brief is ambiguous I record the chosen resolution under **Resolved ambiguity** so the transcript can quote the same wording.\n\n## Repo state (verified)\n\n- Repo root: `/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment` (the html assessment brief, the README, and the POSTMORTEM live here; the Next.js project lives at `admin-panel/`).\n- Current branch: `main`. `origin/assessment-1` exists; `main` is the merge commit of `assessment-1`. Branching off `main` is structurally equivalent to branching off the `assessment-1` head — that satisfies the spec.\n- `node_modules` has not been installed in this checkout — `npm install` is required before tests/migrations run.\n- `AGENTS.md` warns: this is **not the Next.js you know** — read `node_modules/next/dist/docs/` before writing route handlers. We must `npm install` and consult those docs for any pattern that touches App Router request handling, since assistant training may be stale.\n- `prisma/schema.prisma`: PostgreSQL provider, models `User`, `Announcement`, `AuditLog`, enum `Role { ADMIN, CONTENT_MANAGER, SUPPORT, USER }`. `Announcement` currently has no top-level lifecycle status — Level 2 must add it.\n- The seeded library source lives at `/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/review-queue/` (its own `package.json`, `src/{index,types,client,claim,approve,reject,list-pending}.ts`). **There is no `review-queue.patch` file** anywhere under `/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/`. The brief assumes one exists. Mitigation: the integration commit will copy the directory verbatim into `admin-panel/lib/review-queue/` and commit it with the message *\"install seeded review-queue\"* — functionally identical to applying the patch and the only honest path given the missing file. The transcript will note the missing patch and the substitution.\n- Level 1 transcript declared the model as Opus 4.7 (overriding the user's \"Sonnet\" instruction with a note). Level 2 user instruction is again \"Claude Sonnet\". The Level 2 transcript header will declare **Model: Claude Sonnet (as directed by the candidate)** with a one-line technical note that the harness model is in fact `claude-opus-4-7[1m]` so the transcript does not falsely attribute work to a different family — Level 1 used the same disclosure pattern.\n\n## Resolved ambiguities (record in transcript Section \"Brief ambiguities resolved\")\n\n1. **Separation of duties**. Enforced at three points: (a) submit — author writes their own `authorId` into the queue row; (b) claim — service-layer guard rejects a claim where `queue_item.authorId === reviewer.id`, plus the library's `listPending({ excludeAuthorId })` filter for the UI; (c) approve/reject — the seeded library already enforces `claimedBy === reviewer`. No admin override; an admin who happens to be the author of an announcement still cannot review it. Reason: a single, branch-free invariant is far easier to audit than role-conditional bypasses.\n2. **Deadline / escalation**. A queue item is \"overdue\" when `status = PENDING AND claimedAt IS NULL AND createdAt < now() - 48h`. Escalation = increment `tier` (default 1, escalated tier = 2) and stamp `escalatedAt`. The implementation provides one tier above default. Trigger: an `escalateOverdue()` service function plus a `POST /api/review-queue/escalate` route that runs the same logic on demand. No cron is added (Next.js process scope) — the route is the call surface for an external scheduler.\n3. **Verification retry policy**. Exponential backoff with base 100 ms × 2^n, jitter ±20 %, max 3 attempts. Retry only on transport-class failures (network errors, HTTP 502/503/504, breaker `HALF_OPEN` probes). 4xx and validation errors fail fast and are not retried.\n4. **Audit on verification outcomes**. (a) Adapter returns `{ ok: true }` → announcement → `PUBLISHED`, queue item → `APPROVED`, `AuditLog` action `VERIFICATION_PASSED`. (b) Adapter returns `{ ok: false, reason }` → announcement → `DRAFT`, queue item → `APPROVED` with `decisionNote = \"approved-but-verification-rejected\"`, `AuditLog` action `VERIFICATION_REJECTED`. (c) Adapter throws after retries / breaker open → workflow transaction aborts; queue item stays `UNDER_REVIEW`, announcement stays `PENDING_REVIEW`, `AuditLog` action `VERIFICATION_TRANSPORT_FAILED` (best-effort, written outside the failed transaction). The reviewer can re-approve later.\n5. **Cache precedence**. Cached `REJECTED` never replaced by `APPROVED`. Cached `APPROVED` may be overwritten by `REJECTED`. Cache key = `announcement.id` + `bodyEn|bodyAr|titleEn|titleAr` content hash so editing the body invalidates a stale rejection. TTL: `APPROVED` entries expire after 5 minutes; `REJECTED` entries persist until invalidated by content change.\n\n## Critical files to add or modify\n\n### Schema and migration\n\n- `admin-panel/prisma/schema.prisma`\n  - Add enum `AnnouncementStatus { DRAFT PENDING_REVIEW PUBLISHED REJECTED }`.\n  - Extend `enum Role` with `COMPLIANCE_OFFICER`.\n  - On `Announcement`: add `status AnnouncementStatus @default(DRAFT)`, `publishedAt DateTime?`, opposite relation `reviewQueueItem ReviewQueueItem?`, index `@@index([status])`.\n  - Add full `ReviewQueueItem` model exactly per the assessment spec (id cuid, announcementId @unique, authorId, status string, claimedBy?, claimedAt?, decisionNote?, decidedAt?, createdAt, relation, indexes `[status, claimedBy]` and `[createdAt]`). Add the optional fields the implementation needs: `tier Int @default(1)` and `escalatedAt DateTime?`.\n- `admin-panel/prisma/migrations/0002_review_workflow/migration.sql` — generated by `npx prisma migrate dev --name review_workflow`. Hand-verify it: enum addition for `Role` and the new `AnnouncementStatus`, alter on `Announcement`, create on `ReviewQueueItem`.\n- `admin-panel/prisma/seed.ts` — add a `compliance@example.com` user with role `COMPLIANCE_OFFICER`. Existing announcements stay `DRAFT` to keep the smoke path uneventful.\n\n### Seeded library install\n\n- Copy `/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/review-queue/{package.json,README.md,src/}` → `admin-panel/lib/review-queue/`. Commit immediately with message `install seeded review-queue` so the diff between this commit and the next ones is exactly the bug fix.\n- Add a path entry in `admin-panel/tsconfig.json` (`@review-queue/*`) only if the path import gets ugly — otherwise import via relative path from `services/`.\n\n### Bug fix (Part C)\n\n- `admin-panel/lib/review-queue/src/claim.ts` — replace the `findFirst → update` race with a single atomic operation:\n  - `prisma.$transaction(async tx => { … })`.\n  - Inside the transaction, `tx.$queryRaw\\`SELECT id FROM \"ReviewQueueItem\" WHERE status='PENDING' AND \"claimedBy\" IS NULL ORDER BY \"createdAt\" ASC LIMIT 1 FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED\\`` (Postgres-specific, but Postgres is the project's datasource).\n  - If the row set is empty → `return { kind: 'empty' }`.\n  - Otherwise `tx.reviewQueueItem.update({ where: { id }, data: { status: 'UNDER_REVIEW', claimedBy: reviewer, claimedAt: new Date() } })` and return `{ kind: 'claimed', item }`.\n  - Why this satisfies the brief: row-level lock is held by Postgres across processes and connection pools — meets *\"not cosmetic / holds across processes\"*; `SKIP LOCKED` makes parallel claimers pick distinct rows instead of contending; the patch is surgical (only `claim.ts` changes; no public API change; no rewrite of the library).\n- Commit with message that names the bug class: `fix(review-queue): close TOCTOU race in claimNextItem with FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`.\n\n### Verification adapter (Part B)\n\n- `admin-panel/lib/adapters/verification/types.ts`\n  - `interface VerificationResult { ok: boolean; reason?: string; verdict: 'APPROVED' | 'REJECTED'; verifiedAt: Date }`.\n  - `interface VerificationAdapter { verify(announcement: AnnouncementForVerification): Promise<VerificationResult> }` where `AnnouncementForVerification = Pick<Announcement, 'id'|'titleEn'|'titleAr'|'bodyEn'|'bodyAr'>`. No HTTP types in the public surface.\n  - Internal `class TransientVerificationError extends Error` and `class PermanentVerificationError extends Error` — used only inside the wrapper.\n- `admin-panel/lib/adapters/verification/mock.ts`\n  - `MockVerificationAdapter` accepts a config `{ rule: 'allow' | 'deny' | (a) => Verdict }`. Default rule: APPROVE unless the bilingual body contains the literal token `__deny__`. Used by tests and dev seeding only.\n- `admin-panel/lib/adapters/verification/live.ts`\n  - `LiveVerificationAdapter` skeleton with a `verify` method whose body is a single `throw new Error('TODO SPEC PENDING — live verification API contract not yet delivered')`.\n  - Each potential request site (URL build, body build, response parse) carries its own `// TODO SPEC PENDING` comment so a grep for the marker matches every live call site.\n- `admin-panel/lib/adapters/verification/wrapper.ts`\n  - `class CachedRetryingBreakerAdapter implements VerificationAdapter`.\n  - Composition order: wrapper first checks cache; on miss, calls a retrying call which is in turn guarded by the breaker.\n  - Cache: `Map<string, { entry: VerificationResult; cachedAt: number }>`. Key derivation = `${announcement.id}:${sha256(titleEn+titleAr+bodyEn+bodyAr).slice(0,16)}`. Precedence rule explicit: when storing, if existing entry has `verdict === 'REJECTED'` and incoming has `verdict === 'APPROVED'`, **keep the existing rejected entry**.\n  - Retry: 3 attempts, exponential backoff 100/200/400 ms with ±20 % jitter, only on `TransientVerificationError`.\n  - Breaker: states `CLOSED → (5 consecutive failures) → OPEN → (30 s) → HALF_OPEN → (1 success) → CLOSED`. While `OPEN`, all calls reject with `TransientVerificationError('breaker open')`.\n- `admin-panel/lib/adapters/verification/index.ts` — barrel.\n\n### Workflow service layer (Part A)\n\n- `admin-panel/services/review-workflow.ts`\n  - `submitForReview(announcementId, user)` — must be CONTENT_MANAGER or ADMIN, must be `Announcement.status === 'DRAFT'`. Inside `prisma.$transaction`: update announcement to `PENDING_REVIEW`; create `ReviewQueueItem { status: 'PENDING', authorId: user.id, announcementId, tier: 1 }`; write audit `SUBMIT_FOR_REVIEW`; enqueue notification stub (an `AuditLog` with action `NOTIFICATION_QUEUED` and metadata describing recipient = compliance officers — actual delivery is out of scope for the assessment).\n  - `claimNext(reviewer)` — must be COMPLIANCE_OFFICER or ADMIN; service queries `listPending({ excludeAuthorId: reviewer.id })` → if any, calls `queue.claimNextItem(reviewer.id)` and re-checks `result.item.authorId !== reviewer.id` (defence in depth against the library claiming a self-authored row), throwing `SelfReviewError` if violated.\n  - `approveItem(itemId, reviewer, note, deps)` — `deps.verification: VerificationAdapter` injected. Inside `prisma.$transaction`: read item, run `queue.approve(itemId, reviewer, note)`, then call `deps.verification.verify(announcement)`:\n    - `verdict === 'APPROVED'` → `Announcement.status = PUBLISHED`, `publishedAt = now()`, audit `VERIFICATION_PASSED`, notification stub.\n    - `verdict === 'REJECTED'` → `Announcement.status = DRAFT`, audit `VERIFICATION_REJECTED` with `reason`, notification stub.\n    - thrown error → transaction rolls back; outside the transaction we write best-effort `VERIFICATION_TRANSPORT_FAILED` audit and rethrow.\n  - `rejectItem(itemId, reviewer, reason)` — inside `$transaction`: `queue.reject` + `Announcement.status = DRAFT` + audit `REJECTED` + notification stub.\n  - `escalateOverdue(now = new Date())` — within `$transaction`: `prisma.reviewQueueItem.updateMany({ where: { status: 'PENDING', claimedBy: null, tier: 1, createdAt: { lt: now - 48h } }, data: { tier: 2, escalatedAt: now } })`; for each row updated, write `ESCALATED` audit with metadata. Returns `{ escalated: count }`.\n\n### RBAC update\n\n- `admin-panel/lib/rbac.ts` — extend the `Action` union with `'review' | 'submitForReview' | 'escalate'` and the `can()` switch:\n  - `review` → ADMIN, COMPLIANCE_OFFICER.\n  - `submitForReview` → ADMIN, CONTENT_MANAGER.\n  - `escalate` → ADMIN.\n\n### Routes (App Router)\n\nEach handler follows the existing pattern in [admin-panel/app/api/announcements/route.ts](admin-panel/app/api/announcements/route.ts) — auth → RBAC → JSON parse → Zod → service call → JSON response. Status codes: 200/201 success, 401 unauth, 403 forbidden, 404 not found, 409 state conflict, 422 validation, 500 fallback.\n\n- `admin-panel/app/api/announcements/[id]/submit-for-review/route.ts` (POST)\n- `admin-panel/app/api/review-queue/route.ts` (GET — list pending excluding self-authored)\n- `admin-panel/app/api/review-queue/claim/route.ts` (POST — claim next)\n- `admin-panel/app/api/review-queue/[id]/approve/route.ts` (POST — `{ note }`)\n- `admin-panel/app/api/review-queue/[id]/reject/route.ts` (POST — `{ reason }`)\n- `admin-panel/app/api/review-queue/escalate/route.ts` (POST — admin only)\n\nFor each route: read `node_modules/next/dist/docs/` first (see AGENTS.md) and confirm the App Router signature for `params` (Level 1 already uses `params: Promise<{ id: string }>` — same pattern).\n\n### UI\n\n- `admin-panel/app/admin/review-queue/page.tsx` — server component, lists pending items the current reviewer is allowed to claim; only renders when `can(user, 'review')`.\n- `admin-panel/app/admin/review-queue/ReviewActions.tsx` — client component with **Claim Next**, **Approve**, **Reject** buttons; posts to the routes above.\n- Update `admin-panel/app/admin/announcements/[id]/edit/EditForm.tsx` to expose **Submit for review** when `status === 'DRAFT'` and `can(user, 'submitForReview')`. The status badge replaces the implicit \"live\" assumption.\n- Update `admin-panel/app/admin/announcements/page.tsx` list to show `status`.\n- Update `admin-panel/app/admin/layout.tsx` to add a \"Review queue\" sidebar link visible only when `can(user, 'review')`.\n\n### Tests (Vitest)\n\nReuse the existing pattern in `__tests__/announcements.test.ts` (mock-based unit tests). Add the following:\n\n- `__tests__/review-queue-claim-race.test.ts` — DB-backed integration test (Postgres). Seeds 1 PENDING row; spawns N concurrent `claimNextItem` calls via `Promise.all`. Asserts exactly 1 returns `kind:'claimed'` and the rest return either `kind:'empty'` or fail to claim. **The assessment requires this test fail on the original library and pass on the fix.** Skipped by default unless `DATABASE_URL` is set, since pure-mock cannot exercise real lock semantics. The test file declares the skip condition explicitly and the transcript records both runs (pre-fix red, post-fix green).\n- `__tests__/verification-precedence.test.ts` — unit; constructs the wrapper with a stub adapter; first call returns REJECTED, second call returns APPROVED with the same content key; asserts the wrapper still returns REJECTED (cached precedence). Negative case: when the body is edited (cache key changes), the new APPROVED is honoured.\n- `__tests__/verification-breaker.test.ts` — unit; transient errors trip the breaker after threshold; calls during OPEN reject without invoking the adapter; after the half-open delay one call probes through.\n- `__tests__/workflow-separation-of-duties.test.ts` — unit on the service; author cannot claim or approve their own item — expects `SelfReviewError`.\n- `__tests__/workflow-transactional.test.ts` — unit on the service; force the audit write to throw and assert the announcement status did not change. Uses `prisma.$transaction` with a mocked client that simulates rollback.\n\n`npm test` must pass all suites; the race test runs only with `DATABASE_URL` set, which the README documents.\n\n### Documentation and submission artefacts\n\n- `admin-panel/README.md` (root README at repo level — Level 1 uses the repo-root README, no `admin-panel/README.md`) — add Level 2 sections: new run flow, new routes, env vars, mock adapter usage, race-test caveat.\n- `ai-session/transcript.md` — verbatim Level 2 session, generated by `/transcript` as the final step. Header declares model \"Claude Sonnet\" per candidate instruction with a one-line technical disclosure that the harness model is `claude-opus-4-7[1m]` (matches Level 1 pattern). Includes investigation log for Part C: hypothesis, repro (a concurrent `Promise.all` over `findFirst+update`), discovery of the read-modify-write window, fix design (FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED inside a tx), test results before and after.\n- No `SUBMISSION.md`, `DECISIONS.md`, `DESIGN.md`, or `KNOWN_ISSUES.md` — the brief forbids them.\n\n## Implementation order (commits, smallest first)\n\nEach commit is tight and reversible. Commit messages follow Level 1's terse style.\n\n1. `chore: branch from assessment-1; install seeded review-queue` — copy seeded files in.\n2. `feat(schema): add AnnouncementStatus, ReviewQueueItem, COMPLIANCE_OFFICER` — schema + migration + seed update + `npx prisma generate`.\n3. `feat(rbac): add review/submitForReview/escalate actions` — `lib/rbac.ts` + tests.\n4. `feat(verification): adapter interface, mock, live scaffold` — `lib/adapters/verification/{types,mock,live,index}.ts`.\n5. `feat(verification): retry+breaker+cache wrapper with precedence` — `wrapper.ts` + `verification-precedence.test.ts` + `verification-breaker.test.ts`.\n6. `feat(workflow): service layer for submit/claim/approve/reject/escalate` — `services/review-workflow.ts` + unit tests for separation-of-duties and transactional rollback.\n7. `feat(api): review-queue routes and submit-for-review` — six route handlers.\n8. `feat(ui): review queue page and submit-for-review button` — pages + client components + sidebar link.\n9. `test(review-queue): concurrent claim test reproduces the race (red)` — write the failing race test against the un-patched library; commit it red so the bisect shows the moment of fix.\n10. `fix(review-queue): close TOCTOU race in claimNextItem with FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED` — surgical patch to `lib/review-queue/src/claim.ts`; the race test now passes.\n11. `docs(readme): document level 2 routes, env, race-test prerequisite`.\n12. `chore: write verbatim ai session transcript` — generated by `/transcript`.\n\nThe patch-then-fix split (steps 9 and 10) gives the reviewer a concrete diff that proves the test fails before the fix, mirroring the Level 1 buggy-then-fixed health route.\n\n## Verification (end-to-end)\n\nAfter the final commit, run from `admin-panel/`:\n\n1. `npm install` (first time only).\n2. `npx prisma migrate deploy` against a clean Postgres.\n3. `npm run db:seed`.\n4. `npm test` — all suites green when `DATABASE_URL` is set; suites except the race test green even without DB.\n5. `npm run dev` and walk:\n   - Login as `cm@example.com`, create a draft announcement, submit for review.\n   - Login as `compliance@example.com`, claim next, approve.\n   - Inspect the announcement: status `PUBLISHED`, audit chain present.\n   - Repeat with a body containing `__deny__` to exercise mock rejection.\n   - Try to claim an item authored by the same compliance user (after seeding such a row): expect 403 `SELF_REVIEW`.\n6. Manually re-run `npm test` against the un-patched `claim.ts` (revert the `claim.ts` change locally) to confirm the race test fails — record the output in the transcript.\n7. Run `/transcript` once at the very end to write `ai-session/transcript.md` from the live session. Do not edit it after.\n\n## Risks\n\n- **Missing `review-queue.patch`** — addressed by copying the seeded directory verbatim and disclosing the substitution. If the reviewer expects a literal `git apply`, the audit trail (single commit named `install seeded review-queue` and matching tree contents) is still defensible.\n- **Postgres-only `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`** — the schema declares Postgres, so this is safe. If the project ever switches DB engines, the fix would need a portable equivalent (advisory lock or compare-and-swap via `updateMany`).\n- **Race test depends on real Postgres** — without a running DB the test is skipped. README documents this. CI would need a service container; outside scope.\n- **Next.js 16 unfamiliarity** — `AGENTS.md` warns the assistant against guessing. Before each route handler is written, read the relevant doc under `node_modules/next/dist/docs/`. Ditto for any change to `prisma.config.ts` patterns.\n- **Transcript verbatim requirement** — `/transcript` must be the last action, not edited afterwards. No generated docs that the brief forbids.\n"},"type":"attachment","uuid":"6aa26235-a53f-406e-a8b3-cdaeb268cdf5","timestamp":"2026-05-06T10:46:57.092Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}

{"parentUuid":"7c493cf7-1ca9-4254-a987-bd8cef2dac6d","isSidechain":false,"promptId":"18b7c84c-0d9b-4d05-9e73-fe69e1f09c35","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":"This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.\n\nSummary:\n1. Primary Request and Intent:\n   The user is completing a Level 2 Node.js/Next.js assessment that builds on Level 1. Working in `/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment/admin-panel/`. The assessment requires:\n   - Branch `assessment-2` off `assessment-1` head\n   - Install seeded `@assessment/review-queue` library\n   - Part A: multi-tier review workflow with separation of duties, deadline escalation, transactional writes\n   - Part B: external verification adapter (interface + mock + live scaffold + retry/breaker/cache wrapper with precedence rule)\n   - Part C: debug a TOCTOU race in seeded library's `claim.ts`\n   - AI tool declaration in transcript: \"Claude Sonnet\" (with technical disclosure that harness model is `claude-opus-4-7[1m]`)\n   - One continuous session, verbatim transcript at `ai-session/transcript.md`\n   - User wants strict step-by-step execution with explicit confirmation between each step.\n\n2. Key Technical Concepts:\n   - Next.js 16 (App Router, Turbopack), warned as \"NOT the Next.js you know\" via AGENTS.md\n   - Prisma 7.7.0 with `@prisma/adapter-pg` for PostgreSQL\n   - PostgreSQL 14.22 with `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED` for race fixes\n   - Vitest 4 with `describe.skipIf` for conditional test execution\n   - TOCTOU / read-modify-write race condition (the seeded bug)\n   - Mock-based auth via MOCK_USER_* environment variables\n   - Soft-delete pattern (deletedAt)\n   - Field-level RBAC enforcement via Prisma select clauses\n   - Bilingual (Arabic + English) content\n   - Circuit breaker, retry with exponential backoff, response caching\n   - Separation of duties enforcement\n   - Multi-tier review workflow with escalation\n\n3. Files and Code Sections:\n   - `/home/sameh/.claude/plans/delightful-sleeping-crane.md` — approved implementation plan, referenced throughout\n   - `admin-panel/lib/review-queue/` (entire directory, 9 files copied verbatim from `/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/review-queue/`) — the seeded library\n   - `admin-panel/.env` (created)\n     ```\n     DATABASE_URL=\"postgresql://sameh@localhost/admin_panel_l2?host=/var/run/postgresql\"\n     MOCK_USER_ID=\"cmotwu4ga0000tgek0amk2can\"\n     MOCK_USER_EMAIL=\"admin@example.com\"\n     MOCK_USER_NAME=\"Admin User\"\n     MOCK_USER_ROLE=\"ADMIN\"\n     ```\n     - Unix socket connection because TCP md5/scram auth was rejected\n   - `admin-panel/prisma/schema.prisma` — currently shows ReviewQueueItem added (Steps 1-6) but NOT YET updated for Step 7 (no AnnouncementStatus enum, no COMPLIANCE_OFFICER, no status/publishedAt on Announcement). Current state per system reminder:\n     ```prisma\n     enum Role { ADMIN  CONTENT_MANAGER  SUPPORT  USER }\n     model Announcement {\n       id        String    @id @default(cuid())\n       titleEn   String\n       titleAr   String\n       bodyEn    String\n       bodyAr    String\n       deletedAt DateTime?\n       createdAt DateTime  @default(now())\n       updatedAt DateTime  @updatedAt\n       authorId  String\n       author    User      @relation(fields: [authorId], references: [id])\n       auditLogs       AuditLog[]\n       reviewQueueItem ReviewQueueItem?\n       @@index([deletedAt])\n     }\n     model ReviewQueueItem {\n       id             String    @id @default(cuid())\n       announcementId String    @unique\n       authorId       String\n       status         String\n       claimedBy      String?\n       claimedAt      DateTime?\n       decisionNote   String?\n       decidedAt      DateTime?\n       createdAt      DateTime  @default(now())\n       announcement Announcement @relation(fields: [announcementId], references: [id])\n       @@index([status, claimedBy])\n       @@index([createdAt])\n     }\n     ```\n   - `admin-panel/prisma/migrations/0002_review_queue_item/migration.sql` (hand-written) — CreateTable, indexes, foreign key for ReviewQueueItem\n   - `admin-panel/__tests__/review-queue-claim-race.test.ts` (created Step 4) — DB-backed integration test using 5 separate PrismaClient instances, asserts exactly 1 claimed + 4 empty\n   - `admin-panel/lib/review-queue/src/claim.ts` (the fix, Step 5):\n     ```typescript\n     import type { PrismaClient } from '@prisma/client';\n     import type { ClaimResult, PendingItem, UserId } from './types';\n\n     export async function claimNextItem(\n       prisma: PrismaClient,\n       reviewer: UserId\n     ): Promise<ClaimResult> {\n       return prisma.$transaction(async (tx): Promise<ClaimResult> => {\n         const candidates = await tx.$queryRaw<Array<{ id: string }>>`\n           SELECT \"id\" FROM \"ReviewQueueItem\"\n           WHERE \"status\" = 'PENDING' AND \"claimedBy\" IS NULL\n           ORDER BY \"createdAt\" ASC\n           LIMIT 1\n           FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED\n         `;\n         if (candidates.length === 0) {\n           return { kind: 'empty' };\n         }\n         const updated = await tx.reviewQueueItem.update({\n           where: { id: candidates[0].id },\n           data: {\n             status: 'UNDER_REVIEW',\n             claimedBy: reviewer,\n             claimedAt: new Date(),\n           },\n         });\n         return { kind: 'claimed', item: updated as unknown as PendingItem };\n       });\n     }\n     ```\n   - `admin-panel/lib/rbac.ts` (NOT YET MODIFIED — pending Step 7) — needs new actions 'review' | 'submitForReview' | 'escalate'\n   - `admin-panel/prisma/seed.ts` (NOT YET MODIFIED — pending Step 7) — needs compliance@example.com user with COMPLIANCE_OFFICER role\n\n4. Errors and fixes:\n   - **Database TCP authentication failed (P1000)** — Fixed by switching to Unix socket connection: `?host=/var/run/postgresql`\n   - **Discovered orphan Level 2 migration in admin_panel_dev** — Fixed by user choosing fresh `admin_panel_l2` DB\n   - **Bash error: not a git repository** — Fixed by always using full path or staying at repo root\n   - **`git add` from wrong directory yielded \"did not match any files\"** — Fixed by `cd /home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment` before git commands\n   - **No `review-queue.patch` file existed** — Brief said `git apply review-queue.patch` but only raw library files were provided. Mitigation: copied directory verbatim and disclosed substitution\n   - **MOCK_USER_ID didn't exist in DB** — Fixed by reading actual admin user id from seeded DB\n   - **No node_modules** — Fixed by running `npm install` (537 packages)\n   - **No .env or .env.example file** — Fixed by creating .env from scratch (gitignored)\n   \n   User feedback patterns:\n   - User exited auto mode — wants more deliberate confirmation\n   - User wants strict step-by-step execution with confirmation between steps\n   - User specifies exact commit messages to use verbatim\n   - \"AI Tool: Claude Sonnet\" is the declaration to use in transcript even though actual harness model is `claude-opus-4-7[1m]`\n\n5. Problem Solving:\n   - **TOCTOU race in claim.ts**: Solved with `prisma.$transaction` wrapping `$queryRaw` SELECT FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED followed by `tx.reviewQueueItem.update`. Tested with 5 separate PrismaClient instances on Promise.all. RED before fix, GREEN after.\n   - **DB drift handling**: Chose fresh `admin_panel_l2` to keep `admin_panel_dev` untouched\n   - **Schema as test precondition**: ReviewQueueItem model added before test could compile\n   - **Two-commit fix split**: Test (red) committed separately from fix (green) so reviewer can bisect\n   - **Open ambiguity**: User's Step 7 doesn't include `tier` and `escalatedAt` columns on ReviewQueueItem, but Step 9's `escalateOverdue` will need them. Decision was leaning toward strict adherence to user's listed fields and adding necessary columns later if needed.\n\n6.
