{"parentUuid":"779266c0-3c3b-4237-8ab9-98bbc5f06538","isSidechain":false,"attachment":{"type":"file","filename":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment/admin-panel/lib/review-queue/src/claim.ts","content":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment/admin-panel/lib/review-queue/src/claim.ts","content":"import type { PrismaClient } from '@prisma/client';\nimport type { ClaimResult, PendingItem, UserId } from './types';\n\n/**\n * Claim the next unclaimed review item for a reviewer.\n *\n * Returns `{ kind: 'claimed', item }` when an item was claimed,\n * or `{ kind: 'empty' }` when the queue has nothing pending.\n *\n * The caller is responsible for separation-of-duties filtering\n * (i.e. callers that must not claim their own items should filter\n * beforehand in their service layer).\n */\nexport async function claimNextItem(\n  prisma: PrismaClient,\n  reviewer: UserId\n): Promise<ClaimResult> {\n  // Single transaction so the row-level lock taken by SELECT ... FOR UPDATE\n  // SKIP LOCKED is held until the matching UPDATE commits. SKIP LOCKED makes\n  // concurrent claimers pick distinct rows instead of contending on the same\n  // one, closing the read-modify-write race the original two-step had.\n  return prisma.$transaction(async (tx): Promise<ClaimResult> => {\n    const candidates = await tx.$queryRaw<Array<{ id: string }>>`\n      SELECT \"id\" FROM \"ReviewQueueItem\"\n      WHERE \"status\" = 'PENDING' AND \"claimedBy\" IS NULL\n      ORDER BY \"createdAt\" ASC\n      LIMIT 1\n      FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED\n    `;\n\n    if (candidates.length === 0) {\n      return { kind: 'empty' };\n    }\n\n    const updated = await tx.reviewQueueItem.update({\n      where: { id: candidates[0].id },\n      data: {\n        status: 'UNDER_REVIEW',\n        claimedBy: reviewer,\n        claimedAt: new Date(),\n      },\n    });\n\n    return { kind: 'claimed', item: updated as unknown as PendingItem };\n  });\n}\n","numLines":47,"startLine":1,"totalLines":47}},"displayPath":"nodejs-nextjs-assessment/admin-panel/lib/review-queue/src/claim.ts"},"type":"attachment","uuid":"56480ba0-7609-4335-9a7c-a0f3d048a589","timestamp":"2026-05-06T10:48:39.010Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}
{"parentUuid":"56480ba0-7609-4335-9a7c-a0f3d048a589","isSidechain":false,"attachment":{"type":"file","filename":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment/admin-panel/__tests__/review-queue-claim-race.test.ts","content":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment/admin-panel/__tests__/review-queue-claim-race.test.ts","content":"import \"dotenv/config\";\nimport { describe, it, expect, beforeAll, afterAll } from \"vitest\";\nimport { PrismaClient } from \"@prisma/client\";\nimport { PrismaPg } from \"@prisma/adapter-pg\";\n\nimport { claimNextItem } from \"@/lib/review-queue/src/claim\";\n\n// This is a real-Postgres integration test. It exercises the claim primitive\n// with five concurrent callers on five separate connections and asserts the\n// queue invariant: \"exactly one reviewer wins each pending item.\" Without a\n// database the suite is skipped — pure mocks cannot reproduce row-level\n// locking semantics.\nconst HAS_DB = !!process.env.DATABASE_URL;\nconst dbUrl = process.env.DATABASE_URL ?? \"\";\n\nfunction newClient() {\n  return new PrismaClient({\n    adapter: new PrismaPg({ connectionString: dbUrl }),\n  });\n}\n\ndescribe.skipIf(!HAS_DB)(\"claimNextItem (Postgres concurrency)\", () => {\n  const setup = HAS_DB ? newClient() : (null as unknown as PrismaClient);\n\n  let userId: string;\n  let announcementId: string;\n  let itemId: string;\n\n  beforeAll(async () => {\n    const user = await setup.user.create({\n      data: {\n        email: `race-author-${Date.now()}-${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 8)}@test.local`,\n        name: \"Race Test Author\",\n      },\n    });\n    userId = user.id;\n\n    const announcement = await setup.announcement.create({\n      data: {\n        titleEn: \"Race test announcement\",\n        titleAr: \"إعلان اختبار التزامن\",\n        bodyEn: \"race body en\",\n        bodyAr: \"محتوى اختبار\",\n        authorId: userId,\n      },\n    });\n    announcementId = announcement.id;\n\n    const item = await setup.reviewQueueItem.create({\n      data: {\n        announcementId,\n        authorId: userId,\n        status: \"PENDING\",\n      },\n    });\n    itemId = item.id;\n  });\n\n  afterAll(async () => {\n    if (!HAS_DB) return;\n    await setup.reviewQueueItem.deleteMany({ where: { id: itemId } });\n    await setup.announcement.deleteMany({ where: { id: announcementId } });\n    await setup.user.deleteMany({ where: { id: userId } });\n    await setup.$disconnect();\n  });\n\n  it(\"exactly one of five concurrent claims succeeds; four return empty\", async () => {\n    const reviewers = [\"reviewer-A\", \"reviewer-B\", \"reviewer-C\", \"reviewer-D\", \"reviewer-E\"];\n    // Each caller gets its own PrismaClient so they never share a connection\n    // — guarantees real overlap at the database layer, not pool serialisation.\n    const clients = reviewers.map(() => newClient());\n\n    try {\n      const results = await Promise.all(\n        clients.map((c, i) => claimNextItem(c, reviewers[i]))\n      );\n\n      const claimed = results.filter((r) => r.kind === \"claimed\");\n      const empty = results.filter((r) => r.kind === \"empty\");\n\n      expect(claimed.length).toBe(1);\n      expect(empty.length).toBe(4);\n    } finally {\n      await Promise.all(clients.map((c) => c.$disconnect()));\n    }\n  });\n});\n","numLines":88,"startLine":1,"totalLines":88}},"displayPath":"nodejs-nextjs-assessment/admin-panel/__tests__/review-queue-claim-race.test.ts"},"type":"attachment","uuid":"308dbd0a-fac0-4622-bf9d-a5a6fe8c905e","timestamp":"2026-05-06T10:48:39.011Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}
{"parentUuid":"308dbd0a-fac0-4622-bf9d-a5a6fe8c905e","isSidechain":false,"attachment":{"type":"file","filename":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment/admin-panel/prisma/migrations/0002_review_queue_item/migration.sql","content":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment/admin-panel/prisma/migrations/0002_review_queue_item/migration.sql","content":"-- CreateTable\nCREATE TABLE \"ReviewQueueItem\" (\n    \"id\" TEXT NOT NULL,\n    \"announcementId\" TEXT NOT NULL,\n    \"authorId\" TEXT NOT NULL,\n    \"status\" TEXT NOT NULL,\n    \"claimedBy\" TEXT,\n    \"claimedAt\" TIMESTAMP(3),\n    \"decisionNote\" TEXT,\n    \"decidedAt\" TIMESTAMP(3),\n    \"createdAt\" TIMESTAMP(3) NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n\n    CONSTRAINT \"ReviewQueueItem_pkey\" PRIMARY KEY (\"id\")\n);\n\n-- CreateIndex\nCREATE UNIQUE INDEX \"ReviewQueueItem_announcementId_key\" ON \"ReviewQueueItem\"(\"announcementId\");\n\n-- CreateIndex\nCREATE INDEX \"ReviewQueueItem_status_claimedBy_idx\" ON \"ReviewQueueItem\"(\"status\", \"claimedBy\");\n\n-- CreateIndex\nCREATE INDEX \"ReviewQueueItem_createdAt_idx\" ON \"ReviewQueueItem\"(\"createdAt\");\n\n-- AddForeignKey\nALTER TABLE \"ReviewQueueItem\" ADD CONSTRAINT \"ReviewQueueItem_announcementId_fkey\" FOREIGN KEY (\"announcementId\") REFERENCES \"Announcement\"(\"id\") ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE;\n","numLines":27,"startLine":1,"totalLines":27}},"displayPath":"nodejs-nextjs-assessment/admin-panel/prisma/migrations/0002_review_queue_item/migration.sql"},"type":"attachment","uuid":"dad20d31-d8fc-455b-b3ff-f1cf543a16cc","timestamp":"2026-05-06T10:48:39.010Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}
{"parentUuid":"dad20d31-d8fc-455b-b3ff-f1cf543a16cc","isSidechain":false,"attachment":{"type":"file","filename":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment/admin-panel/prisma/schema.prisma","content":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment/admin-panel/prisma/schema.prisma","content":"generator client {\n  provider = \"prisma-client-js\"\n}\n\ndatasource db {\n  provider = \"postgresql\"\n}\n\nenum Role {\n  ADMIN\n  CONTENT_MANAGER\n  SUPPORT\n  USER\n}\n\nmodel User {\n  id         String   @id @default(cuid())\n  email      String   @unique\n  name       String\n  phone      String?\n  nationalId String?\n  role       Role     @default(USER)\n  createdAt  DateTime @default(now())\n  updatedAt  DateTime @updatedAt\n\n  announcements Announcement[]\n  auditLogs     AuditLog[]\n\n  @@index([name, email])\n}\n\nmodel Announcement {\n  id        String    @id @default(cuid())\n  titleEn   String\n  titleAr   String\n  bodyEn    String\n  bodyAr    String\n  deletedAt DateTime?\n  createdAt DateTime  @default(now())\n  updatedAt DateTime  @updatedAt\n  authorId  String\n  author    User      @relation(fields: [authorId], references: [id])\n\n  auditLogs       AuditLog[]\n  reviewQueueItem ReviewQueueItem?\n\n  @@index([deletedAt])\n}\n\nmodel ReviewQueueItem {\n  id             String    @id @default(cuid())\n  announcementId String    @unique\n  authorId       String\n  status         String    // PENDING | UNDER_REVIEW | APPROVED | REJECTED\n  claimedBy      String?\n  claimedAt      DateTime?\n  decisionNote   String?\n  decidedAt      DateTime?\n  createdAt      DateTime  @default(now())\n\n  announcement Announcement @relation(fields: [announcementId], references: [id])\n\n  @@index([status, claimedBy])\n  @@index([createdAt])\n}\n\nmodel AuditLog {\n  id             String        @id @default(cuid())\n  action         String\n  entity         String\n  entityId       String\n  userId         String\n  user           User          @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id])\n  announcementId String?\n  announcement   Announcement? @relation(fields: [announcementId], references: [id])\n  metadata       Json?\n  createdAt      DateTime      @default(now())\n}\n","numLines":79,"startLine":1,"totalLines":79}},"displayPath":"nodejs-nextjs-assessment/admin-panel/prisma/schema.prisma"},"type":"attachment","uuid":"91a100ef-ba76-4846-8713-3b2c4a7b3aa0","timestamp":"2026-05-06T10:48:39.010Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}
{"parentUuid":"91a100ef-ba76-4846-8713-3b2c4a7b3aa0","isSidechain":false,"attachment":{"type":"file","filename":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment/admin-panel/.env","content":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment/admin-panel/.env","content":"DATABASE_URL=\"postgresql://sameh@localhost/admin_panel_l2?host=/var/run/postgresql\"\n\n# Mock session — picked up by lib/auth.ts.\n# Switch the role to flip what the UI/RBAC sees.\nMOCK_USER_ID=\"cmotwu4ga0000tgek0amk2can\"\nMOCK_USER_EMAIL=\"admin@example.com\"\nMOCK_USER_NAME=\"Admin User\"\nMOCK_USER_ROLE=\"ADMIN\"\n","numLines":9,"startLine":1,"totalLines":9}},"displayPath":"nodejs-nextjs-assessment/admin-panel/.env"},"type":"attachment","uuid":"0ab5a6a5-c700-48b5-90d2-0db5df6a5588","timestamp":"2026-05-06T10:48:39.010Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}
{"parentUuid":"0ab5a6a5-c700-48b5-90d2-0db5df6a5588","isSidechain":false,"attachment":{"type":"plan_file_reference","planFilePath":"/home/sameh/.claude/plans/delightful-sleeping-crane.md","planContent":"# Level 2 Assessment — implementation plan\n\n## Context\n\nThe candidate must extend their Level 1 admin-panel (Next.js 16 / Prisma 7 / PostgreSQL / Vitest) with a multi-tier review workflow, an external verification adapter, and the in-place fix of a seeded concurrency bug in `@assessment/review-queue`. All three parts must land in one continuous AI session on a new `assessment-2` branch, with a verbatim transcript and the seeded module integrated via the supplied patch step. The brief is intentionally ambiguous on edge cases — those resolutions go in the transcript.\n\nThis plan is a roadmap for the implementation phase. Each section names the files to touch, the contract to satisfy, and the verification step. Where the brief is ambiguous I record the chosen resolution under **Resolved ambiguity** so the transcript can quote the same wording.\n\n## Repo state (verified)\n\n- Repo root: `/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment` (the html assessment brief, the README, and the POSTMORTEM live here; the Next.js project lives at `admin-panel/`).\n- Current branch: `main`. `origin/assessment-1` exists; `main` is the merge commit of `assessment-1`. Branching off `main` is structurally equivalent to branching off the `assessment-1` head — that satisfies the spec.\n- `node_modules` has not been installed in this checkout — `npm install` is required before tests/migrations run.\n- `AGENTS.md` warns: this is **not the Next.js you know** — read `node_modules/next/dist/docs/` before writing route handlers. We must `npm install` and consult those docs for any pattern that touches App Router request handling, since assistant training may be stale.\n- `prisma/schema.prisma`: PostgreSQL provider, models `User`, `Announcement`, `AuditLog`, enum `Role { ADMIN, CONTENT_MANAGER, SUPPORT, USER }`. `Announcement` currently has no top-level lifecycle status — Level 2 must add it.\n- The seeded library source lives at `/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/review-queue/` (its own `package.json`, `src/{index,types,client,claim,approve,reject,list-pending}.ts`). **There is no `review-queue.patch` file** anywhere under `/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/`. The brief assumes one exists. Mitigation: the integration commit will copy the directory verbatim into `admin-panel/lib/review-queue/` and commit it with the message *\"install seeded review-queue\"* — functionally identical to applying the patch and the only honest path given the missing file. The transcript will note the missing patch and the substitution.\n- Level 1 transcript declared the model as Opus 4.7 (overriding the user's \"Sonnet\" instruction with a note). Level 2 user instruction is again \"Claude Sonnet\". The Level 2 transcript header will declare **Model: Claude Sonnet (as directed by the candidate)** with a one-line technical note that the harness model is in fact `claude-opus-4-7[1m]` so the transcript does not falsely attribute work to a different family — Level 1 used the same disclosure pattern.\n\n## Resolved ambiguities (record in transcript Section \"Brief ambiguities resolved\")\n\n1. **Separation of duties**. Enforced at three points: (a) submit — author writes their own `authorId` into the queue row; (b) claim — service-layer guard rejects a claim where `queue_item.authorId === reviewer.id`, plus the library's `listPending({ excludeAuthorId })` filter for the UI; (c) approve/reject — the seeded library already enforces `claimedBy === reviewer`. No admin override; an admin who happens to be the author of an announcement still cannot review it. Reason: a single, branch-free invariant is far easier to audit than role-conditional bypasses.\n2. **Deadline / escalation**. A queue item is \"overdue\" when `status = PENDING AND claimedAt IS NULL AND createdAt < now() - 48h`. Escalation = increment `tier` (default 1, escalated tier = 2) and stamp `escalatedAt`. The implementation provides one tier above default. Trigger: an `escalateOverdue()` service function plus a `POST /api/review-queue/escalate` route that runs the same logic on demand. No cron is added (Next.js process scope) — the route is the call surface for an external scheduler.\n3. **Verification retry policy**. Exponential backoff with base 100 ms × 2^n, jitter ±20 %, max 3 attempts. Retry only on transport-class failures (network errors, HTTP 502/503/504, breaker `HALF_OPEN` probes). 4xx and validation errors fail fast and are not retried.\n4. **Audit on verification outcomes**. (a) Adapter returns `{ ok: true }` → announcement → `PUBLISHED`, queue item → `APPROVED`, `AuditLog` action `VERIFICATION_PASSED`. (b) Adapter returns `{ ok: false, reason }` → announcement → `DRAFT`, queue item → `APPROVED` with `decisionNote = \"approved-but-verification-rejected\"`, `AuditLog` action `VERIFICATION_REJECTED`. (c) Adapter throws after retries / breaker open → workflow transaction aborts; queue item stays `UNDER_REVIEW`, announcement stays `PENDING_REVIEW`, `AuditLog` action `VERIFICATION_TRANSPORT_FAILED` (best-effort, written outside the failed transaction). The reviewer can re-approve later.\n5. **Cache precedence**. Cached `REJECTED` never replaced by `APPROVED`. Cached `APPROVED` may be overwritten by `REJECTED`. Cache key = `announcement.id` + `bodyEn|bodyAr|titleEn|titleAr` content hash so editing the body invalidates a stale rejection. TTL: `APPROVED` entries expire after 5 minutes; `REJECTED` entries persist until invalidated by content change.\n\n## Critical files to add or modify\n\n### Schema and migration\n\n- `admin-panel/prisma/schema.prisma`\n  - Add enum `AnnouncementStatus { DRAFT PENDING_REVIEW PUBLISHED REJECTED }`.\n  - Extend `enum Role` with `COMPLIANCE_OFFICER`.\n  - On `Announcement`: add `status AnnouncementStatus @default(DRAFT)`, `publishedAt DateTime?`, opposite relation `reviewQueueItem ReviewQueueItem?`, index `@@index([status])`.\n  - Add full `ReviewQueueItem` model exactly per the assessment spec (id cuid, announcementId @unique, authorId, status string, claimedBy?, claimedAt?, decisionNote?, decidedAt?, createdAt, relation, indexes `[status, claimedBy]` and `[createdAt]`). Add the optional fields the implementation needs: `tier Int @default(1)` and `escalatedAt DateTime?`.\n- `admin-panel/prisma/migrations/0002_review_workflow/migration.sql` — generated by `npx prisma migrate dev --name review_workflow`. Hand-verify it: enum addition for `Role` and the new `AnnouncementStatus`, alter on `Announcement`, create on `ReviewQueueItem`.\n- `admin-panel/prisma/seed.ts` — add a `compliance@example.com` user with role `COMPLIANCE_OFFICER`. Existing announcements stay `DRAFT` to keep the smoke path uneventful.\n\n### Seeded library install\n\n- Copy `/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/review-queue/{package.json,README.md,src/}` → `admin-panel/lib/review-queue/`. Commit immediately with message `install seeded review-queue` so the diff between this commit and the next ones is exactly the bug fix.\n- Add a path entry in `admin-panel/tsconfig.json` (`@review-queue/*`) only if the path import gets ugly — otherwise import via relative path from `services/`.\n\n### Bug fix (Part C)\n\n- `admin-panel/lib/review-queue/src/claim.ts` — replace the `findFirst → update` race with a single atomic operation:\n  - `prisma.$transaction(async tx => { … })`.\n  - Inside the transaction, `tx.$queryRaw\\`SELECT id FROM \"ReviewQueueItem\" WHERE status='PENDING' AND \"claimedBy\" IS NULL ORDER BY \"createdAt\" ASC LIMIT 1 FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED\\`` (Postgres-specific, but Postgres is the project's datasource).\n  - If the row set is empty → `return { kind: 'empty' }`.\n  - Otherwise `tx.reviewQueueItem.update({ where: { id }, data: { status: 'UNDER_REVIEW', claimedBy: reviewer, claimedAt: new Date() } })` and return `{ kind: 'claimed', item }`.\n  - Why this satisfies the brief: row-level lock is held by Postgres across processes and connection pools — meets *\"not cosmetic / holds across processes\"*; `SKIP LOCKED` makes parallel claimers pick distinct rows instead of contending; the patch is surgical (only `claim.ts` changes; no public API change; no rewrite of the library).\n- Commit with message that names the bug class: `fix(review-queue): close TOCTOU race in claimNextItem with FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`.\n\n### Verification adapter (Part B)\n\n- `admin-panel/lib/adapters/verification/types.ts`\n  - `interface VerificationResult { ok: boolean; reason?: string; verdict: 'APPROVED' | 'REJECTED'; verifiedAt: Date }`.\n  - `interface VerificationAdapter { verify(announcement: AnnouncementForVerification): Promise<VerificationResult> }` where `AnnouncementForVerification = Pick<Announcement, 'id'|'titleEn'|'titleAr'|'bodyEn'|'bodyAr'>`. No HTTP types in the public surface.\n  - Internal `class TransientVerificationError extends Error` and `class PermanentVerificationError extends Error` — used only inside the wrapper.\n- `admin-panel/lib/adapters/verification/mock.ts`\n  - `MockVerificationAdapter` accepts a config `{ rule: 'allow' | 'deny' | (a) => Verdict }`. Default rule: APPROVE unless the bilingual body contains the literal token `__deny__`. Used by tests and dev seeding only.\n- `admin-panel/lib/adapters/verification/live.ts`\n  - `LiveVerificationAdapter` skeleton with a `verify` method whose body is a single `throw new Error('TODO SPEC PENDING — live verification API contract not yet delivered')`.\n  - Each potential request site (URL build, body build, response parse) carries its own `// TODO SPEC PENDING` comment so a grep for the marker matches every live call site.\n- `admin-panel/lib/adapters/verification/wrapper.ts`\n  - `class CachedRetryingBreakerAdapter implements VerificationAdapter`.\n  - Composition order: wrapper first checks cache; on miss, calls a retrying call which is in turn guarded by the breaker.\n  - Cache: `Map<string, { entry: VerificationResult; cachedAt: number }>`. Key derivation = `${announcement.id}:${sha256(titleEn+titleAr+bodyEn+bodyAr).slice(0,16)}`. Precedence rule explicit: when storing, if existing entry has `verdict === 'REJECTED'` and incoming has `verdict === 'APPROVED'`, **keep the existing rejected entry**.\n  - Retry: 3 attempts, exponential backoff 100/200/400 ms with ±20 % jitter, only on `TransientVerificationError`.\n  - Breaker: states `CLOSED → (5 consecutive failures) → OPEN → (30 s) → HALF_OPEN → (1 success) → CLOSED`. While `OPEN`, all calls reject with `TransientVerificationError('breaker open')`.\n- `admin-panel/lib/adapters/verification/index.ts` — barrel.\n\n### Workflow service layer (Part A)\n\n- `admin-panel/services/review-workflow.ts`\n  - `submitForReview(announcementId, user)` — must be CONTENT_MANAGER or ADMIN, must be `Announcement.status === 'DRAFT'`. Inside `prisma.$transaction`: update announcement to `PENDING_REVIEW`; create `ReviewQueueItem { status: 'PENDING', authorId: user.id, announcementId, tier: 1 }`; write audit `SUBMIT_FOR_REVIEW`; enqueue notification stub (an `AuditLog` with action `NOTIFICATION_QUEUED` and metadata describing recipient = compliance officers — actual delivery is out of scope for the assessment).\n  - `claimNext(reviewer)` — must be COMPLIANCE_OFFICER or ADMIN; service queries `listPending({ excludeAuthorId: reviewer.id })` → if any, calls `queue.claimNextItem(reviewer.id)` and re-checks `result.item.authorId !== reviewer.id` (defence in depth against the library claiming a self-authored row), throwing `SelfReviewError` if violated.\n  - `approveItem(itemId, reviewer, note, deps)` — `deps.verification: VerificationAdapter` injected. Inside `prisma.$transaction`: read item, run `queue.approve(itemId, reviewer, note)`, then call `deps.verification.verify(announcement)`:\n    - `verdict === 'APPROVED'` → `Announcement.status = PUBLISHED`, `publishedAt = now()`, audit `VERIFICATION_PASSED`, notification stub.\n    - `verdict === 'REJECTED'` → `Announcement.status = DRAFT`, audit `VERIFICATION_REJECTED` with `reason`, notification stub.\n    - thrown error → transaction rolls back; outside the transaction we write best-effort `VERIFICATION_TRANSPORT_FAILED` audit and rethrow.\n  - `rejectItem(itemId, reviewer, reason)` — inside `$transaction`: `queue.reject` + `Announcement.status = DRAFT` + audit `REJECTED` + notification stub.\n  - `escalateOverdue(now = new Date())` — within `$transaction`: `prisma.reviewQueueItem.updateMany({ where: { status: 'PENDING', claimedBy: null, tier: 1, createdAt: { lt: now - 48h } }, data: { tier: 2, escalatedAt: now } })`; for each row updated, write `ESCALATED` audit with metadata. Returns `{ escalated: count }`.\n\n### RBAC update\n\n- `admin-panel/lib/rbac.ts` — extend the `Action` union with `'review' | 'submitForReview' | 'escalate'` and the `can()` switch:\n  - `review` → ADMIN, COMPLIANCE_OFFICER.\n  - `submitForReview` → ADMIN, CONTENT_MANAGER.\n  - `escalate` → ADMIN.\n\n### Routes (App Router)\n\nEach handler follows the existing pattern in [admin-panel/app/api/announcements/route.ts](admin-panel/app/api/announcements/route.ts) — auth → RBAC → JSON parse → Zod → service call → JSON response. Status codes: 200/201 success, 401 unauth, 403 forbidden, 404 not found, 409 state conflict, 422 validation, 500 fallback.\n\n- `admin-panel/app/api/announcements/[id]/submit-for-review/route.ts` (POST)\n- `admin-panel/app/api/review-queue/route.ts` (GET — list pending excluding self-authored)\n- `admin-panel/app/api/review-queue/claim/route.ts` (POST — claim next)\n- `admin-panel/app/api/review-queue/[id]/approve/route.ts` (POST — `{ note }`)\n- `admin-panel/app/api/review-queue/[id]/reject/route.ts` (POST — `{ reason }`)\n- `admin-panel/app/api/review-queue/escalate/route.ts` (POST — admin only)\n\nFor each route: read `node_modules/next/dist/docs/` first (see AGENTS.md) and confirm the App Router signature for `params` (Level 1 already uses `params: Promise<{ id: string }>` — same pattern).\n\n### UI\n\n- `admin-panel/app/admin/review-queue/page.tsx` — server component, lists pending items the current reviewer is allowed to claim; only renders when `can(user, 'review')`.\n- `admin-panel/app/admin/review-queue/ReviewActions.tsx` — client component with **Claim Next**, **Approve**, **Reject** buttons; posts to the routes above.\n- Update `admin-panel/app/admin/announcements/[id]/edit/EditForm.tsx` to expose **Submit for review** when `status === 'DRAFT'` and `can(user, 'submitForReview')`. The status badge replaces the implicit \"live\" assumption.\n- Update `admin-panel/app/admin/announcements/page.tsx` list to show `status`.\n- Update `admin-panel/app/admin/layout.tsx` to add a \"Review queue\" sidebar link visible only when `can(user, 'review')`.\n\n### Tests (Vitest)\n\nReuse the existing pattern in `__tests__/announcements.test.ts` (mock-based unit tests). Add the following:\n\n- `__tests__/review-queue-claim-race.test.ts` — DB-backed integration test (Postgres). Seeds 1 PENDING row; spawns N concurrent `claimNextItem` calls via `Promise.all`. Asserts exactly 1 returns `kind:'claimed'` and the rest return either `kind:'empty'` or fail to claim. **The assessment requires this test fail on the original library and pass on the fix.** Skipped by default unless `DATABASE_URL` is set, since pure-mock cannot exercise real lock semantics. The test file declares the skip condition explicitly and the transcript records both runs (pre-fix red, post-fix green).\n- `__tests__/verification-precedence.test.ts` — unit; constructs the wrapper with a stub adapter; first call returns REJECTED, second call returns APPROVED with the same content key; asserts the wrapper still returns REJECTED (cached precedence). Negative case: when the body is edited (cache key changes), the new APPROVED is honoured.\n- `__tests__/verification-breaker.test.ts` — unit; transient errors trip the breaker after threshold; calls during OPEN reject without invoking the adapter; after the half-open delay one call probes through.\n- `__tests__/workflow-separation-of-duties.test.ts` — unit on the service; author cannot claim or approve their own item — expects `SelfReviewError`.\n- `__tests__/workflow-transactional.test.ts` — unit on the service; force the audit write to throw and assert the announcement status did not change. Uses `prisma.$transaction` with a mocked client that simulates rollback.\n\n`npm test` must pass all suites; the race test runs only with `DATABASE_URL` set, which the README documents.\n\n### Documentation and submission artefacts\n\n- `admin-panel/README.md` (root README at repo level — Level 1 uses the repo-root README, no `admin-panel/README.md`) — add Level 2 sections: new run flow, new routes, env vars, mock adapter usage, race-test caveat.\n- `ai-session/transcript.md` — verbatim Level 2 session, generated by `/transcript` as the final step. Header declares model \"Claude Sonnet\" per candidate instruction with a one-line technical disclosure that the harness model is `claude-opus-4-7[1m]` (matches Level 1 pattern). Includes investigation log for Part C: hypothesis, repro (a concurrent `Promise.all` over `findFirst+update`), discovery of the read-modify-write window, fix design (FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED inside a tx), test results before and after.\n- No `SUBMISSION.md`, `DECISIONS.md`, `DESIGN.md`, or `KNOWN_ISSUES.md` — the brief forbids them.\n\n## Implementation order (commits, smallest first)\n\nEach commit is tight and reversible. Commit messages follow Level 1's terse style.\n\n1. `chore: branch from assessment-1; install seeded review-queue` — copy seeded files in.\n2. `feat(schema): add AnnouncementStatus, ReviewQueueItem, COMPLIANCE_OFFICER` — schema + migration + seed update + `npx prisma generate`.\n3. `feat(rbac): add review/submitForReview/escalate actions` — `lib/rbac.ts` + tests.\n4. `feat(verification): adapter interface, mock, live scaffold` — `lib/adapters/verification/{types,mock,live,index}.ts`.\n5. `feat(verification): retry+breaker+cache wrapper with precedence` — `wrapper.ts` + `verification-precedence.test.ts` + `verification-breaker.test.ts`.\n6. `feat(workflow): service layer for submit/claim/approve/reject/escalate` — `services/review-workflow.ts` + unit tests for separation-of-duties and transactional rollback.\n7. `feat(api): review-queue routes and submit-for-review` — six route handlers.\n8. `feat(ui): review queue page and submit-for-review button` — pages + client components + sidebar link.\n9. `test(review-queue): concurrent claim test reproduces the race (red)` — write the failing race test against the un-patched library; commit it red so the bisect shows the moment of fix.\n10. `fix(review-queue): close TOCTOU race in claimNextItem with FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED` — surgical patch to `lib/review-queue/src/claim.ts`; the race test now passes.\n11. `docs(readme): document level 2 routes, env, race-test prerequisite`.\n12. `chore: write verbatim ai session transcript` — generated by `/transcript`.\n\nThe patch-then-fix split (steps 9 and 10) gives the reviewer a concrete diff that proves the test fails before the fix, mirroring the Level 1 buggy-then-fixed health route.\n\n## Verification (end-to-end)\n\nAfter the final commit, run from `admin-panel/`:\n\n1. `npm install` (first time only).\n2. `npx prisma migrate deploy` against a clean Postgres.\n3. `npm run db:seed`.\n4. `npm test` — all suites green when `DATABASE_URL` is set; suites except the race test green even without DB.\n5. `npm run dev` and walk:\n   - Login as `cm@example.com`, create a draft announcement, submit for review.\n   - Login as `compliance@example.com`, claim next, approve.\n   - Inspect the announcement: status `PUBLISHED`, audit chain present.\n   - Repeat with a body containing `__deny__` to exercise mock rejection.\n   - Try to claim an item authored by the same compliance user (after seeding such a row): expect 403 `SELF_REVIEW`.\n6. Manually re-run `npm test` against the un-patched `claim.ts` (revert the `claim.ts` change locally) to confirm the race test fails — record the output in the transcript.\n7. Run `/transcript` once at the very end to write `ai-session/transcript.md` from the live session. Do not edit it after.\n\n## Risks\n\n- **Missing `review-queue.patch`** — addressed by copying the seeded directory verbatim and disclosing the substitution. If the reviewer expects a literal `git apply`, the audit trail (single commit named `install seeded review-queue` and matching tree contents) is still defensible.\n- **Postgres-only `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`** — the schema declares Postgres, so this is safe. If the project ever switches DB engines, the fix would need a portable equivalent (advisory lock or compare-and-swap via `updateMany`).\n- **Race test depends on real Postgres** — without a running DB the test is skipped. README documents this. CI would need a service container; outside scope.\n- **Next.js 16 unfamiliarity** — `AGENTS.md` warns the assistant against guessing. Before each route handler is written, read the relevant doc under `node_modules/next/dist/docs/`. Ditto for any change to `prisma.config.ts` patterns.\n- **Transcript verbatim requirement** — `/transcript` must be the last action, not edited afterwards. No generated docs that the brief forbids.\n"},"type":"attachment","uuid":"27f57a07-a0dc-45dd-b0c9-a1f588a4fddc","timestamp":"2026-05-06T10:48:39.011Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}

{"parentUuid":"75c50fc6-8763-4697-beb8-0c1a109cec63","isSidechain":false,"promptId":"ddc246c7-a480-45ff-a513-8b93ae5ca85d","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Part C is done. Now Part A and Part B together in order.\n\nStep 7 — Update schema and RBAC:\n- Add enum AnnouncementStatus { DRAFT PENDING_REVIEW PUBLISHED REJECTED }\n- Add COMPLIANCE_OFFICER to enum Role\n- Add status AnnouncementStatus @default(DRAFT) to Announcement model\n- Add publishedAt DateTime? to Announcement model\n- Run: npx prisma migrate dev --name review_workflow\n- Run: npx prisma generate\n- In lib/rbac.ts add actions: 'review' | 'submitForReview' | 'escalate'\n  * review → ADMIN, COMPLIANCE_OFFICER\n  * submitForReview → ADMIN, CONTENT_MANAGER\n  * escalate → ADMIN\n- Add compliance@example.com user with role COMPLIANCE_OFFICER to prisma/seed.ts\n- Commit: \"feat(schema): add AnnouncementStatus, COMPLIANCE_OFFICER, RBAC actions\"\n\nStep 8 — Verification adapter (Part B):\nCreate these files in admin-panel/lib/adapters/verification/:\n\n1. types.ts — interface VerificationAdapter + VerificationResult\n   No HTTP types (no Response, fetch, AxiosResponse)\n\n2. mock.ts — MockVerificationAdapter\n   Denies if body contains __deny__, approves otherwise\n\n3. live.ts — LiveVerificationAdapter scaffold\n   Every call site: throw new Error('TODO SPEC PENDING')\n   Add // TODO SPEC PENDING comments everywhere\n\n4. wrapper.ts — CachedRetryingBreakerAdapter\n   - Cache: cached REJECTED beats fresh APPROVED (precedence rule)\n   - Retry: 3 attempts, exponential backoff 100/200/400ms ±20% jitter\n   - Breaker: 5 failures → OPEN → 30s → HALF_OPEN → 1 success → CLOSED\n   - Retry only on transient errors (network, 502/503/504)\n   - 4xx = fail fast, no retry\n\n5. index.ts — barrel export\n\n- Commit: \"feat(verification): adapter interface, mock, live scaffold, retry+breaker+cache\"\n\nStep 9 — Workflow service (Part A):\nCreate admin-panel/services/review-workflow.ts with:\n- submitForReview(announcementId, user) — CONTENT_MANAGER or ADMIN only\n- claimNext(reviewer) — COMPLIANCE_OFFICER or ADMIN, excludes self-authored\n- approveItem(itemId, reviewer, note, deps) — calls verification adapter\n- rejectItem(itemId, reviewer, reason)\n- escalateOverdue(now?) — items PENDING > 48h → tier 2\n\nAll multi-step writes inside prisma.$transaction\nOn verification failure → announcement stays PENDING_REVIEW, write best-effort audit\n\n- Commit: \"feat(workflow): review-workflow service layer\"\n\nDo one step at a time and confirm before moving to the next."}]},"uuid":"a6dee31e-2416-4a2f-8cad-052fd08dc60f","timestamp":"2026-05-06T11:02:13.996Z","permissionMode":"acceptEdits","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}

{ Role } from \"@prisma/client\";\n2\timport type { SessionUser } from \"./auth\";\n3\t\n4\texport type Action = \"create\" | \"edit\" | \"delete\" | \"viewSensitive\" | \"view\";\n5\t\n6\texport function can(user: SessionUser | null, action: Action): boolean {\n7\t  if (!user) return false;\n8\t  switch (action) {\n9\t    case \"create\":\n10\t    case \"edit\":\n11\t    case \"delete\":\n12\t      return user.role === Role.ADMIN || user.role === Role.CONTENT_MANAGER;\n13\t    case \"viewSensitive\":\n14\t      return user.role === Role.ADMIN;\n15\t    case \"view\":\n16\t      return true;\n17\t  }\n18\t}\n19\t\n20\texport function requireRole(\n21\t  user: SessionUser | null,\n22\t  ...roles: Role[]\n23\t): asserts user is SessionUser {\n24\t  if (!user || !roles.includes(user.role)) {\n25\t    throw new Error(\"FORBIDDEN\");\n26\t { Role } from \"@prisma/client\";\nimport type { SessionUser } from \"./auth\";\n\nexport type Action = \"create\" | \"edit\" | \"delete\" | \"viewSensitive\" | \"view\";\n\nexport function can(user: SessionUser | null, action: Action): boolean {\n  if (!user) return false;\n  switch (action) {\n    case \"create\":\n    case \"edit\":\n    case \"delete\":\n      return user.role === Role.ADMIN || user.role === Role.CONTENT_MANAGER;\n    case \"viewSensitive\":\n      return user.role === Role.ADMIN;\n    case \"view\":\n      return true;\n  }\n}\n\nexport function requireRole(\n  user: SessionUser | null,\n  ...roles: Role[]\n): asserts user is SessionUser {\n  if (!user || !roles.includes(user.role)) {\n    throw new Error(\"FORBIDDEN\");\n  }\n}\n","numLines":28,"startLine":1,"totalLines":28}},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"7341ac25-4853-4d05-8a7c-c781314c77ee","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}

\"dotenv/config\";\nimport { PrismaClient, Role } from \"@prisma/client\";\nimport { PrismaPg } from \"@prisma/adapter-pg\";\n\nconst adapter = new PrismaPg({ connectionString: process.env.DATABASE_URL });\nconst prisma = new PrismaClient({ adapter });\n\nasync function main() {\n  const admin = await prisma.user.upsert({\n    where: { email: \"admin@example.com\" },\n    update: {},\n    create: {\n      email: \"admin@example.com\",\n      name: \"Admin User\",\n      role: Role.ADMIN,\n    },\n  });\n\n  await prisma.user.upsert({\n    where: { email: \"cm@example.com\" },\n    update: {},\n    create: {\n      email: \"cm@example.com\",\n      name: \"Content Manager\",\n      role: Role.CONTENT_MANAGER,\n    },\n  });\n\n  await prisma.user.upsert({\n    where: { email: \"support@example.com\" },\n    update: {},\n    create: {\n      email: \"support@example.com\",\n      name: \"Support Agent\",\n      role: Role.SUPPORT,\n      phone: \"+1-555-0100\",\n      nationalId: \"ID-1234567890\",\n    },\n  });\n\n  const announcementCount = await prisma.announcement.count();\n  if (announcementCount === 0) {\n    await prisma.announcement.createMany({\n      data: [\n        {\n          titleEn: \"Scheduled maintenance tonight\",\n          titleAr: \"صيانة مجدولة الليلة\",\n          bodyEn:\n            \"The admin panel will be briefly unavailable between 23:00 and 01:00 local time.\",\n          bodyAr:\n            \"ستكون لوحة الإدارة غير متاحة لفترة وجيزة بين الساعة 23:00 و 01:00 بالتوقيت المحلي.\",\n          authorId: admin.id,\n        },\n        {\n          titleEn: \"New leave policy is now in effect\",\n          titleAr: \"سياسة الإجازات الجديدة سارية المفعول\",\n          bodyEn:\n            \"Please review the updated leave policy in the HR portal before submitting new requests.\",\n          bodyAr:\n            \"يرجى مراجعة سياسة الإجازات المحدثة في بوابة الموارد البشرية قبل تقديم طلبات جديدة.\",\n          authorId: admin.id,\n        },\n      ],\n    });\n  }\n\n  console.log(\"Seed complete.\");\n}\n\nmain()\n  .catch((e) => {\n    console.error(e);\n    process.exit(1);\n  })\n  .finally(async () => {\n    await prisma.$disconnect();\n  });\n","numLines":78,"startLine":1,"totalLines":78}},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"b831571f-6a7b-4dec-bc4c-631d34a9c54f","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}

role       Role     @default(USER)\n  createdAt  DateTime @default(now())\n  updatedAt  DateTime @updatedAt\n\n  announcements Announcement[]\n  auditLogs     AuditLog[]\n\n  @@index([name, email])\n}\n\nmodel Announcement {\n  id        String    @id @default(cuid())\n  titleEn   String\n  titleAr   String\n  bodyEn    String\n  bodyAr    String\n  deletedAt DateTime?\n  createdAt DateTime  @default(now())\n  updatedAt DateTime  @updatedAt\n  authorId  String\n  author    User      @relation(fields: [authorId], references: [id])\n\n  auditLogs       AuditLog[]\n  reviewQueueItem ReviewQueueItem?\n\n  @@index([deletedAt])\n}\n\nmodel ReviewQueueItem {\n  id             String    @id @default(cuid())\n  announcementId String    @unique\n  authorId       String\n DateTime  @default(now())\n\n  announcement Announcement @relation(fields: [announcementId], references: [id])\n\n  @@index([status, claimedBy])\n  @@index([createdAt])\n}\n\nmodel AuditLog {\n  id             String        @id @default(cuid())\n  action         String\n  entity         String\n  entityId       String\n  userId         String\n  user           User          @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id])\n  announcementId String?\n  announcement   Announcement? @relation(fields: [announcementId], references: [id])\n  metadata       Json?\n  createdAt      DateTime type Action = \"create\" | \"edit\" | \"delete\" | \"viewSensitive\" | \"view\";\n\nexport function can(user: SessionUser | null, action: Action): boolean {\n  if (!user) return false;\n  switch (action) {\n    case \"create\":\n    case \"edit\":\n    case \"delete\":\n      return user.role === Role.ADMIN || user.role === Role.CONTENT_MANAGER;\n    case \"viewSensitive\":\n      return await prisma.user.upsert({\n    where: { email: \"admin@example.com\" },\n    update: {},\n    create: {\n      email: \"admin@example.com\",\n      name: \"Admin User\",\n      role: Role.ADMIN,\n    },\n  });\n\n  await prisma.user.upsert({\n    where: { email: \"cm@example.com\" },\n    update: {},\n    create: {\n      email: \"cm@example.com\",\n      name: \"Content Manager\",\n      role: Role.CONTENT_MANAGER,\n    },\n  });\n\n  await prisma.user.upsert({\n    where: { email: \"support@example.com\" },\n    update: {},\n    create: {\n      email: \"support@example.com\",\n      name: \"Support Agent\",\n      role: Role.SUPPORT,\n      phone: \"+1-555-0100\",\n      nationalId: \"ID-1234567890\",\n    },\n  });\n\n  const announcementCount = await prisma.announcement.count();\n  if (announcementCount === 0) {\n    await prisma.announcement.createMany({\n      data: [\n        {\n          titleEn: \"Scheduled maintenance tonight\",\n          titleAr: \"صيانة مجدولة الليلة\",\n          bodyEn:\n            \"The admin panel will be briefly unavailable between 23:00 and 01:00 local time.\",\n          bodyAr:\n            \"ستكون لوحة الإدارة غير متاحة لفترة وجيزة بين الساعة 23:00 و 01:00 بالتوقيت المحلي.\",\n          authorId: admin.id,\n        },\n        {\n          titleEn: \"New leave policy is now in effect\",\n          titleAr: \"سياسة الإجازات الجديدة سارية المفعول\",\n          bodyEn:\n            \"Please review the updated leave policy in the HR portal before submitting new requests.\",\n          bodyAr:\n            \"يرجى مراجعة سياسة الإجازات المحدثة في بوابة الموارد البشرية قبل تقديم طلبات جديدة.\",\n          authorId: admin.id,\n        },\n      ],\n    });\n  }\n\n  console.log(\"Seed complete.\");\n}\n\nmain()\n  .catch((e) => {\n    console.error(e);\n    process.exit(1);\n  })\n  .finally(async () => {\n    await prisma.$disconnect();\n Summary:\n\n- **Schema** — added `enum AnnouncementStatus { DRAFT PENDING_REVIEW PUBLISHED REJECTED }`, added `COMPLIANCE_OFFICER` to `Role`, added `status` (default `DRAFT`) and `publishedAt` to `Announcement`, plus `@@index([status])`.\n- **Migration** — `20260506110255_review_workflow` applied to `admin_panel_l2`. Note: the file is timestamp-prefixed (Prisma's default for `migrate dev`) unlike the older hand-named `0001_init` / `0002_review_queue_item` — I left it as Prisma generated since you specified the exact command.\n- **RBAC** — `lib/rbac.ts` now has `'review' | 'submitForReview' | 'escalate'` with the specified role mappings.\n- **Seed** — `compliance@example.com` user with `COMPLIANCE_OFFICER` upserted; seed run was clean.\n- **Typecheck** — clean.\n- **Commit** — `a3d2054 feat(schema): add AnnouncementStatus, COMPLIANCE_OFFICER, RBAC actions`.\n\nConfirm to proceed with Step 8 (verification adapter)."}],"stop_reason":"end_turn","stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":463,"cache_read_input_tokens":51263,"output_tokens":432,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":463,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[{"input_tokens":1,"output_tokens":432,"cache_read_input_tokens":51263,"cache_creation_input_tokens":463,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":463},"type":"message"}],"speed":"standard"}},"requestId":"req_011CamCpxheP9D27PNAbwr2j","type":"assistant","uuid":"2d33a778-3550-4a04-bf60-bef6a0cf7cc9","timestamp":"2026-05-06T11:04:28.693Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}

{"parentUuid":"2d33a778-3550-4a04-bf60-bef6a0cf7cc9","isSidechain":false,"promptId":"c473d6aa-8336-444b-80bf-a3f8c4ffa788","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Step 7 confirmed. Proceed with Step 8.\n\nStep 8 — Verification adapter (Part B):\nCreate these files in admin-panel/lib/adapters/verification/\n\n1. types.ts:\ninterface AnnouncementForVerification {\n  id: string; titleEn: string; titleAr: string; bodyEn: string; bodyAr: string;\n}\ninterface VerificationResult {\n  ok: boolean; reason?: string; verdict: 'APPROVED' | 'REJECTED'; verifiedAt: Date;\n}\ninterface VerificationAdapter {\n  verify(announcement: AnnouncementForVerification): Promise<VerificationResult>;\n}\nclass TransientVerificationError extends Error {}\nclass PermanentVerificationError extends Error {}\nNo HTTP types anywhere.\n\n2. mock.ts — MockVerificationAdapter:\n- REJECTS if bodyEn or bodyAr contains __deny__\n- APPROVES otherwise\n\n3. live.ts — LiveVerificationAdapter:\n- verify() throws new Error('TODO SPEC PENDING')\n- Add // TODO SPEC PENDING comment on every line that would touch the API\n\n4. wrapper.ts — CachedRetryingBreakerAdapter implements VerificationAdapter:\nCache:\n- Key = announcementId + sha256(titleEn+titleAr+bodyEn+bodyAr).slice(0,16)\n- APPROVED TTL: 5 minutes\n- REJECTED TTL: never expires (until content changes)\n- Precedence rule: if cached verdict is REJECTED and new verdict is APPROVED → keep REJECTED\n- If cached verdict is APPROVED and new verdict is REJECTED → overwrite with REJECTED\n\nRetry:\n- 3 attempts max\n- Backoff: 100ms, 200ms, 400ms with ±20% jitter\n- Retry only on TransientVerificationError\n- PermanentVerificationError → fail fast, no retry\n\nCircuit Breaker:\n- CLOSED → 5 consecutive failures → OPEN\n- OPEN → after 30s → HALF_OPEN\n- HALF_OPEN → 1 success → CLOSED, 1 failure → OPEN\n- While OPEN: throw TransientVerificationError('breaker open')\n\n5. index.ts — barrel export all\n\nAfter creating the files:\n- Run: npx tsc --noEmit\n- Run: npm test (all should pass)\n- Commit: \"feat(verification): adapter interface, mock, live scaffold, retry+breaker+cache\"\n\nConfirm when done."}]},"uuid":"89277421-e91a-42cf-8691-8f75e53450b2","timestamp":"2026-05-06T11:05:44.924Z","permissionMode":"acceptEdits","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}

{"parentUuid":"bb2e8ada-3b46-4fe4-984b-5e6b34c80e72","isSidechain":false,"message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-7","id":"msg_013TxbH3zLVp53VPVbRsNvFv","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01K66gKiVFBQfn7u54dbieFm","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment/admin-panel/lib/adapters/verification/wrapper.ts","content":"import { createHash } from \"node:crypto\";\nimport type {\n  AnnouncementForVerification,\n  VerificationAdapter,\n  VerificationResult,\n} from \"./types\";\nimport {\n  PermanentVerificationError,\n  TransientVerificationError,\n} from \"./types\";\n\nconst APPROVED_TTL_MS = 5 * 60 * 1000;\nconst RETRY_DELAYS_MS = [100, 200, 400] as const;\nconst RETRY_MAX_ATTEMPTS = RETRY_DELAYS_MS.length;\nconst JITTER_FACTOR = 0.2;\nconst BREAKER_FAILURE_THRESHOLD = 5;\nconst BREAKER_OPEN_MS = 30_000;\n\ntype BreakerState = \"CLOSED\" | \"OPEN\" | \"HALF_OPEN\";\n\ninterface CacheEntry {\n  result: VerificationResult;\n  cachedAt: number;\n}\n\nexport interface Clock {\n  now(): number;\n  sleep(ms: number): Promise<void>;\n}\n\nconst defaultClock: Clock = {\n  now: () => Date.now(),\n  sleep: (ms) => new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, ms)),\n};\n\nexport interface WrapperOptions {\n  clock?: Clock;\n  random?: () => number;\n}\n\nexport class CachedRetryingBreakerAdapter implements VerificationAdapter {\n  private readonly cache = new Map<string, CacheEntry>();\n  private readonly clock: Clock;\n  private readonly random: () => number;\n\n  private breakerState: BreakerState = \"CLOSED\";\n  private consecutiveFailures = 0;\n  private openedAt = 0;\n  private halfOpenInFlight = false;\n\n  constructor(\n    private readonly inner: VerificationAdapter,\n    options: WrapperOptions = {}\n  ) {\n    this.clock = options.clock ?? defaultClock;\n    this.random = options.random ?? Math.random;\n  }\n\n  async verify(\n    announcement: AnnouncementForVerification\n  ): Promise<VerificationResult> {\n    const key = this.cacheKey(announcement);\n\n    const cached = this.readCache(key);\n    if (cached) return cached;\n\n    this.maybeTransitionToHalfOpen();\n\n    if (this.breakerState === \"OPEN\") {\n      throw new TransientVerificationError(\"breaker open\");\n    }\n    if (this.breakerState === \"HALF_OPEN\" && this.halfOpenInFlight) {\n      // Only one probe is allowed while half-open; concurrent callers wait for\n      // the next state change.\n      throw new TransientVerificationError(\"breaker open\");\n    }\n\n    if (this.breakerState === \"HALF_OPEN\") this.halfOpenInFlight = true;\n\n    try {\n      const result = await this.callWithRetries(announcement);\n      this.recordSuccess();\n      this.writeCache(key, result);\n      return result;\n    } catch (err) {\n      if (err instanceof TransientVerificationError) {\n        this.recordFailure();\n      }\n      throw err;\n    } finally {\n      this.halfOpenInFlight = false;\n    }\n  }\n\n  private async callWithRetries(\n    announcement: AnnouncementForVerification\n  ): Promise<VerificationResult> {\n    let lastErr: unknown;\n    for (let attempt = 0; attempt < RETRY_MAX_ATTEMPTS; attempt++) {\n      try {\n        return await this.inner.verify(announcement);\n      } catch (err) {\n        lastErr = err;\n        if (err instanceof PermanentVerificationError) throw err;\n        if (!(err instanceof TransientVerificationError)) throw err;\n        if (attempt === RETRY_MAX_ATTEMPTS - 1) throw err;\n        await this.clock.sleep(this.jitter(RETRY_DELAYS_MS[attempt]));\n      }\n    }\n    throw lastErr;\n  }\n\n  private jitter(base: number): number {\n    const delta = base * JITTER_FACTOR * (2 * this.random() - 1);\n    return Math.max(0, base + delta);\n  }\n\n  private cacheKey(a: AnnouncementForVerification): string {\n    const hash = createHash(\"sha256\")\n      .update(`${a.titleEn}|${a.titleAr}|${a.bodyEn}|${a.bodyAr}`)\n      .digest(\"hex\")\n      .slice(0, 16);\n    return `${a.id}:${hash}`;\n  }\n\n  private readCache(key: string): VerificationResult | null {\n    const entry = this.cache.get(key);\n    if (!entry) return null;\n    if (entry.result.verdict === \"REJECTED\") return entry.result;\n    if (this.clock.now() - entry.cachedAt < APPROVED_TTL_MS) {\n      return entry.result;\n    }\n    this.cache.delete(key);\n    return null;\n  }\n\n  private writeCache(key: string, result: VerificationResult): void {\n    const existing = this.cache.get(key);\n    // Precedence: a recorded REJECTED is sticky and cannot be flipped to APPROVED.\n    // An APPROVED entry, by contrast, may be overwritten by REJECTED.\n    if (\n      existing &&\n      existing.result.verdict === \"REJECTED\" &&\n      result.verdict === \"APPROVED\"\n    ) {\n      return;\n    }\n    this.cache.set(key, { result, cachedAt: this.clock.now() });\n  }\n\n  private recordSuccess(): void {\n    this.consecutiveFailures = 0;\n    this.breakerState = \"CLOSED\";\n  }\n\n  private recordFailure(): void {\n    if (this.breakerState === \"HALF_OPEN\") {\n      this.breakerState = \"OPEN\";\n      this.openedAt = this.clock.now();\n      return;\n    }\n    this.consecutiveFailures += 1;\n    if (this.consecutiveFailures >= BREAKER_FAILURE_THRESHOLD) {\n      this.breakerState = \"OPEN\";\n      this.openedAt = this.clock.now();\n    }\n  }\n\n  private maybeTransitionToHalfOpen(): void {\n    if (this.breakerState !== \"OPEN\") return;\n    if (this.clock.now() - this.openedAt >= BREAKER_OPEN_MS) {\n      this.breakerState = \"HALF_OPEN\";\n    }\n  }\n}\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":"tool_use","stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":677,"cache_read_input_tokens":60231,"output_tokens":2285,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":677,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[{"input_tokens":1,"output_tokens":2285,"cache_read_input_tokens":60231,"cache_creation_input_tokens":677,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":677},"type":"message"}],"speed":"standard"}},"requestId":"req_011CamD3VFkhDG2F3nqPdx39","type":"assistant","uuid":"e423576e-ba79-400a-bfc2-76cf36ef06a2","timestamp":"2026-05-06T11:07:32.303Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}

entry.cachedAt < APPROVED_TTL_MS) {\n      return entry.result;\n    }\n    this.cache.delete(key);\n    return null;\n  }\n\n  private writeCache(key: string, result: VerificationResult): void {\n    const existing = this.cache.get(key);\n    // Precedence: a recorded REJECTED is sticky and cannot be flipped to APPROVED.\n    // An APPROVED entry, by contrast, may be overwritten by REJECTED.\n    if (\n      existing &&\n      existing.result.verdict === \"REJECTED\" &&\n      result.verdict === \"APPROVED\"\n    ) {\n      return;\n    }\n    this.cache.set(key, { result, cachedAt: this.clock.now() });\n  }\n\n  private recordSuccess(): void {\n    this.consecutiveFailures = 0;\n    this.breakerState = \"CLOSED\";\n  }\n\n  private recordFailure(): void {\n    if (this.breakerState === \"HALF_OPEN\") {\n      this.breakerState = \"OPEN\";\n      this.openedAt = this.clock.now();\n      return;\n    }\n    this.consecutiveFailures += 1;\n    if (this.consecutiveFailures >= BREAKER_FAILURE_THRESHOLD) {\n      this.breakerState = \"OPEN\";\n      this.openedAt = this.clock.now();\n    }\n  }\n\n  private maybeTransitionToHalfOpen(): void {\n    if (this.breakerState !== \"OPEN\") return;\n    if (this.clock.now() - this.openedAt >= BREAKER_OPEN_MS) {\n      this.breakerState = \"HALF_OPEN\";\n    }\n  }\n}\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null,"userModified":false},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"e423576e-ba79-400a-bfc2-76cf36ef06a2","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}

{"parentUuid":"2ed07844-a9a1-4be8-8ef5-c6fa62f7f518","isSidechain":false,"message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-7","id":"msg_01GCodToyABP7fGsUYdAPqHN","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Step 8 done. Summary:\n\n- **`types.ts`** — `AnnouncementForVerification`, `VerificationResult`, `VerificationAdapter`, plus `TransientVerificationError` and `PermanentVerificationError`. No HTTP types.\n- **`mock.ts`** — `MockVerificationAdapter`. Returns REJECTED with `reason: 'mock-deny-token'` if `bodyEn` or `bodyAr` contains `__deny__`; otherwise APPROVED.\n- **`live.ts`** — `LiveVerificationAdapter`. `verify()` throws `Error('TODO SPEC PENDING')` and every potential call site has its own `// TODO SPEC PENDING` comment (URL, body, auth, request, status classification, parse, verdict mapping).\n- **`wrapper.ts`** — `CachedRetryingBreakerAdapter`:\n  - Cache key = `announcementId:sha256(titleEn|titleAr|bodyEn|bodyAr).slice(0,16)`.\n  - APPROVED entries expire after 5 min; REJECTED entries never expire by TTL.\n  - Precedence: a cached REJECTED is sticky — incoming APPROVED is dropped.\n  - Retry: 3 attempts, delays 100/200/400 ms with ±20% jitter, only on `TransientVerificationError`. `PermanentVerificationError` short-circuits.\n  - Breaker: CLOSED → 5 consecutive failures → OPEN → 30 s → HALF_OPEN → 1 success → CLOSED, 1 failure → OPEN. While OPEN (or while a HALF_OPEN probe is already in flight) callers receive `TransientVerificationError('breaker open')`.\n  - Clock and RNG are injectable via `WrapperOptions` so deterministic tests can be written later.\n- **`index.ts`** — barrel exporting types, errors, all three adapters, and clock options.\n- **Typecheck** clean. **`npm test`** — 4 files / 23 tests passed.\n- **Commit** — `47b1d00 feat(verification): adapter interface, mock, live scaffold, retry+breaker+cache`.\n\nConfirm to proceed with Step 9 (workflow service layer).\n\nNote for Step 9: the `escalateOverdue` operation needs a way to mark items as \"tier 2\" / \"escalated\". Step 7's schema doesn't include `tier` or `escalatedAt` columns on `ReviewQueueItem`. Two options for Step 9:\n1. Store escalation state in `AuditLog` only (no schema change) — query \"items with status PENDING and no `ESCALATED` audit yet\" via a left join.\n2. Add `tier Int @default(1)` and `escalatedAt DateTime?` to `ReviewQueueItem` in a small follow-up migration first.\n\nWhich would you prefer?"}],"stop_reason":"end_turn","stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":350,"cache_read_input_tokens":65383,"output_tokens":976,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":350,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[{"input_tokens":1,"output_tokens":976,"cache_read_input_tokens":65383,"cache_creation_input_tokens":350,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":350},"type":"message"}],"speed":"standard"}},"requestId":"req_011CamD8j2dC9v3PTEwws26E","type":"assistant","uuid":"e2583449-3cf9-42c5-a220-57dffa82b8b5","timestamp":"2026-05-06T11:08:38.405Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}

{"parentUuid":"e2583449-3cf9-42c5-a220-57dffa82b8b5","isSidechain":false,"promptId":"29b73331-6e4d-4b6d-8be0-26de557f14ee","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Step 8 confirmed. Proceed with Step 9.\n\nFirst, add a small migration for escalation fields:\n- Add tier Int @default(1) to ReviewQueueItem\n- Add escalatedAt DateTime? to ReviewQueueItem\n- Run: npx prisma migrate dev --name add_escalation_fields\n- Run: npx prisma generate\n\nThen create admin-panel/services/review-workflow.ts with these functions:\n\n1. submitForReview(announcementId, user):\n- Require role ADMIN or CONTENT_MANAGER (403 if not)\n- Require announcement exists, not deleted, status === 'DRAFT' (409 if not)\n- Inside prisma.$transaction:\n  * Update announcement status → PENDING_REVIEW\n  * Create ReviewQueueItem { status:'PENDING', authorId:user.id, tier:1 }\n  * Write AuditLog action:'SUBMIT_FOR_REVIEW'\n  * Write AuditLog action:'NOTIFICATION_QUEUED' (stub, no real delivery)\n\n2. claimNext(reviewer):\n- Require role ADMIN or COMPLIANCE_OFFICER\n- Call listPending({ excludeAuthorId: reviewer.id }) from the library\n- Call queue.claimNextItem(reviewer.id)\n- After claim: if result.item.authorId === reviewer.id → throw SelfReviewError\n- Return the ClaimResult\n\n3. approveItem(itemId, reviewer, note, deps: { verification: VerificationAdapter }):\n- Inside prisma.$transaction:\n  * Call queue.approve(itemId, reviewer, note)\n  * Call deps.verification.verify(announcement)\n  * If verdict === 'APPROVED' → announcement.status = PUBLISHED, publishedAt = now()\n    Write AuditLog action:'VERIFICATION_PASSED'\n  * If verdict === 'REJECTED' → announcement.status = DRAFT\n    Write AuditLog action:'VERIFICATION_REJECTED' with reason\n- If verification throws (transport error) → transaction rolls back\n  * Outside the failed tx: write best-effort AuditLog action:'VERIFICATION_TRANSPORT_FAILED'\n  * Rethrow the error\n\n4. rejectItem(itemId, reviewer, reason):\n- Inside prisma.$transaction:\n  * Call queue.reject(itemId, reviewer, reason)\n  * Update announcement.status → DRAFT\n  * Write AuditLog action:'REJECTED'\n  * Write AuditLog action:'NOTIFICATION_QUEUED' (stub)\n\n5. escalateOverdue(now = new Date()):\n- Inside prisma.$transaction:\n  * Find all ReviewQueueItem where status='PENDING' AND claimedBy=null AND tier=1\n    AND createdAt < now - 48h\n  * Update each: tier=2, escalatedAt=now\n  * Write AuditLog action:'ESCALATED' for each\n- Return { escalated: count }\n\nExport class SelfReviewError extends Error.\n\nAfter creating the file:\n- Run: npx tsc --noEmit\n- Run: npm test (all should pass)\n- Commit: \"feat(workflow): review-workflow service layer\"\n\nConfirm when done."}]},"uuid":"204e8101-5580-4706-a3fe-0a2c15ab4421","timestamp":"2026-05-06T11:09:26.755Z","permissionMode":"auto","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}

\"prisma-client-js\"\n}\n\ndatasource db {\n  provider = \"postgresql\"\n}\n\nenum Role {\n  ADMIN\n  CONTENT_MANAGER\n  COMPLIANCE_OFFICER\n  SUPPORT\n  USER\n}\n\nenum AnnouncementStatus {\n  DRAFT\n  PENDING_REVIEW\n  PUBLISHED\n  REJECTED\n}\n\nmodel User {\n  id         String   @id @default(cuid())\n  email      String   @unique\n  name       String\n  phone      String?\n  nationalId String?\n  role       Role     @default(USER)\n  createdAt  DateTime @default(now())\n  updatedAt  DateTime @updatedAt\n\n  announcements Announcement[]\n  auditLogs     AuditLog[]\n\n  @@index([name, email])\n}\n\nmodel Announcement {\n  id          String             @id @default(cuid())\n  titleEn     String\n  titleAr     String\n  bodyEn      String\n  bodyAr      String\n  status      AnnouncementStatus @default(DRAFT)\n  publishedAt DateTime?\n  deletedAt   DateTime?\n  createdAt   DateTime           @default(now())\n  updatedAt   DateTime           @updatedAt\n  authorId    String\n  author      User               @relation(fields: [authorId], references: [id])\n\n  auditLogs       AuditLog[]\n  reviewQueueItem ReviewQueueItem?\n\n  @@index([deletedAt])\n  @@index([status])\n}\n\nmodel ReviewQueueItem {\n  id             String    @id @default(cuid())\n  announcementId String    @unique\n  authorId       String\n  status         String    // PENDING | UNDER_REVIEW | APPROVED | REJECTED\n  claimedBy      String?\n  claimedAt      DateTime?\n  decisionNote   String?\n  decidedAt      DateTime?\n  createdAt      DateTime  @default(now())\n\n  announcement Announcement @relation(fields: [announcementId], references: [id])\n\n  @@index([status, claimedBy])\n  @@index([createdAt])\n}\n\nmodel AuditLog {\n  id             String        @id @default(cuid())\n  action         String\n  entity         String\n  entityId       String\n  userId         String\n  user           User          @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id])\n {"parentUuid":"9e1a183f-6a8d-478b-a060-5437204380f2","isSidechain":false,"promptId":"29b73331-6e4d-4b6d-8be0-26de557f14ee","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01QwUDVafaWcD7roDyHKg2LK","type":"tool_result","content":"1\timport type { PrismaClient } from '@prisma/client';\n2\timport type { ItemId, UserId } from './types';\n3\t\n4\t/**\n5\t * Approve a previously claimed review item.\n6\t *\n7\t * Runs inside a transaction so the status change and decision metadata\n8\t * stay consistent with any downstream effects the caller layers on top\n9\t * (audit entry, notification, publish step).\n10\t *\n11\t * Throws if the item does not exist, was not claimed by `reviewer`, or\n12\t * is not currently in the `UNDER_REVIEW` state.\n13\t */\n14\texport async function approve(\n15\t  prisma: PrismaClient,\n16\t  itemId: ItemId,\n17\t  reviewer: UserId,\n18\t  note: string\n19\t): Promise<void> {\n20\t  await prisma.$transaction(async (tx) => {\n21\t    const item = await tx.reviewQueueItem.findUnique({\n22\t      where: { id: itemId },\n23\t    });\n24\t    if (!item) {\n25\t      throw new Error(`review item ${itemId} not found`);\n26\t    }\n27\t    if (item.claimedBy !== reviewer) {\n28\t      throw new Error(\n29\t        `only the claiming reviewer may approve this item`\n30\t      );\n31\t    }\n32\t    if (item.status !== 'UNDER_REVIEW') {\n33\t      throw new Error(\n34\t        `cannot approve an item in status ${item.status}`\n35\t      );\n36\t    }\n37\t\n38\t    await tx.reviewQueueItem.update({\n39\t      where: { id: itemId },\n40\t      data: {\n41\t        status: 'APPROVED',\n42\t        decisionNote: note,\n43\t        decidedAt: new Date(),\n44\t      },\n45\t    });\n46\t  });\n47\t}\n48\t"}]},"uuid":"b023f27e-3d10-4b9f-a527-a71b2d2aba31","timestamp":"2026-05-06T11:10:24.134Z","toolUseResult":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment/admin-panel/lib/review-queue/src/approve.ts","content":"import type { PrismaClient } from '@prisma/client';\nimport type { ItemId, UserId } from './types';\n\n/**\n * Approve a previously claimed review item.\n *\n * Runs inside a transaction so the status change and decision metadata\n * stay consistent with any downstream effects the caller layers on top\n * (audit entry, notification, publish step).\n *\n * Throws if the item does not exist, was not claimed by `reviewer`, or\n * is not currently in the `UNDER_REVIEW` state.\n */\nexport async function approve(\n  prisma: PrismaClient,\n  itemId: ItemId,\n  reviewer: UserId,\n  note: string\n): Promise<void> {\n  await prisma.$transaction(async (tx) => {\n    const item = await tx.reviewQueueItem.findUnique({\n      where: { id: itemId },\n    });\n    if (!item) {\n      throw new Error(`review item ${itemId} not found`);\n    }\n    if (item.claimedBy !== reviewer) {\n      throw new Error(\n        `only the claiming reviewer may approve this item`\n      );\n    }\n    if (item.status !== 'UNDER_REVIEW') {\n      throw new Error(\n        `cannot approve an item in status ${item.status}`\n      );\n    }\n\n    await tx.reviewQueueItem.update({\n      where: { id: itemId },\n      data: {\n        status: 'APPROVED',\n        decisionNote: note,\n        decidedAt: new Date(),\n      },\n    });\n  });\n}\n","numLines":48,"startLine":1,"totalLines":48}},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"9e1a183f-6a8d-478b-a060-5437204380f2","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment/admin-panel","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}

type { PrismaClient } from '@prisma/client';\nimport type { ItemId, UserId } from './types';\n\n/**\n * Reject a previously claimed review item.\n *\n * Runs inside a transaction so the status change and decision metadata\n * stay consistent with any downstream effects the caller layers on top.\n *\n * Throws if the item does not exist, was not claimed by `reviewer`, or\n * is not currently in the `UNDER_REVIEW` state.\n */\nexport async function reject(\n  prisma: PrismaClient,\n  itemId: ItemId,\n  reviewer: UserId,\n  reason: string\n): Promise<void> {\n  await prisma.$transaction(async (tx) => {\n    const item = await tx.reviewQueueItem.findUnique({\n      where: { id: itemId },\n    });\n    if (!item) {\n      throw new Error(`review item ${itemId} not found`);\n    }\n    if (item.claimedBy !== reviewer) {\n      throw new Error(\n        `only the claiming reviewer may reject this item`\n      );\n    }\n    if (item.status !== 'UNDER_REVIEW') {\n      throw new Error(\n        `cannot reject an item in status ${item.status}`\n      );\n    }\n\n    await tx.reviewQueueItem.update({\n      where: { id: itemId },\n      data: {\n        status: 'REJECTED',\n        decisionNote: reason,\n        decidedAt: new Date(),\n      },\n    });\n  });\n}\n","numLines":47,"startLine":1,"totalLines":47}},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"1157a394-4312-4e6d-ad5b-412f7193de59","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment/admin-panel","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}

{"parentUuid":"7feaa310-be1b-4fe4-8c23-639fc0fab4cb","isSidechain":false,"promptId":"29b73331-6e4d-4b6d-8be0-26de557f14ee","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01Bg23FJ7Pqjd1r7K591jLCf","type":"tool_result","content":"1\timport type { PrismaClient } from '@prisma/client';\n2\timport type { PendingFilter, PendingItem } from './types';\n3\t\n4\t/**\n5\t * List items awaiting review. Callers should pass their own user id as\n6\t * `excludeAuthorId` so separation-of-duties filtering happens at the\n7\t * database level rather than after the fact.\n8\t */\n9\texport async function listPending(\n10\t  prisma: PrismaClient,\n11\t  filter: PendingFilter\n12\t): Promise<PendingItem[]> {\n13\t  const rows = await prisma.reviewQueueItem.findMany({\n14\t    where: {\n15\t      status: filter.status ?? 'PENDING',\n16\t      ...(filter.excludeAuthorId && {\n17\t        authorId: { not: filter.excludeAuthorId },\n18\t      }),\n19\t    },\n20\t    orderBy: { createdAt: 'asc' },\n21\t    take: filter.limit ?? 50,\n22\t  });\n23\t\n24\t  return rows as unknown as PendingItem[];\n25\t}\n26\t"}]},"uuid":"a16aadae-57cf-443a-a87b-ce203ae18b17","timestamp":"2026-05-06T11:10:33.638Z","toolUseResult":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment/admin-panel/lib/review-queue/src/list-pending.ts","content":"import type { PrismaClient } from '@prisma/client';\nimport type { PendingFilter, PendingItem } from './types';\n\n/**\n * List items awaiting review. Callers should pass their own user id as\n * `excludeAuthorId` so separation-of-duties filtering happens at the\n * database level rather than after the fact.\n */\nexport async function listPending(\n  prisma: PrismaClient,\n  filter: PendingFilter\n): Promise<PendingItem[]> {\n  const rows = await prisma.reviewQueueItem.findMany({\n    where: {\n      status: filter.status ?? 'PENDING',\n      ...(filter.excludeAuthorId && {\n        authorId: { not: filter.excludeAuthorId },\n      }),\n    },\n    orderBy: { createdAt: 'asc' },\n    take: filter.limit ?? 50,\n  });\n\n  return rows as unknown as PendingItem[];\n}\n","numLines":26,"startLine":1,"totalLines":26}},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"7feaa310-be1b-4fe4-8c23-639fc0fab4cb","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment/admin-panel","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}
