Link from \"next/link\";\nimport { prisma } from \"@/lib/prisma\";\nimport { getSessionUser } from \"@/lib/auth\";\nimport { can } from \"@/lib/rbac\";\nimport { paginationSchema } from \"@/lib/validations\";\nimport SubmitForReviewButton from \"./SubmitForReviewButton\";\n\ntype SearchParams = Promise<{ page?: string; pageSize?: string }>;\n\nexport default async function AnnouncementsPage({\n  searchParams,\n}: {\n  searchParams: SearchParams;\n}) {\n  const sp = await searchParams;\n  const { page, pageSize } = paginationSchema.parse({\n    page: sp.page,\n    pageSize: sp.pageSize,\n  });\n\n  const user = getSessionUser();\n  const canEdit = can(user, \"edit\");\n  const canSubmit = can(user, \"submitForReview\");\n\n  const where = { deletedAt: null };\n  const [items, total] = await Promise.all([\n    prisma.announcement.findMany({\n      where,\n      orderBy: { createdAt: \"desc\" },\n      skip: (page - 1) * pageSize,\n      take: pageSize,\n    }),\n    prisma.announcement.count({ where }),\n  ]);\n\n  const totalPages = Math.max(1, Math.ceil(total / pageSize));\n\n  return (\n    <main className=\"p-6 max-w-4xl mx-auto\">\n      <div className=\"flex justify-between items-center mb-4\">\n        <h1 className=\"text-2xl font-semibold\">Announcements</h1>\n        {canEdit && (\n          <Link\n            href=\"/admin/announcements/new\"\n            className=\"bg-black text-white px-3 py-1.5 rounded text-sm\"\n          >\n            New\n          </Link>\n        )}\n      </div>\n\n      <ul className=\"divide-y border rounded\">\n        {items.map((a) => (\n          <li\n            key={a.id}\n            className=\"p-3 flex justify-between items-center gap-4\"\n          >\n            <div className=\"min-w-0 flex-1\">\n              <div className=\"font-medium truncate\">{a.titleEn}</div>\n              <div\n                className=\"text-sm text-gray-500 truncate\"\n                dir=\"rtl\"\n              >\n                {a.titleAr}\n              </div>\n              <div className=\"text-xs mt-1\">\n                <span className=\"inline-block px-1.5 py-0.5 rounded bg-gray-100 text-gray-700\">\n                  {a.status}\n                </span>\n              </div>\n            </div>\n            <div className=\"flex items-center gap-3 shrink-0\">\n              {canSubmit && a.status === \"DRAFT\" && (\n                <SubmitForReviewButton id={a.id} />\n              )}\n              {canEdit && (\n                <Link\n                  href={`/admin/announcements/${a.id}/edit`}\n                  className=\"underline text-sm\"\n                >\n                  Edit\n                </Link>\n              )}\n            </div>\n          </li>\n        ))}\n        {items.length === 0 && (\n          <li className=\"p-4 text-gray-500 text-sm\">No announcements.</li>\n        )}\n      </ul>\n\n      <nav className=\"flex items-center gap-3 mt-4 text-sm\">\n        {page > 1 ? (\n          <Link\n            href={`/admin/announcements?page=${page - 1}&pageSize=${pageSize}`}\n            className=\"underline\"\n          >\n            Prev\n          </Link>\n        ) : (\n          <span className=\"text-gray-400\">Prev</span>\n        )}\n        <span>\n          Page {page} / {totalPages} &middot; {total} total\n        </span>\n        {page < totalPages ? (\n          <Link\n            href={`/admin/announcements?page=${page + 1}&pageSize=${pageSize}`}\n            className=\"underline\"\n          >\n            Next\n          </Link>\n        ) : (\n          <span className=\"text-gray-400\">Next</span>\n        )}\n      </nav>\n    </main>\n  );\n}\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":"tool_use","stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1183,"cache_read_input_tokens":123469,"output_tokens":1461,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":1183,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[{"input_tokens":1,"output_tokens":1461,"cache_read_input_tokens":123469,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1183,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":1183},"type":"message"}],"speed":"standard"}},"requestId":"req_011CamE7kKMJQkxWjJDXdocS","type":"assistant","uuid":"07ec96e5-6a6f-43ea-88ea-351fc585d502","timestamp":"2026-05-06T11:21:31.670Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}

from \"@/lib/rbac\";\nimport { paginationSchema } from \"@/lib/validations\";\n\ntype SearchParams = Promise<{ page?: string; pageSize?: string }>;\n\nexport default async function AnnouncementsPage({\n  searchParams,\n}: {\n  searchParams: SearchParams;\n}) {\n  const sp = await searchParams;\n  const { page, pageSize } = paginationSchema.parse({\n    page: sp.page,\n    pageSize: sp.pageSize,\n  });\n\n  const user = getSessionUser();\n  const canEdit = can(user, \"edit\");\n\n  const where = { deletedAt: null };\n  const [items, total] = await Promise.all([\n    prisma.announcement.findMany({\n      where,\n      orderBy: { createdAt: \"desc\" },\n      skip: (page - 1) * pageSize,\n      take: pageSize,\n    }),\n    prisma.announcement.count({ where }),\n  ]);\n\n  const totalPages = Math.max(1, Math.ceil(total / pageSize));\n\n  return (\n    <main className=\"p-6 max-w-4xl mx-auto\">\n      <div className=\"flex justify-between items-center mb-4\">\n        <h1 className=\"text-2xl font-semibold\">Announcements</h1>\n        {canEdit && (\n          <Link\n            href=\"/admin/announcements/new\"\n            className=\"bg-black text-white px-3 py-1.5 rounded text-sm\"\n          >\n            New\n          </Link>\n        )}\n      </div>\n\n      <ul className=\"divide-y border rounded\">\n        {items.map((a) => (\n          <li\n            key={a.id}\n            className=\"p-3 flex justify-between items-center gap-4\"\n          >\n            <div className=\"min-w-0 flex-1\">\n              <div className=\"font-medium truncate\">{a.titleEn}</div>\n              <div\n                className=\"text-sm text-gray-500 truncate\"\n                dir=\"rtl\"\n              >\n                {a.titleAr}\n              </div>\n            </div>\n            {canEdit && (\n              <Link\n                href={`/admin/announcements/${a.id}/edit`}\n                className=\"underline text-sm shrink-0\"\n              >\n                Edit\n              </Link>\n            )}\n          </li>\n        ))}\n        {items.length === 0 && (\n          <li className=\"p-4 text-gray-500 text-sm\">No announcements.</li>\n        )}\n      </ul>\n\n      <nav className=\"flex items-center gap-3 mt-4 text-sm\">\n        {page > 1 ? (\n          <Link\n            href={`/admin/announcements?page=${page - 1}&pageSize=${pageSize}`}\n            className=\"underline\"\n          >\n            Prev\n          </Link>\n        ) : (\n          <span className=\"text-gray-400\">Prev</span>\n        )}\n        <span>\n          Page {page} / {totalPages} &middot; {total} total\n        </span>\n        {page < totalPages ? (\n          <Link\n            href={`/admin/announcements?page=${page + 1}&pageSize=${pageSize}`}\n            className=\"underline\"\n          >\n            Next\n          </Link>\n        ) : (\n          <span className=\"text-gray-400\">Next</span>\n        )}\n      </nav>\n    </main>\n  );\n}\n","userModified":false},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"07ec96e5-6a6f-43ea-88ea-351fc585d502","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}

payload.error ?? `Request failed (${res.status})`);\n      return;\n    }\n    router.refresh();\n  }\n\n  return (\n    <div className=\"flex flex-col items-end\">\n      {error && <span className=\"text-red-600 text-xs\">{error}</span>}\n      <button\n        type=\"button\"\n        disabled={busy}\n        onClick={onClick}\n        className=\"border border-black px-2 py-1 rounded text-xs disabled:opacity-50\"\n      >\n        {busy ? \"Submitting...\" : \"Submit for review\"}\n      </button>\n    </div>\n  );\n}\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":"tool_use","stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1550,"cache_read_input_tokens":124652,"output_tokens":554,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":1550,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[{"input_tokens":1,"output_tokens":554,"cache_read_input_tokens":124652,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1550,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":1550},"type":"message"}],"speed":"standard"}},"requestId":"req_011CamE9FrPSonQH4WLBCxbs","type":"assistant","uuid":"02475b3c-6c46-4097-ab4f-1af55cd753dc","timestamp":"2026-05-06T11:21:42.098Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}

{"parentUuid":"7f9d1148-2943-4144-a591-2ec5246e5ea3","isSidechain":false,"message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-7","id":"msg_01WqPAzq4gFMC4Le41UN2aEa","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Both steps done.\n\n**Step 10 — API routes** (commit `94560ab`):\n- [POST /api/announcements/[id]/submit-for-review](admin-panel/app/api/announcements/[id]/submit-for-review/route.ts) — RBAC `submitForReview`, maps `WorkflowNotFoundError` → 404, `WorkflowStateError` → 409.\n- [GET /api/review-queue](admin-panel/app/api/review-queue/route.ts) — RBAC `review`, returns `{ items }` from `listPending({ excludeAuthorId })`.\n- [POST /api/review-queue/claim](admin-panel/app/api/review-queue/claim/route.ts) — RBAC `review`, `SelfReviewError` → 403.\n- [POST /api/review-queue/[id]/approve](admin-panel/app/api/review-queue/[id]/approve/route.ts) — RBAC `review`, body `{ note }` (Zod), wires `MockVerificationAdapter`. Workflow errors map to 404/403/409; verification transport / breaker errors fall through to 503.\n- [POST /api/review-queue/[id]/reject](admin-panel/app/api/review-queue/[id]/reject/route.ts) — RBAC `review`, body `{ reason }` (Zod).\n- [POST /api/review-queue/escalate](admin-panel/app/api/review-queue/escalate/route.ts) — RBAC `escalate`, returns `{ escalated: count }`.\n- All routes use the App Router `params: Promise<{ id: string }>` pattern (verified against `node_modules/next/dist/docs/01-app/03-api-reference/03-file-conventions/route.md`).\n- Validation schemas added to [lib/validations.ts](admin-panel/lib/validations.ts): `reviewApproveSchema`, `reviewRejectSchema`.\n\n**Step 11 — UI** (commit `5d423fb`):\n- [app/admin/review-queue/page.tsx](admin-panel/app/admin/review-queue/page.tsx) — server component, redirects when `!can(user, 'review')`. Fetches both PENDING (excluding self-author) and the reviewer's own UNDER_REVIEW items in parallel so the table is the reviewer's full active surface; columns: title, status, tier, created, actions. Bypasses `listPending` here to do the announcement-title join in one query — comment in the file calls this out.\n- [app/admin/review-queue/ReviewActions.tsx](admin-panel/app/admin/review-queue/ReviewActions.tsx) — client component with two modes: `claim-next` for the global button; `decide` for per-row Approve/Reject with a shared note/reason input.\n- [app/admin/announcements/page.tsx](admin-panel/app/admin/announcements/page.tsx) — added a status badge on each row and a `SubmitForReviewButton` that only shows when `status === 'DRAFT'` and `can(user, 'submitForReview')`.\n- [app/admin/announcements/SubmitForReviewButton.tsx](admin-panel/app/admin/announcements/SubmitForReviewButton.tsx) — client component, POSTs to `/api/announcements/{id}/submit-for-review` and refreshes on success.\n- [app/admin/layout.tsx](admin-panel/app/admin/layout.tsx) — sidebar now shows \"Review queue\" only when `can(user, 'review')`.\n- **Typecheck** clean, **`npm test`** — 4 files / 23 tests passed for both steps.\n\nNote: I have not run the dev server or browser-tested the UI in this session. Per the brief I should still verify the golden path in a browser (login as `cm@example.com` → submit, login as `compliance@example.com` → claim/approve). Want me to start the dev server next, or proceed straight to the README + transcript?"}],"stop_reason":"end_turn","stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":286,"cache_read_input_tokens":129356,"output_tokens":1306,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":286,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[{"input_tokens":1,"output_tokens":1306,"cache_read_input_tokens":129356,"cache_creation_input_tokens":286,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":286},"type":"message"}],"speed":"standard"}},"requestId":"req_011CamEECwARvsY7Fo6X9Hyf","type":"assistant","uuid":"d53f4a73-0afb-4ed4-9159-cea9255e565e","timestamp":"2026-05-06T11:23:02.750Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}

{"parentUuid":"d53f4a73-0afb-4ed4-9159-cea9255e565e","isSidechain":false,"promptId":"012390d8-d956-4ff3-97f4-989553cc9c8d","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Steps 10 and 11 confirmed.\n\nStep 12 — Add the missing tests:\n\nCreate these test files in admin-panel/__tests__/\n\n1. __tests__/verification-precedence.test.ts\n- Unit test for CachedRetryingBreakerAdapter precedence rule\n- Test 1: first call returns REJECTED, second call (same content) returns APPROVED\n  → wrapper must still return REJECTED (cached precedence wins)\n- Test 2 (negative): edit the body (cache key changes), new call returns APPROVED\n  → wrapper must now return APPROVED (new content = new cache entry)\n- Use MockVerificationAdapter as inner adapter with controllable responses\n\n2. __tests__/verification-breaker.test.ts\n- Unit test for circuit breaker\n- Test 1: 5 consecutive TransientVerificationErrors → breaker trips to OPEN\n- Test 2: while OPEN → calls reject with TransientVerificationError('breaker open')\n  without invoking the inner adapter\n- Test 3: after 30s (use injectable clock) → HALF_OPEN → 1 success → CLOSED\n- Use injectable Clock from WrapperOptions to control time\n\n3. __tests__/workflow-separation-of-duties.test.ts\n- Unit test for review-workflow service\n- Mock prisma and the review-queue library\n- Test: author tries to claimNext on their own announcement → SelfReviewError thrown\n- Test: author tries to approveItem on their own item → WorkflowAuthorizationError or SelfReviewError\n\n4. __tests__/workflow-transactional.test.ts\n- Unit test for transactional rollback\n- Mock prisma.$transaction to simulate a failure mid-transaction\n- Test: if audit write throws inside submitForReview → announcement status does NOT change\n- Test: if verification throws inside approveItem → announcement stays PENDING_REVIEW\n\nAfter creating tests:\n- Run: npm test (all should pass, target: 23 + new tests)\n- Commit: \"test: verification precedence, breaker, separation-of-duties, transactional rollback\"\n\nThen Step 13 — Update README:\n\nAdd a Level 2 section to the root README.md with:\n- New run instructions: npm run db:seed includes compliance@example.com\n- New routes list (all 6 review-queue routes)\n- Environment variables needed\n- Note: race test requires DATABASE_URL set explicitly\n- Note: MockVerificationAdapter is used in dev; deny with __deny__ in body\n\nCommit: \"docs: update README with Level 2 instructions\"\n\nConfirm when both steps are done."}]},"uuid":"5571678b-5d25-453e-b4cf-a054ee971173","timestamp":"2026-05-06T11:23:48.337Z","permissionMode":"auto","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}

{"parentUuid":"61cf6115-1589-4ad5-a448-b1f1ed92d9c8","isSidechain":false,"message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-7","id":"msg_01LMLABUqGQrkorN28AfZaAP","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01VwdCkmPkYy5Ldd2sVckS79","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment/admin-panel/__tests__/verification-precedence.test.ts","content":"import { describe, it, expect } from \"vitest\";\n\nimport {\n  CachedRetryingBreakerAdapter,\n  type AnnouncementForVerification,\n  type VerificationAdapter,\n  type VerificationResult,\n} from \"@/lib/adapters/verification\";\n\nfunction announcement(\n  overrides: Partial<AnnouncementForVerification> = {}\n): AnnouncementForVerification {\n  return {\n    id: \"ann-1\",\n    titleEn: \"Title\",\n    titleAr: \"عنوان\",\n    bodyEn: \"Body\",\n    bodyAr: \"نص\",\n    ...overrides,\n  };\n}\n\nclass StubAdapter implements VerificationAdapter {\n  calls = 0;\n  responses: Array<VerificationResult | Error> = [];\n\n  async verify(): Promise<VerificationResult> {\n    this.calls += 1;\n    const r = this.responses.shift();\n    if (r === undefined) {\n      throw new Error(\"StubAdapter: no response queued\");\n    }\n    if (r instanceof Error) throw r;\n    return r;\n  }\n}\n\ndescribe(\"CachedRetryingBreakerAdapter — cached REJECTED is sticky\", () => {\n  it(\"a REJECTED verdict is returned on subsequent calls with the same content even if the inner would say APPROVED\", async () => {\n    const inner = new StubAdapter();\n    inner.responses.push(\n      { ok: false, verdict: \"REJECTED\", reason: \"first-deny\", verifiedAt: new Date(0) },\n      { ok: true, verdict: \"APPROVED\", verifiedAt: new Date(0) }\n    );\n\n    const wrapper = new CachedRetryingBreakerAdapter(inner);\n\n    const first = await wrapper.verify(announcement());\n    expect(first.verdict).toBe(\"REJECTED\");\n\n    const second = await wrapper.verify(announcement());\n    expect(second.verdict).toBe(\"REJECTED\");\n    expect(second.reason).toBe(\"first-deny\");\n\n    // Inner was never called the second time — cache hit served REJECTED.\n    expect(inner.calls).toBe(1);\n  });\n\n  it(\"editing the body changes the cache key, so a new APPROVED verdict is honoured\", async () => {\n    const inner = new StubAdapter();\n    inner.responses.push(\n      { ok: false, verdict: \"REJECTED\", reason: \"first-deny\", verifiedAt: new Date(0) },\n      { ok: true, verdict: \"APPROVED\", verifiedAt: new Date(0) }\n    );\n\n    const wrapper = new CachedRetryingBreakerAdapter(inner);\n\n    const first = await wrapper.verify(announcement());\n    expect(first.verdict).toBe(\"REJECTED\");\n\n    // Same announcement id, different body → different cache key.\n    const second = await wrapper.verify(announcement({ bodyEn: \"Edited body\" }));\n    expect(second.verdict).toBe(\"APPROVED\");\n    expect(inner.calls).toBe(2);\n  });\n});\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":"tool_use","stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":11341,"cache_read_input_tokens":131822,"output_tokens":1585,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":11341,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[{"input_tokens":1,"output_tokens":1585,"cache_read_input_tokens":131822,"cache_creation_input_tokens":11341,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":11341},"type":"message"}],"speed":"standard"}},"requestId":"req_011CamEV2yYSZqckQ1gST6nC","type":"assistant","uuid":"09bc72fc-0458-47c0-8b8a-b563b39c9d27","timestamp":"2026-05-06T11:26:22.898Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}

Error(\"StubAdapter: no response queued\");\n    }\n    if (r instanceof Error) throw r;\n    return r;\n  }\n}\n\ndescribe(\"CachedRetryingBreakerAdapter — cached REJECTED is sticky\", () => {\n  it(\"a REJECTED verdict is returned on subsequent calls with the same content even if the inner would say APPROVED\", async () => {\n    const inner = new StubAdapter();\n    inner.responses.push(\n      { ok: false, verdict: \"REJECTED\", reason: \"first-deny\", verifiedAt: new Date(0) },\n      { ok: true, verdict: \"APPROVED\", verifiedAt: new Date(0) }\n    );\n\n    const wrapper = new CachedRetryingBreakerAdapter(inner);\n\n    const first = await wrapper.verify(announcement());\n    expect(first.verdict).toBe(\"REJECTED\");\n\n    const second = await wrapper.verify(announcement());\n    expect(second.verdict).toBe(\"REJECTED\");\n    expect(second.reason).toBe(\"first-deny\");\n\n    // Inner was never called the second time — cache hit served REJECTED.\n    expect(inner.calls).toBe(1);\n  });\n\n  it(\"editing the body changes the cache key, so a new APPROVED verdict is honoured\", async () => {\n    const inner = new StubAdapter();\n    inner.responses.push(\n      { ok: false, verdict: \"REJECTED\", reason: \"first-deny\", verifiedAt: new Date(0) },\n      { ok: true, verdict: \"APPROVED\", verifiedAt: new Date(0) }\n    );\n\n    const wrapper = new CachedRetryingBreakerAdapter(inner);\n\n    const first = await wrapper.verify(announcement());\n    expect(first.verdict).toBe(\"REJECTED\");\n\n    // Same announcement id, different body → different cache key.\n    const second = await wrapper.verify(announcement({ bodyEn: \"Edited body\" }));\n    expect(second.verdict).toBe(\"APPROVED\");\n    expect(inner.calls).toBe(2);\n  });\n});\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null,"userModified":false},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"09bc72fc-0458-47c0-8b8a-b563b39c9d27","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}
{"parentUuid":"6edfb89a-5e99-4c4b-b765-92cd309646af","isSidechain":false,"message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-7","id":"msg_01JRttcF9bBgdhTFr9iZ1FzY","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_018dn8PCWavcCiud6GMKA6BV","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment/admin-panel/__tests__/verification-breaker.test.ts","content":"import { describe, it, expect } from \"vitest\";\n\nimport {\n  CachedRetryingBreakerAdapter,\n  TransientVerificationError,\n  type AnnouncementForVerification,\n  type Clock,\n  type VerificationAdapter,\n  type VerificationResult,\n} from \"@/lib/adapters/verification\";\n\nfunction announcement(id: string): AnnouncementForVerification {\n  return {\n    id,\n    titleEn: `t-${id}`,\n    titleAr: `t-ar-${id}`,\n    bodyEn: `b-${id}`,\n    bodyAr: `b-ar-${id}`,\n  };\n}\n\ninterface FakeClock extends Clock {\n  state: { value: number };\n  advance(ms: number): void;\n}\n\nfunction fakeClock(): FakeClock {\n  const state = { value: 0 };\n  return {\n    state,\n    now: () => state.value,\n    sleep: async (ms) => {\n      state.value += ms;\n    },\n    advance: (ms: number) => {\n      state.value += ms;\n    },\n  };\n}\n\nclass StubAdapter implements VerificationAdapter {\n  calls = 0;\n  next: VerificationResult | Error | null = null;\n\n  async verify(): Promise<VerificationResult> {\n    this.calls += 1;\n    const r = this.next;\n    if (r === null) throw new Error(\"StubAdapter: no response set\");\n    if (r instanceof Error) throw r;\n    return r;\n  }\n}\n\ndescribe(\"CachedRetryingBreakerAdapter — circuit breaker\", () => {\n  it(\"trips OPEN after 5 consecutive failed verify calls and short-circuits subsequent calls\", async () => {\n    const inner = new StubAdapter();\n    inner.next = new TransientVerificationError(\"upstream 503\");\n    const clock = fakeClock();\n    const wrapper = new CachedRetryingBreakerAdapter(inner, {\n      clock,\n      random: () => 0.5,\n    });\n\n    for (let i = 0; i < 5; i++) {\n      await expect(\n        wrapper.verify(announcement(`a-${i}`))\n      ).rejects.toBeInstanceOf(TransientVerificationError);\n    }\n    // Each failed verify makes 3 inner attempts (3 retries).\n    expect(inner.calls).toBe(15);\n\n    // Breaker is now OPEN — next verify must short-circuit without invoking inner.\n    const callsAfterTrip = inner.calls;\n    await expect(\n      wrapper.verify(announcement(\"post-trip\"))\n    ).rejects.toThrow(/breaker open/);\n    expect(inner.calls).toBe(callsAfterTrip);\n  });\n\n  it(\"transitions OPEN → HALF_OPEN after 30s and CLOSED on a successful probe\", async () => {\n    const inner = new StubAdapter();\n    inner.next = new TransientVerificationError(\"upstream 503\");\n    const clock = fakeClock();\n    const wrapper = new CachedRetryingBreakerAdapter(inner, {\n      clock,\n      random: () => 0.5,\n    });\n\n    for (let i = 0; i < 5; i++) {\n      await expect(\n        wrapper.verify(announcement(`a-${i}`))\n      ).rejects.toBeInstanceOf(TransientVerificationError);\n    }\n    const callsAfterTrip = inner.calls;\n\n    // Just under 30s past openedAt — still OPEN.\n    clock.advance(29_000);\n    await expect(\n      wrapper.verify(announcement(\"still-open\"))\n    ).rejects.toThrow(/breaker open/);\n    expect(inner.calls).toBe(callsAfterTrip);\n\n    // Push past the 30s open window — next call should attempt the inner (HALF_OPEN probe).\n    clock.advance(2_000);\n    inner.next = {\n      ok: true,\n      verdict: \"APPROVED\",\n      verifiedAt: new Date(0),\n    };\n\n    const probe = await wrapper.verify(announcement(\"probe\"));\n    expect(probe.verdict).toBe(\"APPROVED\");\n    expect(inner.calls).toBe(callsAfterTrip + 1);\n\n    // Breaker should now be CLOSED — another call goes straight through.\n    const after = await wrapper.verify(announcement(\"after-close\"));\n    expect(after.verdict).toBe(\"APPROVED\");\n    expect(inner.calls).toBe(callsAfterTrip + 2);\n  });\n});\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":"tool_use","stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1673,"cache_read_input_tokens":143163,"output_tokens":1623,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":1673,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[{"input_tokens":1,"output_tokens":1623,"cache_read_input_tokens":143163,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1673,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":1673},"type":"message"}],"speed":"standard"}},"requestId":"req_011CamEXHEMF42Rb3Hf9gGS9","type":"assistant","uuid":"0604c8c3-492a-4c6d-bbc2-77bce46e52e3","timestamp":"2026-05-06T11:26:51.362Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}

created successfully at: /home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment/admin-panel/__tests__/verification-breaker.test.ts (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)"}]},"uuid":"26759865-c97a-4ccd-b58c-260009bf131e","timestamp":"2026-05-06T11:26:52.381Z","toolUseResult":{"type":"create","filePath":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment/admin-panel/__tests__/verification-breaker.test.ts","content":"import { describe, it, expect } from \"vitest\";\n\nimport {\n  CachedRetryingBreakerAdapter,\n  TransientVerificationError,\n  type AnnouncementForVerification,\n  type Clock,\n  type VerificationAdapter,\n  type VerificationResult,\n} from \"@/lib/adapters/verification\";\n\nfunction announcement(id: string): AnnouncementForVerification {\n  return {\n    id,\n    titleEn: `t-${id}`,\n    titleAr: `t-ar-${id}`,\n    bodyEn: `b-${id}`,\n    bodyAr: `b-ar-${id}`,\n  };\n}\n\ninterface FakeClock extends Clock {\n  state: { value: number };\n  advance(ms: number): void;\n}\n\nfunction fakeClock(): FakeClock {\n  const state = { value: 0 };\n  return {\n    state,\n    now: () => state.value,\n    sleep: async (ms) => {\n      state.value += ms;\n    },\n    advance: (ms: number) => {\n      state.value += ms;\n    },\n  };\n}\n\nclass StubAdapter implements VerificationAdapter {\n  calls = 0;\n  next: VerificationResult | Error | null = null;\n\n  async verify(): Promise<VerificationResult> {\n    this.calls += 1;\n    const r = this.next;\n    if (r === null) throw new Error(\"StubAdapter: no response set\");\n    if (r instanceof Error) throw r;\n    return r;\n  }\n}\n\ndescribe(\"CachedRetryingBreakerAdapter — circuit breaker\", () => {\n  it(\"trips OPEN after 5 consecutive failed verify calls and short-circuits subsequent calls\", async () => {\n    const inner = new StubAdapter();\n    inner.next = new TransientVerificationError(\"upstream 503\");\n    const clock = fakeClock();\n    const wrapper = new CachedRetryingBreakerAdapter(inner, {\n      clock,\n      random: () => 0.5,\n    });\n\n    for (let i = 0; i < 5; i++) {\n      await expect(\n        wrapper.verify(announcement(`a-${i}`))\n      ).rejects.toBeInstanceOf(TransientVerificationError);\n    }\n    // Each failed verify makes 3 inner attempts (3 retries).\n    expect(inner.calls).toBe(15);\n\n    // Breaker is now OPEN — next verify must short-circuit without invoking inner.\n    const callsAfterTrip = inner.calls;\n    await expect(\n      wrapper.verify(announcement(\"post-trip\"))\n    ).rejects.toThrow(/breaker open/);\n    expect(inner.calls).toBe(callsAfterTrip);\n  });\n\n  it(\"transitions OPEN → HALF_OPEN after 30s and CLOSED on a successful probe\", async () => {\n    const inner = new StubAdapter();\n    inner.next = new TransientVerificationError(\"upstream 503\");\n    const clock = fakeClock();\n    const wrapper = new CachedRetryingBreakerAdapter(inner, {\n      clock,\n      random: () => 0.5,\n    });\n\n    for (let i = 0; i < 5; i++) {\n      await expect(\n        wrapper.verify(announcement(`a-${i}`))\n      ).rejects.toBeInstanceOf(TransientVerificationError);\n    }\n    const callsAfterTrip = inner.calls;\n\n    // Just under 30s past openedAt — still OPEN.\n    clock.advance(29_000);\n    await expect(\n      wrapper.verify(announcement(\"still-open\"))\n    ).rejects.toThrow(/breaker open/);\n    expect(inner.calls).toBe(callsAfterTrip);\n\n    // Push past the 30s open window — next call should attempt the inner (HALF_OPEN probe).\n    clock.advance(2_000);\n    inner.next = {\n      ok: true,\n      verdict: \"APPROVED\",\n      verifiedAt: new Date(0),\n    };\n\n    const probe = await wrapper.verify(announcement(\"probe\"));\n    expect(probe.verdict).toBe(\"APPROVED\");\n    expect(inner.calls).toBe(callsAfterTrip + 1);\n\n    // Breaker should now be CLOSED — another call goes straight through.\n    const after = await wrapper.verify(announcement(\"after-close\"));\n    expect(after.verdict).toBe(\"APPROVED\");\n    expect(inner.calls).toBe(callsAfterTrip + 2);\n  });\n});\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null,"userModified":false},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"0604c8c3-492a-4c6d-bbc2-77bce46e52e3","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}

{"parentUuid":"2f2c3342-9e87-490d-bc85-eafbc9d43ff1","isSidechain":false,"message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-7","id":"msg_0157i9fZKzcmwaSPwV6bFy4H","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_011ig2KLUEAjbLktQnVBY8TG","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment/admin-panel/__tests__/workflow-separation-of-duties.test.ts","content":"import { describe, it, expect, vi } from \"vitest\";\n\nvi.mock(\"@prisma/client\", () => ({\n  PrismaClient: class {},\n  Role: {\n    ADMIN: \"ADMIN\",\n    CONTENT_MANAGER: \"CONTENT_MANAGER\",\n    COMPLIANCE_OFFICER: \"COMPLIANCE_OFFICER\",\n    SUPPORT: \"SUPPORT\",\n    USER: \"USER\",\n  },\n  AnnouncementStatus: {\n    DRAFT: \"DRAFT\",\n    PENDING_REVIEW: \"PENDING_REVIEW\",\n    PUBLISHED: \"PUBLISHED\",\n    REJECTED: \"REJECTED\",\n  },\n}));\n\nconst queueMocks = {\n  listPending: vi.fn(),\n  claimNextItem: vi.fn(),\n  approve: vi.fn(),\n  reject: vi.fn(),\n};\n\nvi.mock(\"@/lib/review-queue/src\", () => ({\n  createReviewQueueClient: () => queueMocks,\n}));\n\nimport { Role } from \"@prisma/client\";\nimport type { SessionUser } from \"@/lib/auth\";\nimport {\n  SelfReviewError,\n  approveItem,\n  claimNext,\n} from \"@/services/review-workflow\";\nimport { MockVerificationAdapter } from \"@/lib/adapters/verification\";\n\nconst compliance: SessionUser = {\n  id: \"user-compliance\",\n  email: \"c@x\",\n  name: \"C\",\n  role: Role.COMPLIANCE_OFFICER,\n};\n\ndescribe(\"review-workflow — separation of duties\", () => {\n  it(\"claimNext throws SelfReviewError when SKIP LOCKED races to a self-authored row, and releases the row\", async () => {\n    queueMocks.listPending.mockResolvedValueOnce([\n      { id: \"item-other\", authorId: \"another-user\" },\n    ]);\n    queueMocks.claimNextItem.mockResolvedValueOnce({\n      kind: \"claimed\",\n      item: {\n        id: \"item-1\",\n        announcementId: \"ann-1\",\n        authorId: compliance.id,\n        status: \"UNDER_REVIEW\",\n        claimedBy: compliance.id,\n        claimedAt: new Date(0),\n        decisionNote: null,\n        decidedAt: null,\n        createdAt: new Date(0),\n      },\n    });\n\n    const update = vi.fn().mockResolvedValue({});\n    const prisma = {\n      reviewQueueItem: { update },\n    } as never;\n\n    await expect(claimNext(prisma, compliance)).rejects.toBeInstanceOf(\n      SelfReviewError\n    );\n\n    // The defence-in-depth release back to PENDING ran.\n    expect(update).toHaveBeenCalledWith(\n      expect.objectContaining({\n        where: { id: \"item-1\" },\n        data: expect.objectContaining({\n          status: \"PENDING\",\n          claimedBy: null,\n        }),\n      })\n    );\n  });\n\n  it(\"approveItem throws SelfReviewError when the item author equals the reviewer\", async () => {\n    const item = {\n      id: \"item-1\",\n      announcementId: \"ann-1\",\n      authorId: compliance.id,\n      claimedBy: compliance.id,\n      status: \"UNDER_REVIEW\",\n      announcement: {\n        id: \"ann-1\",\n        titleEn: \"t\",\n        titleAr: \"ت\",\n        bodyEn: \"b\",\n        bodyAr: \"ب\",\n      },\n    };\n\n    const tx = {\n      reviewQueueItem: {\n        findUnique: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue(item),\n        update: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue({}),\n      },\n      announcement: { update: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue({}) },\n      auditLog: { create: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue({}) },\n    };\n\n    const $transaction = vi.fn().mockImplementation((cb) => cb(tx));\n    const prisma = {\n      $transaction,\n      auditLog: { create: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue({}) },\n    } as never;\n\n    await expect(\n      approveItem(prisma, \"item-1\", compliance, \"looks fine\", {\n        verification: new MockVerificationAdapter(),\n      })\n    ).rejects.toBeInstanceOf(SelfReviewError);\n\n    // Self-review is detected before any state mutation.\n    expect(tx.reviewQueueItem.update).not.toHaveBeenCalled();\n    expect(tx.announcement.update).not.toHaveBeenCalled();\n  });\n});\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":"tool_use","stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":2299,"cache_read_input_tokens":144836,"output_tokens":1686,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":2299,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[{"input_tokens":1,"output_tokens":1686,"cache_read_input_tokens":144836,"cache_creation_input_tokens":2299,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":2299},"type":"message"}],"speed":"standard"}},"requestId":"req_011CamEYs6aptucpwoGfgqdW","type":"assistant","uuid":"cf63cf0b-80c5-4eb0-84f0-d4119fae5ab1","timestamp":"2026-05-06T11:27:20.739Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}

back)"}]},"uuid":"624c5b6d-a318-411c-b68c-ec55cdbcc6f5","timestamp":"2026-05-06T11:27:21.756Z","toolUseResult":{"type":"create","filePath":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment/admin-panel/__tests__/workflow-separation-of-duties.test.ts","content":"import { describe, it, expect, vi } from \"vitest\";\n\nvi.mock(\"@prisma/client\", () => ({\n  PrismaClient: class {},\n  Role: {\n    ADMIN: \"ADMIN\",\n    CONTENT_MANAGER: \"CONTENT_MANAGER\",\n    COMPLIANCE_OFFICER: \"COMPLIANCE_OFFICER\",\n    SUPPORT: \"SUPPORT\",\n    USER: \"USER\",\n  },\n  AnnouncementStatus: {\n    DRAFT: \"DRAFT\",\n    PENDING_REVIEW: \"PENDING_REVIEW\",\n    PUBLISHED: \"PUBLISHED\",\n    REJECTED: \"REJECTED\",\n  },\n}));\n\nconst queueMocks = {\n  listPending: vi.fn(),\n  claimNextItem: vi.fn(),\n  approve: vi.fn(),\n  reject: vi.fn(),\n};\n\nvi.mock(\"@/lib/review-queue/src\", () => ({\n  createReviewQueueClient: () => queueMocks,\n}));\n\nimport { Role } from \"@prisma/client\";\nimport type { SessionUser } from \"@/lib/auth\";\nimport {\n  SelfReviewError,\n  approveItem,\n  claimNext,\n} from \"@/services/review-workflow\";\nimport { MockVerificationAdapter } from \"@/lib/adapters/verification\";\n\nconst compliance: SessionUser = {\n  id: \"user-compliance\",\n  email: \"c@x\",\n  name: \"C\",\n  role: Role.COMPLIANCE_OFFICER,\n};\n\ndescribe(\"review-workflow — separation of duties\", () => {\n  it(\"claimNext throws SelfReviewError when SKIP LOCKED races to a self-authored row, and releases the row\", async () => {\n    queueMocks.listPending.mockResolvedValueOnce([\n      { id: \"item-other\", authorId: \"another-user\" },\n    ]);\n    queueMocks.claimNextItem.mockResolvedValueOnce({\n      kind: \"claimed\",\n      item: {\n        id: \"item-1\",\n        announcementId: \"ann-1\",\n        authorId: compliance.id,\n        status: \"UNDER_REVIEW\",\n        claimedBy: compliance.id,\n        claimedAt: new Date(0),\n        decisionNote: null,\n        decidedAt: null,\n        createdAt: new Date(0),\n      },\n    });\n\n    const update = vi.fn().mockResolvedValue({});\n    const prisma = {\n      reviewQueueItem: { update },\n    } as never;\n\n    await expect(claimNext(prisma, compliance)).rejects.toBeInstanceOf(\n      SelfReviewError\n    );\n\n    // The defence-in-depth release back to PENDING ran.\n    expect(update).toHaveBeenCalledWith(\n      expect.objectContaining({\n        where: { id: \"item-1\" },\n        data: expect.objectContaining({\n          status: \"PENDING\",\n          claimedBy: null,\n        }),\n      })\n    );\n  });\n\n  it(\"approveItem throws SelfReviewError when the item author equals the reviewer\", async () => {\n    const item = {\n      id: \"item-1\",\n      announcementId: \"ann-1\",\n      authorId: compliance.id,\n      claimedBy: compliance.id,\n      status: \"UNDER_REVIEW\",\n      announcement: {\n        id: \"ann-1\",\n        titleEn: \"t\",\n        titleAr: \"ت\",\n        bodyEn: \"b\",\n        bodyAr: \"ب\",\n      },\n    };\n\n    const tx = {\n      reviewQueueItem: {\n        findUnique: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue(item),\n        update: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue({}),\n      },\n      announcement: { update: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue({}) },\n      auditLog: { create: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue({}) },\n    };\n\n    const $transaction = vi.fn().mockImplementation((cb) => cb(tx));\n    const prisma = {\n      $transaction,\n      auditLog: { create: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue({}) },\n    } as never;\n\n    await expect(\n      approveItem(prisma, \"item-1\", compliance, \"looks fine\", {\n        verification: new MockVerificationAdapter(),\n      })\n    ).rejects.toBeInstanceOf(SelfReviewError);\n\n    // Self-review is detected before any state mutation.\n    expect(tx.reviewQueueItem.update).not.toHaveBeenCalled();\n    expect(tx.announcement.update).not.toHaveBeenCalled();\n  });\n});\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null,"userModified":false},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"cf63cf0b-80c5-4eb0-84f0-d4119fae5ab1","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}
{"parentUuid":"624c5b6d-a318-411c-b68c-ec55cdbcc6f5","isSidechain":false,"message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-7","id":"msg_01W4VoriZaRXpDwhUUYj56CR","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_014RiPqWPnseXFR4Ha74eTmj","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment/admin-panel/__tests__/workflow-transactional.test.ts","content":"import { describe, it, expect, vi } from \"vitest\";\n\nvi.mock(\"@prisma/client\", () => ({\n  PrismaClient: class {},\n  Role: {\n    ADMIN: \"ADMIN\",\n    CONTENT_MANAGER: \"CONTENT_MANAGER\",\n    COMPLIANCE_OFFICER: \"COMPLIANCE_OFFICER\",\n    SUPPORT: \"SUPPORT\",\n    USER: \"USER\",\n  },\n  AnnouncementStatus: {\n    DRAFT: \"DRAFT\",\n    PENDING_REVIEW: \"PENDING_REVIEW\",\n    PUBLISHED: \"PUBLISHED\",\n    REJECTED: \"REJECTED\",\n  },\n}));\n\nvi.mock(\"@/lib/review-queue/src\", () => ({\n  createReviewQueueClient: () => ({\n    listPending: vi.fn(),\n    claimNextItem: vi.fn(),\n    approve: vi.fn(),\n    reject: vi.fn(),\n  }),\n}));\n\nimport { Role } from \"@prisma/client\";\nimport type { SessionUser } from \"@/lib/auth\";\nimport {\n  approveItem,\n  submitForReview,\n} from \"@/services/review-workflow\";\nimport {\n  TransientVerificationError,\n  type VerificationAdapter,\n} from \"@/lib/adapters/verification\";\n\n// A small in-memory transaction simulator: writes are staged and only flushed\n// to `committed` if the callback resolves; on throw, staged writes are\n// dropped — modelling the rollback semantics our service relies on.\nfunction buildPrismaMock() {\n  const committed: Array<[string, unknown]> = [];\n  let staged: Array<[string, unknown]> = [];\n\n  const stage = (op: string) =>\n    vi.fn().mockImplementation(async (args: unknown) => {\n      staged.push([op, args]);\n      // Return something shaped like a Prisma result for the create / update sites.\n      return { id: `mock-${committed.length + staged.length}`, ...((args as { data?: unknown })?.data ?? {}) };\n    });\n\n  const tx = {\n    announcement: {\n      findUnique: vi.fn(),\n      update: stage(\"announcement.update\"),\n    },\n    reviewQueueItem: {\n      findUnique: vi.fn(),\n      create: stage(\"reviewQueueItem.create\"),\n      update: stage(\"reviewQueueItem.update\"),\n    },\n    auditLog: {\n      create: stage(\"auditLog.create\"),\n    },\n  };\n\n  const $transaction = vi.fn().mockImplementation(async (cb: (tx: unknown) => unknown) => {\n    staged = [];\n    try {\n      const result = await cb(tx);\n      committed.push(...staged);\n      staged = [];\n      return result;\n    } catch (err) {\n      staged = []; // rollback\n      throw err;\n    }\n  });\n\n  const auditLogCreateOutsideTx = vi.fn().mockResolvedValue({});\n\n  const prisma = {\n    $transaction,\n    auditLog: { create: auditLogCreateOutsideTx },\n  };\n\n  return { prisma, tx, committed, auditLogCreateOutsideTx };\n}\n\ndescribe(\"review-workflow — transactional rollback\", () => {\n  it(\"submitForReview rolls back when an audit write inside the tx throws — no announcement state change is committed\", async () => {\n    const { prisma, tx, committed } = buildPrismaMock();\n\n    tx.announcement.findUnique.mockResolvedValue({\n      id: \"ann-1\",\n      status: \"DRAFT\",\n      deletedAt: null,\n    });\n    // First create call (review queue item) succeeds; first audit fails.\n    tx.auditLog.create.mockRejectedValueOnce(new Error(\"audit DB down\"));\n\n    const user: SessionUser = {\n      id: \"user-cm\",\n      email: \"cm@x\",\n      name: \"CM\",\n      role: Role.CONTENT_MANAGER,\n    };\n\n    await expect(\n      submitForReview(prisma as never, \"ann-1\", user)\n    ).rejects.toThrow(\"audit DB down\");\n\n    // Tx rolled back — nothing committed.\n    expect(committed).toEqual([]);\n  });\n\n  it(\"approveItem leaves announcement state unchanged when verification throws and writes a best-effort transport audit outside the failed tx\", async () => {\n    const { prisma, tx, committed, auditLogCreateOutsideTx } = buildPrismaMock();\n\n    const reviewer: SessionUser = {\n      id: \"user-r\",\n      email: \"r@x\",\n      name: \"R\",\n      role: Role.COMPLIANCE_OFFICER,\n    };\n\n    tx.reviewQueueItem.findUnique.mockResolvedValue({\n      id: \"item-1\",\n      announcementId: \"ann-1\",\n      authorId: \"author-other\",\n      claimedBy: reviewer.id,\n      status: \"UNDER_REVIEW\",\n      announcement: {\n        id: \"ann-1\",\n        titleEn: \"t\",\n        titleAr: \"ت\",\n        bodyEn: \"b\",\n        bodyAr: \"ب\",\n      },\n    });\n\n    const verification: VerificationAdapter = {\n      verify: vi\n        .fn()\n        .mockRejectedValue(new TransientVerificationError(\"upstream down\")),\n    };\n\n    await expect(\n      approveItem(prisma as never, \"item-1\", reviewer, \"ok\", { verification })\n    ).rejects.toBeInstanceOf(TransientVerificationError);\n\n    // Tx rolled back — no committed writes (queue update + announcement update + audit all dropped).\n    expect(committed).toEqual([]);\n\n    // Best-effort transport audit lives OUTSIDE the failed tx.\n    expect(auditLogCreateOutsideTx).toHaveBeenCalledWith(\n      expect.objectContaining({\n        data: expect.objectContaining({\n          action: \"VERIFICATION_TRANSPORT_FAILED\",\n          entityId: \"item-1\",\n          announcementId: \"ann-1\",\n          userId: reviewer.id,\n        }),\n      })\n    );\n  });\n});\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":"tool_use","stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1779,"cache_read_input_tokens":147135,"output_tokens":2193,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":1779,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[{"input_tokens":1,"output_tokens":2193,"cache_read_input_tokens":147135,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1779,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":1779},"type":"message"}],"speed":"standard"}},"requestId":"req_011CamEb2EpEC1rKnDo3DDTr","type":"assistant","uuid":"62af182e-7654-48bb-8fd5-692c1f4ad660","timestamp":"2026-05-06T11:27:47.565Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}

state unchanged when verification throws and writes a best-effort transport audit outside the failed tx\", async () => {\n    const { prisma, tx, committed, auditLogCreateOutsideTx } = buildPrismaMock();\n\n    const reviewer: SessionUser = {\n      id: \"user-r\",\n      email: \"r@x\",\n      name: \"R\",\n      role: Role.COMPLIANCE_OFFICER,\n    };\n\n    tx.reviewQueueItem.findUnique.mockResolvedValue({\n      id: \"item-1\",\n      announcementId: \"ann-1\",\n      authorId: \"author-other\",\n      claimedBy: reviewer.id,\n      status: \"UNDER_REVIEW\",\n      announcement: {\n        id: \"ann-1\",\n        titleEn: \"t\",\n        titleAr: \"ت\",\n        bodyEn: \"b\",\n        bodyAr: \"ب\",\n      },\n    });\n\n    const verification: VerificationAdapter = {\n      verify: vi\n        .fn()\n        .mockRejectedValue(new TransientVerificationError(\"upstream down\")),\n    };\n\n    await expect(\n      approveItem(prisma as never, \"item-1\", reviewer, \"ok\", { verification })\n    ).rejects.toBeInstanceOf(TransientVerificationError);\n\n    // Tx rolled back — no committed writes (queue update + announcement update + audit all dropped).\n    expect(committed).toEqual([]);\n\n    // Best-effort transport audit lives OUTSIDE the failed tx.\n    expect(auditLogCreateOutsideTx).toHaveBeenCalledWith(\n      expect.objectContaining({\n        data: expect.objectContaining({\n          action: \"VERIFICATION_TRANSPORT_FAILED\",\n          entityId: \"item-1\",\n          announcementId: \"ann-1\",\n          userId: reviewer.id,\n        }),\n      })\n    );\n  });\n});\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null,"userModified":false},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"62af182e-7654-48bb-8fd5-692c1f4ad660","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}

s/SubmitForReviewButton.tsx":{"backupFileName":"b5a26ba6414f0197@v2","version":2,"backupTime":"2026-05-06T11:23:48.349Z"},"nodejs-nextjs-assessment/admin-panel/app/api/review-queue/claim/route.ts":{"backupFileName":"7ea5d15a6ba8bcce@v2","version":2,"backupTime":"2026-05-06T11:23:48.349Z"},"nodejs-nextjs-assessment/admin-panel/lib/validations.ts":{"backupFileName":"1d3637398226a296@v2","version":2,"backupTime":"2026-05-06T11:23:48.349Z"},"nodejs-nextjs-assessment/admin-panel/__tests__/verification-precedence.test.ts":{"backupFileName":null,"version":1,"backupTime":"2026-05-06T11:26:22.907Z"},"nodejs-nextjs-assessment/admin-panel/__tests__/verification-breaker.test.ts":{"backupFileName":null,"version":1,"backupTime":"2026-05-06T11:26:51.374Z"},"nodejs-nextjs-assessment/admin-panel/__tests__/workflow-separation-of-duties.test.ts":{"backupFileName":null,"version":1,"backupTime":"2026-05-06T11:27:20.749Z"},"nodejs-nextjs-assessment/admin-panel/__tests__/workflow-transactional.test.ts":{"backupFileName":null,"version":1,"backupTime":"2026-05-06T11:27:47.592Z"},"nodejs-nextjs-assessment/README.md":{"backupFileName":"0baeeb17865ef0f2@v1","version":1,"backupTime":"2026-05-06T11:29:28.512Z"}},"timestamp":"2026-05-06T11:23:48.349Z"},"isSnapshotUpdate":true}
{"parentUuid":"ac342ccd-15b4-49c7-9131-83d24d95b846","isSidechain":false,"message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-7","id":"msg_01Yab29C8RRg6NCqaUJxYQv6","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01JdPUSjYCvTRPqaunV5Jgcn","name":"Edit","input":{"replace_all":false,"file_path":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment/README.md","old_string":"- http://localhost:3000/api/announcements — `POST` create\n- http://localhost:3000/api/announcements/[id] — `PATCH` / `DELETE`\n- http://localhost:3000/api/users — `GET` with `?q=&page=&pageSize=`","new_string":"- http://localhost:3000/api/announcements — `POST` create\n- http://localhost:3000/api/announcements/[id] — `PATCH` / `DELETE`\n- http://localhost:3000/api/users — `GET` with `?q=&page=&pageSize=`\n\n## Level 2 — Review workflow, verification, race fix\n\nLevel 2 layers a multi-tier review workflow, an external verification adapter, and an in-place race fix on the seeded `@assessment/review-queue` library.\n\n### Run (Level 2)\n\n```bash\ncd admin-panel && npm install && cp .env.example .env\nnpx prisma migrate deploy   # or: npx prisma migrate dev\nnpm run db:seed\nnpm run dev\n```\n\n`db:seed` upserts four users:\n\n- `admin@example.com` (ADMIN)\n- `cm@example.com` (CONTENT_MANAGER)\n- `compliance@example.com` (COMPLIANCE_OFFICER) — **new in Level 2**\n- `support@example.com` (SUPPORT)\n\nSwitch the active session by editing `MOCK_USER_ROLE` (and `MOCK_USER_ID` / `MOCK_USER_EMAIL` / `MOCK_USER_NAME`) in `.env`.\n\n### Environment variables\n\n| Variable | Purpose |\n| --- | --- |\n| `DATABASE_URL` | PostgreSQL connection string. Required for the app, the seed, and the concurrent-claim integration test. |\n| `MOCK_USER_ID` / `MOCK_USER_EMAIL` / `MOCK_USER_NAME` / `MOCK_USER_ROLE` | Mock session — Level 1 pattern. `MOCK_USER_ROLE` controls what the UI/RBAC sees. |\n\n### New routes\n\n- `POST /api/announcements/[id]/submit-for-review` — ADMIN / CONTENT_MANAGER. Moves a `DRAFT` announcement to `PENDING_REVIEW` and creates a `ReviewQueueItem` (tier 1).\n- `GET /api/review-queue` — ADMIN / COMPLIANCE_OFFICER. Lists pending items the caller is allowed to claim (excludes self-authored).\n- `POST /api/review-queue/claim` — ADMIN / COMPLIANCE_OFFICER. Claims the next non-self-authored pending item (`SELECT … FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`). Returns `{ kind: 'claimed', item }` or `{ kind: 'empty' }`.\n- `POST /api/review-queue/[id]/approve` — body `{ note }`. Runs the verification adapter inside a single transaction: APPROVED → announcement `PUBLISHED`; REJECTED → announcement back to `DRAFT`; transport / breaker error → tx rolls back, best-effort `VERIFICATION_TRANSPORT_FAILED` audit, 503 to caller.\n- `POST /api/review-queue/[id]/reject` — body `{ reason }`. Item → `REJECTED`, announcement → `DRAFT`, audit + notification stub.\n- `POST /api/review-queue/escalate` — ADMIN. Bumps `tier` to 2 and stamps `escalatedAt` for unclaimed `PENDING` items older than 48h. Returns `{ escalated: count }`. There is no cron — call this from an external scheduler.\n\n### Verification adapter\n\n`admin-panel/lib/adapters/verification/` ships:\n\n- `MockVerificationAdapter` — used in dev. APPROVES by default; **rejects when `bodyEn` or `bodyAr` contains the literal token `__deny__`**. The approve route wires this in by default, so you can deny end-to-end without any extra config.\n- `LiveVerificationAdapter` — scaffold only. Every wire-touching site is annotated `// TODO SPEC PENDING`. `verify()` throws `Error('TODO SPEC PENDING')`.\n- `CachedRetryingBreakerAdapter` — wraps any inner adapter with:\n  - **Cache** keyed by `announcementId + sha256(titleEn|titleAr|bodyEn|bodyAr).slice(0,16)`. APPROVED entries expire after 5 min; REJECTED entries are sticky (only an explicit body edit invalidates them). Precedence: cached REJECTED beats fresh APPROVED.\n  - **Retry**: 3 attempts, exponential backoff 100/200/400 ms with ±20 % jitter. Only `TransientVerificationError` is retried; `PermanentVerificationError` (e.g. 4xx) fails fast.\n  - **Breaker**: CLOSED → 5 consecutive failures → OPEN → 30 s → HALF_OPEN → 1 success → CLOSED, 1 failure → OPEN. While OPEN, callers receive `TransientVerificationError('breaker open')` without ever touching the inner adapter.\n\n### Tests\n\n```bash\nnpm test              # all suites; the race test only runs when DATABASE_URL is set\nDATABASE_URL=… npm test\n```\n\n- `__tests__/announcements.test.ts` — Level 1 RBAC + create/edit/delete (carry-over).\n- `__tests__/health-bug.test.ts`, `__tests__/users-privacy.test.ts` — Level 1 carry-overs.\n- `__tests__/review-queue-claim-race.test.ts` — **Postgres integration test for Part C**. Seeds one PENDING row, then has 5 concurrent `claimNextItem` callers race on 5 separate `PrismaClient` instances. Asserts exactly one wins and four get `{ kind: 'empty' }`. **Requires `DATABASE_URL`** — the file uses `describe.skipIf(!HAS_DB)` so without a database the suite is skipped (pure mocks cannot reproduce row-level lock semantics).\n- `__tests__/verification-precedence.test.ts` — cached REJECTED is sticky; an edited body is treated as a new key and accepts a fresh APPROVED.\n- `__tests__/verification-breaker.test.ts` — uses an injectable `Clock` to advance time. Verifies the breaker trips after 5 failures, short-circuits while OPEN, and recovers on HALF_OPEN probe success.\n- `__tests__/workflow-separation-of-duties.test.ts` — the author cannot claim or approve their own item; on a SKIP LOCKED race the row is released back to `PENDING`.\n- `__tests__/workflow-transactional.test.ts` — when an audit write or the verification call throws, the workflow transaction rolls back (no committed writes) and the best-effort `VERIFICATION_TRANSPORT_FAILED` audit is written outside the failed tx.\n\n### Part C — race fix\n\nThe seeded `claimNextItem` did `findFirst` then `update`, leaving a TOCTOU window where two reviewers could both see and update the same row. Fix: a single `prisma.$transaction` runs `SELECT id … FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED LIMIT 1` followed by `update`. The Postgres row-level lock is held across processes and connection pools, and `SKIP LOCKED` makes concurrent claimers pick distinct rows. See `admin-panel/lib/review-queue/src/claim.ts` and the integration test above for repro + assertion."},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":"tool_use","stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":595,"cache_read_input_tokens":152431,"output_tokens":2767,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":595,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[{"input_tokens":1,"output_tokens":2767,"cache_read_input_tokens":152431,"cache_creation_input_tokens":595,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":595},"type":"message"}],"speed":"standard"}},"requestId":"req_011CamEh3ZbjD3v3ZgffETnx","type":"assistant","uuid":"f70ad54f-a638-48ab-8e32-fa8b476c5cb4","timestamp":"2026-05-06T11:29:28.507Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}

Lists pending items the caller is allowed to claim (excludes self-authored).\n- `POST /api/review-queue/claim` — ADMIN / COMPLIANCE_OFFICER. Claims the next non-self-authored pending item (`SELECT … FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`). Returns `{ kind: 'claimed', item }` or `{ kind: 'empty' }`.\n- `POST /api/review-queue/[id]/approve` — body `{ note }`. Runs the verification adapter inside a single transaction: APPROVED → announcement `PUBLISHED`; REJECTED → announcement back to `DRAFT`; transport / breaker error → tx rolls back, best-effort `VERIFICATION_TRANSPORT_FAILED` audit, 503 to caller.\n- `POST /api/review-queue/[id]/reject` — body `{ reason }`. Item → `REJECTED`, announcement → `DRAFT`, audit + notification stub.\n- `POST /api/review-queue/escalate` — ADMIN. Bumps `tier` to 2 and stamps `escalatedAt` for unclaimed `PENDING` items older than 48h. Returns `{ escalated: count }`. There is no cron — call this from an external scheduler.\n\n### Verification adapter\n\n`admin-panel/lib/adapters/verification/` ships:\n\n- `MockVerificationAdapter` — used in dev. APPROVES by default; **rejects when `bodyEn` or `bodyAr` contains the literal token `__deny__`**. The approve route wires this in by default, so you can deny end-to-end without any extra config.\n- `LiveVerificationAdapter` — scaffold only. Every wire-touching site is annotated `// TODO SPEC PENDING`. `verify()` throws `Error('TODO SPEC PENDING')`.\n- `CachedRetryingBreakerAdapter` — wraps any inner adapter with:\n  - **Cache** keyed by `announcementId + sha256(titleEn|titleAr|bodyEn|bodyAr).slice(0,16)`. APPROVED entries expire after 5 min; REJECTED entries are sticky (only an explicit body edit invalidates them). Precedence: cached REJECTED beats fresh APPROVED.\n  - **Retry**: 3 attempts, exponential backoff 100/200/400 ms with ±20 % jitter. Only `TransientVerificationError` is retried; `PermanentVerificationError` (e.g. 4xx) fails fast.\n  - **Breaker**: CLOSED → 5 consecutive failures → OPEN → 30 s → HALF_OPEN → 1 success → CLOSED, 1 failure → OPEN. While OPEN, callers receive `TransientVerificationError('breaker open')` without ever touching the inner adapter.\n\n### Tests\n\n```bash\nnpm test              # all suites; the race test only runs when DATABASE_URL is set\nDATABASE_URL=… npm test\n```\n\n- `__tests__/announcements.test.ts` — Level 1 RBAC + create/edit/delete (carry-over).\n- `__tests__/health-bug.test.ts`, `__tests__/users-privacy.test.ts` — Level 1 carry-overs.\n- `__tests__/review-queue-claim-race.test.ts` — **Postgres integration test for Part C**. Seeds one PENDING row, then has 5 concurrent `claimNextItem` callers race on 5 separate `PrismaClient` instances. Asserts exactly one wins and four get `{ kind: 'empty' }`. **Requires `DATABASE_URL`** — the file uses `describe.skipIf(!HAS_DB)` so without a database the suite is skipped (pure mocks cannot reproduce row-level lock semantics).\n- `__tests__/verification-precedence.test.ts` — cached REJECTED is sticky; an edited body is treated as a new key and accepts a fresh APPROVED.\n- `__tests__/verification-breaker.test.ts` — uses an injectable `Clock` to advance time. Verifies the breaker trips after 5 failures, short-circuits while OPEN, and recovers on HALF_OPEN probe success.\n- `__tests__/workflow-separation-of-duties.test.ts` — the author cannot claim or approve their own item; on a SKIP LOCKED race the row is released back to `PENDING`.\n- `__tests__/workflow-transactional.test.ts` — when an audit write or the verification call throws, the workflow transaction rolls back (no committed writes) and the best-effort `VERIFICATION_TRANSPORT_FAILED` audit is written outside the failed tx.\n\n### Part C — race fix\n\nThe seeded `claimNextItem` did `findFirst` then `update`, leaving a TOCTOU window where two reviewers could both see and update the same row. Fix: a single `prisma.$transaction` runs `SELECT id … FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED LIMIT 1` followed by `update`. The Postgres row-level lock is held across processes and connection pools, and `SKIP LOCKED` makes concurrent claimers pick distinct rows. See `admin-panel/lib/review-queue/src/claim.ts` and the integration test above for repro + assertion.","originalFile":"# Node.js / Next.js Level 1 Assessment\n\nAdmin-panel feature set on Next.js App Router with Prisma, PostgreSQL, Zod, Vitest, and RBAC.\n\n## Run\n\n```bash\ncd admin-panel && cp .env.example .env && npx prisma migrate dev && npm run dev\n```\n\n## Routes (after `npm run dev`)\n\n- http://localhost:3000/admin — redirects to `/admin/announcements`\n- http://localhost:3000/admin/announcements — list (non-deleted, paginated)\n- http://localhost:3000/admin/announcements/new — bilingual create form\n- http://localhost:3000/admin/announcements/[id]/edit — edit + soft-delete\n- http://localhost:3000/admin/users — user lookup (SUPPORT: 4 cols, ADMIN: 7)\n- http://localhost:3000/api/health — `{ status: \"ok\" }`\n- http://localhost:3000/api/health?include=latest — latest announcement or `{ latest: null }`\n- http://localhost:3000/api/announcements — `POST` create\n- http://localhost:3000/api/announcements/[id] — `PATCH` / `DELETE`\n- http://localhost:3000/api/users — `GET` with `?q=&page=&pageSize=`\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":20,"oldLines":3,"newStart":20,"newLines":71,"lines":[" - http://localhost:3000/api/announcements — `POST` create"," - http://localhost:3000/api/announcements/[id] — `PATCH` / `DELETE`"," - http://localhost:3000/api/users — `GET` with `?q=&page=&pageSize=`","+","+## Level 2 — Review workflow, verification, race fix","+","+Level 2 layers a multi-tier review workflow, an external verification adapter, and an in-place race fix on the seeded `@assessment/review-queue` library.","+","+### Run (Level 2)","+","+```bash","+cd admin-panel && npm install && cp .env.example .env","+npx prisma migrate deploy   # or: npx prisma migrate dev","+npm run db:seed","+npm run dev","+```","+","+`db:seed` upserts four users:","+","+- `admin@example.com` (ADMIN)","+- `cm@example.com` (CONTENT_MANAGER)","+- `compliance@example.com` (COMPLIANCE_OFFICER) — **new in Level 2**","+- `support@example.com` (SUPPORT)","+","+Switch the active session by editing `MOCK_USER_ROLE` (and `MOCK_USER_ID` / `MOCK_USER_EMAIL` / `MOCK_USER_NAME`) in `.env`.","+","+### Environment variables","+","+| Variable | Purpose |","+| --- | --- |","+| `DATABASE_URL` | PostgreSQL connection string. Required for the app, the seed, and the concurrent-claim integration test. |","+| `MOCK_USER_ID` / `MOCK_USER_EMAIL` / `MOCK_USER_NAME` / `MOCK_USER_ROLE` | Mock session — Level 1 pattern. `MOCK_USER_ROLE` controls what the UI/RBAC sees. |","+","+### New routes","+","+- `POST /api/announcements/[id]/submit-for-review` — ADMIN / CONTENT_MANAGER. APPROVES by default; **rejects when `bodyEn` or `bodyAr` contains the literal token `__deny__`**. The approve route wires this in by default, so you can deny end-to-end without any extra config.","+- `LiveVerificationAdapter` — scaffold only. Every wire-touching site is annotated `// TODO SPEC PENDING`. `verify()` throws `Error('TODO SPEC PENDING')`.","+- `CachedRetryingBreakerAdapter` — wraps any inner adapter with:","+  - **Cache** keyed by `announcementId + sha256(titleEn|titleAr|bodyEn|bodyAr).slice(0,16)`. APPROVED entries expire after 5 min; REJECTED entries are sticky (only an explicit body edit invalidates them). Precedence: cached REJECTED beats fresh APPROVED.","+  - **Retry**: 3 attempts, exponential backoff 100/200/400 ms with ±20 % jitter. Only `TransientVerificationError` is retried; `PermanentVerificationError` (e.g. 4xx) fails fast.","+  - **Breaker**: CLOSED → 5 consecutive failures → OPEN → 30 s → HALF_OPEN → 1 success → CLOSED, 1 failure → OPEN. While OPEN, callers receive `TransientVerificationError('breaker open')` without ever touching the inner adapter.","+","+### Tests","+","+```bash","+npm test              # all suites; the race test only runs when DATABASE_URL is set","+DATABASE_URL=… npm test","+```","+","+- `__tests__/announcements.test.ts` — Level 1 RBAC + create/edit/delete (carry-over).","+- `__tests__/health-bug.test.ts`, `__tests__/users-privacy.test.ts` — Level 1 carry-overs.","+- `__tests__/review-queue-claim-race.test.ts` — **Postgres integration test for Part C**. Seeds one PENDING row, then has 5 concurrent `claimNextItem` callers race on 5 separate `PrismaClient` instances. Asserts exactly one wins and four get `{ kind: 'empty' }`. **Requires `DATABASE_URL`** — the file uses `describe.skipIf(!HAS_DB)` so without a database the suite is skipped (pure mocks cannot reproduce row-level lock semantics).","+- `__tests__/verification-precedence.test.ts` — cached REJECTED is sticky; an edited body is treated as a new key and accepts a fresh APPROVED.","+- `__tests__/verification-breaker.test.ts` — uses an injectable `Clock` to advance time. Verifies the breaker trips after 5 failures, short-circuits while OPEN, and recovers on HALF_OPEN probe success.","+- `__tests__/workflow-separation-of-duties.test.ts` — the author cannot claim or approve their own item; on a SKIP LOCKED race the row is released back to `PENDING`.","+- `__tests__/workflow-transactional.test.ts` — when an audit write or the verification call throws, the workflow transaction rolls back (no committed writes) and the best-effort `VERIFICATION_TRANSPORT_FAILED` audit is written outside the failed tx.","+","+### Part C — race fix","+","+The seeded `claimNextItem` did `findFirst` then `update`, leaving a TOCTOU window where two reviewers could both see and update the same row. Fix: a single `prisma.$transaction` runs `SELECT id … FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED LIMIT 1` followed by `update`. The Postgres row-level lock is held across processes and connection pools, and `SKIP LOCKED` makes concurrent claimers pick distinct rows. See `admin-panel/lib/review-queue/src/claim.ts` and the integration test above for repro + assertion."]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"f70ad54f-a638-48ab-8e32-fa8b476c5cb4","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}

{"parentUuid":"2f9c7c06-5cb6-43de-8f0b-003d429e00fb","isSidechain":false,"message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-7","id":"msg_01DJ2DaP2kCTrc1WXpY22Pzq","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01V694YT8xrh9wM8M1huamwi","name":"Edit","input":{"replace_all":false,"file_path":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment/README.md","old_string":"### Run (Level 2)\n\n```bash\ncd admin-panel && npm install && cp .env.example .env\nnpx prisma migrate deploy   # or: npx prisma migrate dev\nnpm run db:seed\nnpm run dev\n```","new_string":"### Run (Level 2)\n\n```bash\ncd admin-panel && npm install\n# Create .env (see \"Environment variables\" below) — DATABASE_URL + MOCK_USER_*\nnpx prisma migrate deploy   # or: npx prisma migrate dev\nnpm run db:seed\nnpm run dev\n```"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":"tool_use","stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":312,"cache_read_input_tokens":156453,"output_tokens":564,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":312,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[{"input_tokens":1,"output_tokens":564,"cache_read_input_tokens":156453,"cache_creation_input_tokens":312,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":312},"type":"message"}],"speed":"standard"}},"requestId":"req_011CamEmAdsBBdvZdnwa4v8R","type":"assistant","uuid":"69bd213e-0f52-4c51-a945-f727a5548bf5","timestamp":"2026-05-06T11:29:50.943Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}
{"parentUuid":"69bd213e-0f52-4c51-a945-f727a5548bf5","isSidechain":false,"promptId":"012390d8-d956-4ff3-97f4-989553cc9c8d","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01V694YT8xrh9wM8M1huamwi","type":"tool_result","content":"The file /home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment/README.md has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)"}]},"uuid":"0fb94c66-7005-42fc-b938-c90bb6fc4d5e","timestamp":"2026-05-06T11:29:51.956Z","toolUseResult":{"filePath":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment/README.md","oldString":"### Run (Level 2)\n\n```bash\ncd admin-panel && npm install && cp .env.example .env\nnpx prisma migrate deploy   # or: npx prisma migrate dev\nnpm run db:seed\nnpm run dev\n```","newString":"### Run (Level 2)\n\n```bash\ncd admin-panel && npm install\n# Create .env (see \"Environment variables\" below) — DATABASE_URL + MOCK_USER_*\nnpx prisma migrate deploy   # or: npx prisma migrate dev\nnpm run db:seed\nnpm run dev\n```","originalFile":"# Node.js / Next.js Level 1 Assessment\n\nAdmin-panel feature set on Next.js App Router with Prisma, PostgreSQL, Zod, Vitest, and RBAC.\n\n## Run\n\n```bash\ncd admin-panel && cp .env.example .env && npx prisma migrate dev && npm run dev\n```\n\n## Routes (after `npm run dev`)\n\n- http://localhost:3000/admin — redirects to `/admin/announcements`\n- http://localhost:3000/admin/announcements — list (non-deleted, paginated)\n- http://localhost:3000/admin/announcements/new — bilingual create form\n- http://localhost:3000/admin/announcements/[id]/edit — edit + soft-delete\n- http://localhost:3000/admin/users — user lookup (SUPPORT: 4 cols, ADMIN: 7)\n- http://localhost:3000/api/health — `{ status: \"ok\" }`\n- http://localhost:3000/api/health?include=latest — latest announcement or `{ latest: null }`\n- http://localhost:3000/api/announcements — `POST` create\n- http://localhost:3000/api/announcements/[id] — `PATCH` / `DELETE`\n- http://localhost:3000/api/users — `GET` with `?q=&page=&pageSize=`\n\n## Level 2 — Review workflow, verification, race fix\n\nLevel 2 layers a multi-tier review workflow, an external verification adapter, and an in-place race fix on the seeded `@assessment/review-queue` library.\n\n### Run (Level 2)\n\n```bash\ncd admin-panel && npm install && cp .env.example .env\nnpx prisma migrate deploy   # or: npx prisma migrate dev\nnpm run db:seed\nnpm run dev\n```\n\n`db:seed` upserts four users:\n\n- `admin@example.com` (ADMIN)\n- `cm@example.com` (CONTENT_MANAGER)\n- `compliance@example.com` (COMPLIANCE_OFFICER) — **new in Level 2**\n- `support@example.com` (SUPPORT)\n\nSwitch the active session by editing `MOCK_USER_ROLE` (and `MOCK_USER_ID` / `MOCK_USER_EMAIL` / `MOCK_USER_NAME`) in `.env`.\n\n### Environment variables\n\n| Variable | Purpose |\n| --- | --- |\n| `DATABASE_URL` | PostgreSQL connection string. Required for the app, the seed, and the concurrent-claim integration test. |\n| `MOCK_USER_ID` / `MOCK_USER_EMAIL` / `MOCK_USER_NAME` / `MOCK_USER_ROLE` | Mock session — Level 1 pattern. `MOCK_USER_ROLE` controls what the UI/RBAC sees. |\n\n### New routes\n\n- `POST /api/announcements/[id]/submit-for-review` — ADMIN / CONTENT_MANAGER. Moves a `DRAFT` announcement to `PENDING_REVIEW` and creates a `ReviewQueueItem` (tier 1).\n- `GET /api/review-queue` — ADMIN / COMPLIANCE_OFFICER. Lists pending items the caller is allowed to claim (excludes self-authored).\n- `POST /api/review-queue/claim` — ADMIN / COMPLIANCE_OFFICER. Claims the next non-self-authored pending item (`SELECT … FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`). Returns `{ kind: 'claimed', item }` or `{ kind: 'empty' }`.\n- `POST /api/review-queue/[id]/approve` — body `{ note }`. Runs the verification adapter inside a single transaction: APPROVED → announcement `PUBLISHED`; REJECTED → announcement back to `DRAFT`; transport / breaker error → tx rolls back, best-effort `VERIFICATION_TRANSPORT_FAILED` audit, 503 to caller.\n- `POST /api/review-queue/[id]/reject` — body `{ reason }`. Item → `REJECTED`, announcement → `DRAFT`, audit + notification stub.\n- `POST /api/review-queue/escalate` — ADMIN. Bumps `tier` to 2 and stamps `escalatedAt` for unclaimed `PENDING` items older than 48h. Returns `{ escalated: count }`. There is no cron — call this from an external scheduler.\n\n### Verification adapter\n\n`admin-panel/lib/adapters/verification/` ships:\n\n- `MockVerificationAdapter` — used in dev. APPROVES by default; **rejects when `bodyEn` or `bodyAr` contains the literal token `__deny__`**. The approve route wires this in by default, so you can deny end-to-end without any extra config.\n- `LiveVerificationAdapter` — scaffold only. Every wire-touching site is annotated `// TODO SPEC PENDING`. `verify()` throws `Error('TODO SPEC PENDING')`.\n- `CachedRetryingBreakerAdapter` — wraps any inner adapter with:\n  - **Cache** keyed by `announcementId + sha256(titleEn|titleAr|bodyEn|bodyAr).slice(0,16)`. APPROVED entries expire after 5 min; REJECTED entries are sticky (only an explicit body edit invalidates them). Precedence: cached REJECTED beats fresh APPROVED.\n  - **Retry**: 3 attempts, exponential backoff 100/200/400 ms with ±20 % jitter. Only `TransientVerificationError` is retried; `PermanentVerificationError` (e.g. 4xx) fails fast.\n  - **Breaker**: CLOSED → 5 consecutive failures → OPEN → 30 s → HALF_OPEN → 1 success → CLOSED, 1 failure → OPEN. While OPEN, callers receive `TransientVerificationError('breaker open')` without ever touching the inner adapter.\n\n### Tests\n\n```bash\nnpm test              # all suites; the race test only runs when DATABASE_URL is set\nDATABASE_URL=… npm test\n```\n\n- `__tests__/announcements.test.ts` — Level 1 RBAC + create/edit/delete (carry-over).\n- `__tests__/health-bug.test.ts`, `__tests__/users-privacy.test.ts` — Level 1 carry-overs.\n- `__tests__/review-queue-claim-race.test.ts` — **Postgres integration test for Part C**. Seeds one PENDING row, then has 5 concurrent `claimNextItem` callers race on 5 separate `PrismaClient` instances. Asserts exactly one wins and four get `{ kind: 'empty' }`. **Requires `DATABASE_URL`** — the file uses `describe.skipIf(!HAS_DB)` so without a database the suite is skipped (pure mocks cannot reproduce row-level lock semantics).\n- `__tests__/verification-precedence.test.ts` — cached REJECTED is sticky; an edited body is treated as a new key and accepts a fresh APPROVED.\n- `__tests__/verification-breaker.test.ts` — uses an injectable `Clock` to advance time. Verifies the breaker trips after 5 failures, short-circuits while OPEN, and recovers on HALF_OPEN probe success.\n- `__tests__/workflow-separation-of-duties.test.ts` — the author cannot claim or approve their own item; on a SKIP LOCKED race the row is released back to `PENDING`.\n- `__tests__/workflow-transactional.test.ts` — when an audit write or the verification call throws, the workflow transaction rolls back (no committed writes) and the best-effort `VERIFICATION_TRANSPORT_FAILED` audit is written outside the failed tx.\n\n### Part C — race fix\n\nThe seeded `claimNextItem` did `findFirst` then `update`, leaving a TOCTOU window where two reviewers could both see and update the same row. Fix: a single `prisma.$transaction` runs `SELECT id … FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED LIMIT 1` followed by `update`. The Postgres row-level lock is held across processes and connection pools, and `SKIP LOCKED` makes concurrent claimers pick distinct rows. See `admin-panel/lib/review-queue/src/claim.ts` and the integration test above for repro + assertion.\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":28,"oldLines":7,"newStart":28,"newLines":8,"lines":[" ### Run (Level 2)"," "," ```bash","-cd admin-panel && npm install && cp .env.example .env","+cd admin-panel && npm install","+# Create .env (see \"Environment variables\" below) — DATABASE_URL + MOCK_USER_*"," npx prisma migrate deploy   # or: npx prisma migrate dev"," npm run db:seed"," npm run dev"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"69bd213e-0f52-4c51-a945-f727a5548bf5","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}
