drop admin-panel/README\n07d1a68 chore: remove assessment brief HTML from repo\ne57d4c1 feat: scaffold — schema, helpers, validations\nff331a4 Fix: guard null before destructuring in /api/health; add POSTMORTEM","stderr":"","interrupted":false,"isImage":false,"noOutputExpected":false},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"25c75169-4f31-4710-ad34-2dc0464e4352","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}
{"parentUuid":"072f3186-fcbf-4049-a2e7-2e87bab28118","isSidechain":false,"message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-7","id":"msg_01TnonmeKTEErie4bxAK8rmC","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Both Steps 12 and 13 done.\n\n**Step 12 — tests** (commit `f614c89`):\n- [verification-precedence.test.ts](admin-panel/__tests__/verification-precedence.test.ts) — uses a per-call queueable `StubAdapter`. First call returns REJECTED → cached. Second call with same content returns REJECTED (cache hit, inner not called — `inner.calls === 1`). Negative case: editing `bodyEn` produces a different cache key, so a new APPROVED is honoured.\n- [verification-breaker.test.ts](admin-panel/__tests__/verification-breaker.test.ts) — injectable `Clock` whose `sleep` and `advance` mutate `state.value`. Test 1: 5 failed verifies (with `random: () => 0.5` so no jitter) → 15 inner calls (3 retries × 5) → 6th verify rejects with `breaker open` and inner is NOT called. Test 2: at 29 s past the open it's still OPEN; at 31 s the next call probes (HALF_OPEN), succeeds, breaker → CLOSED, subsequent call also goes through.\n- [workflow-separation-of-duties.test.ts](admin-panel/__tests__/workflow-separation-of-duties.test.ts) — mocks `@/lib/review-queue/src` to simulate a SKIP LOCKED race grabbing a self-authored row. Asserts `claimNext` throws `SelfReviewError` and `prisma.reviewQueueItem.update` was called to release the row back to PENDING. Second test: `approveItem` short-circuits with `SelfReviewError` before any state mutation when `item.authorId === reviewer.id`.\n- [workflow-transactional.test.ts](admin-panel/__tests__/workflow-transactional.test.ts) — `buildPrismaMock()` simulates rollback by buffering writes inside `$transaction` and dropping them on throw. (1) `submitForReview` with a failing audit write → throws \"audit DB down\" and `committed === []`. (2) `approveItem` with verification rejecting `TransientVerificationError` → throws, `committed === []`, and a `VERIFICATION_TRANSPORT_FAILED` audit was created via the *outside-tx* `prisma.auditLog.create`.\n- **`npm test` — 8 files / 31 tests passed** (was 4/23).\n\n**Step 13 — README** (commit `51c21fa`):\n- Added a \"Level 2 — Review workflow, verification, race fix\" section with the run flow (`npm install` + `.env` + `migrate deploy` + `db:seed` + `dev`), the four seeded users (calls out the new `compliance@example.com`), the env var table, the six new routes with their RBAC + behaviour summary, the verification adapter trio (mock with `__deny__` token / live with `TODO SPEC PENDING` / wrapper with cache + retry + breaker), the test layout including the `DATABASE_URL`-required race test, and a one-paragraph Part C explanation pointing at `claim.ts`.\n- Adjusted: skipped the `cp .env.example .env` line because that file doesn't exist in this repo — the section now points back to the env-var table for what to put in `.env`.\n\n**Branch state — all assessment-2 commits**:\n```\n51c21fa docs: update README with Level 2 instructions\nf614c89 test: verification precedence, breaker, separation-of-duties, transactional rollback\n5d423fb feat(ui): review queue page and submit-for-review button\n94560ab feat(api): review-queue routes and submit-for-review\n0ee857f feat(workflow): review-workflow service layer\n47b1d00 feat(verification): adapter interface, mock, live scaffold, retry+breaker+cache\na3d2054 feat(schema): add AnnouncementStatus, COMPLIANCE_OFFICER, RBAC actions\n313365d fix(review-queue): close TOCTOU race in claimNextItem with FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED\nb871372 test(review-queue): concurrent claim test reproduces TOCTOU race\n24ee099 feat(schema): add ReviewQueueItem model for seeded library\naafabc0 install seeded review-queue\n```\n\nTwo open notes:\n1. I have *not* yet started the dev server / browsed the UI end-to-end (login as `cm`, submit, login as `compliance`, claim/approve). Want me to do that now?\n2. The transcript (`/transcript` → `ai-session/transcript.md`) hasn't been generated yet — that should be the final action of this session per the brief.\n\nConfirm next step."}],"stop_reason":"end_turn","stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":536,"cache_read_input_tokens":157792,"output_tokens":1563,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":536,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[{"input_tokens":1,"output_tokens":1563,"cache_read_input_tokens":157792,"cache_creation_input_tokens":536,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":536},"type":"message"}],"speed":"standard"}},"requestId":"req_011CamEp3kxQAgiY2xkmEc7A","type":"assistant","uuid":"cdbe4a3b-126a-42c2-9b10-07f0d0e10665","timestamp":"2026-05-06T11:30:48.865Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}

{"parentUuid":"b4754b37-c29d-4680-b821-723815fa540b","isSidechain":false,"message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-7","id":"msg_01PcrCN4D2NEFSZvny1Ud5Kx","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Status confirmed — that matches my view of the branch.\n\nSuggested order for the three remaining items:\n\n1. **UI smoke test** first — best done before the transcript so the transcript records actual results.\n2. **transcript.md** next-to-last — per the brief, `/transcript` should be the final authoring action and not edited afterwards.\n3. **git push** last.\n\nFor the smoke test, my real constraint is that I can't drive a browser DOM from here. The closest equivalent is starting `npm run dev` in the background and exercising the routes via `curl` — flipping `MOCK_USER_*` in `.env` between `cm@example.com` and `compliance@example.com` to walk the full path:\n\n- `cm` creates a DRAFT → submits for review → queue item appears\n- `compliance` lists queue → claims → approves (and a separate run with `__deny__` body to exercise the REJECTED branch)\n- A negative pass: `compliance` tries to claim something they authored → 403 `SELF_REVIEW`\n- Verify audit chain in DB\n\nWant me to do it that way (API smoke via curl + DB inspection), or would you rather do the browser pass yourself and I just go straight to the transcript?"}],"stop_reason":"end_turn","stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":6,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1725,"cache_read_input_tokens":158328,"output_tokens":1340,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":1725,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[{"input_tokens":6,"output_tokens":1340,"cache_read_input_tokens":158328,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1725,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":1725},"type":"message"}],"speed":"standard"}},"requestId":"req_011CamEuxd4H7nbngF8vHV6v","type":"assistant","uuid":"0eca39e2-dcf9-425e-af3b-ccf591ba4f61","timestamp":"2026-05-06T11:32:04.675Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}

Two final steps.\n\nStep 14 — Quick UI smoke test:\nStart the dev server if not already running.\nThen verify this golden path manually:\n\n1. Set .env MOCK_USER to cm@example.com (CONTENT_MANAGER)\n   - Go to /admin/announcements\n   - Confirm \"Submit for Review\" button appears on a DRAFT announcement\n   - Click it → status should change to PENDING_REVIEW\n\n2. Set .env MOCK_USER to compliance@example.com (COMPLIANCE_OFFICER)\n   - Go to /admin/review-queue\n   - Confirm the pending item appears\n   - Click \"Claim Next\" → item moves to UNDER_REVIEW\n   - Click \"Approve\" → announcement moves to PUBLISHED\n\n3. Repeat with a body containing __deny__\n   - After approve → announcement returns to DRAFT (mock rejection)\n\nReport what you see. If anything breaks, fix it.\n\nStep 15 — Generate transcript (FINAL STEP):\nThis is the last action of the session.\nRun: /transcript\nThis will create ai-session/transcript.md with the verbatim session.\nDo NOT edit the file after it is generated.\n\nThen run:\ngit add ai-session/transcript.md\ngit commit -m \"chore: write verbatim ai session transcript\"\ngit push origin assessment-2\n\nThen show me the branch URL to send to the reviewer."}]},"uuid":"f0a225bb-fd70-469a-b84c-b03c04bb2006","timestamp":"2026-05-06T11:32:04.716Z","permissionMode":"auto","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}

{"parentUuid":"ab39718a-4c8b-44ec-90bc-76d1737e539c","isSidechain":false,"message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-7","id":"msg_01TuxoLtE3brXZrRdB2aP84t","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01AwCpEs6CoNbMCRMAiKpudw","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"npx tsx -e \"\nimport 'dotenv/config';\nimport { PrismaClient } from '@prisma/client';\nimport { PrismaPg } from '@prisma/adapter-pg';\nconst p = new PrismaClient({ adapter: new PrismaPg({ connectionString: process.env.DATABASE_URL }) });\n(async () => {\n  const anns = await p.announcement.findMany({ where: { id: { in: ['cmotwu4h70004tgekijnfjz6j','cmotwu4h70003tgekkrbxv9nq'] } }, select: { id:true, titleEn:true, status:true, publishedAt:true } });\n  const queue = await p.reviewQueueItem.findMany({ select: { id:true, announcementId:true, status:true, claimedBy:true, decisionNote:true } });\n  const audit = await p.auditLog.findMany({ where: { action: { in: Content is best-effort recall from this session, not byte-for-byte verbatim. Disclosed per assessment rules.\n\n## Brief Ambiguities Resolved\n1. Separation of duties: enforced at submit + claim + approve. No admin override.\n2. Deadline: PENDING + claimedBy=null + createdAt < now-48h → tier=2 + escalatedAt\n3. Verification retry: 3 attempts, exponential backoff 100/200/400ms ±20% jitter, transient only\n4. Audit on verification failure: VERIFICATION_TRANSPORT_FAILED outside failed tx, queue stays UNDER_REVIEW\n5. Cache precedence: cached REJECTED beats fresh APPROVED. APPROVED TTL=5min, REJECTED=permanent until content changes\n---\n\nThen write the full Level 2 session covering:\n- Every step from branch creation to final commit\n- Part C investigation: hypothesis → repro → root cause → fix → test results\n- All commits with their hashes\n- Test results (RED before fix, GREEN after)\n- Final: 31/31 tests passing\n\nAfter writing:\ngit add ai-session/transcript.md\ngit commit -m \"chore: write verbatim ai session transcript\"\ngit push origin assessment-2\n\nShow me the final branch URL."}]},"uuid":"ace08845-ca69-40d9-bc3b-60e62d38bba1","timestamp":"2026-05-06T11:46:28.539Z","permissionMode":"auto","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}

{"parentUuid":"d1618c35-43f9-477b-8513-78fc1392e493","isSidechain":false,"message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-7","id":"msg_01GcapkgLcHP4dSkmxbSp1Ma","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_0138MhGeQddCvNfHuw3NpBtq","name":"Edit","input":{"replace_all":false,"file_path":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/nodejs-nextjs-assessment/ai-session/transcript.md","old_string":"## End of session\n","new_string":"## End of session\n\n---\n---\n\n# ============================================================\n# LEVEL 2 SESSION — APPENDED 2026-05-06\n# ============================================================\n\n---\n## Level 2 Session Header\n- AI Tool: Claude Code CLI\n- Model: claude-opus-4-7[1m] (reported by environment)\n- Date: 2026-05-06\n- Assessment: Node.js & Next.js Level 2\n- Transcript method: Manual reconstruction — /transcript skill unavailable in this environment. Content is best-effort recall from this session, not byte-for-byte verbatim. Disclosed per assessment rules.\n\n## Brief Ambiguities Resolved\n1. Separation of duties: enforced at submit + claim + approve. No admin override.\n2. Deadline: PENDING + claimedBy=null + createdAt < now-48h → tier=2 + escalatedAt\n3. Verification retry: 3 attempts, exponential backoff 100/200/400ms ±20% jitter, transient only\n4. Audit on verification failure: VERIFICATION_TRANSPORT_FAILED outside failed tx, queue stays UNDER_REVIEW\n5. Cache precedence: cached REJECTED beats fresh APPROVED. APPROVED TTL=5min, REJECTED=permanent until content changes\n---\n\n## Branch + library install (Steps 1–3)\n\n**User:** Start Level 2. Step 1: branch `assessment-2` off `assessment-1`. Step 2: install seeded review-queue. Step 3: read the library to understand the bug.\n\n**Assistant:** Created `assessment-2` from the existing `assessment-1` head. The brief mentioned applying `review-queue.patch` via `git apply`, but no such patch file existed anywhere under `/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/`. Disclosed that gap and substituted the equivalent: copied the seeded library verbatim from `/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/review-queue/` into `admin-panel/lib/review-queue/`.\n\nTwo commits to keep the substitution auditable:\n- `aafabc0  install seeded review-queue` — verbatim file copy of seven library sources (`index.ts`, `client.ts`, `claim.ts`, `approve.ts`, `reject.ts`, `list-pending.ts`, `types.ts`).\n- `24ee099  feat(schema): add ReviewQueueItem model for seeded library` — added the `ReviewQueueItem` Prisma model required by the library, plus the matching migration `0002_review_queue_item`.\n\nDatabase setup hit a snag: `DATABASE_URL` originally used TCP auth, which the local Postgres rejected with SCRAM-SERVER-FIRST-MESSAGE: client password must be a string. Switched to a Unix-socket connection string `postgresql://sameh@localhost/admin_panel_l2?host=/var/run/postgresql`. Chose a fresh database (`admin_panel_l2`) so the Level 1 `admin_panel_dev` stayed untouched.\n\n`MOCK_USER_ID` from .env didn't match an actual seeded user; pulled the real admin id (`cmotwu4ga0000tgek0amk2can`) out of the database and wrote `.env` from scratch (no `.env.example` exists in this repo).\n\nRead the library. The bug is in `claim.ts`:\n\n```ts\n// SEEDED (BUGGY)\nexport async function claimNextItem(prisma, reviewer) {\n  const item = await prisma.reviewQueueItem.findFirst({\n    where: { status: 'PENDING', claimedBy: null },\n    orderBy: { createdAt: 'asc' },\n  });\n  if (!item) return { kind: 'empty' };\n  await prisma.reviewQueueItem.update({\n    where: { id: item.id },\n    data: { status: 'UNDER_REVIEW', claimedBy: reviewer, claimedAt: new Date() },\n  });\n  return { kind: 'claimed', item: { ...item, /* … */ } };\n}\n```\n\n**Hypothesis:** classic TOCTOU. Two reviewers calling this concurrently can both pass `findFirst` (no lock taken) and both attempt the `update` on the same row. Whichever `update` lands second silently overwrites `claimedBy` — the queue's \"exactly one reviewer per item\" invariant is broken.\n\n---\n\n## Part C: race repro (Step 4) and fix (Step 5)\n\n**User:** Step 4 — write the concurrent test FIRST, before the fix. Step 5 — fix `claim.ts` in place. Step 6 — commit with the exact message `fix(review-queue): close TOCTOU race in claimNextItem with FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`.\n\n**Assistant:** The race is observable only against a real database — pure mocks can't reproduce row-level lock semantics. Wrote `__tests__/review-queue-claim-race.test.ts` (vitest) that:\n\n1. Skips itself with `describe.skipIf(!HAS_DB)` when `DATABASE_URL` is unset.\n2. In `beforeAll`, seeds a fresh `User` → `Announcement` → `ReviewQueueItem(status: PENDING)`.\n3. In the test body, instantiates **five separate `PrismaClient` instances** (one per reviewer) so the callers cannot piggy-back on a shared connection-pool serialisation.\n4. `Promise.all`s five `claimNextItem` calls and asserts exactly one returns `kind: 'claimed'` and four return `kind: 'empty'`.\n\nCommitted the test against the un-patched library so the failure is on the record:\n\n- `b871372  test(review-queue): concurrent claim test reproduces TOCTOU race` — **RED** against the seeded `claim.ts`.\n\nTest output (pre-fix):\n```\n× claimNextItem (Postgres concurrency) > exactly one of five concurrent claims succeeds; four return empty\n   AssertionError: expected 5 to be 1\n   - Expected: 1\n   + Received: 5\n```\n\nAll five reviewers \"claimed\" the same row — exactly the TOCTOU we predicted.\n\n**Fix** (`admin-panel/lib/review-queue/src/claim.ts`):\n\n```ts\nexport async function claimNextItem(prisma, reviewer) {\n  return prisma.$transaction(async (tx) => {\n    const candidates = await tx.$queryRaw`\n      SELECT \"id\" FROM \"ReviewQueueItem\"\n      WHERE \"status\" = 'PENDING' AND \"claimedBy\" IS NULL\n      ORDER BY \"createdAt\" ASC\n      LIMIT 1\n      FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED\n    `;\n    if (candidates.length === 0) return { kind: 'empty' };\n    const updated = await tx.reviewQueueItem.update({\n      where: { id: candidates[0].id },\n      data: { status: 'UNDER_REVIEW', claimedBy: reviewer, claimedAt: new Date() },\n    });\n    return { kind: 'claimed', item: updated };\n  });\n}\n```\n\nWhy this satisfies the brief:\n- The lock is taken by Postgres at the **row** level inside a single `$transaction`, so it holds across processes and connection pools — not cosmetic.\n- `SKIP LOCKED` makes concurrent claimers each pick **different** rows (or none) instead of contending on the same row.\n- Surgical: only `claim.ts` changed, no public API change, no library rewrite.\n\nCommitted:\n- `313365d  fix(review-queue): close TOCTOU race in claimNextItem with FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED` — **GREEN**. Race test passes.\n\nTest output (post-fix):\n```\n✓ claimNextItem (Postgres concurrency) > exactly one of five concurrent claims succeeds; four return empty\n```\n\n---\n\n## Part A schema + RBAC (Step 7)\n\n**User:** Step 7 — extend schema with `AnnouncementStatus`, `COMPLIANCE_OFFICER`, `status` and `publishedAt`. Run `prisma migrate dev --name review_workflow`, regenerate, extend RBAC, add `compliance@example.com` to seed.\n\n**Assistant:** Edited `prisma/schema.prisma`:\n- Added `enum AnnouncementStatus { DRAFT PENDING_REVIEW PUBLISHED REJECTED }`.\n- Added `COMPLIANCE_OFFICER` to `enum Role`.\n- Added `status AnnouncementStatus @default(DRAFT)`, `publishedAt DateTime?`, and `@@index([status])` to `Announcement`.\n\nRan `npx prisma migrate dev --name review_workflow`. Prisma generated `20260506110255_review_workflow/migration.sql` (timestamp-prefixed; the older Level-1 migrations are hand-named without timestamps — left the new one as Prisma generated it since the user gave the exact command).\n\nExtended `lib/rbac.ts`'s `Action` union with `'review' | 'submitForReview' | 'escalate'` and added the role mappings:\n- `review` → ADMIN, COMPLIANCE_OFFICER\n- `submitForReview` → ADMIN, CONTENT_MANAGER\n- `escalate` → ADMIN\n\nUpserted `compliance@example.com` (role `COMPLIANCE_OFFICER`) in `prisma/seed.ts`. Ran the seed cleanly.\n\n`npx tsc --noEmit` → clean. `npm test` → 4/4 files, 23/23 tests passing.\n\n- `a3d2054  feat(schema): add AnnouncementStatus, COMPLIANCE_OFFICER, RBAC actions`\n\nSurfaced an open question to the user: Step 9's `escalateOverdue` will need `tier` and `escalatedAt` fields on `ReviewQueueItem`, but Step 7 didn't list them. Asked whether to fold them in now or add a follow-up migration. User chose follow-up (in Step 9).\n\n---\n\n## Part B verification adapter (Step 8)\n\n**User:** Step 8 — create five files in `lib/adapters/verification/`: `types.ts`, `mock.ts`, `live.ts`, `wrapper.ts`, `index.ts`. No HTTP types in the public surface. Mock denies on `__deny__`. Live throws `TODO SPEC PENDING` everywhere. Wrapper enforces cache precedence (REJECTED is sticky), retry (3 attempts, 100/200/400 ms, ±20% jitter, transient only), and breaker (5 failures → OPEN → 30s → HALF_OPEN → 1 success → CLOSED).\n\n**Assistant:**\n- `types.ts` — `AnnouncementForVerification`, `VerificationResult { ok, reason?, verdict, verifiedAt }`, `VerificationAdapter`, plus error classes `TransientVerificationError` and `PermanentVerificationError`. No `Response`, `fetch`, or HTTP types leak through.\n- `mock.ts` — `MockVerificationAdapter` returns REJECTED with `reason: 'mock-deny-token'` if `bodyEn` or `bodyAr` contains `__deny__`; otherwise APPROVED.\n- `live.ts` — `LiveVerificationAdapter.verify()` throws `Error('TODO SPEC PENDING')` and every potential call site (URL build, body build, auth headers, HTTP request, status classification, response parse, verdict mapping) carries its own `// TODO SPEC PENDING` comment so a single grep finds them all.\n- `wrapper.ts` — `CachedRetryingBreakerAdapter`:\n  - Cache key: `announcementId + \":\" + sha256(titleEn|titleAr|bodyEn|bodyAr).slice(0,16)`. APPROVED entries TTL = 5 min; REJECTED entries never TTL out.\n  - Precedence: a cached REJECTED is sticky — if a fresh APPROVED arrives, `writeCache` keeps the REJECTED.\n  - Retry loop: `for attempt in 0..2`, `await this.clock.sleep(this.jitter(RETRY_DELAYS_MS[attempt]))` between attempts. Only `TransientVerificationError` retries; `PermanentVerificationError` short-circuits.\n  - Breaker: state machine with single in-flight HALF_OPEN probe. While OPEN (or HALF_OPEN with a probe in flight) callers receive `TransientVerificationError('breaker open')` without ever touching the inner adapter.\n  - `Clock` and `random` are injectable via `WrapperOptions` — designed for deterministic tests.\n- `index.ts` — barrel exporting types, errors, all three adapters, and clock options.\n\n`npx tsc --noEmit` → clean. `npm test` → 4/4 files, 23/23 tests passing.\n\n- `47b1d00  feat(verification): adapter interface, mock, live scaffold, retry+breaker+cache`\n\n---\n\n## Part A workflow service (Step 9)\n\n**User:** Step 9 — first add a small migration for `tier` and `escalatedAt` on `ReviewQueueItem`. Then build `services/review-workflow.ts` with `submitForReview`, `claimNext`, `approveItem`, `rejectItem`, `escalateOverdue`. All multi-step writes inside `prisma.$transaction`. Verification failure → announcement stays PENDING_REVIEW + best-effort audit.\n\n**Assistant:** Schema first: added `tier Int @default(1)` and `escalatedAt DateTime?` to `ReviewQueueItem`. Ran `npx prisma migrate dev --name add_escalation_fields` — Prisma created `20260506110950_add_escalation_fields`.\n\nWrote `services/review-workflow.ts` (≈400 LOC). Four error classes (`SelfReviewError`, `WorkflowAuthorizationError`, `WorkflowStateError`, `WorkflowNotFoundError`) and five functions:\n\n- `submitForReview` — RBAC ADMIN/CONTENT_MANAGER. Inside one tx: read announcement (must be DRAFT, not deleted), bump to `PENDING_REVIEW`, create `ReviewQueueItem(authorId = user.id, tier = 1)`, audit `SUBMIT_FOR_REVIEW`, audit `NOTIFICATION_QUEUED` stub.\n- `claimNext` — RBAC ADMIN/COMPLIANCE_OFFICER. Pre-filter via `listPending({ excludeAuthorId: reviewer.id, limit: 1 })` to skip the claim entirely when nothing is visible. Then call `queue.claimNextItem`. Defence-in-depth post-check: if the SKIP LOCKED claim happened to grab a self-authored row (race vs. listPending), release the row back to PENDING and throw `SelfReviewError`.\n- `approveItem` — single tx. Re-read item with `include: { announcement: true }`, validate ownership/state/separation-of-duties. **Inlined** the queue update (`status → APPROVED`, decisionNote, decidedAt) instead of calling `queue.approve`, because the seeded library wraps its update in its own `prisma.$transaction` and Prisma forbids interactive nested transactions on a `TransactionClient`. Inlining preserves the atomicity guarantee. Then call `deps.verification.verify(...)` — APPROVED → announcement → PUBLISHED + publishedAt + `VERIFICATION_PASSED` audit; REJECTED → announcement → DRAFT + `VERIFICATION_REJECTED` audit. Transport throw → tx rolls back, then a best-effort `VERIFICATION_TRANSPORT_FAILED` audit is written outside the failed tx and the original error is rethrown. Workflow validation errors are excluded from the best-effort audit.\n- `rejectItem` — single tx. Same inline-and-atomic pattern: queue → REJECTED, announcement → DRAFT, audit `REJECTED`, audit `NOTIFICATION_QUEUED` stub.\n- `escalateOverdue(now = new Date())` — single tx. `findMany` for `PENDING && claimedBy = null && tier = 1 && createdAt < now - 48h`, `updateMany` to bump `tier = 2` + `escalatedAt = now`, then `createMany` audit rows. The function has no actor parameter (it's a system / scheduler op); the audit `userId` is attributed to the item's author so the AuditLog → User foreign key stays valid. Comment in the code calls this out.\n\n`npx tsc --noEmit` → clean. `npm test` → 4/4 files, 23/23 tests passing.\n\n- `0ee857f  feat(workflow): review-workflow service layer`\n\n---\n\n## API routes (Step 10)\n\n**User:** Step 10 — six App Router routes. Auth → RBAC → service call → JSON. Use `params: Promise<{ id: string }>`.\n\n**Assistant:** Read `node_modules/next/dist/docs/01-app/03-api-reference/03-file-conventions/route.md` to confirm the App Router signature (`AGENTS.md` warns: \"this is NOT the Next.js you know\"). Confirmed the Promise-wrapped `params` pattern.\n\nAdded `reviewApproveSchema` and `reviewRejectSchema` to `lib/validations.ts` (Zod, single required string field each).\n\nSix routes, all following the existing pattern (auth → RBAC → JSON parse → Zod → service call → response). Workflow errors map to HTTP:\n- `WorkflowNotFoundError` → 404\n- `SelfReviewError` → 403 `SELF_REVIEW`\n- `WorkflowAuthorizationError` → 403 `FORBIDDEN`\n- `WorkflowStateError` → 409 `STATE_CONFLICT`\n- Verification transport / breaker errors (in `approve`) → 503 `VERIFICATION_UNAVAILABLE`\n\nRoutes:\n- `POST /api/announcements/[id]/submit-for-review`\n- `GET  /api/review-queue`\n- `POST /api/review-queue/claim`\n- `POST /api/review-queue/[id]/approve` (body `{ note }`, wires `MockVerificationAdapter`)\n- `POST /api/review-queue/[id]/reject` (body `{ reason }`)\n- `POST /api/review-queue/escalate`\n\n`npx tsc --noEmit` → clean. `npm test` → 4/4 files, 23/23 passing.\n\n- `94560ab  feat(api): review-queue routes and submit-for-review`\n\n---\n\n## UI pages (Step 11)\n\n**User:** Step 11 — review-queue page (server), `ReviewActions` (client), status column on announcements list, sidebar link.\n\n**Assistant:**\n- `app/admin/review-queue/page.tsx` — server component, `redirect('/admin')` when `!can(user, 'review')`. Fetches both PENDING (excluding self-author) and the reviewer's own UNDER_REVIEW items in parallel so the table is the reviewer's full active surface (claim-candidates + decide-candidates). Reads via Prisma directly so the announcement title can be joined in one query — comment in the file explains this is a deliberate departure from `listPending`.\n- `app/admin/review-queue/ReviewActions.tsx` — client component, two modes: `claim-next` (single button POSTs `/api/review-queue/claim`); `decide` (per-row Approve / Reject with shared note input).\n- `app/admin/announcements/page.tsx` — added a `<span>` status badge on each row and a `SubmitForReviewButton` shown only when `status === 'DRAFT'` and `can(user, 'submitForReview')`.\n- `app/admin/announcements/SubmitForReviewButton.tsx` — client component, POSTs `/api/announcements/{id}/submit-for-review` and `router.refresh()` on success.\n- `app/admin/layout.tsx` — sidebar \"Review queue\" link visible only when `can(user, 'review')`.\n\n`npx tsc --noEmit` → clean. `npm test` → 4/4 files, 23/23 passing.\n\n- `5d423fb  feat(ui): review queue page and submit-for-review button`\n\n---\n\n## Tests (Step 12)\n\n**User:** Step 12 — four new test files: precedence, breaker, separation-of-duties, transactional rollback.\n\n**Assistant:**\n- `__tests__/verification-precedence.test.ts` — `StubAdapter` queues per-call responses. First call returns REJECTED → cached. Second call (same content) returns REJECTED via cache hit; inner is **not** invoked (`inner.calls === 1`). Negative case: editing `bodyEn` → different cache key → fresh APPROVED is honoured, inner called twice.\n- `__tests__/verification-breaker.test.ts` — injectable `Clock` whose `sleep` and `advance` mutate the shared `state.value`. Test 1: 5 failed `verify` calls (with `random: () => 0.5` to zero out jitter) → 15 inner calls (3 retries × 5) → 6th `verify` rejects with `breaker open` and inner is **not** called. Test 2: 29s past `openedAt` → still OPEN; 31s → next call probes (HALF_OPEN), succeeds → CLOSED → subsequent calls go through.\n- `__tests__/workflow-separation-of-duties.test.ts` — mocks `@/lib/review-queue/src` to simulate a SKIP LOCKED race grabbing a self-authored row. Asserts `claimNext` throws `SelfReviewError` AND that the row was released back to PENDING. Second test: `approveItem` short-circuits with `SelfReviewError` before any state mutation when `item.authorId === reviewer.id`.\n- `__tests__/workflow-transactional.test.ts` — `buildPrismaMock()` simulates rollback by buffering writes inside `$transaction` and dropping them on throw. (1) `submitForReview` with a failing audit → throws \"audit DB down\" and `committed === []`. (2) `approveItem` with verification rejecting `TransientVerificationError` → throws, `committed === []`, AND a `VERIFICATION_TRANSPORT_FAILED` audit was written via the **outside-tx** `prisma.auditLog.create`.\n\nFinal test results:\n```\n Test Files  8 passed (8)\n      Tests  31 passed (31)\n```\n\n(The Postgres-required race test runs because `DATABASE_URL` is set; otherwise `describe.skipIf` would skip it.)\n\n- `f614c89  test: verification precedence, breaker, separation-of-duties, transactional rollback`\n\n---\n\n## README (Step 13)\n\n**User:** Step 13 — Level 2 section to root README.\n\n**Assistant:** Appended a \"Level 2 — Review workflow, verification, race fix\" section: run flow, four seeded users (calls out `compliance@example.com`), env-var table, six route summaries with RBAC + behaviour, the verification adapter trio (mock with `__deny__` token / live with `TODO SPEC PENDING` / wrapper with cache + retry + breaker), test layout including the `DATABASE_URL`-required race test, and a Part C explanation. Adjusted the Level 2 run snippet to skip `cp .env.example .env` because that file doesn't exist in this repo.\n\n- `51c21fa  docs: update README with Level 2 instructions`\n\n---\n\n## UI smoke (Step 14)\n\n**User:** Step 14 — quick UI smoke test. Killed it, restarted with the fresh client, and the announcements page rendered the status badge + Submit-for-review buttons correctly.\n\nWalked the flow with role-switches via `.env`:\n\n**Phase 1 (cm CONTENT_MANAGER):**\n- `GET /admin/announcements` → 3× \"DRAFT\" status badges, 3× \"Submit for review\" buttons (one per DRAFT row). ✓\n- `POST /api/announcements/{ann1}/submit-for-review` → 200 `{\"ok\":true}`. ✓\n- `PATCH /api/announcements/{ann2}` to set `bodyEn` containing `__deny__` → 200. ✓\n- `POST /api/announcements/{ann2}/submit-for-review` → 200. ✓\n- DB confirms: both announcements `PENDING_REVIEW`; two queue items with `authorId = cm.id`, `status = PENDING`. ✓\n\n**Phase 2 (compliance COMPLIANCE_OFFICER):**\n- `GET /admin/review-queue` → both items shown; \"Claim next\" button rendered. ✓\n- Sidebar shows `COMPLIANCE_OFFICER` (.env hot-reloaded by Next dev). ✓\n- `POST /api/review-queue/claim` → claimed ann1 (oldest); item → `UNDER_REVIEW`, `claimedBy = compliance.id`. ✓\n- `POST /api/review-queue/{item1}/approve { note: \"smoke test approval\" }` → 200, verdict `{ok:true, verdict:'APPROVED'}`. Content is best-effort recall from this session, not byte-for-byte verbatim. Disclosed per assessment rules.\n\n## Brief Ambiguities Resolved\n1. Separation of duties: enforced at submit + claim + approve. No admin override.\n2. Deadline: PENDING + claimedBy=null + createdAt < now-48h → tier=2 + escalatedAt\n3. Verification retry: 3 attempts, exponential backoff 100/200/400ms ±20% jitter, transient only\n4. Audit on verification failure: VERIFICATION_TRANSPORT_FAILED outside failed tx, queue stays UNDER_REVIEW\n5. Cache precedence: cached REJECTED beats fresh APPROVED. APPROVED TTL=5min, REJECTED=permanent until content changes\n---\n\n## Branch + library install (Steps 1–3)\n\n**User:** Start Level 2. Step 1: branch `assessment-2` off `assessment-1`. Step 2: install seeded review-queue. Step 3: read the library to understand the bug.\n\n**Assistant:** Created `assessment-2` from the existing `assessment-1` head. The brief mentioned applying `review-queue.patch` via `git apply`, but no such patch file existed anywhere under `/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/`. Disclosed that gap and substituted the equivalent: copied the seeded library verbatim from `/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/review-queue/` into `admin-panel/lib/review-queue/`.\n\nTwo commits to keep the substitution auditable:\n- `aafabc0  install seeded review-queue` — verbatim file copy of seven library sources (`index.ts`, `client.ts`, `claim.ts`, `approve.ts`, `reject.ts`, `list-pending.ts`, `types.ts`).\n- `24ee099  feat(schema): add ReviewQueueItem model for seeded library` — added the `ReviewQueueItem` Prisma model required by the library, plus the matching migration `0002_review_queue_item`.\n\nDatabase setup hit a snag: `DATABASE_URL` originally used TCP auth, which the local Postgres rejected with SCRAM-SERVER-FIRST-MESSAGE: client password must be a string. Switched to a Unix-socket connection string `postgresql://sameh@localhost/admin_panel_l2?host=/var/run/postgresql`. Chose a fresh database (`admin_panel_l2`) so the Level 1 `admin_panel_dev` stayed untouched.\n\n`MOCK_USER_ID` from .env didn't match an actual seeded user; pulled the real admin id (`cmotwu4ga0000tgek0amk2can`) out of the database and wrote `.env` from scratch (no `.env.example` exists in this repo).\n\nRead the library. The bug is in `claim.ts`:\n\n```ts\n// SEEDED (BUGGY)\nexport async function claimNextItem(prisma, reviewer) {\n  const item = await prisma.reviewQueueItem.findFirst({\n    where: { status: 'PENDING', claimedBy: null },\n    orderBy: { createdAt: 'asc' },\n  });\n  if (!item) return { kind: 'empty' };\n  await prisma.reviewQueueItem.update({\n    where: { id: item.id },\n    data: { status: 'UNDER_REVIEW', claimedBy: reviewer, claimedAt: new Date() },\n  });\n  return { kind: 'claimed', item: { ...item, /* … */ } };\n}\n```\n\n**Hypothesis:** classic TOCTOU. Two reviewers calling this concurrently can both pass `findFirst` (no lock taken) and both attempt the `update` on the same row. Whichever `update` lands second silently overwrites `claimedBy` — the queue's \"exactly one reviewer per item\" invariant is broken.\n\n---\n\n## Part C: race repro (Step 4) and fix (Step 5)\n\n**User:** Step 4 — write the concurrent test FIRST, before the fix. Step 5 — fix `claim.ts` in place. Step 6 — commit with the exact message `fix(review-queue): close TOCTOU race in claimNextItem with FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`.\n\n**Assistant:** The race is observable only against a real database — pure mocks can't reproduce row-level lock semantics. Wrote `__tests__/review-queue-claim-race.test.ts` (vitest) that:\n\n1. Skips itself with `describe.skipIf(!HAS_DB)` when `DATABASE_URL` is unset.\n2. In `beforeAll`, seeds a fresh `User` → `Announcement` → `ReviewQueueItem(status: PENDING)`.\n3. In the test body, instantiates **five separate `PrismaClient` instances** (one per reviewer) so the callers cannot piggy-back on a shared connection-pool serialisation.\n4. `Promise.all`s five `claimNextItem` calls and asserts exactly one returns `kind: 'claimed'` and four return `kind: 'empty'`.\n\nCommitted the test against the un-patched library so the failure is on the record:\n\n- `b871372  test(review-queue): concurrent claim test reproduces TOCTOU race` — **RED** against the seeded `claim.ts`.\n\nTest output (pre-fix):\n```\n× claimNextItem (Postgres concurrency) > exactly one of five concurrent claims succeeds; four return empty\n   AssertionError: expected 5 to be 1\n   - Expected: 1\n   + Received: 5\n```\n\nAll five reviewers \"claimed\" the same row — exactly the TOCTOU we predicted.\n\n**Fix** (`admin-panel/lib/review-queue/src/claim.ts`):\n\n```ts\nexport async function claimNextItem(prisma, reviewer) {\n  return prisma.$transaction(async (tx) => {\n    const candidates = await tx.$queryRaw`\n      SELECT \"id\" FROM \"ReviewQueueItem\"\n      WHERE \"status\" = 'PENDING' AND \"claimedBy\" IS NULL\n      ORDER BY \"createdAt\" ASC\n      LIMIT 1\n      FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED\n    `;\n    if (candidates.length === 0) return { kind: 'empty' };\n    const updated = await tx.reviewQueueItem.update({\n      where: { id: candidates[0].id },\n      data: { status: 'UNDER_REVIEW', claimedBy: reviewer, claimedAt: new Date() },\n    });\n    return { kind: 'claimed', item: updated };\n  });\n}\n```\n\nWhy this satisfies the brief:\n- The lock is taken by Postgres at the **row** level inside a single `$transaction`, so it holds across processes and connection pools — not cosmetic.\n- `SKIP LOCKED` makes concurrent claimers each pick **different** rows (or none) instead of contending on the same row.\n- Surgical: only `claim.ts` changed, no public API change, no library rewrite.\n\nCommitted:\n- `313365d  fix(review-queue): close TOCTOU race in claimNextItem with FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED` — **GREEN**. Race test passes.\n\nTest output (post-fix):\n```\n✓ claimNextItem (Postgres concurrency) > exactly one of five concurrent claims succeeds; four return empty\n```\n\n---\n\n## Part A schema + RBAC (Step 7)\n\n**User:** Step 7 — extend schema with `AnnouncementStatus`, `COMPLIANCE_OFFICER`, `status` and `publishedAt`. Run `prisma migrate dev --name review_workflow`, regenerate, extend RBAC, add `compliance@example.com` to seed.\n\n**Assistant:** Edited `prisma/schema.prisma`:\n- Added `enum AnnouncementStatus { DRAFT PENDING_REVIEW PUBLISHED REJECTED }`.\n- Added `COMPLIANCE_OFFICER` to `enum Role`.\n- Added `status AnnouncementStatus @default(DRAFT)`, `publishedAt DateTime?`, and `@@index([status])` to `Announcement`.\n\nRan `npx prisma migrate dev --name review_workflow`. Prisma generated `20260506110255_review_workflow/migration.sql` (timestamp-prefixed; the older Level-1 migrations are hand-named without timestamps — left the new one as Prisma generated it since the user gave the exact command).\n\nExtended `lib/rbac.ts`'s `Action` union with `'review' | 'submitForReview' | 'escalate'` and added the role mappings:\n- `review` → ADMIN, COMPLIANCE_OFFICER\n- `submitForReview` → ADMIN, CONTENT_MANAGER\n- `escalate` → ADMIN\n\nUpserted `compliance@example.com` (role `COMPLIANCE_OFFICER`) in `prisma/seed.ts`. Ran the seed cleanly.\n\n`npx tsc --noEmit` → clean. `npm test` → 4/4 files, 23/23 tests passing.\n\n- `a3d2054  feat(schema): add AnnouncementStatus, COMPLIANCE_OFFICER, RBAC actions`\n\nSurfaced an open question to the user: Step 9's `escalateOverdue` will need `tier` and `escalatedAt` fields on `ReviewQueueItem`, but Step 7 didn't list them. Asked whether to fold them in now or add a follow-up migration. User chose follow-up (in Step 9).\n\n---\n\n## Part B verification adapter (Step 8)\n\n**User:** Step 8 — create five files in `lib/adapters/verification/`: `types.ts`, `mock.ts`, `live.ts`, `wrapper.ts`, `index.ts`. No HTTP types in the public surface. Mock denies on `__deny__`. Live throws `TODO SPEC PENDING` everywhere. Wrapper enforces cache precedence (REJECTED is sticky), retry (3 attempts, 100/200/400 ms, ±20% jitter, transient only), and breaker (5 failures → OPEN → 30s → HALF_OPEN → 1 success → CLOSED).\n\n**Assistant:**\n- `types.ts` — `AnnouncementForVerification`, `VerificationResult { ok, reason?, verdict, verifiedAt }`, `VerificationAdapter`, plus error classes `TransientVerificationError` and `PermanentVerificationError`. No `Response`, `fetch`, or HTTP types leak through.\n- `mock.ts` — `MockVerificationAdapter` returns REJECTED with `reason: 'mock-deny-token'` if `bodyEn` or `bodyAr` contains `__deny__`; otherwise APPROVED.\n- `live.ts` — `LiveVerificationAdapter.verify()` throws `Error('TODO SPEC PENDING')` and every potential call site (URL build, body build, auth headers, HTTP request, status classification, response parse, verdict mapping) carries its own `// TODO SPEC PENDING` comment so a single grep finds them all.\n- `wrapper.ts` — `CachedRetryingBreakerAdapter`:\n  - Cache key: `announcementId + \":\" + sha256(titleEn|titleAr|bodyEn|bodyAr).slice(0,16)`. APPROVED entries TTL = 5 min; REJECTED entries never TTL out.\n  - Precedence: a cached REJECTED is sticky — if a fresh APPROVED arrives, `writeCache` keeps the REJECTED.\n  - Retry loop: `for attempt in 0..2`, `await this.clock.sleep(this.jitter(RETRY_DELAYS_MS[attempt]))` between attempts. Only `TransientVerificationError` retries; `PermanentVerificationError` short-circuits.\n  - Breaker: state machine with single in-flight HALF_OPEN probe. While OPEN (or HALF_OPEN with a probe in flight) callers receive `TransientVerificationError('breaker open')` without ever touching the inner adapter.\n  - `Clock` and `random` are injectable via `WrapperOptions` — designed for deterministic tests.\n- `index.ts` — barrel exporting types, errors, all three adapters, and clock options.\n\n`npx tsc --noEmit` → clean. `npm test` → 4/4 files, 23/23 tests passing.\n\n- `47b1d00  feat(verification): adapter interface, mock, live scaffold, retry+breaker+cache`\n\n---\n\n## Part A workflow service (Step 9)\n\n**User:** Step 9 — first add a small migration for `tier` and `escalatedAt` on `ReviewQueueItem`. Then build `services/review-workflow.ts` with `submitForReview`, `claimNext`, `approveItem`, `rejectItem`, `escalateOverdue`. All multi-step writes inside `prisma.$transaction`. Verification failure → announcement stays PENDING_REVIEW + best-effort audit.\n\n**Assistant:** Schema first: added `tier Int @default(1)` and `escalatedAt DateTime?` to `ReviewQueueItem`. Ran `npx prisma migrate dev --name add_escalation_fields` — Prisma created `20260506110950_add_escalation_fields`.\n\nWrote `services/review-workflow.ts` (≈400 LOC). Four error classes (`SelfReviewError`, `WorkflowAuthorizationError`, `WorkflowStateError`, `WorkflowNotFoundError`) and five functions:\n\n- `submitForReview` — RBAC ADMIN/CONTENT_MANAGER. Inside one tx: read announcement (must be DRAFT, not deleted), bump to `PENDING_REVIEW`, create `ReviewQueueItem(authorId = user.id, tier = 1)`, audit `SUBMIT_FOR_REVIEW`, audit `NOTIFICATION_QUEUED` stub.\n- `claimNext` — RBAC ADMIN/COMPLIANCE_OFFICER. Pre-filter via `listPending({ excludeAuthorId: reviewer.id, limit: 1 })` to skip the claim entirely when nothing is visible. Then call `queue.claimNextItem`. Defence-in-depth post-check: if the SKIP LOCKED claim happened to grab a self-authored row (race vs. listPending), release the row back to PENDING and throw `SelfReviewError`.\n- `approveItem` — single tx. Re-read item with `include: { announcement: true }`, validate ownership/state/separation-of-duties. **Inlined** the queue update (`status → APPROVED`, decisionNote, decidedAt) instead of calling `queue.approve`, because the seeded library wraps its update in its own `prisma.$transaction` and Prisma forbids interactive nested transactions on a `TransactionClient`. Inlining preserves the atomicity guarantee. Then call `deps.verification.verify(...)` — APPROVED → announcement → PUBLISHED + publishedAt + `VERIFICATION_PASSED` audit; REJECTED → announcement → DRAFT + `VERIFICATION_REJECTED` audit. Transport throw → tx rolls back, then a best-effort `VERIFICATION_TRANSPORT_FAILED` audit is written outside the failed tx and the original error is rethrown. Workflow validation errors are excluded from the best-effort audit.\n- `rejectItem` — single tx. Same inline-and-atomic pattern: queue → REJECTED, announcement → DRAFT, audit `REJECTED`, audit `NOTIFICATION_QUEUED` stub.\n- `escalateOverdue(now = new Date())` — single tx. `findMany` for `PENDING && claimedBy = null && tier = 1 && createdAt < now - 48h`, `updateMany` to bump `tier = 2` + `escalatedAt = now`, then `createMany` audit rows. The function has no actor parameter (it's a system / scheduler op); the audit `userId` is attributed to the item's author so the AuditLog → User foreign key stays valid. Comment in the code calls this out.\n\n`npx tsc --noEmit` → clean. `npm test` → 4/4 files, 23/23 tests passing.\n\n- `0ee857f  feat(workflow): review-workflow service layer`\n\n---\n\n## API routes (Step 10)\n\n**User:** Step 10 — six App Router routes. Auth → RBAC → service call → JSON. Use `params: Promise<{ id: string }>`.\n\n**Assistant:** Read `node_modules/next/dist/docs/01-app/03-api-reference/03-file-conventions/route.md` to confirm the App Router signature (`AGENTS.md` warns: \"this is NOT the Next.js you know\"). Confirmed the Promise-wrapped `params` pattern.\n\nAdded `reviewApproveSchema` and `reviewRejectSchema` to `lib/validations.ts` (Zod, single required string field each).\n\nSix routes, all following the existing pattern (auth → RBAC → JSON parse → Zod → service call → response). Workflow errors map to HTTP:\n- `WorkflowNotFoundError` → 404\n- `SelfReviewError` → 403 `SELF_REVIEW`\n- `WorkflowAuthorizationError` → 403 `FORBIDDEN`\n- `WorkflowStateError` → 409 `STATE_CONFLICT`\n- Verification transport / breaker errors (in `approve`) → 503 `VERIFICATION_UNAVAILABLE`\n\nRoutes:\n- `POST /api/announcements/[id]/submit-for-review`\n- `GET  /api/review-queue`\n- `POST /api/review-queue/claim`\n- `POST /api/review-queue/[id]/approve` (body `{ note }`, wires `MockVerificationAdapter`)\n- `POST /api/review-queue/[id]/reject` (body `{ reason }`)\n- `POST /api/review-queue/escalate`\n\n`npx tsc --noEmit` → clean. `npm test` → 4/4 files, 23/23 passing.\n\n- `94560ab  feat(api): review-queue routes and submit-for-review`\n\n---\n\n## UI pages (Step 11)\n\n**User:** Step 11 — review-queue page (server), `ReviewActions` (client), status column on announcements list, sidebar link.\n\n**Assistant:**\n- `app/admin/review-queue/page.tsx` — server component, `redirect('/admin')` when `!can(user, 'review')`. Fetches both PENDING (excluding self-author) and the reviewer's own UNDER_REVIEW items in parallel so the table is the reviewer's full active surface (claim-candidates + decide-candidates). Reads via Prisma directly so the announcement title can be joined in one query — comment in the file explains this is a deliberate departure from `listPending`.\n- `app/admin/review-queue/ReviewActions.tsx` — client component, two modes: `claim-next` (single button POSTs `/api/review-queue/claim`); `decide` (per-row Approve / Reject with shared note input).\n- `app/admin/announcements/page.tsx` — added a `<span>` status badge on each row and a `SubmitForReviewButton` shown only when `status === 'DRAFT'` and `can(user, 'submitForReview')`.\n- `app/admin/announcements/SubmitForReviewButton.tsx` — client component, POSTs `/api/announcements/{id}/submit-for-review` and `router.refresh()` on success.\n- `app/admin/layout.tsx` — sidebar \"Review queue\" link visible only when `can(user, 'review')`.\n\n`npx tsc --noEmit` → clean. `npm test` → 4/4 files, 23/23 passing.\n\n- `5d423fb  feat(ui): review queue page and submit-for-review button`\n\n---\n\n## Tests (Step 12)\n\n**User:** Step 12 — four new test files: precedence, breaker, separation-of-duties, transactional rollback.\n\n**Assistant:**\n- `__tests__/verification-precedence.test.ts` — `StubAdapter` queues per-call responses. First call returns REJECTED → cached. Second call (same content) returns REJECTED via cache hit; inner is **not** invoked (`inner.calls === 1`). Negative case: editing `bodyEn` → different cache key → fresh APPROVED is honoured, inner called twice.\n- `__tests__/verification-breaker.test.ts` — injectable `Clock` whose `sleep` and `advance` mutate the shared `state.value`. Test 1: 5 failed `verify` calls (with `random: () => 0.5` to zero out jitter) → 15 inner calls (3 retries × 5) → 6th `verify` rejects with `breaker open` and inner is **not** called. Test 2: 29s past `openedAt` → still OPEN; 31s → next call probes (HALF_OPEN), succeeds → CLOSED → subsequent calls go through.\n- `__tests__/workflow-separation-of-duties.test.ts` — mocks `@/lib/review-queue/src` to simulate a SKIP LOCKED race grabbing a self-authored row. Asserts `claimNext` throws `SelfReviewError` AND that the row was released back to PENDING. Second test: `approveItem` short-circuits with `SelfReviewError` before any state mutation when `item.authorId === reviewer.id`.\n- `__tests__/workflow-transactional.test.ts` — `buildPrismaMock()` simulates rollback by buffering writes inside `$transaction` and dropping them on throw. (1) `submitForReview` with a failing audit → throws \"audit DB down\" and `committed === []`. (2) `approveItem` with verification rejecting `TransientVerificationError` → throws, `committed === []`, AND a `VERIFICATION_TRANSPORT_FAILED` audit was written via the **outside-tx** `prisma.auditLog.create`.\n\nFinal test results:\n```\n Test Files  8 passed (8)\n      Tests  31 passed (31)\n```\n\n(The Postgres-required race test runs because `DATABASE_URL` is set; otherwise `describe.skipIf` would skip it.)\n\n- `f614c89  test: verification precedence, breaker, separation-of-duties, transactional rollback`\n\n---\n\n## README (Step 13)\n\n**User:** Step 13 — Level 2 section to root README.\n\n**Assistant:** Appended a \"Level 2 — Review workflow, verification, race fix\" section: run flow, four seeded users (calls out `compliance@example.com`), env-var table, six route summaries with RBAC + behaviour, the verification adapter trio (mock with `__deny__` token / live with `TODO SPEC PENDING` / wrapper with cache + retry + breaker), test layout including the `DATABASE_URL`-required race test, and a Part C explanation. Adjusted the Level 2 run snippet to skip `cp .env.example .env` because that file doesn't exist in this repo.\n\n- `51c21fa  docs: update README with Level 2 instructions`\n\n---\n\n## UI smoke (Step 14)\n\n**User:** Step 14 — quick UI smoke test. Walk the golden path with cm and compliance roles.\n\n**Assistant:** First attempt at starting the dev server failed: an existing `next-server` from earlier in the session (PID 58463, ~1h elapsed) was still listening on :3000 with a stale Prisma client (no `status` field). Killed it, restarted with the fresh client, and the announcements page rendered the status badge + Submit-for-review buttons correctly.\n\nWalked the flow with role-switches via `.env`:\n\n**Phase 1 (cm CONTENT_MANAGER):**\n- `GET /admin/announcements` → 3× \"DRAFT\" status badges, 3× \"Submit for review\" buttons (one per DRAFT row). ✓\n- `POST /api/announcements/{ann1}/submit-for-review` → 200 `{\"ok\":true}`. ✓\n- `PATCH /api/announcements/{ann2}` to set `bodyEn` containing `__deny__` → 200. ✓\n- `POST /api/announcements/{ann2}/submit-for-review` → 200. ✓\n- DB confirms: both announcements `PENDING_REVIEW`; two queue items with `authorId = cm.id`, `status = PENDING`. ✓\n\n**Phase 2 (compliance COMPLIANCE_OFFICER):**\n- `GET /admin/review-queue` → both items shown; \"Claim next\" button rendered. ✓\n- Sidebar shows `COMPLIANCE_OFFICER` (.env hot-reloaded by Next dev). ✓\n- `POST /api/review-queue/claim` → claimed ann1 (oldest); item → `UNDER_REVIEW`, `claimedBy = compliance.id`. ✓\n- `POST /api/review-queue/{item1}/approve { note: \"smoke test approval\" }` → 200, verdict `{ok:true, verdict:'APPROVED'}`. Ann1 → `PUBLISHED`, `publishedAt` set. ✓\n- `POST /api/review-queue/claim` → claimed ann2. ✓\n- `POST /api/review-queue/{item2}/approve { note: \"smoke test deny path\" }` → 200, verdict `{ok:false, verdict:'REJECTED', reason:'mock-deny-token'}`. Ann2 → `DRAFT`. ✓\n\nAudit chain: 6 entries — 2× `SUBMIT_FOR_REVIEW`, 2× `NOTIFICATION_QUEUED`, 1× `VERIFICATION_PASSED`, 1× `VERIFICATION_REJECTED`. No `VERIFICATION_TRANSPORT_FAILED` (no transport failures occurred during smoke).\n\nRestored `.env` to `admin@example.com`. Stopped the dev server. Port 3000 free.\n\n---\n\n## Final state\n\n**Branch:** `assessment-2`. Eleven commits since `assessment-1`:\n\n```\n51c21fa docs: update README with Level 2 instructions\nf614c89 test: verification precedence, breaker, separation-of-duties, transactional rollback\n5d423fb feat(ui): review queue page and submit-for-review button\n94560ab feat(api): review-queue routes and submit-for-review\n0ee857f feat(workflow): review-workflow service layer\n47b1d00 feat(verification): adapter interface, mock, live scaffold, retry+breaker+cache\na3d2054 feat(schema): add AnnouncementStatus, COMPLIANCE_OFFICER, RBAC actions\n313365d fix(review-queue): close TOCTOU race in claimNextItem with FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED\nb871372 test(review-queue): concurrent claim test reproduces TOCTOU race\n24ee099 feat(schema): add ReviewQueueItem model for seeded library\naafabc0 install seeded review-queue\n```\n\n(A 12th commit, `chore: write verbatim ai session transcript`, is added immediately after this file is written.)\n\n**Tests:** 8 files / 31 tests passing. Race test runs against Postgres when `DATABASE_URL` is set.\n\n**Smoke:** golden path + deny path both verified end-to-end via the live dev server and direct DB inspection.\n\n**Disclosures:**\n- No `review-queue.patch` file existed; the seeded library was copied directly with a clear `install seeded review-queue` commit.\n- Step 7's migration name is timestamp-prefixed (`20260506110255_review_workflow`) per Prisma's default — older Level-1 migrations are hand-named without timestamps; left as Prisma generated since the user gave the exact `migrate dev --name` command.\n- `escalateOverdue` has no actor parameter; audit rows attribute to the item's author (FK requirement) — caller (route handler) enforces RBAC.\n- `approveItem` and `rejectItem` inline the queue-row update inside the workflow tx (rather than calling `queue.approve` / `queue.reject` directly) because the seeded library wraps its update in its own `prisma.$transaction` and Prisma forbids interactive nested transactions. The same status / claimedBy / state guards as the library are preserved inline; comment in the file calls this out.\n- The dev server's first run had a stale Prisma client (it started before `prisma generate` ran for the new schema). Restart resolved it. No code change required.\n\n## End of Level 2 session\n","originalFile":null,"structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":624,"oldLines":3,"newStart":624,"newLines":332,"lines":[" ---"," "," ## End of session","+","+---","+---","+","+# ============================================================","+# LEVEL 2 SESSION — APPENDED 2026-05-06","+# ============================================================","+","+---","+## Level 2 Session Header","+- AI Tool: Claude Code CLI","+- Model: claude-opus-4-7[1m] (reported by environment)","+- Date: 2026-05-06","+- Assessment: Node.js & Next.js Level 2","+- Transcript method: Manual reconstruction — /transcript skill unavailable in this environment. Content is best-effort recall from this session, not byte-for-byte verbatim. Disclosed per assessment rules.","+","+## Brief Ambiguities Resolved","+1. Separation of duties: enforced at submit + claim + approve. No admin override.","+2. Deadline: PENDING + claimedBy=null + createdAt < now-48h → tier=2 + escalatedAt","+3. Verification retry: 3 attempts, exponential backoff 100/200/400ms ±20% jitter, transient only","+4. Audit on verification failure: VERIFICATION_TRANSPORT_FAILED outside failed tx, queue stays UNDER_REVIEW","+5. Cache precedence: cached REJECTED beats fresh APPROVED. APPROVED TTL=5min, REJECTED=permanent until content changes","+---","+","+## Branch + library install (Steps 1–3)","+","+**User:** Start Level 2. Step 1: branch `assessment-2` off `assessment-1`. Step 2: install seeded review-queue. Step 3: read the library to understand the bug.","+","+**Assistant:** Created `assessment-2` from the existing `assessment-1` head. The brief mentioned applying `review-queue.patch` via `git apply`, but no such patch file existed anywhere under `/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/`. Disclosed that gap and substituted the equivalent: copied the seeded library verbatim from `/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2/review-queue/` into `admin-panel/lib/review-queue/`.","+","+Two commits to keep the substitution auditable:","+- `aafabc0  install seeded review-queue` — verbatim file copy of seven library sources (`index.ts`, `client.ts`, `claim.ts`, `approve.ts`, `reject.ts`, `list-pending.ts`, `types.ts`).","+- `24ee099  feat(schema): add ReviewQueueItem model for seeded library` — added the `ReviewQueueItem` Prisma model required by the library, plus the matching migration `0002_review_queue_item`.","+","+Database setup hit a snag: `DATABASE_URL` originally used TCP auth, which the local Postgres rejected with SCRAM-SERVER-FIRST-MESSAGE: client password must be a string. Switched to a Unix-socket connection string `postgresql://sameh@localhost/admin_panel_l2?host=/var/run/postgresql`. Chose a fresh database (`admin_panel_l2`) so the Level 1 `admin_panel_dev` stayed untouched.","+","+`MOCK_USER_ID` from .env didn't match an actual seeded user; pulled the real admin id (`cmotwu4ga0000tgek0amk2can`) out of the database and wrote `.env` from scratch (no `.env.example` exists in this repo).","+","+Read the library. The bug is in `claim.ts`:","+","+```ts","+// SEEDED (BUGGY)","+export async function claimNextItem(prisma, reviewer) {","+  const item = await prisma.reviewQueueItem.findFirst({","+    where: { status: 'PENDING', claimedBy: null },","+    orderBy: { createdAt: 'asc' },","+  });","+  if (!item) return { kind: 'empty' };","+  await prisma.reviewQueueItem.update({","+    where: { id: item.id },","+    data: { status: 'UNDER_REVIEW', claimedBy: reviewer, claimedAt: new Date() },","+  });","+  return { kind: 'claimed', item: { ...item, /* … */ } };","+}","+```","+","+**Hypothesis:** classic TOCTOU. Two reviewers calling this concurrently can both pass `findFirst` (no lock taken) and both attempt the `update` on the same row. Whichever `update` lands second silently overwrites `claimedBy` — the queue's \"exactly one reviewer per item\" invariant is broken.","+","+---","+","+## Part C: race repro (Step 4) and fix (Step 5)","+","+**User:** Step 4 — write the concurrent test FIRST, before the fix. Step 5 — fix `claim.ts` in place. Step 6 — commit with the exact message `fix(review-queue): close TOCTOU race in claimNextItem with FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`.","+","+**Assistant:** The race is observable only against a real database — pure mocks can't reproduce row-level lock semantics. Wrote `__tests__/review-queue-claim-race.test.ts` (vitest) that:","+","+1. Skips itself with `describe.skipIf(!HAS_DB)` when `DATABASE_URL` is unset.","+2. In `beforeAll`, seeds a fresh `User` → `Announcement` → `ReviewQueueItem(status: PENDING)`.","+3. In the test body, instantiates **five separate `PrismaClient` instances** (one per reviewer) so the callers cannot piggy-back on a shared connection-pool serialisation.","+4. `Promise.all`s five `claimNextItem` calls and asserts exactly one returns `kind: 'claimed'` and four return `kind: 'empty'`.","+","+Committed the test against the un-patched library so the failure is on the record:","+","+- `b871372  test(review-queue): concurrent claim test reproduces TOCTOU race` — **RED** against the seeded `claim.ts`.","+","+Test output (pre-fix):","+```","+× claimNextItem (Postgres concurrency) > exactly one of five concurrent claims succeeds; four return empty","+   AssertionError: expected 5 to be 1","+   - Expected: 1","+   + Received: 5","+```","+","+All five reviewers \"claimed\" the same row — exactly the TOCTOU we predicted.","+","+**Fix** (`admin-panel/lib/review-queue/src/claim.ts`):","+","+```ts","+export async function claimNextItem(prisma, reviewer) {","+  return prisma.$transaction(async (tx) => {","+    const candidates = await tx.$queryRaw`","+      SELECT \"id\" FROM \"ReviewQueueItem\"","+      WHERE \"status\" = 'PENDING' AND \"claimedBy\" IS NULL","+      ORDER BY \"createdAt\" ASC","+      LIMIT 1","+      FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED","+    `;","+    if (candidates.length === 0) return { kind: 'empty' };","+    const updated = await tx.reviewQueueItem.update({","+      where: { id: candidates[0].id },","+      data: { status: 'UNDER_REVIEW', claimedBy: reviewer, claimedAt: new Date() },","+    });","+    return { kind: 'claimed', item: updated };","+  });","+}","+```","+","+Why this satisfies the brief:","+- The lock is taken by Postgres at the **row** level inside a single `$transaction`, so it holds across processes and connection pools — not cosmetic.","+- `SKIP LOCKED` makes concurrent claimers each pick **different** rows (or none) instead of contending on the same row.","+- Surgical: only `claim.ts` changed, no public API change, no library rewrite.","+","+Committed:","+- `313365d  fix(review-queue): close TOCTOU race in claimNextItem with FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED` — **GREEN**. Race test passes.","+","+Test output (post-fix):","+```","+✓ claimNextItem (Postgres concurrency) > exactly one of five concurrent claims succeeds; four return empty","+```","+","+---","+","+## Part A schema + RBAC (Step 7)","+","+**User:** Step 7 — extend schema with `AnnouncementStatus`, `COMPLIANCE_OFFICER`, `status` and `publishedAt`. Run `prisma migrate dev --name review_workflow`, regenerate, extend RBAC, add `compliance@example.com` to seed.","+","+**Assistant:** Edited `prisma/schema.prisma`:","+- Added `enum AnnouncementStatus { DRAFT PENDING_REVIEW PUBLISHED REJECTED }`.","+- Added `COMPLIANCE_OFFICER` to `enum Role`.","+- Added `status AnnouncementStatus @default(DRAFT)`, `publishedAt DateTime?`, and `@@index([status])` to `Announcement`.","+","+Ran `npx prisma migrate dev --name review_workflow`. Prisma generated `20260506110255_review_workflow/migration.sql` (timestamp-prefixed; the older Level-1 migrations are hand-named without timestamps — left the new one as Prisma generated it since the user gave the exact command).","+","+Extended `lib/rbac.ts`'s `Action` union with `'review' | 'submitForReview' | 'escalate'` and added the role mappings:","+- `review` → ADMIN, COMPLIANCE_OFFICER","+- `submitForReview` → ADMIN, CONTENT_MANAGER","+- `escalate` → ADMIN","+","+Upserted `compliance@example.com` (role `COMPLIANCE_OFFICER`) in `prisma/seed.ts`. Ran the seed cleanly.","+","+`npx tsc --noEmit` → clean. `npm test` → 4/4 files, 23/23 tests passing.","+","+- `a3d2054  feat(schema): add AnnouncementStatus, COMPLIANCE_OFFICER, RBAC actions`","+","+Surfaced an open question to the user: Step 9's `escalateOverdue` will need `tier` and `escalatedAt` fields on `ReviewQueueItem`, but Step 7 didn't list them. Asked whether to fold them in now or add a follow-up migration. User chose follow-up (in Step 9).","+","+---","+","+## Part B verification adapter (Step 8)","+","+**User:** Step 8 — create five files in `lib/adapters/verification/`: `types.ts`, `mock.ts`, `live.ts`, `wrapper.ts`, `index.ts`. No HTTP types in the public surface. Mock denies on `__deny__`. Live throws `TODO SPEC PENDING` everywhere. Wrapper enforces cache precedence (REJECTED is sticky), retry (3 attempts, 100/200/400 ms, ±20% jitter, transient only), and breaker (5 failures → OPEN → 30s → HALF_OPEN → 1 success → CLOSED).","+","+**Assistant:**","+- `types.ts` — `AnnouncementForVerification`, `VerificationResult { ok, reason?, verdict, verifiedAt }`, `VerificationAdapter`, plus error classes `TransientVerificationError` and `PermanentVerificationError`. No `Response`, `fetch`, or HTTP types leak through.","+- `mock.ts` — `MockVerificationAdapter` returns REJECTED with `reason: 'mock-deny-token'` if `bodyEn` or `bodyAr` contains `__deny__`; otherwise APPROVED.","+- `live.ts` — `LiveVerificationAdapter.verify()` throws `Error('TODO SPEC PENDING')` and every potential call site (URL build, body build, auth headers, HTTP request, status classification, response parse, verdict mapping) carries its own `// TODO SPEC PENDING` comment so a single grep finds them all.","+- `wrapper.ts` — `CachedRetryingBreakerAdapter`:","+  - Cache key: `announcementId + \":\" + sha256(titleEn|titleAr|bodyEn|bodyAr).slice(0,16)`. APPROVED entries TTL = 5 min; REJECTED entries never TTL out.","+  - Precedence: a cached REJECTED is sticky — if a fresh APPROVED arrives, `writeCache` keeps the REJECTED.","+  - Retry loop: `for attempt in 0..2`, `await this.clock.sleep(this.jitter(RETRY_DELAYS_MS[attempt]))` between attempts. Only `TransientVerificationError` retries; `PermanentVerificationError` short-circuits.","+  - Breaker: state machine with single in-flight HALF_OPEN probe. While OPEN (or HALF_OPEN with a probe in flight) callers receive `TransientVerificationError('breaker open')` without ever touching the inner adapter.","+  - `Clock` and `random` are injectable via `WrapperOptions` — designed for deterministic tests.","+- `index.ts` — barrel exporting types, errors, all three adapters, and clock options.","+","+`npx tsc --noEmit` → clean. `npm test` → 4/4 files, 23/23 tests passing.","+","+- `47b1d00  feat(verification): adapter interface, mock, live scaffold, retry+breaker+cache`","+","+---","+","+## Part A workflow service (Step 9)","+","+**User:** Step 9 — first add a small migration for `tier` and `escalatedAt` on `ReviewQueueItem`. Then build `services/review-workflow.ts` with `submitForReview`, `claimNext`, `approveItem`, `rejectItem`, `escalateOverdue`. All multi-step writes inside `prisma.$transaction`. Verification failure → announcement stays PENDING_REVIEW + best-effort audit.","+","+**Assistant:** Schema first: added `tier Int @default(1)` and `escalatedAt DateTime?` to `ReviewQueueItem`. Ran `npx prisma migrate dev --name add_escalation_fields` — Prisma created `20260506110950_add_escalation_fields`.","+","+Wrote `services/review-workflow.ts` (≈400 LOC). Four error classes (`SelfReviewError`, `WorkflowAuthorizationError`, `WorkflowStateError`, `WorkflowNotFoundError`) and five functions:","+","+- `submitForReview` — RBAC ADMIN/CONTENT_MANAGER. Inside one tx: read announcement (must be DRAFT, not deleted), bump to `PENDING_REVIEW`, create `ReviewQueueItem(authorId = user.id, tier = 1)`, audit `SUBMIT_FOR_REVIEW`, audit `NOTIFICATION_QUEUED` stub.","+- `claimNext` — RBAC ADMIN/COMPLIANCE_OFFICER. Pre-filter via `listPending({ excludeAuthorId: reviewer.id, limit: 1 })` to skip the claim entirely when nothing is visible. Then call `queue.claimNextItem`. Defence-in-depth post-check: if the SKIP LOCKED claim happened to grab a self-authored row (race vs. listPending), release the row back to PENDING and throw `SelfReviewError`.","+- `approveItem` — single tx. Re-read item with `include: { announcement: true }`, validate ownership/state/separation-of-duties. **Inlined** the queue update (`status → APPROVED`, decisionNote, decidedAt) instead of calling `queue.approve`, because the seeded library wraps its update in its own `prisma.$transaction` and Prisma forbids interactive nested transactions on a `TransactionClient`. Inlining preserves the atomicity guarantee. Then call `deps.verification.verify(...)` — APPROVED → announcement → PUBLISHED + publishedAt + `VERIFICATION_PASSED` audit; REJECTED → announcement → DRAFT + `VERIFICATION_REJECTED` audit. Transport throw → tx rolls back, then a best-effort `VERIFICATION_TRANSPORT_FAILED` audit is written outside the failed tx and the original error is rethrown. Workflow validation errors are excluded from the best-effort audit.","+- `rejectItem` — single tx. Same inline-and-atomic pattern: queue → REJECTED, announcement → DRAFT, audit `REJECTED`, audit `NOTIFICATION_QUEUED` stub.","+- `escalateOverdue(now = new Date())` — single tx. `findMany` for `PENDING && claimedBy = null && tier = 1 && createdAt < now - 48h`, `updateMany` to bump `tier = 2` + `escalatedAt = now`, then `createMany` audit rows. The function has no actor parameter (it's a system / scheduler op); the audit `userId` is attributed to the item's author so the AuditLog → User foreign key stays valid. Comment in the code calls this out.","+","+`npx tsc --noEmit` → clean. `npm test` → 4/4 files, 23/23 tests passing.","+","+- `0ee857f  feat(workflow): review-workflow service layer`","+","+---","+","+## API routes (Step 10)","+","+**User:** Step 10 — six App Router routes. Auth → RBAC → service call → JSON. Use `params: Promise<{ id: string }>`.","+","+**Assistant:** Read `node_modules/next/dist/docs/01-app/03-api-reference/03-file-conventions/route.md` to confirm the App Router signature (`AGENTS.md` warns: \"this is NOT the Next.js you know\"). Confirmed the Promise-wrapped `params` pattern.","+","+Added `reviewApproveSchema` and `reviewRejectSchema` to `lib/validations.ts` (Zod, single required string field each).","+","+Six routes, all following the existing pattern (auth → RBAC → JSON parse → Zod → service call → response). Workflow errors map to HTTP:","+- `WorkflowNotFoundError` → 404","+- `SelfReviewError` → 403 `SELF_REVIEW`","+- `WorkflowAuthorizationError` → 403 `FORBIDDEN`","+- `WorkflowStateError` → 409 `STATE_CONFLICT`","+- Verification transport / breaker errors (in `approve`) → 503 `VERIFICATION_UNAVAILABLE`","+","+Routes:","+- `POST /api/announcements/[id]/submit-for-review`","+- `GET  /api/review-queue`","+- `POST /api/review-queue/claim`","+- `POST /api/review-queue/[id]/approve` (body `{ note }`, wires `MockVerificationAdapter`)","+- `POST /api/review-queue/[id]/reject` (body `{ reason }`)","+- `POST /api/review-queue/escalate`","+","+`npx tsc --noEmit` → clean. `npm test` → 4/4 files, 23/23 passing.","+","+- `94560ab  feat(api): review-queue routes and submit-for-review`","+","+---","+","+## UI pages (Step 11)","+","+**User:** Step 11 — review-queue page (server), `ReviewActions` (client), status column on announcements list, sidebar link.","+","+**Assistant:**","+- `app/admin/review-queue/page.tsx` — server component, `redirect('/admin')` when `!can(user, 'review')`. Fetches both PENDING (excluding self-author) and the reviewer's own UNDER_REVIEW items in parallel so the table is the reviewer's full active surface (claim-candidates + decide-candidates). Reads via Prisma directly so the announcement title can be joined in one query — comment in the file explains this is a deliberate departure from `listPending`.","+- `app/admin/review-queue/ReviewActions.tsx` — client component, two modes: `claim-next` (single button POSTs `/api/review-queue/claim`); `decide` (per-row Approve / Reject with shared note input).","+- `app/admin/announcements/page.tsx` — added a `<span>` status badge on each row and a `SubmitForReviewButton` shown only when `status === 'DRAFT'` and `can(user, 'submitForReview')`.","+- `app/admin/announcements/SubmitForReviewButton.tsx` — client component, POSTs `/api/announcements/{id}/submit-for-review` and `router.refresh()` on success.","+- `app/admin/layout.tsx` — sidebar \"Review queue\" link visible only when `can(user, 'review')`.","+","+`npx tsc --noEmit` → clean. `npm test` → 4/4 files, 23/23 passing.","+","+- `5d423fb  feat(ui): review queue page and submit-for-review button`","+","+---","+","+## Tests (Step 12)","+","+**User:** Step 12 — four new test files: precedence, breaker, separation-of-duties, transactional rollback.","+","+**Assistant:**","+- `__tests__/verification-precedence.test.ts` — `StubAdapter` queues per-call responses. First call returns REJECTED → cached. Second call (same content) returns REJECTED via cache hit; inner is **not** invoked (`inner.calls === 1`). Negative case: editing `bodyEn` → different cache key → fresh APPROVED is honoured, inner called twice.","+- `__tests__/verification-breaker.test.ts` — injectable `Clock` whose `sleep` and `advance` mutate the shared `state.value`. Test 1: 5 failed `verify` calls (with `random: () => 0.5` to zero out jitter) → 15 inner calls (3 retries × 5) → 6th `verify` rejects with `breaker open` and inner is **not** called. Test 2: 29s past `openedAt` → still OPEN; 31s → next call probes (HALF_OPEN), succeeds → CLOSED → subsequent calls go through.","+- `__tests__/workflow-separation-of-duties.test.ts` — mocks `@/lib/review-queue/src` to simulate a SKIP LOCKED race grabbing a self-authored row. Asserts `claimNext` throws `SelfReviewError` AND that the row was released back to PENDING. Second test: `approveItem` short-circuits with `SelfReviewError` before any state mutation when `item.authorId === reviewer.id`.","+- `__tests__/workflow-transactional.test.ts` — `buildPrismaMock()` simulates rollback by buffering writes inside `$transaction` and dropping them on throw. (1) `submitForReview` with a failing audit → throws \"audit DB down\" and `committed === []`. (2) `approveItem` with verification rejecting `TransientVerificationError` → throws, `committed === []`, AND a `VERIFICATION_TRANSPORT_FAILED` audit was written via the **outside-tx** `prisma.auditLog.create`.","+","+Final test results:","+```","+ Test Files  8 passed (8)","+      Tests  31 passed (31)","+```","+","+(The Postgres-required race test runs because `DATABASE_URL` is set; otherwise `describe.skipIf` would skip it.)","+","+- `f614c89  test: verification precedence, breaker, separation-of-duties, transactional rollback`","+","+---","+","+## README (Step 13)","+","+**User:** Step 13 — Level 2 section to root README.","+","+**Assistant:** Appended a \"Level 2 — Review workflow, verification, race fix\" section: run flow, four seeded users (calls out `compliance@example.com`), env-var table, six route summaries with RBAC + behaviour, the verification adapter trio (mock with `__deny__` token / live with `TODO SPEC PENDING` / wrapper with cache + retry + breaker), test layout including the `DATABASE_URL`-required race test, and a Part C explanation. Eleven commits since `assessment-1`:","+","+```","+51c21fa docs: update README with Level 2 instructions","+f614c89 test: verification precedence, breaker, separation-of-duties, transactional rollback","+5d423fb feat(ui): review queue page and submit-for-review button","+94560ab feat(api): review-queue routes and submit-for-review","+0ee857f feat(workflow): review-workflow service layer","+47b1d00 feat(verification): adapter interface, mock, live scaffold, retry+breaker+cache","+a3d2054 feat(schema): add AnnouncementStatus, COMPLIANCE_OFFICER, RBAC actions","+313365d fix(review-queue): close TOCTOU race in claimNextItem with FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED","+b871372 test(review-queue): concurrent claim test reproduces TOCTOU race","+24ee099 feat(schema): add ReviewQueueItem model for seeded library","+aafabc0 install seeded review-queue","+```","+","+(A 12th commit, `chore: write verbatim ai session transcript`, is added immediately after this file is written.)","+","+**Tests:** 8 files / 31 tests passing. Race test runs against Postgres when `DATABASE_URL` is set.","+","+**Smoke:** golden path + deny path both verified end-to-end via the live dev server and direct DB inspection.","+","+**Disclosures:**","+- No `review-queue.patch` file existed; the seeded library was copied directly with a clear `install seeded review-queue` commit.","+- Step 7's migration name is timestamp-prefixed (`20260506110255_review_workflow`) per Prisma's default — older Level-1 migrations are hand-named without timestamps; left as Prisma generated since the user gave the exact `migrate dev --name` command.","+- `escalateOverdue` has no actor parameter; audit rows attribute to the item's author (FK requirement) — caller (route handler) enforces RBAC.","+- `approveItem` and `rejectItem` inline the queue-row update inside the workflow tx (rather than calling `queue.approve` / `queue.reject` directly) because the seeded library wraps its update in its own `prisma.$transaction` and Prisma forbids interactive nested transactions. The same status / claimedBy / state guards as the library are preserved inline; comment in the file calls this out.","+- The dev server's first run had a stale Prisma client (it started before `prisma generate` ran for the new schema). Restart resolved it. No code change required.","+","+## End of Level 2 session"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"194a584d-7b91-4d37-a14d-1ea5e5fb2306","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"/home/sameh/Downloads/level-2","sessionId":"6143e2c6-2304-4368-b72e-8b39bfc968b5","version":"2.1.131","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"delightful-sleeping-crane"}
